## 5:1-2 — verses 1-2 (shared commentary)

### Translation

# Tafsir of Surat al-Ma'idah

[It is Medinan]

Imam Ahmad said: Abu al-Nadr told us, Abu Mu'awiyah Shayban told us, from Layth, from Shahr ibn Hawshab, from Asma bint Yazid, who said: "I was holding the halter of al-Adba, the she-camel of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, when the whole of al-Ma'idah was sent down upon him, and it nearly broke the camel's foreleg from its weight."

Ibn Mardawayh narrated, through the hadith of Salih ibn Suhayl, from Asim al-Ahwal, who said: Umm Amr told me, from her paternal uncle, that he was traveling with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ when Surat al-Ma'idah was sent down upon him, and the neck of the riding beast broke from its weight.

Ahmad also said: Hasan told us, Ibn Lahi'ah told us, Huyayy ibn Abdullah told me, from Abu Abd al-Rahman al-Hubuli, from Abdullah ibn Amr, who said: "Surat al-Ma'idah was sent down upon the Messenger of Allah ﷺ while he was riding his mount, and it could not bear him, so he dismounted from it."

Ahmad alone transmitted this. Al-Tirmidhi narrated, from Qutaybah, from Abdullah ibn Wahb, from Huyayy, from Abu Abd al-Rahman, from Abdullah ibn Amr, who said: "The last surah to be sent down was Surat al-Ma'idah and al-Fath." Al-Tirmidhi then said: this is a gharib (singular), hasan (sound) hadith. It has also been reported from Ibn Abbas that he said: the last surah to be sent down was {When the help of Allah comes, and victory} [Surat al-Nasr: 1].

Al-Hakim narrated in his Mustadrak, through the route of Abdullah ibn Wahb with his chain, something similar to al-Tirmidhi's report, then said: sound according to the standard of the two Shaykhs (Bukhari and Muslim), though they did not extract it.

Al-Hakim also said: Abu al-Abbas Muhammad ibn Ya'qub told us, Bahr ibn Nasr told us, saying: it was read out to Abdullah ibn Wahb, Mu'awiyah ibn Salih informed me, from Abu al-Zahiriyyah, from Jubayr ibn Nufayr, who said: "I performed the pilgrimage and entered upon A'ishah, and she said to me: 'O Jubayr, do you recite al-Ma'idah?' I said: 'Yes.' She said: 'Indeed it is the last surah to be sent down, so whatever you find lawful in it, hold it lawful, and whatever you find forbidden in it, hold it forbidden.'" He then said: sound according to the standard of the two Shaykhs, though they did not extract it.

Imam Ahmad narrated it, from Abd al-Rahman ibn Mahdi, from Mu'awiyah ibn Salih, with an addition: "And I asked her about the character of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and she said: 'The Qur'an.'" Al-Nasa'i narrated it through the hadith of Ibn Mahdi.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: My father told us, Nu'aym ibn Hammad told us, Abdullah ibn al-Mubarak told us, Mis'ar told us, Ma'n and Awf, or one of them, told me, that a man came to Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) and said: "Give me a charge (advice)." He said: "When you hear Allah say {O you who believe}, lend it your ear, for it is either good He is commanding, or evil He is forbidding."

He also said: Ali ibn al-Husayn told us, Abd al-Rahman ibn Ibrahim, Duhaym, told us, al-Walid told us, al-Awza'i told us, from al-Zuhri, who said: "When Allah says {O you who believe}, do such and such, then the Prophet ﷺ is among them."

He told us: Ahmad ibn Sinan told us, Muhammad ibn Ubayd told us, al-A'mash told us, from Khaythamah, who said: "Everything in the Qur'an that reads {O you who believe} is, in the Torah, 'O you poor ones.'"

As for what he narrated from Zayd ibn Isma'il al-Sa'igh al-Baghdadi, Mu'awiyah, meaning Ibn Hisham, told us, from Isa ibn Rashid, from Ali ibn Budhaymah, from Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas, who said: "There is no verse in the Qur'an beginning {O you who believe} except that Ali is its master, its noble one, and its commander, and there is not one of the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ who was not rebuked somewhere in the Qur'an except Ali ibn Abi Talib, for he was never rebuked in any part of it." This is a gharib (singular) report, its wording contains objectionable elements, and there is reservation regarding its chain.

Al-Bukhari said: this Isa ibn Rashid is unknown (majhul), and his report is objectionable (munkar). I say: and Ali ibn Budhaymah, though trustworthy, was an extreme Shi'i, and his report in such a matter is suspect and thus not accepted. His statement, "and there remained none of the Companions except that he was rebuked in the Qur'an except Ali," seems to point to the verse commanding charity before private consultation, for more than one scholar has mentioned that no one acted upon it except Ali, and the verse {Have you feared to offer charity before your private consultation? Since you have not done so, and Allah has turned to you in mercy...} [Surat al-Mujadilah: 13] was sent down. But there is reservation as to whether this constitutes a rebuke, for it has been said that the command was recommended, not obligatory, and it was then abrogated for them before it was acted upon, so no failure to comply was seen from any of them. And his statement about Ali, "he was never rebuked in anything in the Qur'an," is likewise subject to reservation, for the verse in al-Anfal containing the rebuke over taking ransom encompassed all who had counseled taking it, and none was spared from it except Umar ibn al-Khattab, may Allah be pleased with him. From this, and from what preceded, the weakness of this report is known. And Allah knows best.

Ibn Jarir said: al-Muthanna told me, Abdullah ibn Salih told us, al-Layth told us, Yunus told me, saying: Muhammad ibn Muslim said: "I read the letter of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ which he wrote for Amr ibn Hazm when he sent him to Najran, and the letter was in the possession of Abu Bakr ibn Hazm. In it was: 'This is a clarification from Allah and His Messenger: {O you who believe, fulfill your contracts}'" and he wrote out the verses from it until reaching {Indeed Allah is swift in reckoning}.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: Abu Sa'id told us, Yunus ibn Bukayr told us, Muhammad ibn Ishaq told us, Abdullah ibn Abi Bakr ibn Muhammad ibn Amr ibn Hazm told me, from his father, who said: "This is the letter of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ that we have, which he wrote for Amr ibn Hazm when he sent him to Yemen to instruct its people in religion, teach them the Sunnah, and collect their charitable dues (sadaqat). He wrote him a letter and a charge, commanding him in it with his instructions. He wrote: 'In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. This is a letter from Allah and His Messenger: {O you who believe, fulfill your contracts}. A charge from Muhammad, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, to Amr ibn Hazm when he sent him to Yemen, commanding him to fear Allah in all his affairs, for Allah is with those who fear Him and those who do good.'"

His statement, Most High, {fulfill your contracts}: Ibn Abbas, Mujahid, and more than one other said: by "contracts" is meant covenants. Ibn Jarir related that there is consensus on this, saying: the covenants are what they used to pledge to one another regarding alliances and other matters. Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas, regarding His saying {O you who believe, fulfill your contracts}: by "covenants" is meant what Allah made lawful and what He made unlawful, and what He made obligatory and what He set as limits throughout the whole Qur'an, so do not betray and do not break faith. Then He emphasized this severely, saying: {And those who break the covenant of Allah after ratifying it, and sever what Allah has commanded to be joined...} up to His saying {the evil abode} [al-Ra'd: 25].

Al-Dahhak said: {fulfill your contracts} means: what Allah made lawful and what He made unlawful, and the covenant Allah took from whoever affirms belief in the Prophet ﷺ and the Book, that they fulfill the obligations Allah took upon them regarding the lawful and the unlawful.

Zayd ibn Aslam said: {fulfill your contracts}, he said: these are six: the covenant with Allah, the contract of alliance, the contract of partnership, the contract of sale, the contract of marriage, and the contract of an oath.

Muhammad ibn Ka'b said: they are five, among them: the alliance of the Jahiliyyah (pre-Islamic era) and the partnership of mutual investment.

Some of those who held that there is no option (khiyar) at the session of sale have used this verse, {fulfill your contracts}, as evidence, saying: this indicates the binding nature and fixity of the contract, which implies negating the option of the session. This is the position of Abu Hanifah and Malik. Al-Shafi'i, Ahmad ibn Hanbal, and the majority opposed them both, and the evidence for this is what is established in the two Sahihs, from Ibn Umar, who said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The two parties to a sale have the option (khiyar) as long as they have not parted." In a wording of al-Bukhari's: "When two men conduct a sale, each of them has the option as long as they have not parted." This is explicit in establishing the option of the session following the contract of sale, and this does not contradict the binding nature of the contract, but rather is among its requirements according to the Shari'ah, for adhering to it is part of the complete fulfillment of the contract.

His statement, Most High, {Lawful for you are the grazing livestock}: these are camels, cattle, and sheep. So said al-Hasan, Qatadah, and more than one other. Ibn Jarir said: and this is also the case among the Arabs.

Ibn Umar, Ibn Abbas, and others cited this verse as evidence for the permissibility of the fetus found dead in the belly of its mother when she is slaughtered. A hadith on this matter has come down in the Sunan works, related by Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, and Ibn Majah, through the route of Mujalid, from Abu al-Waddak Jabr ibn Nawf, from Abu Sa'id, who said: We said, "O Messenger of Allah, we slaughter the camel by cutting its throat, and we slaughter the cow or the sheep, and in its belly is a fetus. Should we discard it or eat it?" He said, "Eat it if you wish, for its slaughter is the slaughter of its mother." Al-Tirmidhi said: this is a hasan (sound) hadith.

Abu Dawud said: Muhammad ibn Yahya ibn Faris told us, Ishaq ibn Ibrahim told us, Attab ibn Bashir told us, Ubaydullah ibn Abi Ziyad al-Qaddah al-Makki told us, from Abu al-Zubayr, from Jabir ibn Abdullah, from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, who said: "The slaughter of the fetus is the slaughter of its mother." Abu Dawud alone related this.

Regarding His saying, {except what is recited to you}: Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas: he means by this the dead animal, blood, and the flesh of swine.

Qatadah said: he means by this the dead animal and that over which the name of Allah has not been mentioned.

The apparent meaning, and Allah knows best, is that what is intended is His saying: {Forbidden to you are dead animals, blood, the flesh of swine, that which is slaughtered in the name of other than Allah, the animal killed by strangling, that killed by a violent blow, that which dies by falling, that which is gored to death, that which is eaten by a wild beast}. For these, although they are among the grazing livestock, become forbidden through these accidental causes. This is why He said {except what you have properly slaughtered} and {that which is sacrificed to idols}, meaning: from among them, for it is forbidden and cannot afterward be remedied or corrected. This is why the Almighty said: {Lawful to you are the beasts of cattle, except what is recited to you}, meaning: except what will be recited to you concerning the prohibition of some of them under certain circumstances.

His saying, {while not making game lawful when you are in the state of pilgrim sanctity (ihram)}: some said this is in the accusative case as a circumstantial clause (hal). What is meant by "cattle" is what encompasses the domestic animals, camels, cows, and sheep, and what encompasses the wild ones, such as gazelles, wild cattle, and wild asses. So what was mentioned earlier was excepted from the domestic animals, and hunted game during the state of ihram was excepted from the wild ones.

It was also said: what is meant is, "We have made lawful for you the cattle, except what has been excepted, for one who has committed himself to the prohibition of hunting, which is forbidden," similar to His saying: {But whoever is compelled by necessity, not desiring it nor exceeding the limit}, meaning: We permitted the eating of dead meat for one in extreme necessity, on condition that he not be desiring it or transgressing the limit. That is, just as We have made the cattle lawful for you in all circumstances, so forbid hunting during the state of ihram, for Allah has decreed this, and He is the Wise in all that He commands and forbids. This is why He said: {Indeed, Allah decrees whatever He wills}.

Then He said: {O you who believe, do not violate the sacred rites of Allah}. Ibn Abbas said: he means by this the rites of the Hajj.

Mujahid said: al-Safa and al-Marwah, the sacrificial animal (hady), and the sacrificial camels (budn) are among the sacred rites of Allah.

It was also said: the sacred rites of Allah are His prohibited things, which He has forbidden, meaning: do not violate the prohibited things of Allah which He, the Almighty, has forbidden. This is why He, the Almighty, said, {nor the sacred month}, meaning by this its inviolability and the acknowledgment of its sanctity, and the abandonment of what Allah has forbidden engaging in during it, namely initiating fighting, and the emphasis on avoiding forbidden things, as the Almighty said: {They ask you about the sacred month, about fighting therein. Say: Fighting therein is a great matter} [al-Baqarah: 217], and the Almighty said: {Indeed, the number of months with Allah is twelve months, in the record of Allah since the day He created the heavens and the earth. Of these, four are sacred. That is the correct religion, so do not wrong yourselves during them} [al-Tawbah: 36].

In Sahih al-Bukhari, from Abu Bakrah, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said during the Farewell Pilgrimage: "Indeed, time has come full circle as it was on the day Allah created the heavens and the earth. The year is twelve months, of which four are sacred: three consecutive ones, Dhu al-Qa'dah, Dhu al-Hijjah, and al-Muharram, and Rajab Mudar, which is between Jumada and Sha'ban."

This indicates the continuation of their sanctity until the final period, as is the position of a group of the early scholars (salaf).

Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas, regarding His saying {nor the sacred month}: he means, do not consider fighting therein permissible. Muqatil ibn Hayyan and Abd al-Karim ibn Malik al-Jazari said likewise, and Ibn Jarir also chose this view. The majority, however, held that this ruling has been abrogated, and that it is permissible to initiate fighting during the sacred months. They argued from His saying: {Then when the sacred months have passed, kill the polytheists wherever you find them} [al-Tawbah: 5]. They said: what is meant is the four months of respite granted, {kill the polytheists wherever you find them}. They said: no sacred month was excepted from the others.

Imam Abu Ja'far (may Allah have mercy on him) related the consensus that Allah has made lawful fighting against the people of polytheism during the sacred months as well as the other months of the year. He said: likewise they agreed that if a polytheist were to hang around his neck or his forearms the bark of every tree of the sacred precinct (al-Haram), that would not grant him safety from being killed, if he had not previously secured a covenant of protection (dhimmah) or safe conduct from the Muslims. This matter has further discussion elsewhere, in a more extensive place than this.

His saying, {nor the sacrificial animals nor the garlanded ones}, means: do not abandon the sending of sacrificial offerings to the House, for in it is honor given to the sacred rites of Allah, and do not abandon garlanding them around their necks so that they may be distinguished from the rest of the cattle, and so that it be known that they are offerings intended for the Ka'bah, so that whoever intends them harm avoids them, and so that whoever sees them is moved to bring the like of them. For whoever calls to an offering has a reward like the reward of those who follow him in it, without anything being diminished from their own rewards. This is why, when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed the Hajj, he spent the night at Dhu al-Hulayfah, which is the valley of al-Aqiq. When morning came he went around to his wives, who were nine, then he performed ghusl, applied perfume, and prayed two rak'ahs. Then he marked his sacrificial animal (by making an incision in its hump) and garlanded it, and entered the state of ihram for both Hajj and Umrah together. His sacrificial animals were many camels, exceeding sixty, of the finest forms and colors, as the Almighty said: {That is so. And whoever honors the sacred rites of Allah, it is from the piety of hearts} [al-Hajj: 32].

Some of the early scholars said: honoring them means regarding them as excellent and choosing them to be fattened.

Ali ibn Abi Talib said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ commanded us to examine closely the eye and the ear. Related by the compilers of the Sunan.

Muqatil ibn Hayyan said, regarding {nor the garlanded ones}: do not consider them permissible. The people of pre-Islamic ignorance (Jahiliyyah), when they went out from their homelands outside the sacred months, would garland themselves with hair and wool, and the polytheists of the sacred precinct would garland themselves with the bark of the trees of the Haram, and thereby be granted safety.

Related by Ibn Abi Hatim, who then said: Muhammad ibn Ammar told us, Sa'id ibn Sulayman told us, Abbad ibn al-Awwam told us, from Sufyan ibn Husayn, from al-Hakam, from Mujahid, from Ibn Abbas, who said: two verses of this surah have been abrogated: the verse of the garlanded animals, and His saying {But if they come to you, judge between them or turn away from them} [al-Ma'idah: 42].

And al-Mundhir ibn Shadhan told us, Zakariyya ibn Adi told us, Muhammad ibn Abi Adi told us, from Ibn Awn, who said: I said to al-Hasan, "Has anything from al-Ma'idah been abrogated?" He said, "No."

Ata said: they used to garland themselves with the trees of the sacred precinct and thereby be granted safety, so Allah forbade the cutting of its trees. Mutarrif ibn Abdullah said likewise.

His saying, {nor those making for the sacred House, seeking bounty from their Lord and His good pleasure}, means: do not consider it permissible to fight those heading toward the sacred House of Allah, which whoever enters is safe, and likewise whoever makes for it seeking the bounty of Allah and desiring His good pleasure, so do not turn them away, nor prevent them, nor provoke them.

Mujahid, Ata, Abu al-Aliyah, Mutarrif ibn Abdullah, Abdullah ibn Ubayd ibn Umayr, al-Rabi ibn Anas, Qatadah, and Muqatil ibn Hayyan said regarding His saying {seeking bounty from their Lord}: he means by this trade.

This is as was mentioned earlier regarding His saying: {There is no blame upon you in seeking bounty from your Lord} [al-Baqarah: 198].

And His saying {and good pleasure}: Ibn Abbas said: they seek to please Allah through their pilgrimage.

'Ikrimah, al-Suddi, and Ibn Jurayj mentioned that this verse was revealed concerning al-Hutam ibn Hind al-Bakri, who had raided the grazing livestock of Medina. Then, in the following year, he performed 'umrah to the House, and some of the Companions wanted to intercept him on his way to the House, so God, mighty and exalted, revealed: {nor those coming to the Sacred House seeking bounty from their Lord and His pleasure}.

Ibn Jarir related a consensus that it is permissible to kill a polytheist, if he has no guarantee of safety, even if he intends the Sacred House or the Farthest Mosque (Jerusalem); for this ruling has been abrogated with respect to them, and God knows best. As for one who intends it with the aim of desecration, associating partners with God there, and disbelief in it, such a person is to be barred, as He, exalted is He, said: {O you who believe, the polytheists are truly impure, so let them not approach the Sacred Mosque after this year of theirs} [al-Tawbah: 28]. For this reason, the Messenger of God ﷺ sent 'Ali in the year nine, when he had appointed al-Siddiq (Abu Bakr) over the pilgrims, and commanded him to proclaim, on behalf of the Messenger of God ﷺ, the declaration of disavowal (Bara'ah), and that no polytheist should perform the pilgrimage after that year, and that no one should circle the House naked.

['Ali] ibn Abi Talhah said, on the authority of Ibn 'Abbas, regarding His words: {nor those coming to the Sacred House}: meaning whoever heads toward the Sacred House. The believers and the polytheists used to make pilgrimage to the Sacred House, so God forbade the believers from preventing anyone, believer or disbeliever, from making pilgrimage to the House or from obstructing him. Then God revealed after it: {the polytheists are truly impure, so let them not approach the Sacred Mosque after this year of theirs} [al-Tawbah: 28], and He, exalted is He, said: {It is not for the polytheists to maintain the mosques of God} [al-Tawbah: 17], and He said: {Only he who believes in God and the Last Day maintains the mosques of God} [al-Tawbah: 18]. Thus He excluded the polytheists from the Sacred Mosque.

'Abd al-Razzaq said: Ma'mar narrated to us, from Qatadah, regarding His words: {nor the garlanded animals, nor those coming to the Sacred House}, he said: this is abrogated. In the Jahiliyyah (pre-Islamic era), a man, when he left his home intending the pilgrimage, would hang a garland of tree branches around his neck, and no one would molest him; and when he returned, he would hang a garland of hair, and no one would molest him. In those days the polytheist was not barred from the House. Then they were commanded not to fight during the sacred month nor near the House, and this was abrogated by His words: {so kill the polytheists wherever you find them} [al-Tawbah: 5].

Ibn Jarir preferred that the meaning of His words {nor the garlanded animals} is: if they garland an animal from the sanctuary, then grant it safety. He said: the Arabs never ceased to reproach whoever violated that. The poet said:

"Did you not kill the two hirja-birds when they appeared exposed to you... as the braided bark-cord passes between the hands?"

Regarding His words: {but when you are free from ihram, then hunt}, that is: when you have finished with your state of ihram and have exited it, We have made lawful for you what was forbidden to you during ihram, namely hunting. This is a command coming after a prohibition, and the correct position, established upon careful examination, is that the ruling reverts to what it was before the prohibition: if it had been obligatory, it returns to being obligatory; if recommended, then recommended; or if permissible, then permissible. Whoever says that it indicates obligation is refuted by many verses, and whoever says that it indicates mere permissibility is refuted by other verses. What reconciles all the evidences is what we have mentioned, as some scholars of legal theory have preferred, and God knows best.

Regarding His words: {And let not the hatred of a people who barred you from the Sacred Mosque incite you to transgress}. Some reciters read "an saddukum" with a fatha on the alif of "an," and its meaning is clear: let not the hatred of a people who had barred you from reaching the Sacred Mosque (that being in the year of al-Hudaybiyyah) lead you to transgress against God's ruling concerning them, retaliating against them unjustly and with hostility. Rather, judge according to what God has commanded you of justice toward everyone. This verse is like what will come later in His words: {And let not the hatred of a people lead you to injustice. Be just, that is nearer to piety} [al-Ma'idah: 8], meaning: let not hatred of certain peoples carry you to abandon justice, for justice is obligatory upon everyone, toward everyone, in every circumstance.

Some of the salaf (early generations) said: "You have not dealt with one who disobeys God concerning you better than by obeying God concerning him," and by justice the heavens and the earth stand firm.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Zayd ibn Aslam said: The Messenger of God ﷺ was at al-Hudaybiyyah along with his Companions, when the polytheists had barred them from the House, and that weighed heavily upon them. Then some polytheists from the people of the east passed by them, intending 'umrah. The Companions of the Prophet ﷺ said: "Let us bar these as their companions barred us." So God revealed this verse.

Al-shana'an means hatred, as Ibn 'Abbas and others said. It is a verbal noun from "shana'tuhu ashna'uhu shana'an," with the vowel movement, like their sayings "jamazan," "darajan," and "rafalan," from "jamaza," "daraja," and "rafala." Ibn Jarir said: some Arabs drop the vowel movement in "shana'an," saying "shannan." He said: I do not know of anyone who recited it that way. From this comes the poet's saying:

"Life is nothing but what you love and desire, even if the one full of hatred blames and reproaches for it..."

Regarding His words: {And cooperate in righteousness and piety, but do not cooperate in sin and transgression}, God, exalted is He, commands His believing servants to cooperate in doing good deeds, which is righteousness (birr), and in abandoning wrongdoing, which is piety (taqwa), and He forbids them from mutual support in falsehood.

And from cooperating in sins and forbidden things.

Ibn Jarir said: sin (ithm) is abandoning what God has commanded to be done, and transgression ('udwan) is exceeding the limits God has set in your religion, and exceeding what He has imposed upon you regarding yourselves and others.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Anas ibn Malik said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Help your brother, whether he is a wrongdoer or one wronged." It was said: "O Messenger of God, we help him when he is wronged, but how do we help him when he is a wrongdoer?" He said: "You restrain him, you prevent him; that is helping him."

Al-Bukhari alone narrated it from the hadith of Hushaym in a similar manner, and both of them (al-Bukhari and Muslim) narrated it via the route of Thabit, from Anas, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Help your brother, whether he is a wrongdoer or one wronged." It was said: "O Messenger of God, we help him when he is wronged, but how do we help him when he is a wrongdoer?" He said: "You prevent him from wrongdoing; that is your helping him."

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] a man among the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ said: "The believer who mixes with people and bears patiently their harm has a greater reward than the one who does not mix with people and does not bear patiently their harm."

Ahmad also narrated it in the Musnad of 'Abdullah ibn 'Umar: [chain] a shaykh among the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ [al-A'mash said: he is Ibn 'Umar, from the Prophet ﷺ] that he said: "The believer who mixes with people and bears patiently their harm is better than the one who does not mix with them and does not bear patiently their harm."

Al-Tirmidhi likewise narrated it from the hadith of Shu'bah, and Ibn Majah from the route of Ishaq ibn Yusuf, both of them from al-A'mash, with it.

Al-Hafiz Abu Bakr al-Bazzar said: [chain] 'Abdullah said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "The one who guides to good is like the one who does it." Then he said: "We do not know it to be narrated except by this chain."

I (Ibn Kathir) say: it has a corroborating witness in the Sahih: "Whoever calls to guidance will have a reward equal to the rewards of those who follow him, until the Day of Resurrection, without that diminishing their rewards in any way; and whoever calls to misguidance will bear a sin equal to the sins of those who follow him, until the Day of Resurrection, without that diminishing their sins in any way."

Abu al-Qasim al-Tabarani said: [chain] Abu al-Hasan Nimran ibn Mukhammar narrated to him that the Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Whoever walks with a wrongdoer to help him, while knowing that he is a wrongdoer, has gone out of Islam."

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

تَفْسِيرُ سُورَةِ الْمَائِدَةِ

[وَهِيَ مَدَنِيَّةٌ] 

قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو النَّضر، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ شَيْبان، عَنْ لَيْث، عَنْ شَهر بْنِ حَوْشَب، عَنْ أَسْمَاءَ بِنْتِ يَزِيدَ قَالَتْ: إِنِّي لَآخِذَةٌ بزِمَام العَضْباء ناقةِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ، إِذْ نَزَلَتْ عَلَيْهِ الْمَائِدَةُ كُلُّهَا، وَكَادَتْ مِنْ ثِقْلِهَا تَدُقّ عَضُد الناقةَ .

وَرَوَى ابْنُ مَرْدُويه مِنْ حَدِيثِ صَالِحِ بنِ سُهَيْل، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ الْأَحْوَلِ قَالَ: حَدَّثَتْنِي أُمُّ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ عَمِّهَا؛ أَنَّهُ كَانَ فِي مَسِير مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ، فنزلت عَلَيْهِ سُورَةُ الْمَائِدَةِ، فاندَقَّ عُنُق الرَّاحِلَةِ مِنْ ثِقْلِهَا .

وَقَالَ أَحْمَدُ أَيْضًا: حَدَّثَنَا حَسَنٌ، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ لَهِيعَة، حَدَّثَنِي حُيَيُّ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الحُبُلي عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو قَالَ: أُنْزِلَتْ عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ سُورَةُ الْمَائِدَةِ وَهُوَ رَاكِبٌ عَلَى رَاحِلَتِهِ، فَلَمْ تَسْتَطِعْ أَنْ تَحْمِلَهُ، فَنَزَلَ عَنْهَا.

تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ أَحْمَدُ وَقَدْ رَوَى التِّرْمِذِيُّ عَنْ قُتَيْبَة، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ وَهْب، عَنْ حُيَيٍّ، عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو قَالَ: آخِرُ سُورَةٍ أُنْزِلَتْ: سُورَةُ الْمَائِدَةِ وَالْفَتْحِ، ثُمَّ قَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: هَذَا حَدِيثٌ غَرِيبٌ حَسَنٌ. وَقَدْ رُوِيَ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: آخِرُ سُورَةٍ أُنْزِلَتْ: " إِذَا جَاءَ نَصْرُ اللَّهِ وَالْفَتْحُ " [سُورَةُ النَّصْرِ: ١] .

وَقَدْ رَوَى الْحَاكِمُ فِي مُسْتَدْرَكِهِ، مِنْ طَرِيقِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ وَهْبٍ بِإِسْنَادِهِ نَحْوَ رِوَايَةِ التِّرْمِذِيِّ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: صَحِيحٌ عَلَى شَرْطِ الشَّيْخَيْنِ وَلَمْ يُخَرِّجَاهُ .

وَقَالَ الْحَاكِمُ أَيْضًا: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْعَبَّاسِ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَعْقُوبَ، حَدَّثَنَا بَحْرُ بْنُ نَصْرٍ قَالَ: قُرئ عَلَى عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ وَهْب، أَخْبَرَنِي مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الزَّاهِرِيَّةِ، عَنْ جُبَيْرِ بْنِ نُفَيْر قَالَ: حَجَجْتُ فَدَخَلْتُ عَلَى عَائِشَةَ، فَقَالَتْ لِي: يَا جُبَيْرُ، تَقْرَأُ الْمَائِدَةَ؟ فَقُلْتُ: نَعَمْ. فَقَالَتْ: أَمَا إِنَّهَا آخِرُ سُورَةٍ نَزَلَتْ فَمَا وَجَدْتُمْ فِيهَا مِنْ حَلَالٍ فَاسْتَحِلُّوهُ، وَمَا وَجَدْتُمْ فِيهَا من حرام فحرموه. ثم قال: صَحِيحٌ عَلَى شَرْطِ الشَّيْخَيْنِ وَلَمْ يُخَرِّجَاهُ.

وَرَوَاهُ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، وَزَادَ: وَسَأَلْتُهَا عَنْ خُلُق رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ، فَقَالَتِ: الْقُرْآنُ. وَرَاوَهُ النَّسَائِيُّ مِنْ حَدِيثِ ابْنِ مَهْدِيٍّ .

* *

قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا نُعَيْم بْنُ حَمَّادٍ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ الْمُبَارَكِ، حَدَّثَنَا مِسْعَر، حَدَّثَنِي مَعْن وعَوْف -أَوْ: أَحَدِهِمَا-أَنَّ رَجُلًا أَتَى عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ مَسْعُودٍ [رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ] فَقَالَ: اعْهَدْ إليَّ. فَقَالَ: إِذَا سَمِعْتَ اللَّهَ يَقُولُ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا﴾ فارْعِها سَمْعَك، فَإِنَّهُ خَيْر يَأْمُرُ بِهِ، أَوْ شَر يَنْهَى عَنْهُ.

وَقَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ -دُحيم-حَدَّثَنَا الْوَلِيدُ، حَدَّثَنَا الْأَوْزَاعِيُّ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ قَالَ: إِذَا قَالَ اللَّهُ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا﴾ افْعَلُوا، فَالنَّبِيُّ ﷺ مِنْهُمْ.

وَحَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ سنَان، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عُبيد حَدَّثَنَا الْأَعْمَشُ، عَنْ خَيْثَمَة قَالَ: كُلُّ شَيْءٍ فِي الْقُرْآنِ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا﴾ فَهُوَ فِي التَّوْرَاةِ: "يَا يها الْمَسَاكِينُ".

فَأَمَّا مَا رَوَاهُ عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ الصَّائِغِ الْبَغْدَادِيِّ، حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاوِيَةُ -يَعْنِي: ابْنَ هِشَامٍ-عَنْ عِيسَى بْنِ رَاشِدٍ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ بُذَيْمَة، عَنْ عِكْرِمة، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: مَا فِي الْقُرْآنِ آيَةُ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا﴾ إِلَّا أَنَّ عَلِيًّا سَيِّدُهَا وَشَرِيفُهَا وَأَمِيرُهَا، وَمَا مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ أَحَدٌ إِلَّا قَدْ عُوتِبَ فِي الْقُرْآنِ إِلَّا عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَالِبٍ، فَإِنَّهُ لَمْ يعاتبْ فِي شَيْءٍ مِنْهُ. فَهُوَ أَثَرٌ غَرِيبٌ وَلَفْظُهُ فِيهِ نَكَارَةٌ، وَفِي إِسْنَادِهِ نَظَرٌ.

قَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ: عِيسَى بْنُ رَاشِدٍ هَذَا مَجْهُولٌ، وَخَبَرُهُ مُنْكَرٌ. قُلْتُ: وَعَلِيُّ بْنُ بَذِيمَةَ -وَإِنْ كَانَ ثِقَةً-إِلَّا أَنَّهُ شِيعِيٌّ غالٍ، وَخَبَرُهُ فِي مِثْلِ هَذَا فِيهِ تُهمة فَلَا يُقْبَلُ. وَقَوْلُهُ: "وَلَمْ يَبْقَ أَحَدٌ مِنَ الصَّحَابَةِ إِلَّا عُوتِبَ فِي الْقُرْآنِ إِلَّا عَلِيًّا" إِنَّمَا يُشِيرُ بِهِ إِلَى الْآيَةِ الْآمِرَةِ بِالصَّدَقَةِ بَيْنَ يَدَيِ النَّجْوَى، فَإِنَّهُ قَدْ ذَكَر غَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ أَنَّهُ لَمْ يَعْمَلْ بِهَا أَحَدٌ إِلَّا عليٌّ، وَنَزَلَ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿أَأَشْفَقْتُمْ أَنْ تُقَدِّمُوا بَيْنَ يَدَيْ نَجْوَاكُمْ صَدَقَاتٍ فَإِذْ لَمْ تَفْعَلُوا وَتَابَ اللَّهُ﴾ الْآيَةَ [سُورَةُ الْمُجَادِلَةِ: ١٣] وَفِي كَوْنِ هَذَا عِتَابًا نَظَرٌ؛ فَإِنَّهُ قَدْ قِيلَ: إِنَّ الْأَمْرَ كَانَ نَدْبًا لَا إِيجَابًا، ثُمَّ قَدْ نُسِخَ ذَلِكَ عَنْهُمْ قَبْلَ الْفِعْلِ، فَلَمْ يَرَ مِنْ أَحَدٍ مِنْهُمْ خِلَافَهُ. وَقَوْلُهُ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ: "إِنَّهُ لَمْ يُعَاتَبْ فِي شَيْءٍ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ" فِيهِ نَظَرٌ أَيْضًا؛ فَإِنَّ الْآيَةَ الَّتِي فِي الْأَنْفَالِ الَّتِي فِيهَا الْمُعَاتَبَةُ عَلَى أَخْذِ الفِداء عَمَّت جَمِيعَ مَنْ أَشَارَ بِأَخْذِهِ، وَلَمْ يَسْلَمْ مِنْهَا إِلَّا عُمر بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، فَعُلِمَ بِهَذَا، وَبِمَا تَقَدَّمَ ضَعفُ هَذَا الْأَثَرِ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنِي الْمُثَنَّى، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ صَالِحٍ، حَدَّثَنَا اللَّيْث، حَدَّثَنِي يُونُسُ قَالَ: قَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ: قَرَأْتُ كِتَابَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ الَّذِي كُتب لِعَمْرِو بْنِ حَزْم حِينَ بَعَثَهُ إِلَى نَجْران، وَكَانَ الْكِتَابُ عِنْدَ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ حَزْمٍ، فِيهِ: هَذَا بَيَانٌ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَوْفُوا بِالْعُقُودِ﴾ فَكَتَبَ الْآيَاتِ مِنْهَا حَتَّى بَلَغَ: ﴿إِنَّ اللَّهَ سَرِيعُ الْحِسَابِ﴾ .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَعِيدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا يُونُسُ بْنُ بُكَيْر، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ حَزْمٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ: هَذَا كتابُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ عِنْدَنَا، الَّذِي كَتَبَهُ لِعَمْرِو بْنِ حَزْم، حِينَ بَعَثَهُ إِلَى الْيَمَنِ يُفَقه أَهْلَهَا وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ السُّنَّةَ، وَيَأْخُذَ صَدَقَاتِهِمْ. فَكَتَبَ لَهُ كِتَابًا وَعَهْدًا، وَأَمَرَهُ فِيهِ بِأَمْرِهِ، فَكَتَبَ: " بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ، هَذَا كِتَابٌ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَوْفُوا بِالْعُقُودِ﴾ عَهْدٌ مِنْ مُحَمَّدٍ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ لِعَمْرِو بْنِ حَزْمٍ، حِينَ بَعَثَهُ إِلَى الْيَمَنِ، أَمَرَهُ بِتَقْوَى اللَّهِ فِي أَمْرِهِ كُلِّهِ، فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ مَعَ الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ مُحْسِنُونَ" .

قَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى ﴿أَوْفُوا بِالْعُقُودِ﴾ قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ وَمُجَاهِدٌ وَغَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ: يَعْنِي بِالْعُقُودِ: الْعُهُودَ. وَحَكَى ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ الْإِجْمَاعَ عَلَى ذَلِكَ قَالَ: وَالْعُهُودُ مَا كَانُوا يَتَعَاهَدُونَ عَلَيْهِ مِنَ الْحِلْفِ وَغَيْرِهِ. وَقَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَوْفُوا بِالْعُقُودِ﴾ يَعْنِي بِالْعُهُودِ: يَعْنِي مَا أَحَلَّ اللَّهُ وَمَا حَرَّمَ، وَمَا فَرَضَ وَمَا حَد فِي الْقُرْآنِ كُلِّهِ، فَلَا تَغْدِرُوا وَلَا تَنْكُثُوا، ثُمَّ شَدَّدَ فِي ذَلِكَ فَقَالَ: ﴿وَالَّذِينَ يَنْقُضُونَ عَهْدَ اللَّهِ مِنْ بَعْدِ مِيثَاقِهِ وَيَقْطَعُونَ مَا أَمَرَ اللَّهُ بِهِ أَنْ يُوصَلَ﴾ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿سُوءُ الدَّارِ﴾ [الرَّعْدِ: ٢٥] .

وَقَالَ الضَّحَّاكُ: ﴿أَوْفُوا بِالْعُقُودِ﴾ قَالَ: مَا أَحَلَّ اللَّهُ وَمَا حَرَّمَ وَمَا أَخَذَ اللَّهُ مِنَ الْمِيثَاقِ عَلَى مَنْ أَقَرَّ بِالْإِيمَانِ بِالنَّبِيِّ [ﷺ] وَالْكِتَابِ أَنْ يُوفُوا بِمَا أَخَذَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنَ الْفَرَائِضِ مِنَ الْحَلَالِ وَالْحَرَامِ.

وَقَالَ زَيْدُ بْنُ أَسْلَمَ: ﴿أَوْفُوا بِالْعُقُودِ﴾ قَالَ: هِيَ سِتَّةٌ: عَهْدُ اللَّهِ، وَعَقْدُ الْحِلْفِ، وَعَقْدُ الشَّرِكَةِ، وَعَقْدُ الْبَيْعِ، وَعَقْدِ النِّكَاحِ، وَعَقْدِ الْيَمِينِ.

وَقَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ كَعْبٍ: هِيَ خَمْسَةٌ مِنْهَا: حِلْفُ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ، وَشَرِكَةُ الْمُفَاوَضَةِ.

وَقَدِ اسْتَدَلَّ بَعْضُ مَنْ ذَهَبَ إِلَى أَنَّهُ لَا خِيَارَ فِي مَجْلِسِ الْبَيْعِ بِهَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: ﴿أَوْفُوا بِالْعُقُودِ﴾ قَالَ: فَهَذَا يَدُلُّ عَلَى لُزُومِ الْعَقْدِ وَثُبُوتِهِ، فَيَقْتَضِي نَفْيَ خِيَارِ الْمَجْلِسِ، وَهَذَا مَذْهَبُ أَبِي حَنِيفَةَ، وَمَالِكٍ. وَخَالَفَهُمَا الشَّافِعِيُّ وَأَحْمَدُ بْنُ حَنْبَلٍ وَالْجُمْهُورُ، وَالْحُجَّةُ فِي ذَلِكَ مَا ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "البَيِّعان بِالْخِيَارِ مَا لَمْ يَتَفرَّقا" وَفِي لفْظ لِلْبُخَارِيِّ: "إِذَا تَبَايَعَ الرَّجُلَانِ فَكُلُّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا بِالْخِيَارِ مَا لَمْ يَتَفَرَّقَا" وَهَذَا صَرِيحٌ فِي إِثْبَاتِ خِيَارِ الْمَجْلِسِ الْمُتَعَقِّبِ لِعَقْدِ الْبَيْعِ، وَلَيْسَ هَذَا مُنَافِيًا لِلُزُومِ الْعَقْدِ، بَلْ هُوَ مِنْ مُقْتَضَيَاتِهِ شَرْعًا، فَالْتِزَامُهُ مِنْ تَمَامِ الْوَفَاءِ بِالْعَقْدِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿أُحِلَّتْ لَكُمْ بَهِيمَةُ الأنْعَامِ﴾ هِيَ: الْإِبِلُ وَالْبَقَرُ، وَالْغَنَمُ. قَالَهُ الْحَسَنُ وَقَتَادَةُ وَغَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ. قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: وَكَذَلِكَ هُوَ عِنْدَ الْعَرَبِ. وَقَدِ اسْتَدَلَّ ابْنُ عُمَرَ، وَابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ، وَغَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ بِهَذِهِ الْآيَةِ عَلَى إِبَاحَةِ الْجَنِينِ إِذَا وُجِدَ مَيِّتًا فِي بَطْنِ أُمِّهِ إِذَا ذُبِحَتْ، وَقَدْ وَرَدَ فِي ذَلِكَ حَدِيثٌ فِي السُّنَنِ، رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ، مِنْ طَرِيقِ مُجالد، عَنْ أَبِي الودَّاك جَبْرِ بْنِ نَوْف، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: قُلْنَا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، نَنْحَرُ النَّاقَةَ، وَنَذْبَحُ الْبَقَرَةَ أَوِ الشَّاةَ فِي بَطْنِهَا الْجَنِينُ، أَنُلْقِيهِ أَمْ نَأْكُلُهُ؟ فَقَالَ: "كُلُوهُ إِنْ شِئْتُمْ؛ فَإِنَّ ذَكَاتَهُ ذَكَاةُ أُمِّهِ". وَقَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: حَدِيثٌ حَسَنٌ .

[وَ] قَالَ أَبُو دَاوُدَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى بْنِ فَارِسٍ، حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، حَدَّثَنَا عَتَّاب بْنُ بَشِيرٍ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبِيدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ أَبِي زِيَادٍ الْقَدَّاحُ الْمَكِّيُّ، عَنْ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: " ذَكَاةُ الْجَنِينِ ذَكَاةُ أُمِّهِ". تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ أَبُو دَاوُدَ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿إِلا مَا يُتْلَى عَلَيْكُمْ﴾ قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: يَعْنِي بِذَلِكَ: الْمَيْتَةَ، وَالدَّمَ، وَلَحْمَ الْخِنْزِيرِ.

وَقَالَ قَتَادَةُ: يَعْنِي بِذَلِكَ الْمَيْتَةَ، وَمَا لَمْ يُذْكَرِ اسْمُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ.

وَالظَّاهِرُ -وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ-أَنَّ الْمُرَادَ بِذَلِكَ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَيْتَةُ وَالدَّمُ وَلَحْمُ الْخِنزيرِ وَمَا أُهِلَّ لِغَيْرِ اللَّهِ بِهِ وَالْمُنْخَنِقَةُ وَالْمَوْقُوذَةُ وَالْمُتَرَدِّيَةُ وَالنَّطِيحَةُ وَمَا أَكَلَ السَّبُعُ﴾ فَإِنَّ هَذِهِ وَإِنْ كَانَتْ مِنَ الْأَنْعَامِ إِلَّا أَنَّهَا تَحْرُمُ بِهَذِهِ الْعَوَارِضِ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿إِلا مَا ذَكَّيْتُمْ وَمَا ذُبِحَ عَلَى النُّصُبِ﴾ يَعْنِي: مِنْهَا. فَإِنَّهُ حَرَامٌ لَا يُمْكِنُ اسْتِدْرَاكُهُ، وتلاحقُه؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿أُحِلَّتْ لَكُمْ بَهِيمَةُ الأنْعَامِ إِلا مَا يُتْلَى عَلَيْكُمْ﴾ أَيْ: إِلَّا مَا سَيُتْلَى عَلَيْكُمْ مِنْ تَحْرِيمِ بعضها في بعض الأحوال.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿غَيْرَ مُحِلِّي الصَّيْدِ وَأَنْتُمْ حُرُمٌ﴾ قَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ: هَذَا مَنْصُوبٌ عَلَى الْحَالِ. وَالْمُرَادُ مِنَ الْأَنْعَامِ: مَا يَعُمُّ الْإِنْسِيَّ مِنَ الْإِبِلِ وَالْبَقَرِ وَالْغَنَمِ، وَمَا يَعُمُّ الْوَحْشِيَّ كَالظِّبَاءِ وَالْبَقَرِ وَالْحُمُرِ، فَاسْتَثْنَى مِنَ الْإِنْسِيِّ مَا تَقَدَّمَ، وَاسْتَثْنَى مِنَ الْوَحْشِيِّ الصَّيْدَ فِي حَالِ الْإِحْرَامِ.

وَقِيلَ: الْمُرَادُ [أَحْلَلْنَا لَكُمُ الْأَنْعَامَ إِلَّا مَا اسْتُثْنِيَ لِمَنِ الْتَزَمَ تَحْرِيمَ الصَّيْدِ وَهُوَ حَرَامٌ، كَقَوْلِهِ: ﴿فَمَنِ اضْطُرَّ غَيْرَ بَاغٍ وَلا عَادٍ﴾ أَيْ: أَبَحْنَا تَنَاوُلَ الْمَيْتَةِ لِلْمُضْطَرِّ بِشَرْطِ أَنْ يَكُونَ غَيْرَ بَاغٍ وَلَا عَادٍ، أَيْ: كَمَا] أَحْلَلْنَا الْأَنْعَامَ لَكُمْ فِي جَمِيعِ الْأَحْوَالِ، فَحَرِّمُوا الصَّيْدَ فِي حَالِ الْإِحْرَامِ، فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ قَدْ حَكَمَ بِهَذَا وَهُوَ الْحَكِيمُ فِي جَمِيعِ مَا يَأْمُرُ بِهِ وَيَنْهَى عَنْهُ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَحْكُمُ مَا يُرِيدُ﴾

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تُحِلُّوا شَعَائِرَ اللَّهِ﴾ قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: يَعْنِي بِذَلِكَ مَنَاسِكَ الْحَجِّ.

وَقَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ: الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةَ وَالْهَدْيَ والبُدن مِنْ شَعَائِرِ اللَّهِ.

وَقِيلَ: شَعَائِرُ اللَّهِ مَحَارِمُهُ [الَّتِي حَرَّمَهَا] أَيْ: لَا تُحِلُّوا مَحَارِمَ اللَّهِ الَّتِي حَرَّمَهَا تَعَالَى؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ [تَعَالَى] ﴿وَلا الشَّهْرَ الْحَرَامَ﴾ يَعْنِي بِذَلِكَ تَحْرِيمَهُ وَالِاعْتِرَافَ بِتَعْظِيمِهِ، وَتَرْكَ مَا نَهَى اللَّهُ عَنْ تَعَاطِيهِ فِيهِ مِنَ الِابْتِدَاءِ بِالْقِتَالِ وَتَأْكِيدَ اجْتِنَابِ الْمَحَارِمِ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿يَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنِ الشَّهْرِ الْحَرَامِ قِتَالٍ فِيهِ قُلْ قِتَالٌ فِيهِ كَبِيرٌ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ٢١٧] ، وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿إِنَّ عِدَّةَ الشُّهُورِ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ اثْنَا عَشَرَ شَهْرًا [فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ يَوْمَ خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضَ مِنْهَا أَرْبَعَةٌ حُرُمٌ ذَلِكَ الدِّينُ الْقَيِّمُ فَلا تَظْلِمُوا فِيهِنَّ أَنْفُسَكُمْ] ﴾ الْآيَةَ. [التَّوْبَةِ: ٣٦] .

وَفِي صَحِيحِ الْبُخَارِيِّ: عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرَةَ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى الله عليه وسلم قال في حجة الوداع: "إِنَّ الزَّمَانَ قَدِ اسْتَدَارَ كَهَيْئَتِهِ يَوْمَ خَلَقَ اللَّهُ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ، السَّنَةُ اثْنَا عَشَرَ شَهْرًا، مِنْهَا أَرْبَعَةٌ حُرُم، ثَلَاثٌ مُتَوَالِيَاتٌ: ذُو القَعْدة، وَذُو الْحِجَّةِ، وَالْمُحَرَّمُ، وَرَجَبُ مُضَر الَّذِي بَيْنَ جُمادى وَشَعْبَانَ".

وَهَذَا يَدُلُّ عَلَى اسْتِمْرَارِ تَحْرِيمِهَا إِلَى آخِرِ وَقْتٍ، كَمَا هُوَ مَذْهَبُ طَائِفَةٍ مِنَ السَّلَفِ.

وَقَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَلا الشَّهْرَ الْحَرَامَ﴾ يَعْنِي: لَا تَسْتَحِلُّوا قِتَالًا فِيهِ. وَكَذَا قَالَ مُقَاتل بْنُ حَيَّان، وَعَبْدُ الْكَرِيمِ بْنُ مَالِكٍ الجزَريُّ، وَاخْتَارَهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ أَيْضًا، وَقَدْ ذَهَبَ الْجُمْهُورُ إِلَى أَنَّ ذَلِكَ مَنْسُوخٌ، وَأَنَّهُ يَجُوزُ ابْتِدَاءُ الْقِتَالِ فِي الْأَشْهُرِ الْحُرُمِ، وَاحْتَجُّوا بِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿فَإِذَا انْسَلَخَ الأشْهُرُ الْحُرُمُ فَاقْتُلُوا الْمُشْرِكِينَ حَيْثُ وَجَدْتُمُوهُمْ﴾ [التَّوْبَةِ:٥] قَالُوا: وَالْمُرَادُ أَشْهَرُ التَّسْيِيرِ الْأَرْبَعَةُ، [ ﴿فَاقْتُلُوا الْمُشْرِكِينَ حَيْثُ وَجَدْتُمُوهُمْ﴾ ] قَالُوا: فَلَمْ يَسْتَثْنِ شَهْرًا حَرَامًا مِنْ غَيْرِهِ.

وَقَدْ حَكَى الْإِمَامُ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ [رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ] الْإِجْمَاعَ عَلَى أَنَّ اللَّهَ قَدْ أَحَلَّ قِتَالَ أَهْلِ الشِّرْكِ فِي الْأَشْهُرِ الْحُرُمِ، وَغَيْرِهَا مِنْ شُهُورِ السَّنَةِ، قَالَ: وَكَذَلِكَ أَجْمَعُوا عَلَى أَنَّ الْمُشْرِكَ لو قلد عنقه أو ذِرَاعَيْهِ بِلِحَاءِ جَمِيعِ أَشْجَارِ الْحَرَمِ، لَمْ يَكُنْ ذَلِكَ لَهُ أَمَانًا مِنَ الْقَتْلِ، إِذَا لَمْ يَكُنْ تَقَدَّمَ لَهُ عَقْدُ ذِمَّةٍ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ أَوْ أَمَانٍ وَلِهَذِهِ الْمَسْأَلَةِ بِحْثٌ آخَرُ، لَهُ مَوْضِعٌ أَبْسَطُ مِنْ هَذَا.

[وَ] قَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلا الْهَدْيَ وَلا الْقَلائِدَ﴾ يَعْنِي: لَا تَتْرُكُوا الْإِهْدَاءَ إِلَى الْبَيْتِ؛ فَإِنَّ فِيهِ تَعْظِيمًا لِشَعَائِرِ اللَّهِ، وَلَا تَتْرُكُوا تَقْلِيدَهَا فِي أَعْنَاقِهَا لِتَتَمَيَّزَ بِهِ عَمَّا عَدَاهَا مِنَ الْأَنْعَامِ، وَلِيَعْلَمَ أَنَّهَا هَدْيٌ إِلَى الْكَعْبَةِ فَيَجْتَنِبُهَا مَنْ يُرِيدُهَا بِسُوءٍ، وَتَبْعَثُ مَنْ يَرَاهَا عَلَى الْإِتْيَانِ بِمِثْلِهَا، فَإِنَّ مَنْ دَعَا إِلَى هدْيٍ كَانَ لَهُ مِنَ الْأَجْرِ مِثْلُ أُجُورِ مَنِ اتَّبَعَهُ، مِنْ غَيْرِ أَنْ يَنْقُصَ مِنْ أُجُورِهِمْ شَيْئًا؛ وَلِهَذَا لَمَّا حَج رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ بَاتَ بِذِي الحُلَيْفة، وَهُوَ وَادِي العَقيق، فَلَمَّا أَصْبَحَ طَافَ عَلَى نِسَائِهِ، وَكُنَّ تِسْعًا، ثُمَّ اغْتَسَلَ وتَطيَّب وصلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ أَشْعَرَ هَدْيَه وقلَّدَه، وأهَلَّ بِالْحَجِّ وَالْعُمْرَةِ وَكَانَ هَدْيُهُ إِبِلًا كَثِيرَةً تنيفُ عَلَى السِّتِّينَ، مِنْ أَحْسَنِ الْأَشْكَالِ وَالْأَلْوَانِ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿ذَلِكَ وَمَنْ يُعَظِّمْ شَعَائِرَ اللَّهِ فَإِنَّهَا مِنْ تَقْوَى الْقُلُوبِ﴾ [الْحَجُّ:٣٢] .

قَالَ بَعْضُ السَّلَفِ: إِعْظَامُهَا: اسْتِحْسَانُهَا وَاسْتِسْمَانُهَا.

وَقَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَالِبٍ: أَمَرَنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ أَنْ نَسْتَشْرِفَ الْعَيْنَ وَالْأُذُنَ. رَوَاهُ أَهْلُ السُّنَنِ 

وَقَالَ مُقاتل بْنُ حَيَّان: ﴿وَلا الْقَلائِدَ﴾ فَلَا تَسْتَحِلُّوا وَكَانَ أَهْلُ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ إِذَا خَرَجُوا مِنْ أَوْطَانِهِمْ فِي غَيْرِ الْأَشْهُرِ الْحُرُمِ قلَّدوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ بالشَّعْر والوَبَر، وَتَقَلَّدَ مُشْرِكُو الْحَرَمِ مِنْ لَحاء شَجَرِ الْحَرَمِ، فَيَأْمَنُونَ بِهِ.

رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَمَّار، حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّاد بْنُ العَوَّام، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ بْنِ حُسَيْنٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: نُسِخَ مِنْ هَذِهِ السُّورَةِ آيَتَانِ: آيَةُ الْقَلَائِدِ، وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿فَإِنْ جَاءُوكَ فَاحْكُمْ بَيْنَهُمْ أَوْ أَعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ﴾ [الْمَائِدَةِ:٤٢] .

وَحَدَّثَنَا الْمُنْذِرُ بْنُ شَاذَّانَ، حَدَّثَنَا زَكَرِيَّا بْنُ عَدِيّ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَدِيّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْن قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِلْحُسْنِ: نُسِخَ مِنَ الْمَائِدَةِ شَيْءٌ؟ قَالَ: لَا.

وَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ: كَانُوا يَتَقَلَّدُونَ مِنْ شَجَرِ الْحَرَمِ، فَيَأْمَنُونَ، فَنَهَى اللَّهُ عَنْ قَطْعِ شَجَرِهِ. وَكَذَا قَالَ مُطرِّف بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلا آمِّينَ الْبَيْتَ الْحَرَامَ يَبْتَغُونَ فَضْلا مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ وَرِضْوَانًا﴾ أَيْ: وَلَا تَسْتَحِلُّوا قِتَالَ الْقَاصِدِينَ إِلَى بَيْتِ اللَّهِ الْحَرَامِ، الَّذِي مَنْ دَخَلَهُ كَانَ آمِنًا، وَكَذَا مَنْ قَصَدَهُ طَالِبًا فَضْلَ اللَّهِ وَرَاغِبًا فِي رِضْوَانِهِ، فَلَا تَصُدُّوهُ وَلَا تَمْنَعُوهُ وَلَا تُهَيِّجُوهُ.

قَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ، وَعَطَاءٌ، وَأَبُو الْعَالِيَةِ، ومُطَرِّف بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، وَعَبْدُ اللَّهِ بن عُبَيد بن عُمير، والربيع بْنُ أَنَسٍ، وَقَتَادَةُ، ومُقاتل بْنُ حَيَّان فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿يَبْتَغُونَ فَضْلا مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ﴾ يَعْنِي بِذَلِكَ: التِّجَارَةَ.

وَهَذَا كَمَا تَقَدَّمَ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿لَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ أَنْ تَبْتَغُوا فَضْلا مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ١٩٨]

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَرِضْوَانَا﴾ قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: يترضَّون اللَّهَ بِحَجِّهِمْ.

وَقَدْ ذَكَرَ عِكْرِمة، والسُّدِّي، وَابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ: أَنَّ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ نَزَلَتْ فِي الحُطم بْنِ هِنْدٍ الْبَكْرِيِّ، كَانَ قَدْ أَغَارَ عَلَى سَرْح الْمَدِينَةِ، فَلَمَّا كَانَ مِنَ الْعَامِ الْمُقْبِلِ اعْتَمَرَ إِلَى الْبَيْتِ، فَأَرَادَ بَعْضُ الصَّحَابَةِ أَنْ يَعْتَرِضُوا فِي طَرِيقِهِ إِلَى الْبَيْتِ، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ﴿وَلا آمِّينَ الْبَيْتَ الْحَرَامَ يَبْتَغُونَ فَضْلا مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ وَرِضْوَانًا﴾ .

وَقَدْ حَكَى ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ الْإِجْمَاعَ عَلَى أَنَّ الْمُشْرِكَ يَجُوزُ قَتْلُهُ، إِذَا لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ أَمَانٌ، وَإِنْ أمَّ الْبَيْتَ الْحَرَامَ أَوِ بَيْتَ الْمَقْدِسِ؛ فَإِنَّ هَذَا الْحُكْمَ مَنْسُوخٌ فِي حَقِّهِمْ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ. فَأَمَّا مَنْ قَصَدَهُ بِالْإِلْحَادِ فِيهِ وَالشِّرْكِ عِنْدَهُ وَالْكُفْرِ بِهِ، فَهَذَا يُمْنَعُ كَمَا قَالَ [تَعَالَى] ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّمَا الْمُشْرِكُونَ نَجَسٌ فَلا يَقْرَبُوا الْمَسْجِدَ الْحَرَامَ بَعْدَ عَامِهِمْ هَذَا﴾ [التَّوْبَةِ: ٢٨] وَلِهَذَا بَعَثَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ عَامَ تِسْعٍ -لَمَّا أمَّر الصِّدِّيقَ عَلَى الْحَجِيجِ-علِيّا، وَأَمَرَهُ أَنْ يُنَادِيَ عَلَى سَبِيلِ النِّيَابَةِ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ بِبَرَاءَةٍ، وَأَلَّا يَحُجَّ بَعْدَ الْعَامِ مُشْرِك، وَلَا يَطُوفَنَّ بِالْبَيْتِ عُرْيان .

وَقَالَ [عَلِيُّ] بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلا آمِّينَ الْبَيْتَ الْحَرَامَ﴾ يَعْنِي مَنْ تَوَجَّهَ قِبَل الْبَيْتِ الْحَرَامِ، فَكَانَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَالْمُشْرِكُونَ يَحُجُّونَ الْبَيْتَ الْحَرَامَ، فَنَهَى اللَّهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَنْ يَمْنَعُوا أَحَدًا يَحُجَّ الْبَيْتَ أَوْ يَعْرِضُوا لَهُ مِنْ مُؤْمِنٍ أَوْ كَافِرٍ، ثُمَّ أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ بَعْدَهَا: ﴿إِنَّمَا الْمُشْرِكُونَ نَجَسٌ فَلا يَقْرَبُوا الْمَسْجِدَ الْحَرَامَ بَعْدَ عَامِهِمْ هَذَا﴾ [التَّوْبَةِ: ٢٨] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿مَا كَانَ لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ أَنْ يَعْمُرُوا مَسَاجِدَ اللَّهِ﴾ [التَّوْبَةِ: ١٧] وَقَالَ [تَعَالَى] : ﴿إِنَّمَا يَعْمُرُ مَسَاجِدَ اللَّهِ مَنْ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الآخِرِ﴾ [التَّوْبَةِ: ١٨] فَنَفَى الْمُشْرِكِينَ مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ.

وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْمَر، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَلا الْقَلائِدَ وَلا آمِّينَ الْبَيْتَ الْحَرَامَ﴾ قَالَ: مَنْسُوخٌ، كَانَ الرَّجُلُ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ إِذَا خَرَجَ مِنْ بَيْتِهِ يُرِيدُ الْحَجَّ تَقلَّد مِنَ الشَّجَرِ، فَلَمْ يَعْرِضْ لَهُ أَحَدٌ، وَإِذَا رَجَعَ تَقَلَّدَ قِلَادَةً مِنْ شَعرٍ فَلَمْ يَعْرِضْ لَهُ أَحَدٌ. وَكَانَ الْمُشْرِكُ يَوْمئِذٍ لَا يُصَدُّ عَنِ الْبَيْتِ، فَأُمِرُوا أَلَّا يُقَاتِلُوا فِي الشَّهْرِ الْحَرَامِ وَلَا عِنْدَ الْبَيْتِ، فَنَسَخَهَا قَوْلُهُ: ﴿فَاقْتُلُوا الْمُشْرِكِينَ حَيْثُ وَجَدْتُمُوهُمْ﴾ [التَّوْبَةِ: ٥] . .

وَقَدِ اخْتَارَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ أَنَّ الْمُرَادَ بِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَلا الْقَلائِدَ﴾ يعني: إن تقلدوا قِلَادَةً مِنَ الْحَرَمِ فَأَمِّنُوهُ، قَالَ: وَلَمْ تَزَلِ الْعَرَبُ تُعِيرُ مَنْ أَخَفَرَ ذَلِكَ، قَالَ الشَّاعِرُ : ألَمْ تَقْتُلا الحرْجَين إِذْ أَعْوَرَا لَكُمْ ... يمرَّان الأيدي اللَّحاء المُضَفَّرا 

وَقَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَإِذَا حَلَلْتُمْ فَاصْطَادُوا﴾ أَيْ: إِذَا فَرَغْتُمْ مِنْ إِحْرَامِكُمْ وَأَحْلَلْتُمْ مِنْهُ، فَقَدْ أَبَحْنَا لَكُمْ مَا كَانَ مُحَرَّمًا عَلَيْكُمْ فِي حَالِ الْإِحْرَامِ مِنَ الصَّيْدِ. وَهَذَا أَمُرٌ بَعْدَ الْحَظْرِ، وَالصَّحِيحُ الَّذِي يَثْبُتُ عَلَى السَّبْر: أَنَّهُ يُرَدُّ الْحُكْمُ إِلَى مَا كَانَ عَلَيْهِ قَبْلَ النَّهْيِ، فَإِنْ كَانَ وَاجِبًا رَدَّهُ وَاجِبًا، وَإِنْ كَانَ مُسْتَحَبًّا فَمُسْتَحَبٌّ، أَوْ مُبَاحًا فَمُبَاحٌ. وَمَنْ قَالَ: إِنَّهُ عَلَى الْوُجُوبِ، يُنْتَقَضُ عَلَيْهِ بِآيَاتٍ كَثِيرَةٍ، وَمَنْ قَالَ: إِنَّهُ لِلْإِبَاحَةِ، يَرُدُّ عَلَيْهِ آيَاتٌ أُخَرُ، وَالَّذِي يَنْتَظِمُ الْأَدِلَّةَ كُلَّهَا هَذَا الَّذِي ذَكَرْنَاهُ، كَمَا اخْتَارَهُ بَعْضُ عُلَمَاءِ الْأُصُولِ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلا يَجْرِمَنَّكُمْ شَنَآنُ قَوْمٍ أَنْ صَدُّوكُمْ عَنِ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ أَنْ تَعْتَدُوا﴾ وَمِنَ الْقُرَّاءِ مَنْ قَرَأَ: "أَنْ صَدُّوكُمْ" بِفَتْحِ الْأَلْفِ مِنْ "أَنْ" وَمَعْنَاهَا ظَاهِرٌ، أَيْ: لَا يَحَمِّلَنَكُمْ بُغْضُ قَوْمٍ قَدْ كَانُوا صَدُّوكُمْ عَنِ الْوُصُولِ إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ، وَذَلِكَ عَامَ الْحُدَيْبِيَةِ، عَلَى أَنْ تَعْتَدُوا [فِي] حُكْمِ اللَّهِ فِيكُمْ فَتَقْتَصُّوا مِنْهُمْ ظُلْمًا وَعُدْوَانًا، بَلِ احْكُمُوا بِمَا أَمَرَكُمُ اللَّهُ بِهِ مِنَ الْعَدْلِ فِي كُلِّ أَحَدٍ. وَهَذِهِ الْآيَةُ كَمَا سَيَأْتِي مِنْ قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَلا يَجْرِمَنَّكُمْ شَنَآنُ قَوْمٍ عَلَى أَلا تَعْدِلُوا اعْدِلُوا هُوَ أَقْرَبُ لِلتَّقْوَى﴾ [الْمَائِدَةِ: ٨] أَيْ: لَا يَحْمِلَنَّكُمْ بُغْضِ أَقْوَامٍ عَلَى تَرْكِ الْعَدْلِ، فَإِنَّ الْعَدْلَ وَاجِبٌ عَلَى كُلِّ أَحَدٍ، فِي كُلِّ أَحَدٍ فِي كُلِّ حَالٍ.

وَقَالَ بَعْضُ السَّلَفِ: مَا عاملتَ مَنْ عَصَى اللَّهَ فِيكَ بِمِثْلِ أَنْ تُطِيعَ اللَّهَ فِيهِ، وَالْعَدْلُ بِهِ قَامَتِ السَّمَوَاتُ وَالْأَرْضُ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا سَهْل بْنُ عُثْمَانَ حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ قَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ بِالْحُدَيْبِيَةِ وَأَصْحَابُهُ حِينَ صَدَّهُمُ الْمُشْرِكُونَ عَنِ الْبَيْتِ، وَقَدِ اشْتَدَّ ذَلِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ، فَمَرَّ بِهِمْ أُنَاسٌ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْمَشْرِقِ، يُرِيدُونَ الْعُمْرَةَ، فَقَالَ أَصْحَابُ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ: نَصُدُّ هَؤُلَاءِ كَمَا صَدَّنَا أَصْحَابُهُمْ. فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ .

وَالشَّنَآنُ هُوَ: الْبُغْضُ. قَالَهُ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ وَغَيْرُهُ، وَهُوَ مَصْدَرٌ مِنْ شنَأته أَشْنَؤُهُ شَنَآنًا، بِالتَّحْرِيكِ، مِثْلُ قَوْلِهِمْ: جَمَزَان، ودَرَجَان ورَفَلان، مِنْ جَمَزَ، وَدَرَجَ، وَرَفَلَ. قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: مِنَ الْعَرَبِ مَنْ يُسْقِطُ التَّحْرِيكَ فِي شَنَآنِ، فَيَقُولُ: شَنَّانِ. قَالَ: وَلَمْ أَعْلَمْ أَحَدًا قَرَأَ بِهَا، وَمِنْهُ قَوْلُ الشَّاعِرِ :

ومَا العيشُ إِلَّا مَا تُحبُّ وتَشْتَهي وَإنْ لامَ فِيهِ ذُو الشنَّان وفَنَّدَا ...

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَتَعَاوَنُوا عَلَى الْبِرِّ وَالتَّقْوَى وَلا تَعَاوَنُوا عَلَى الإثْمِ وَالْعُدْوَانِ﴾ يَأْمُرُ تَعَالَى عِبَادَهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ بِالْمُعَاوَنَةِ عَلَى فِعْلِ الْخَيْرَاتِ، وَهُوَ الْبَرُّ، وَتَرْكِ الْمُنْكِرَاتِ وَهُوَ التَّقْوَى، وَيَنْهَاهُمْ عَنِ التناصر على الباطل.

وَالتَّعَاوُنِ عَلَى الْمَآثِمِ وَالْمَحَارِمِ.

قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: الْإِثْمُ: تَرْكُ مَا أَمَرَ اللَّهُ بِفِعْلِهِ، وَالْعُدْوَانُ: مُجَاوَزَةُ مَا حَدَّ اللَّهُ فِي دِينِكُمْ، وَمُجَاوَزَةُ مَا فَرَضَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِي أَنْفُسِكُمْ وَفِي غَيْرِكُمْ .

وَقَدْ قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْم، حَدَّثَنَا عَبِيدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "انْصُرْ أَخَاكَ ظَالِمًا أَوْ مَظْلُومًا". قِيلَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، هَذَا نَصَرْتُه مَظْلُومًا، فَكَيْفَ أَنْصُرُهُ إِذَا كَانَ ظَالِمًا؟ قَالَ: "تَحْجِزُهُ تَمْنَعُهُ فَإِنَّ ذَلِكَ نَصْرُهُ".

انْفَرَدَ بِهِ الْبُخَارِيُّ مِنْ حَدِيثِ هُشَيْم بِهِ نَحْوَهُ وَأَخْرَجَاهُ مِنْ طَرِيقِ ثَابِتٍ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "انْصُرْ أَخَاكَ ظَالِمًا أَوْ مَظْلُومًا". قِيلَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، هَذَا نَصَرْتُهُ مَظْلُومًا، فَكَيْفَ أَنْصُرُهُ ظَالِمًا؟ قَالَ: "تَمْنَعُهُ مِنَ الظُّلْمَ، فَذَاكَ نَصْرُكَ إِيَّاهُ".

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ، حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ وَثَّاب، عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قَالَ: "الْمُؤْمِنُ الَّذِي يُخَالِطُ النَّاسَ وَيَصْبِرُ عَلَى أَذَاهُمْ، أَعْظَمُ أَجْرًا مِنَ الَّذِي لَا يُخَالِطُ النَّاسَ وَلَا يَصْبِرُ عَلَى أَذَاهُمْ" .

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ أَحْمَدُ أَيْضًا فِي مُسْنَدِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَجَّاجٌ، حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ وَثَّابٍ، عَنْ شَيْخٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، [قَالَ الْأَعْمَشُ: هُوَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ] أَنَّهُ قَالَ:"الْمُؤْمِنُ الَّذِي يُخَالِطُ النَّاسَ وَيَصْبِرُ عَلَى أَذَاهُمْ، خَيْرٌ مِنَ الَّذِي لَا يُخَالِطُهُمْ وَلَا يَصْبِرُ عَلَى أَذَاهُمْ".

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ مِنْ حَدِيثِ شُعْبَةَ، وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ مِنْ طَرِيقِ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ يُوسُفَ، كِلَاهُمَا عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، بِهِ .

وَقَالَ الْحَافِظُ أَبُو بَكْرٍ الْبَزَّارُ: حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ أَبُو شَيْبَةَ الْكُوفِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا بَكْرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ الْمُخْتَارِ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ فُضَيْل بْنِ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ أَبِي وَائِلٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "الدَّالُّ عَلَى الْخَيْرِ كَفَاعِلِهِ". ثُمَّ قَالَ: لَا نَعْلَمُهُ يُرْوَى إِلَّا بِهَذَا الإسناد .

قُلْتُ: وَلَهُ شَاهِدٌ فِي الصَّحِيحِ: "مَنْ دَعَا إِلَى هَدْيٍ كَانَ لَهُ مِنَ الْأَجْرِ مِثْلُ أُجُورِ مَنِ اتَّبَعَهُ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ، لَا يَنْقُصُ ذَلِكَ مِنْ أُجُورِهِمْ شَيْئًا، وَمَنْ دَعَا إِلَى ضَلَالَةٍ كَانَ عَلَيْهِ مِنَ الْإِثْمِ مِثْلُ آثَامِ مَنِ اتَّبَعَهُ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ، لَا يَنْقُصُ ذَلِكَ مِنْ آثَامِهِمْ شَيْئًا" .

وَقَالَ أَبُو الْقَاسِمِ الطَّبَرَانِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا عَمْرُو بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ الْعَلَاءِ بْنِ زِبْرِيقٍ الْحِمْصِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا عَمْرُو بْنُ الْحَارِثِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ، عَنِ الزُّبَيْدِيِّ، قَالَ عَبَّاسُ بْنُ يُونُسَ: إِنَّ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ نِمْرَان بْنَ مخُمر حَدَّثَهُ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "مَنْ مَشَى مَعَ ظَالِمٍ لِيُعِينَهُ، وَهُوَ يَعْلَمُ أَنَّهُ ظَالِمٌ، فَقَدْ خَرَجَ من الإسلام" .

---

## 5:3 — verse 3

### Translation

Almighty God informs His servants with a report that includes a prohibition against consuming these forbidden things among the dead animals, that is: whatever dies among animals of its own accord, without slaughter or hunting. This is only because of the harm within it, on account of the retained blood in it, which is harmful both to religion and to the body. For this reason God, Mighty and Majestic, forbade it. Excepted from carrion is fish, for it is lawful whether it dies by slaughter or otherwise, based on what Malik related in his Muwatta, al-Shafi'i and Ahmad in their Musnads, Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasa'i, and Ibn Majah in their Sunans, and Ibn Khuzaymah and Ibn Hibban in their Sahihs, from Abu Hurayrah: that the Messenger of God ﷺ was asked about seawater, and he said: "It is pure in its water, lawful in its dead creatures."

The same applies to locusts, as will come in the hadith. His statement, {and blood}, means the blood that is poured forth, based on His statement, {or blood poured forth} [al-An'am: 145]. This was said by Ibn Abbas and Sa'id ibn Jubayr.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Kathir ibn Shihab al-Madhhiji told us, Muhammad ibn Sa'id ibn Sabiq told us, Amr, that is Ibn Qays, told us, from Simak, from Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas: that he was asked about the spleen, and he said: Eat it. They said: But it is blood. He said: Only the poured-forth blood has been forbidden to you.

Hammad ibn Salamah related likewise, from Yahya ibn Sa'id, from al-Qasim, from Aishah, who said: He only prohibited the poured-forth blood.

Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Idris al-Shafi'i said: [chain] Abd al-Rahman ibn Zayd ibn Aslam told us, from his father, from Ibn Umar, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Two dead creatures and two bloods have been made lawful for us. As for the two dead creatures, they are fish and locusts, and as for the two bloods, they are the liver and the spleen."

Ahmad ibn Hanbal, Ibn Majah, al-Daraqutni, and al-Bayhaqi related this likewise, from the hadith of Abd al-Rahman ibn Zayd ibn Aslam, who is weak. The hafiz al-Bayhaqi said: Isma'il ibn Abi Idris also related it, from Usamah, and Abdullah, and Abd al-Rahman ibn Zayd ibn Aslam, from Ibn Umar, as a marfu' (elevated) report.

I say: all three of them are weak, though some are more acceptable than others. Sulayman ibn Bilal, one of the reliable narrators, related it from Zayd ibn Aslam, from Ibn Umar, and some narrators made it a mawquf (halted report) stopping at him. The hafiz Abu Zur'ah al-Razi said: This is more authentic.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Ali ibn al-Hasan told us, Muhammad ibn Abd al-Malik ibn Abi al-Shawarib told us, Bashir ibn Surayj told us, from Abu Ghalib, from Abu Umamah, that is Sudayy ibn Ajlan, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ sent me to my people to call them to God and His Messenger, and to present to them the laws of Islam. So I came to them, and while we were engaged in that, they brought a bowl of blood, and gathered around it eating it. They said: Come, O Sudayy, eat. I said: Woe to you! I have only come to you from the one who forbids this to you, and God has revealed it to him. They said: What is that? He said: So I recited to them this verse: {Forbidden to you are carrion, blood, [and the flesh of swine]} to the end of the verse.

The hafiz Abu Bakr ibn Mardawayh related it from the hadith of Ibn Abi al-Shawarib with a similar chain, and added after this account: He said: So I began calling them to Islam, and they refused me. I said to them: Woe to you, give me a drink of water, for I am very thirsty, he said, and I had my cloak on, but they said: No, but we will leave you until you die of thirst. He said: So I became distressed and put my head down in my cloak, and slept on the hot ground in intense heat. He said: Then someone came to me in my sleep with a glass cup, the like of which people had never seen anything more beautiful, containing a drink the like of which people had never tasted anything more delicious. He gave it to me and I drank it. When I had finished my drink, I awoke, and by God, I was never thirsty nor exposed to heat after that drink. Al-Hakim related it in his Mustadrak, from Ali ibn Humshadh, from Abdullah ibn Ahmad ibn Hanbal: Abdullah ibn Salamah ibn Ayyash al-Amiri told me, Sadaqah ibn Hurmuz told us, from Abu Ghalib, from Abu Umamah, who mentioned something similar and added after his statement "after that drink": Then I heard them saying: A man from the nobles of your people came to you, and you did not give him even a sip of diluted milk to drink at night; bring me some diluted milk. I said: I have no need of it, for God has fed me and given me drink. And I showed them my belly, and they all accepted Islam, every one of them.

How excellent is what al-A'sha recited in his poem which Ibn Ishaq mentioned:

"And beware of the dead carcasses, do not approach them, nor take a sharp bone and slit a vein with it..."

That is: do not do as the people of the Jahiliyyah (pre-Islamic ignorance) used to do. This was because when one of them grew hungry, he would take something sharp, a bone or the like, and slit a vein of his camel or any animal of whatever kind, and collect what blood came out of it and drink it. For this reason God forbade blood to this Ummah (community). Then al-A'sha said:

"And that erected idol, do not approach it, nor worship idols, but worship God alone..."

His statement, {and the flesh of swine} means both its domesticated and wild kinds. "Flesh" encompasses all its parts, even its fat, and there is no need for the excessive rigidity of the Zahiris in their stubbornness here and their forced interpretation in citing His statement, {for indeed it is filth} or {disobedience}, meaning His statement: {unless it be carrion, or blood poured forth, or the flesh of swine, for indeed it is filth} [al-An'am: 145]. They made the pronoun, as they understood it, refer back to the swine itself, so as to encompass all its parts. But this is far-fetched from the standpoint of language, for the pronoun can only refer back to the first term of an idafa (genitive construction), not to the second term. What is more apparent is that "flesh" encompasses all the parts, as is understood from the language of the Arabs and from established custom. In Sahih Muslim, from Buraydah ibn al-Khasib al-Aslami, may God be pleased with him, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Whoever plays nard (backgammon) is as if he dipped his hand in the flesh and blood of swine." If this is the aversion attached to mere touching, how much more severe is the threat and stern warning against eating it and nourishing oneself with it. In this is evidence that "flesh" encompasses all parts, including fat and other things.

In the two Sahihs: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "God has forbidden the sale of wine, carrion, swine, and idols." It was said: O Messenger of God, what do you say about the fat of carrion, for it is used to coat ships, grease hides, and people use it for lighting lamps? He said: "No, it is forbidden."

In Sahih al-Bukhari, from the hadith of Abu Sufyan: that he said to Heraclius, the king of the Byzantines: "He has forbidden us carrion and blood."

His statement, {and that which has been dedicated to other than God} means: whatever is slaughtered and the name of other than God is mentioned over it, it is forbidden. Because God has made it obligatory that His creatures be slaughtered in His great name, so whenever that is departed from and the name of something else is mentioned over it, whether an idol, a false deity, an image, or anything else among all creatures, then it is forbidden by consensus. The scholars only differed concerning that over which the naming (of God) was omitted, whether intentionally or through forgetfulness, as will be established in Surat al-An'am.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Ali ibn al-Hasan al-Hisinjani told us, Nu'aym ibn Hammad told us, Ibn Fudayl told us, from al-Walid ibn Jumay', from Abu al-Tufayl, who said: Adam (peace be upon him) came down with the prohibition of four things: carrion, blood, the flesh of swine, and that which is dedicated to other than God. And these four things were never lawful, and have remained forbidden ever since God created the heavens and the earth. When the Children of Israel came, God forbade them good things that had been made lawful for them, because of their sins. Then when God sent Jesus son of Mary (peace be upon him), he came down with the original command that Adam (peace be upon him) had brought, and made lawful for them what was besides that, but they denied him and disobeyed him. This is a gharib (singular) report.

Ibn Abi Hatim also said: [chain] My father told us, Ahmad ibn Yunus told us, Rib'i ibn Abdullah told us, he said: I heard al-Jarud ibn Abi Sabrah, he said: he is my grandfather, say: There was a man from Banu Riyah called Ibn Wathil, who was a poet. He challenged, in a contest, Abu al-Farazdaq's father at a watering place on the outskirts of Kufah, on condition that this one would hamstring a hundred of his camels and that one would hamstring a hundred of his camels, when they came to the water. When they came to the water, the two of them stood over the camels with swords and began cutting their hamstrings.

He said: The people went out on donkeys and mules seeking the meat. He said: And Ali was in Kufa. He said: Ali went out on the white mule of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ calling out: O people, do not eat of its meat, for it was slaughtered dedicated to other than Allah.

This is a gharib (singular) athar (report), and what Abu Dawud narrated bears witness to its soundness: [chain] Ibn Abbas said: The Prophet ﷺ forbade mu'aqarah of the bedouins.

Then Abu Dawud said: Muhammad ibn Ja'far, that is, Ghundar, related it as mawquf (halted report) attributed to Ibn Abbas. Abu Dawud alone transmitted it.

Abu Dawud also said: [chain] I heard Ikrimah say: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade that the food of the two competing parties be eaten.

Then Abu Dawud said: Most of those who narrated it from Jarir do not mention Ibn Abbas in it. He alone transmitted it as well.

His saying, {and the strangled}: this is what dies from strangulation, whether intentionally or by chance, such as when it becomes entangled in its tether and dies from it. It is forbidden.

As for {the beaten to death}, this is what is struck with something heavy and not sharp until it dies, as Ibn Abbas and others said: it is what is beaten with wood until it is battered by it and dies.

Qatadah said: The people of the Jahiliyyah (pre-Islamic ignorance) used to beat it with sticks, and when it died they would eat it.

In the Sahih: Adi ibn Hatim said: I said, O Messenger of Allah, I shoot game with the mi'rad (blunt-headed arrow) and I hit it. He said: "If you shoot with the mi'rad and it pierces, eat it, but if it strikes with its broad side, then it is waqidh (beaten to death), so do not eat it."

He thus distinguished between what is struck by an arrow, or a spear or the like, with its sharp edge, and permitted it, and what is struck with its broad side, and he made it waqidh and did not permit it. The jurists have agreed unanimously on this ruling here, but they differed regarding the case when the trained hunting animal strikes the game and kills it by its weight without wounding it, into two positions, which are two positions attributed to al-Shafi'i, may Allah have mercy on him:

The first: that it is not permitted, as in the case of the arrow, the common factor being that in both cases the animal died without a wound, so it is waqidh.

The second: that it is permitted, because he ruled on the permissibility of what the dog caught without asking for further detail, which indicates the permissibility of what we have mentioned, since it falls under the general wording. I have established a discussion on this matter, so let it be written here.

A discussion:

The scholars, may Allah have mercy on them, differed regarding the case when one releases a dog upon game and it kills it by its weight without wounding it, or strikes it, whether it is permitted or not, into two positions:

The first: that this is permitted, due to the general wording of His saying, {so eat of what they catch for you} [al-Ma'idah: 4], and likewise the general wordings of the hadith of Adi ibn Hatim. This is a position that the companions (of al-Shafi'i) have related from al-Shafi'i, may Allah have mercy on him, and some of the later scholars have declared it correct, among them al-Nawawi and al-Rafi'i.

I say: this is not apparent from al-Shafi'i's words in al-Umm and al-Mukhtasar, for he said in both places: "it bears two meanings," then he explained the reasoning for each of them. The companions took this from him and stated it generally as two positions attributed to him, except that in his discussion, in relating the position of permissibility, he gave it slight preference, but did not explicitly declare either one nor affirm it decisively. As for the position of permissibility, Ibn al-Sabbagh related it from Abu Hanifah, from the narration of al-Hasan ibn Ziyad from him, and mentioned nothing else. As for Abu Ja'far ibn Jarir, he related it in his tafsir from Salman al-Farisi, Abu Hurayrah, Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, and Ibn Umar. This is very gharib (strange/singular), and it is not found explicitly stated by them, except that it is from his own handling of the matter, may Allah have mercy on him and be pleased with him.

The second position: that this is not permitted, and it is one of the two positions attributed to al-Shafi'i, may Allah have mercy on him, and al-Muzani chose it, and it appears from Ibn al-Sabbagh's words that he also gave it preference, and Allah knows best. Abu Yusuf and Muhammad narrated it from Abu Hanifah, and it is the well-known position of Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal, may Allah be pleased with him. This position is closer to being correct, and Allah knows best, because it accords more with the principles of jurisprudence and is more closely tied to the foundations of the Sacred Law. Ibn al-Sabbagh argued for it with the hadith of Rafi' ibn Khadij: I said, O Messenger of Allah, we will meet the enemy tomorrow and we have no knives with us, shall we slaughter with reed? He said: "Whatever causes the blood to flow and the name of Allah has been mentioned over it, eat it." The hadith in its entirety is found in the two Sahihs.

Although this was given in response to a specific occasion, the consideration is given to the generality of the wording according to the majority of scholars, both in matters of principle and in derived rulings, just as when he, peace be upon him, was asked about al-bat' (which is mead made from honey) and he said: "Every drink that intoxicates is forbidden." Would any jurist say that this wording is restricted to honey drink? Likewise here, just as they asked him about a matter concerning slaughter and he gave them a general statement that encompasses what was asked about and other than it, because he, peace be upon him, was given comprehensive speech (jawami' al-kalim).

Once this is established, then what the dog strikes or crushes by its weight is not among what causes blood to flow, so it is not permitted, by the implication (mafhum) of this hadith. If it is said: this hadith is not of this kind at all, because they only asked about the instrument with which slaughter is performed, and did not ask about the thing that is slaughtered; this is why he excepted from that the tooth and the claw, saying: "Except the tooth and the claw, and I will tell you about that: as for the tooth, it is bone, and as for the claw, it is the knife of the Abyssinians." And the exception indicates the genus of that from which it is excepted, otherwise it would not be connected, which indicates that what was asked about was the instrument, so there remains no indication in it for what you have mentioned.

The answer to this: there is something in the wording that presents a difficulty for you as well, in that he says: "Whatever causes the blood to flow and the name of Allah has been mentioned over it, eat it," and he did not say "so slaughter with it." So from this both rulings are taken together: the ruling regarding the instrument with which slaughter is performed, and the ruling regarding the thing slaughtered, namely that it is necessary for its blood to be made to flow by an instrument that is neither a tooth nor a claw. This is one approach.

The second approach: the method of al-Muzani, which is that regarding the arrow, it has come explicitly that if it kills with its broad side, do not eat it, and if it pierces, eat it. Regarding the dog, it came unrestricted, so it is to be carried upon what was restricted there by piercing, because the two share the same cause requiring the ruling, namely game, so it is necessary to carry this over here even though the causes differ, just as the unrestricted freeing of a slave in zihar was carried over to the restriction by faith mentioned regarding killing. Indeed this case is even more deserving of it. This approach is directed at one who accepts the basic principle of this rule as such, and there is no disagreement among the companions altogether regarding it, so they must give an answer to this. And he may say: this animal was killed by the dog through its weight, so it is not permitted, by analogy with what was killed by the arrow's broad side, the common factor being that each of them is an instrument for hunting, and death occurred in both by weight. And this is not contradicted by the generality of the verse, because analogy (qiyas) takes precedence over general wording, as is the position of the four Imams and the majority. This too is a good approach.

Another approach: His saying, Most High, {so eat of what they catch for you} [al-Ma'idah: 4] is general regarding what they kill by wounding or otherwise, but this animal killed in this disputed manner cannot but be either something gored (or in its ruling), or something strangled (or in its ruling), and whichever it is, the ruling of this verse (the verse of prohibited things) must be given precedence over that verse, for several reasons:

First: the Lawgiver has taken into account the ruling of this verse in the case of hunting, as he says to Adi ibn Hatim: "But if it strikes with its broad side, then it is waqidh, so do not eat it." We do not know of any scholar who separated between one ruling and another within this verse, saying that waqidh is considered in the case of hunting but nutaih (gored) is not considered, for then holding to the permissibility of the disputed case would be a breach of consensus with no one holding to it, which is forbidden according to many scholars.

Second: that verse, {so eat of what they catch for you} [al-Ma'idah: 4], is not, by consensus, upon its full generality, rather it is specified to what they catch of edible animals, and non-edible animals are excluded from its general wording by agreement, and a preserved generality takes precedence over one that is not preserved.

Another approach: this game, in this situation, is in the ruling of carrion, the same as it, because the blood and the moisture that follows it have become congested within it, so it is not permitted, by analogy with carrion.

The other approach is that the verse of prohibition, meaning His saying: {Forbidden to you is carrion} to the end of it, is muhkam (of clear, unabrogated ruling) which has not been entered by either abrogation or specification. Likewise the verse of permission ought to be muhkam, meaning His saying: {They ask you what has been made lawful for them. Say: lawful for you are the good things [and what you have trained of hunting animals, training them as hounds]} [al-Ma'idah: 4]. So there should be no contradiction between them at all, and the Sunnah came to clarify that. The evidence for this is the story of the arrow: for he mentioned the ruling of what falls under this verse, which is when the mi'rad (blunt hunting missile) strikes it with its edge, in which case it is lawful because it is among the good things; and the ruling of what falls under the other verse, the verse of prohibition, which is when it strikes with its flat side, in which case it is not eaten, because it is waqidh (killed by a blow, without bleeding), and so it becomes one of the instances of the verse of prohibition. Likewise, the ruling of this matter must be the same: if the dog wounded it, it falls under the ruling of the verse of permission; but if it did not wound it, rather struck it or killed it by its weight, then it is natihah (killed by butting) or under its ruling, and is not lawful.

If it is said: why did he not detail the ruling of the dog, saying what you have mentioned: if it wounds it, it is lawful, and if it does not wound it, it is unlawful?

The answer is that this is rare, because it is the nature of the dog to kill with its claw or its fang, or both together, whereas its colliding with the game is rare, and likewise its killing it by its weight. So there was no need to guard against that because of its rarity, or because its ruling is evident to one who knows the prohibition of carrion, the strangled, the beaten to death, the one that falls to its death, and the gored. As for the arrow and the mi'rad, it sometimes misses due to the poor aim of its shooter, or the wind, or something similar; indeed its missing is more frequent than its hitting. This is why he detailed each of its two rulings separately, and Allah knows best. And likewise, since it is the nature of the dog that it may eat from the game, he mentioned the ruling for when it eats from the game, saying: "If it eats, then do not eat, for I fear that it caught it for itself." This is sound and established in the two Sahihs, and it is also specified as an exception from the general meaning of the verse of permission according to many, who said: what the dog has eaten from is not lawful. This has been related from Abu Hurayrah and Ibn Abbas, and al-Hasan, al-Sha'bi, and al-Nakha'i held this view. Abu Hanifah and his two companions, Ahmad ibn Hanbal, and al-Shafi'i in his well-known position went to this view as well.

Ibn Jarir related in his tafsir from Ali, Sa'd, Salman, Abu Hurayrah, Ibn Umar, and Ibn Abbas that the game is eaten even if the dog has eaten from it, to the point that Sa'd, Salman, Abu Hurayrah, Ibn Umar, and others said: it is eaten even if nothing remains of it but a small piece. Malik held this view, as did al-Shafi'i in his old position, while in his new position he indicated two views; this was stated by the imam Abu Nasr Ibn al-Sabbagh and others among his companions on his authority.

Abu Dawud related, with a good and strong chain, from Abu Tha'labah al-Khushani, from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, that he said regarding the game caught by a dog: "If you send out your dog and mention the name of Allah, then eat, even if it has eaten from it, and eat whatever your hand retrieves for you."

Al-Nasa'i also related it from the hadith of Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather: that a bedouin called Abu Tha'labah said, "O Messenger of Allah," and mentioned something similar.

Muhammad ibn Jarir said in his tafsir: [chain] Salman al-Farisi, from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, who said: "If a man sends his dog after game and reaches it after it has eaten from it, let him eat what remains."

Then Ibn Jarir found fault with it, noting that Qatadah and others related it from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, from Salman, as mawquf (halted report, i.e. attributed only to the Companion). As for the majority, they gave precedence to the hadith of Adi over this, and sought to weaken the hadith of Abu Tha'labah and others. Some scholars interpreted it to mean that if the dog ate after having waited for its owner, and a long interval passed and he did not come, and it ate from hunger or the like, then there is no harm in that, because in this situation there is no fear that it caught it for itself, unlike if it ate from it at the very first moment, for in that case it appears that it caught it for itself. And Allah knows best.

As for birds of prey, al-Shafi'i explicitly stated that they are like dogs, so what they eat from is forbidden according to the majority, and not forbidden according to others. Al-Muzani, among our companions, chose the view that eating what birds and birds of prey have eaten from is not forbidden, and this is the position of Abu Hanifah and Ahmad. They said: because it is not possible to train them as the dog is trained, by striking and the like; and also because they can only be trained by eating from the game, so this is excused; and also because the textual ruling came specifically regarding the dog, not the bird. Shaykh Abu Ali said in al-Ifsah: if we say what the dog has eaten from is forbidden, then there are two views regarding the prohibition of what the bird has eaten from.
