حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو كُرَيْب، حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْم، عَنِ الخَصيب بْنِ زَيْدٍ التَّمِيمِيِّ حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ، أَنَّ رَجُلًا سَأَلَ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ فَقَالَ: [إِلَى] مَتَى يَحِلُّ [لِي] الْحَرَامُ؟ قَالَ: فَقَالَ: "إِلَى مَتَى يَرْوى أَهْلُكَ مِنَ اللَّبَنِ، أَوْ تَجِيءُ مِيرَتُهم ".

حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ حُمَيْدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا سَلَمَةُ، عَنِ ابْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، حَدَّثَنَا عُمَرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ عُرْوَةَ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ جَدَّتِهِ ؛ أَنَّ رَجُلًا مِنَ الْأَعْرَابِ أَتَى النَّبِيَّ ﷺ يَسْتَفْتِيهِ فِي الَّذِي حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ، وَالَّذِي أَحَلَّ لَهُ، فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "تَحِلُّ لَكَ الطَّيِّبَاتُ، وتَحْرُم عَلَيْكَ الْخَبَائِثُ إِلَّا أَنْ تَفْتَقِر إِلَى طَعَامٍ لَا يَحِلُّ لَكَ، فَتَأْكُلَ مِنْهُ حَتَّى تَسْتَغْنِيَ عَنْهُ". فَقَالَ الرَّجُلُ: وَمَا فَقْرِي الَّذِي يُحِلُّ لِي؟ وَمَا غِنَايَ الَّذِي يُغْنِينِي عَنْ ذَلِكَ؟ فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "إِذَا كُنْتَ تَرْجُو نِتَاجًا، فَتَبْلُغُ بلُحُوم مَاشِيَتِكَ إِلَى نِتَاجِكَ، أَوْ كُنْتَ تَرْجُو غِنًى، تَطْلُبُهُ، فَتَبْلُغُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ شَيْئًا، فَأَطْعِمْ أَهْلَكَ مَا بَدَا لَكَ حَتَّى تَسْتَغْنِيَ عَنْهُ". فَقَالَ الْأَعْرَابِيُّ: مَا غِنَايَ الَّذِي أَدَعُهُ إِذَا وَجَدَتُهُ؟ فَقَالَ [النَّبِيُّ] ﷺ: "إِذَا أَرُوِيَتْ أَهْلُكَ غَبُوقا مِنَ اللَّيْلِ، فَاجْتَنِبْ مَا حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ طَعَامٍ، وَأَمَّا مَالُكَ فَإِنَّهُ مَيْسُورٌ كُلُّهُ، لَيْسَ فِيهِ حَرَامٌ". 

وَمَعْنَى قَوْلِهِ: "مَا لَمْ تَصْطَبِحُوا": يَعْنِي بِهِ: الْغَدَاءَ، "وَمَا لَمْ تَغْتَبِقُوا": يَعْنِي بِهِ: الْعَشَاءَ، "أَوْ تَخْتَفِئُوا بَقْلًا فَشَأْنُكُمْ بِهَا" [أَيْ] فَكُلُوا مِنْهَا. وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: يُرْوَى هَذَا الْحَرْفُ -يَعْنِي قَوْلَهُ: "أَوْ تَخْتَفِئُوا [بَقْلًا] عَلَى أَرْبَعَةِ أَوْجُهٍ: "تَخْتَفِئُوا" بِالْهَمْزَةِ، "وَتَحْتَفِيُوا" بِتَخْفِيفِ الْيَاءِ وَالْحَاءِ، "وَتَحْتَفُوا" بِتَشْدِيدِ [الْفَاءِ] وَتَحْتَفُوا" بِالْحَاءِ وَبِالتَّخْفِيفِ، وَيُحْتَمَلُ الْهَمْزُ، كَذَا ذَكَرَهُ فِي التَّفْسِيرِ.

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ أَبُو دَاوُدَ: حَدَّثَنَا هَارُونُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، حَدَّثَنَا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْن، حَدَّثَنَا عُقْبَة بْنُ وَهْب بْنِ عُقْبَةَ الْعَامِرِيُّ سَمِعْتُ أَبِي يُحَدِّثُ عَنِ الْفَجِيعِ الْعَامِرِيِّ؛ أَنَّهُ أَتَى رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم فقال: مَا يَحِلُّ لَنَا مِنَ الْمِيتَةِ؟ قَالَ: "مَا طَعَامُكُمْ؟ " قُلْنَا: نَغْتَبِقُ وَنَصْطَبِحُ. قَالَ أَبُو نُعَيْمٍ: فَسَّرَه لِي عُقْبَةُ: قَدَحُ غُدوة، وَقَدَحُ عَشيَّة قَالَ: "ذَاكَ وَأَبِي الجُوعُ". وَأُحِلَّ لَهُمُ الْمَيْتَةُ عَلَى هَذِهِ الْحَالِ.

تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ أَبُو دَاوُدَ وَكَأَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا يَصْطَبِحُونَ وَيَغْتَبِقُونَ شَيْئًا لَا يَكْفِيهِمْ، فَأَحلَّ لَهُمُ الْمَيْتَةَ لِتَمَامِ كِفَايَتِهِمْ، وَقَدْ يَحْتَجُّ بِهِ مَنْ يَرَى جَوَازَ الْأَكْلِ مِنْهَا حَتَّى يَبْلُغَ حَدَّ الشِّبَعِ، وَلَا يَتَقَيَّدُ ذَلِكَ بِسَدِّ الرَّمَق، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ أَبُو دَاوُدَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُوسَى بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادٌ، حَدَّثَنَا سِمَاكٌ، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ سَمُرَة، أَنْ رَجُلًا نَزَلَ الحَرَّةَ، وَمَعَهُ أَهْلُهُ وَوَلَدُهُ، فَقَالَ لَهُ رَجُلٌ: إِنَّ نَاقَةً لِي ضَلَّت، فَإِنْ وَجَدْتَهَا فَأَمْسِكْهَا، فَوَجَدَهَا وَلَمْ يَجِدْ صَاحِبَهَا، فَمَرِضَتْ فَقَالَتِ امْرَأَتُهُ: انْحَرْهَا، فَأَبَى، فَنَفَقَتْ، فَقَالَتْ لَهُ امْرَأَتُهُ: اسْلُخْهَا حَتَّى نُقدد شَحْمَها وَلَحْمَهَا فَنَأْكُلَهُ. فَقَالَ: حَتَّى أَسْأَلَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ، فَأَتَاهُ فَسَأَلَهُ، فَقَالَ: "هَلْ عِنْدَكَ غِنًى يُغْنِيك؟ " قَالَ: لَا. قَالَ: " فَكُلُوهَا". قَالَ: فَجَاءَ صَاحِبُهَا فَأَخْبَرَهُ الْخَبَرَ، فَقَالَ: هَلَّا كُنْتَ نَحَرْتَهَا؟ قَالَ: اسْتَحْيَيْتُ مِنْكَ.

تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ وَقَدْ يَحْتَجُّ بِهِ مَنْ يُجوز الْأَكْلَ وَالشِّبَعَ، وَالتَّزَوُّدُ مِنْهَا مُدَّةً يَغْلِبُ عَلَى ظَنِّهِ الِاحْتِيَاجُ إِلَيْهَا وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿غَيْرَ مُتَجَانِفٍ لإثْمٍ﴾ أَيْ: [غَيْرَ] مُتَعَاطٍ لِمَعْصِيَةِ اللَّهِ، فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ قَدْ أَبَاحَ ذَلِكَ لَهُ وَسَكَتَ عَنِ الْآخَرِ، كَمَا قَالَ فِي سُورَةِ الْبَقَرَةِ: ﴿فَمَنِ اضْطُرَّ غَيْرَ بَاغٍ وَلا عَادٍ فَلا إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ﴾ [الْآيَةَ: ١٧٣] .

وَقَدِ اسْتَدَلَّ بِهَذِهِ الْآيَةِ مَنْ يَقُولُ بِأَنَّ الْعَاصِي بِسَفَرِهِ لَا يَتَرَخَّصُ بِشَيْءٍ مِنْ رُخَصِ السَّفَرِ؛ لِأَنَّ الرُّخَصَ لَا تَنَالُ بِالْمَعَاصِي، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

---

## 5:4 — verse 4

### Translation

When Allah, may He be exalted, mentioned in the preceding verse what He had forbidden of the harmful unclean things, harmful either to the body of the one who partakes of them, or to his religion, or to both, and made exception for the case of necessity, as He said: {And He has explained in detail to you what He has forbidden you, except that to which you are compelled} [al-An'am: 119], He then said after that: {They ask you what has been made lawful for them. Say, lawful for you are the good things}, just as He said in Surat al-A'raf, describing Muhammad ﷺ, that he {makes lawful for them the good things and forbids them the unclean things} [verse 157].

Ibn Abi Hatim said: Abu Zur'ah related to us, Yahya ibn Abdullah ibn Bukayr related to us, Abdullah ibn Lahi'ah related to me, Ata' ibn Dinar related to me, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Adi ibn Hatim and Zayd ibn al-Muhalhil, both of the Ta'i tribe, who asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, saying: O Messenger of Allah, Allah has forbidden carrion, so what is lawful for us from it? Then this was revealed: {They ask you what has been made lawful for them. Say, lawful for you are the good things}. Sa'id ibn Jubayr said: meaning, the lawful slaughtered animals that are good for them. Muqatil ibn Hayyan said, regarding His statement {Say, lawful for you are the good things}: the good things are what has been made lawful for them to obtain of anything, that is, the lawful provision. Al-Zuhri was asked about drinking urine for medicinal treatment, and he said: it is not among the good things.

Ibn Abi Hatim related this. Ibn Wahb said: Malik was asked about selling the clay that people eat, and he said: it is not among the good things.

His statement, may He be exalted: {and what you have trained of hunting animals, teaching them to hunt} means: made lawful for you are the slaughtered animals over which the name of Allah has been mentioned, and the good things of provision, and made lawful for you is what you catch with hunting animals, which are of the dogs, leopards, falcons, and the like, as is the position of the majority of the companions, the successors, and the leading scholars. Among those who said this: Ali ibn Abi Talhah, from Ibn Abbas, regarding His statement {and what you have trained of hunting animals, teaching them to hunt}: they are the trained dogs and the hawk, and every bird trained for hunting. Al-jawarih (hunting animals) means the predatory dogs, the leopards, the falcons, and the like.

Ibn Abi Hatim related this, then said: similar reports have come from Khaythamah, Tawus, Mujahid, Makhul, and Yahya ibn Abi Kathir. It is reported from al-Hasan that he said: the hawk and the falcon are among the jawarih. A similar statement is reported from Ali ibn al-Husayn. It is then reported from Mujahid that he disliked all hunting with birds, and he recited the statement of Allah, mighty and exalted: {and what you have trained of hunting animals, teaching them to hunt}. A similar report comes from Sa'id ibn Jubayr.

Ibn Jarir transmitted the same from al-Dahhak and al-Suddi, then said: Hannad related to us, Ibn Abi Za'idah related to us, Ibn Jurayj informed us, from Nafi', from Ibn Umar, who said: as for what is caught by birds, hawks and other birds, whatever you catch it while it is still alive is yours, otherwise do not eat it.

I say: what is reported from the majority is that hunting with birds is like hunting with dogs, because they seize the prey with their claws just as dogs seize it with their teeth, so there is no difference. This is the position of the four leading imams and others, and Ibn Jarir chose it, citing as evidence what he related from Hannad: Isa ibn Yunus related to us, from Majalid, from al-Sha'bi, from Adi ibn Hatim, who said: I asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ about hunting with the hawk, and he said: "Whatever it catches for you, eat." Imam Ahmad made an exception for hunting with a black dog, because in his view it is among what must be killed and is not lawful to keep, based on what is established in Sahih Muslim from Abu Dharr, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The prayer is interrupted by the donkey, the woman, and the black dog." I said: what is the difference between the black dog and the red one? He said: "The black dog is a devil." In another hadith: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ ordered the killing of dogs, then said: "What is the matter with them and the matter of dogs? Kill every solid black one among them."

These animals used for hunting are called jawarih, from al-jurh, meaning acquisition or earning. As the Arabs say: so-and-so jaraha his family good, meaning he earned good for them. And they say: so-and-so has no jarih, meaning he has no one who earns for him. Allah, may He be exalted, said: {And He is the one who takes your souls by night and knows what you have earned (jarahtum) by day} [al-An'am: 60], meaning what you have earned of good and evil.

Regarding the occasion of the revelation of this noble verse, the hadith related by Ibn Abi Hatim has been mentioned: Hajjaj ibn Hamzah related to us, Zayd ibn al-Hubab related to us, Musa ibn Ubaydah related to me, Aban ibn Salih related to me, from al-Qa'qa' ibn Hakim, from Salma the mother of Rafi', from Abu Rafi', the freed servant of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ: that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ ordered that dogs be killed, and they were killed, and the people came and said: O Messenger of Allah, what is lawful for us from this community that you ordered killed? He remained silent, and then Allah revealed: {They ask you what has been made lawful for them. Say, lawful for you are the good things, and what you have trained of hunting animals, teaching them to hunt} the verse. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ then said: "If a man releases his dog and mentions the name of Allah, and it catches the prey for him, let him eat, provided it has not eaten of it."

Ibn Jarir related it likewise, from Abu Kurayb, from Zayd ibn al-Hubab, with his chain, from Abu Rafi', who said: Gabriel came to the Prophet ﷺ to ask permission to enter, and permission was granted to him, so he said: we have granted you permission, O Messenger of Allah. He said: yes, but we do not enter a house in which there is a dog. Abu Rafi' said: he then ordered me to kill every dog in Medina, so I killed them, until I came to a woman who had a dog that was barking to protect her, and I left it out of mercy for her. Then I came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and informed him, and he gave me an order, so I returned to the dog and killed it. Then the people came and said: O Messenger of Allah, what is lawful for us from this community that you ordered killed? The Messenger of Allah ﷺ remained silent, and then Allah, mighty and exalted, revealed: {They ask you what has been made lawful for them. Say, lawful for you are the good things, and what you have trained of hunting animals, teaching them to hunt}.

Al-Hakim related it in his Mustadrak through the route of Muhammad ibn Ishaq, from Aban ibn Salih, with the same content, and said: sound, though the two [Bukhari and Muslim] did not include it. Ibn Jarir said: al-Qasim related to us, al-Husayn related to us, Hajjaj related to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from Ikrimah: that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent Abu Rafi' to kill dogs, until he reached al-'Awali, and Asim ibn Adi, Sa'd ibn Khaythamah, and Uwaym ibn Sa'idah came and said: what has been made lawful for us, O Messenger of Allah? Then this was revealed: {They ask you what has been made lawful for them. Say, lawful for you are the good things, and what you have trained of hunting animals, teaching them to hunt} the verse.

Al-Hakim related it through the route of Simak, from Ikrimah. Muhammad ibn Ka'b al-Qurazi said likewise regarding the occasion of the revelation of this verse: that it concerns the killing of dogs.

His statement, may He be exalted, {mukallibin (training them to hunt)}: it is possible that it is a circumstantial clause (hal) referring to the pronoun in {you have trained}, in which case it would be a circumstantial description of the subject. It is also possible that it is a circumstantial clause referring to the object, which is {hunting animals}, meaning: and what you have trained of hunting animals in the state of their being trained to hunt, that is, when the hunting animal seizes the prey with its claws or talons. From this it is inferred, in this case, that if the hunting animal kills the prey by striking it or with its claw or talon, it is not lawful, as is one of the two positions of al-Shafi'i and a group of scholars. This is why He said: {you teach them from what Allah has taught you}, meaning that when it is released, it goes forth, and when it is called back, it comes back, and when it seizes the prey, it holds it for its owner until he comes to it, and does not keep it for itself. This is why Allah, may He be exalted, said: {So eat of what they catch for you, and mention the name of Allah over it}. So whenever the hunting animal is trained and catches the prey for its owner, and the name of Allah has been mentioned at the time of its release, the game becomes lawful, even if it kills it, by consensus.

The Sunnah has come with the like of what this noble verse indicates, as is established in the two Sahihs from Adi ibn Hatim, who said: I said, O Messenger of Allah, I release trained dogs and mention the name of Allah. He said: "If you release your trained dog and mention the name of Allah, eat whatever it catches for you." I said: even if they kill it? He said: "Even if they kill it, so long as no other dog that is not among them shares in it, for you have only mentioned the name over your own dog and not over another." I said to him: what if I shoot the prey with the mi'rad (a blunt hunting arrow) and I hit it? He said: "If you shoot with the mi'rad and it pierces, eat it, but if it strikes with its broad side, then it is waqidh (killed by a blunt blow), so do not eat it."

In another wording of theirs both: "When you release your dog, mention the name of Allah. If it catches it for you and you find it still alive, slaughter it. And if you find it already killed and it has not eaten from it, eat it, for the dog's seizing of it is its manner of slaughter (dhaka)." In another narration of theirs both: "But if it has eaten, then do not eat, for I fear that it caught it for itself." This is evidence for the majority, and it is the sound position within the school of al-Shafi'i, namely that if the dog eats from the game it becomes unlawful absolutely, and they did not make further distinctions, just as the hadith came in that form. It has been related from a group of the Salaf (early generations) that they said it does not become unlawful absolutely.

Mention of the reports on this matter:

Ibn Jarir said: Hannad narrated to us, Waki' narrated to us, from Shu'bah, from Qatadah, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, who said: Salman al-Farisi said: Eat, even if it has eaten two-thirds of it, meaning the game, if the dog has eaten from it. Sa'id ibn Abi Arubah and Umar ibn Amir likewise related this from Qatadah. Muhammad ibn Zayd likewise related it from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, from Salman.

Ibn Jarir also related it from Mujahid ibn Musa, from Yazid, from Bakr ibn Abdullah al-Muzani and al-Qasim, that Salman said: If the dog eats, then eat, even if it has eaten two-thirds of it.

Ibn Jarir said: Yunus ibn Abd al-A'la narrated to us, Ibn Wahb informed us, Makhramah ibn Bukayr informed me from his father, from Humayd ibn Malik ibn Khuthaym al-Du'ali, that he asked Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas about game from which the dog has eaten. He said: Eat, even if nothing remains of it but a hidhyah, meaning a piece.

Shu'bah related it from Abd Rabbihi ibn Sa'id, from Bukayr ibn al-Ashajj, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, from Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, who said: Eat, even if it has eaten two-thirds of it.

Ibn Jarir said: Ibn al-Muthanna narrated to us, Abd al-A'la narrated to us, Dawud narrated to us, from Amir, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: If you release your dog and it eats from it, if it has eaten two-thirds of it and a third remains, then eat.

Ibn Jarir said: Muhammad ibn Abd al-A'la narrated to us, al-Mu'tamir narrated to us, saying: I heard Ubaydullah, and Hannad narrated to us, Abdah narrated to us, from Ubaydullah ibn Umar, from Nafi', from Abdullah ibn Umar, who said: If you release your trained dog and mention the name of Allah, then eat whatever it catches for you, whether it has eaten or not.

Ubaydullah ibn Umar, Ibn Abi Dhi'b, and more than one other likewise related it from Nafi'.

These reports are established from Salman, Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, Abu Hurayrah, and Ibn Umar. It is also related from Ali and Ibn Abbas. There is disagreement about it from Ata and al-Hasan al-Basri. It is the position of al-Zuhri, Rabi'ah, and Malik. Al-Shafi'i held to it in his earlier position, and hinted at it in his later position as well.

It has been related through the path of Salman al-Farisi as a marfu' (elevated) report. Ibn Jarir said: Imran ibn Bakkar al-Kula'i narrated to us, Abd al-Aziz ibn Musa al-Lahuni narrated to us, Muhammad ibn Dinar, that is al-Tahi, narrated to us, from Abu Iyas Mu'awiyah ibn Qurrah, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, from Salman al-Farisi, from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, who said: "If a man releases his dog upon the game and finds it after it has eaten from it, let him eat what remains."

Ibn Jarir then said: There is something questionable in the chain of this hadith, and it is not known that Sa'id heard from Salman. The reliable narrators relate it as the speech of Salman, not as marfu'.

What Ibn Jarir said here is correct. However, this meaning has been related as marfu' through other channels. Abu Dawud said: Muhammad ibn Minhal al-Darir narrated to us, Yazid ibn Zurayi' narrated to us, Habib al-Mu'allim narrated to us, from Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather, that a Bedouin called Abu Tha'labah said: O Messenger of Allah, I have trained hunting dogs, so give me a ruling concerning their game. The Prophet ﷺ said: "If you have trained hunting dogs, then eat from what they catch for you." He said: Whether slaughtered or not slaughtered? He said: "Yes." He said: And even if it has eaten from it? He said: "Yes, even if it has eaten from it." He said: O Messenger of Allah, give me a ruling concerning my bow. He said: "Eat whatever your bow returns to you." He said: Whether slaughtered or not slaughtered? He said: "Even if it disappears from you as long as it has not [visibly] died from something else, or you find on it a trace other than that of your arrow." He said: Give me a ruling concerning the vessels of the Magians when we are compelled to use them. He said: "Wash them and eat from them."

This is how Abu Dawud related it, and al-Nasa'i also transmitted it. Abu Dawud likewise related it through the path of Busr ibn Ubaydullah, from Abu Idris al-Khawlani, from Abu Tha'labah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "When you release your dog and mention the name of Allah, eat, even if it has eaten from it, and eat whatever your hand returns to you."

These are two sound chains. Al-Thawri related from Simak ibn Harb, from Adi, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whatever a trained predatory dog catches for you, eat." I said: Even if it has eaten? He said: "Yes."

Abd al-Malik ibn Habib related: Asad ibn Musa narrated to us, from Ibn Abi Za'idah, from al-Sha'bi, from Adi, similarly.

These are reports indicating that it is excused if the dog eats from it. Those who did not consider the game unlawful due to the dog's eating or the like of it have used these as evidence, as previously related from those we mentioned. Others took a middle position, saying: if it eats immediately after catching it, then it becomes unlawful, based on the hadith of Adi ibn Hatim, and on account of the reasoning to which the Prophet ﷺ alluded: "If it has eaten, then do not eat, for I fear that it caught it for itself." But if it caught it and then waited for its owner, and the wait grew long and it became hungry and so ate from the game due to its hunger, this does not affect the ruling of prohibition. They applied the hadith of Abu Tha'labah al-Khushani to this case. This is a fine distinction and a sound reconciliation between the two hadiths. The teacher Abu al-Ma'ali al-Juwayni wished, in his book al-Nihayah, that someone would make precisely this distinction, and Allah fulfilled his wish. A group among his companions held to this position and distinction. Others held a fourth position on the matter, namely distinguishing between the dog's eating, which makes it unlawful because of the hadith of Adi, and the eating of falcons and the like, which does not make it unlawful, because a falcon cannot be trained except by means of eating.

Ibn Jarir said: Abu Kurayb narrated to us, Asbat ibn Muhammad narrated to us, Abu Ishaq al-Shaybani narrated to us, from Hammad, from Ibrahim, from Ibn Abbas, that he said concerning birds of prey: If you release it and it kills, then eat, for the dog, when you strike it, does not return to its previous state, whereas the bird is trained to return to its owner and is not struck. So if it eats from the game and plucks the feathers, then eat.

Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, al-Sha'bi, and Hammad ibn Abi Sulayman said likewise.

Evidence may be adduced for this group from what Ibn Abi Hatim related: Abu Sa'id narrated to us, al-Muharibi narrated to us, Mujalid narrated to us, from al-Sha'bi, from Adi ibn Hatim, who said: I said, O Messenger of Allah, we are a people who hunt with dogs and falcons, so what is lawful for us from them? He said: "Lawful for you is what you have trained among the trained predators, training them according to what Allah has taught you. So eat from what they catch for you, and mention the name of Allah over it." Then he said: "Whatever dog you release and mention the name of Allah over it, eat from what it catches for you." I said: Even if it kills it? He said: "Even if it kills it, as long as it has not eaten." I said: O Messenger of Allah, what if our dogs mix with other dogs? He said: "Then do not eat until you know that it was your dog that caught it." He said: I said: We are a people who shoot arrows, so what is lawful for us? He said: "Whatever you have mentioned the name of Allah over and it has pierced through, then eat."

The point of evidence for this group is that he stipulated concerning the dog that it must not eat, but did not stipulate this concerning falcons, which indicates a distinction between them in ruling. And Allah knows best.

Concerning His saying, {So eat from what they catch for you, and mention the name of Allah over it}: that is, at the moment of release, just as the Prophet ﷺ said to Adi ibn Hatim: "When you release your trained dog and mention the name of Allah, eat whatever it catches for you." In the hadith of Abu Tha'labah, recorded also in the two Sahihs: "When you release your dog, mention the name of Allah, and when you shoot your arrow, mention the name of Allah." For this reason, those scholars who stipulated it, such as Ahmad ibn Hanbal, according to the well known report from him, made the mentioning of the name of Allah's name a condition at the time of releasing the dog and shooting the arrow, based on this verse and this hadith. This position is what is well known from the majority: that what is meant by this verse is the command to mention Allah's name at the moment of release, as al-Suddi and more than one other have said.

Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas, concerning His saying: {And mention the name of Allah over it}, he said: when you release your hunting animal, say "In the name of Allah," and if you forget, there is no blame.

Some people said: what is meant by this verse is the command to mention Allah's name at eating, as is established in the authentic collection, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ taught his stepson Umar ibn Abi Salamah, saying: "Mention Allah's name, eat with your right hand, and eat from what is nearest to you." And in the Sahih of al-Bukhari, from Aisha, that they said: O Messenger of Allah, people come to us, newly emerged from disbelief, bringing meat, and we do not know whether Allah's name was mentioned over it or not. He said: "You mention Allah's name yourselves and eat."

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Yazid told us, Hisham told us, from Budayl, from Abdullah ibn Ubayd ibn Umayr, from Aisha; that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was eating food with six of his companions, when a bedouin came and ate it in two mouthfuls. The Prophet ﷺ said: "Indeed, had he mentioned the name of Allah, it would have sufficed you all. So when any of you eats, let him mention the name of Allah, and if he forgets to mention the name of Allah at its beginning, let him say: 'In the name of Allah, at its beginning and its end.'"

Ibn Majah likewise related it from Abu Bakr ibn Abi Shaybah, from Yazid ibn Harun, with the same wording. This is disconnected between Abdullah ibn Ubayd ibn Umayr and Aisha, for he did not hear this hadith from her, as is shown by what Imam Ahmad related:

[chain] Abd al-Wahhab told us, Hisham, meaning Ibn Abi Abdullah al-Dastawa'i, informed us, from Budayl, from Abdullah ibn Ubayd ibn Umayr; that a woman among them, called Umm Kulthum, told him, from Aisha: that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was eating food with six of his companions, when a hungry bedouin came and ate it in two mouthfuls. He said: "Indeed, had he mentioned the name of Allah, it would have sufficed you all. So when any of you eats, let him mention the name of Allah, and if he forgets the name of Allah at its beginning, let him say: 'In the name of Allah, at its beginning and its end.'"

Ahmad also related it, as did Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, and al-Nasa'i, through more than one route, from Hisham al-Dastawa'i, with the same wording. Al-Tirmidhi said: hasan sahih (sound and authentic).

Another hadith: Ahmad said: [chain] Ali ibn Abdullah told us, Yahya ibn Sa'id told us, Jabir ibn Subh told us, al-Muthanna ibn Abd al-Rahman al-Khuza'i told me, and I accompanied him to Wasit, and he used to mention Allah's name at the beginning of his food and at the last mouthful he would say: "In the name of Allah, at its beginning and its end."

So I said to him: you mention Allah's name at the beginning of what you eat, what do you make of your saying at the end of what you eat, "In the name of Allah, at its beginning and its end"? He said: I will tell you about that. Indeed my grandfather Umayyah ibn Mukhashshi, who was among the companions of the Prophet ﷺ, I heard him say: a man was eating while the Prophet was watching, and he did not mention Allah's name, until at the last mouthful of his food he said: "In the name of Allah, at its beginning and its end." The Prophet ﷺ said: "By Allah, Satan kept eating with him until he mentioned Allah's name, and then nothing remained in his stomach until he vomited it up."

Abu Dawud and al-Nasa'i likewise related it, from the hadith of Jabir ibn Subh al-Rasibi, Abu Bishr al-Basri. Ibn Ma'in and al-Nasa'i deemed him reliable, while Abu al-Fath al-Azdi said: no proof can be established by him.

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Abu Mu'awiyah told us, al-A'mash told us, from Khaythamah, from Abu Hudhayfah, Abu Abd al-Rahman Abdullah ibn Imam Ahmad said: his name was Salamah ibn al-Haytham ibn Suhayb, one of the companions of Ibn Mas'ud, from Hudhayfah, who said: whenever we were present with the Prophet ﷺ at a meal, we would not place our hands until the Messenger of Allah ﷺ began and placed his hand first. Once we were present with him at a meal, and a young slave-girl came, as if pushed, and went to place her hand in the food, so the Messenger of Allah ﷺ seized her hand. Then a bedouin came, as if pushed, and went to place his hand in the food, so the Messenger of Allah ﷺ seized his hand, and said: "Satan seeks to make the food lawful for himself when Allah's name is not mentioned over it. He brought this girl in order to make it lawful for himself through her, so I seized her hand, and he brought this bedouin in order to make it lawful for himself through him, so I seized his hand. By the One in whose hand is my soul, his hand," meaning Satan's, "is in my hand along with both of their hands."

Muslim, Abu Dawud, and al-Nasa'i likewise related it, from the hadith of al-A'mash, with the same content.

Another hadith: Muslim and the compilers of the Sunan, except al-Tirmidhi, related, through the route of Ibn Jurayj, from Abu al-Zubayr, from Jabir ibn Abdullah, from the Prophet ﷺ, that he said: "When a man enters his house and mentions Allah upon entering and at his meal, Satan says: there is no lodging for you tonight and no supper. But if he enters without mentioning the name of Allah upon entering, Satan says: you have attained lodging. And if he does not mention the name of Allah at his meal, Satan says: you have attained lodging and supper." This is the wording of Abu Dawud.

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Yazid ibn Abd Rabbihi told us, al-Walid ibn Muslim told us, from Wahshi ibn Harb ibn Wahshi ibn Harb, from his father, from his grandfather; that a man said to the Prophet ﷺ: we eat and are not satisfied? He said: "Perhaps you eat separately. Gather together over your food and mention the name of Allah, and it will be blessed for you."

Abu Dawud and Ibn Majah related it, through the route of al-Walid ibn Muslim.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

لَمَّا ذَكَرَ تَعَالَى مَا حَرَّمَهُ فِي الْآيَةِ الْمُتَقَدِّمَةِ مِنَ الْخَبَائِثِ الضَّارَّةِ لِمُتَنَاوِلِهَا، إِمَّا فِي بَدَنِه، أَوْ فِي دِينِهِ، أَوْ فِيهِمَا، وَاسْتَثْنَى مَا اسْتَثْنَاهُ فِي حَالَةِ الضَّرُورَةِ، كَمَا قَالَ: ﴿وَقَدْ فَصَّلَ لَكُمْ مَا حَرَّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِلا مَا اضْطُرِرْتُمْ إِلَيْهِ﴾ [الْأَنْعَامِ: ١١٩] قَالَ بَعْدَهَا: ﴿يَسْأَلُونَكَ مَاذَا أُحِلَّ لَهُمْ قُلْ أُحِلَّ لَكُمُ الطَّيِّبَاتُ﴾ كَمَا [قَالَ] فِي سُورَةِ الْأَعْرَافِ فِي صِفَةِ مُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ: أَنَّهُ ﴿يُحِلُّ لَهُمُ الطَّيِّبَاتِ وَيُحَرِّمُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْخَبَائِثَ﴾ [الْآيَةَ: ١٥٧] .

قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو زُرْعَة، حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ بُكَيْر، حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ لَهِيعة، حَدَّثَنِي عَطَاءُ بْنُ دِينَارٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْر، عَنْ عَدِيّ بْنِ حَاتِمٍ، وَزَيْدِ بْنِ المهَلْهِل الطَّائِيَّيْنِ سَأَلَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ، فَقَالَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، قَدْ حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ الْمَيْتَةَ، فَمَاذَا يَحِلُّ لَنَا مِنْهَا؟ فَنَزَلَتْ: ﴿يَسْأَلُونَكَ مَاذَا أُحِلَّ لَهُمْ قُلْ أُحِلَّ لَكُمُ الطَّيِّبَاتُ﴾ قَالَ سَعِيدُ [بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ] يَعْنِي: الذَّبَائِحَ الْحَلَالَ الطَّيِّبَةَ لَهُمْ. وَقَالَ مُقَاتِلُ: [بْنُ حَيَّانَ] [فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿قُلْ أُحِلَّ لَكُمُ الطَّيِّبَاتُ﴾ ] فَالطَّيِّبَاتُ مَا أُحِلَّ لَهُمْ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ أَنْ يُصِيبُوهُ وَهُوَ الْحَلَالُ مِنَ الرِّزْقِ. وَقَدْ سُئِلَ الزُّهْرِيُّ عَنْ شُرْبِ الْبَوْلِ لِلتَّدَاوِي فَقَالَ: لَيْسَ هُوَ مِنَ الطَّيِّبَاتِ.

رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ وَقَالَ ابْنُ وَهْبٍ: سُئِلَ مَالِكٌ عَنْ بَيْعِ الطِّينِ الَّذِي يَأْكُلُهُ النَّاسُ. فَقَالَ: لَيْسَ هُوَ مِنَ الطَّيِّبَاتِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَا عَلَّمْتُمْ مِنَ الْجَوَارِحِ مُكَلِّبِينَ﴾ أَيْ: أُحِلَّ لَكُمُ الذَّبَائِحُ الَّتِي ذُكِرَ اسْمُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهَا وَالطَّيِّبَاتُ مِنَ الرِّزْقِ، وَأُحِلَّ لَكُمْ مَا اصْطَدْتُمُوهُ بِالْجَوَارِحِ، وَهِيَ مِنَ الْكِلَابِ وَالْفُهُودِ وَالصُّقُورِ وَأَشْبَاهِ ذَلِكَ، كَمَا هُوَ مَذْهَبُ الْجُمْهُورِ مِنَ الصَّحَابَةِ وَالتَّابِعِينَ وَالْأَئِمَّةِ، وَمِمَّنْ قَالَ ذَلِكَ: عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَمَا عَلَّمْتُمْ مِنَ الْجَوَارِحِ مُكَلِّبِينَ﴾ وَهُنَّ الْكِلَابُ الْمُعَلَّمَةُ وَالْبَازِي، وَكُلُّ طَيْرٍ يُعَلَّمُ لِلصَّيْدِ وَالْجَوَارِحُ: يَعْنِي الْكِلَابَ الضَّوَارِي وَالْفُهُودَ وَالصُّقُورَ وَأَشْبَاهَهَا.

رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: وَرُوِيَ عَنْ خَيْثَمَة، وَطَاوُسٍ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، وَمَكْحُولٍ، وَيَحْيَى بْنِ أَبِي كَثِيرٍ، نَحْوُ ذَلِكَ. وَرُوِيَ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: الْبَازُ وَالصَّقْرُ مِنَ الْجَوَارِحِ. وَرُوِيَ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ مَثَلُهُ. ثُمَّ رُوِيَ عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ صَيْدَ الطَّيْرِ كُلِّهِ، وَقَرَأَ قَوْلَ اللَّهِ [عَزَّ وَجَلَّ] ﴿وَمَا عَلَّمْتُمْ مِنَ الْجَوَارِحِ مُكَلِّبِينَ﴾ قَالَ: وَرُوِيَ عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ نَحْوَ ذَلِكَ.

وَنَقَلَهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ والسُّدِّي، ثُمَّ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هَنَّاد، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، أَخْبَرْنَا ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: أَمَّا مَا صَادَ مِنَ الطَّيْرِ البُزاة وَغَيْرُهَا مِنَ الطَّيْرِ، فَمَا أدركتَ فَهُوَ لَكَ، وَإِلَّا فَلَا تَطْعَمْهُ.

قُلْتُ: وَالْمَحْكِيُّ عَنِ الْجُمْهُورِ أَنَّ صَيْدَ الطُّيُورِ كَصَيْدِ الْكِلَابِ ؛ لِأَنَّهَا تَكْلَبُ الصَّيْدَ بِمَخَالِبِهَا كَمَا تَكْلُبُهُ الْكِلَابُ، فَلَا فَرْقَ. وَهَذَا مَذْهَبُ الْأَئِمَّةِ الْأَرْبَعَةِ وَغَيْرِهِمْ، وَاخْتَارَهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ، وَاحْتَجَّ فِي ذَلِكَ بِمَا رَوَاهُ عَنْ هَنَّادٍ، حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ يُونُسَ، عَنْ مَجَالِدٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ عَدِيِّ بْنِ حَاتِمٍ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم عَنْ صَيْدِ الْبَازِي، فَقَالَ: "مَا أَمْسَكَ عَلَيْكَ فَكُلْ". وَاسْتَثْنَى الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ صَيْدَ الْكَلْبِ الْأَسْوَدِ؛ لِأَنَّهُ عِنْدَهُ مِمَّا يَجِبُ قَتْلُهُ وَلَا يَحِلُّ اقْتِنَاؤُهُ؛ لِمَا ثَبَتَ فِي صَحِيحِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي ذَرٍّ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "يَقْطَع الصلاةَ الحمارُ والمرأةُ والكلبُ الأسودُ" فَقُلْتُ: مَا بَالُ الْكَلْبِ الْأَسْوَدِ مِنَ الْأَحْمَرِ ؟ فَقَالَ: "الْكَلْبُ الْأَسْوَدُ شَيْطَانٌ" وَفِي الْحَدِيثِ الْآخَرِ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ أَمَرَ بِقَتْلِ الْكِلَابِ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: "مَا بَالُهُمْ وَبَالُ الْكِلَابِ، اقْتُلُوا مِنْهَا كُلَّ أَسْوَدٍ بَهِيم". 

وَسُمِّيَتْ هَذِهِ الْحَيَوَانَاتُ الَّتِي يُصْطَادُ بِهِنَّ: جَوَارِحَ، مِنَ الْجُرْحِ، وَهُوَ: الْكَسْبُ. كَمَا تَقُولُ الْعَرَبُ: فُلَانٌ جَرح أَهْلَهُ خَيْرًا، أَيْ: كَسَبَهُمْ خَيْرًا. وَيَقُولُونَ: فُلَانٌ لَا جَارِحَ لَهُ، أَيْ: لَا كَاسِبَ لَهُ، وَقَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَهُوَ الَّذِي يَتَوَفَّاكُمْ بِاللَّيْلِ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا جَرَحْتُمْ بِالنَّهَارِ﴾ [الْأَنْعَامِ: ٦٠] أَيْ: مَا كَسَبْتُمْ مِنْ خَيْرٍ وَشَرٍّ.

وَقَدْ ذُكِرَ فِي سَبَبِ نُزُولِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ الْكَرِيمَةِ الْحَدِيثُ الَّذِي رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا حَجَّاجُ بْنُ حَمْزَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ الحُبَاب، حَدَّثَنِي مُوسَى بْنُ عُبَيْدَةَ، حَدَّثَنِي أَبَانُ بْنُ صَالِحٍ، عَنِ الْقَعْقَاعِ بْنِ حَكِيمٍ، عَنْ سَلْمَى أَمِّ رَافِعٍ، عَنْ أَبِي رَافِعٍ مَوْلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ؛ أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم أمر بِقَتْلِ الْكِلَابِ، فَقُتِلَتْ، فَجَاءَ النَّاسُ فَقَالُوا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، مَا يَحِلُّ لَنَا مِنْ هَذِهِ الْأُمَّةِ الَّتِي أَمَرْتَ بِقَتْلِهَا؟ قَالَ: فَسَكَتَ، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿يَسْأَلُونَكَ مَاذَا أُحِلَّ لَهُمْ قُلْ أُحِلَّ لَكُمُ الطَّيِّبَاتُ وَمَا عَلَّمْتُمْ مِنَ الْجَوَارِحِ مُكَلِّبِينَ﴾ الْآيَةَ. فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "إِذَا أَرْسَلَ الرَّجُلُ كَلْبَهُ وسَمَّى، فَأَمْسَكَ عَلَيْهِ، فَلْيَأْكُلْ مَا لَمْ يَأْكُلْ ".

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ، عَنْ أَبِي كُرَيْب، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ الْحُبَابِ بِإِسْنَادِهِ، عَنْ أَبِي رَافِعٍ قَالَ: جَاءَ جِبْرِيلُ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ﷺ لِيَسْتَأْذِنَ عَلَيْهِ، فَأَذِنَ لَهُ فَقَالَ: قَدْ أَذِنَّا لَكَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ. قَالَ: أَجَلْ، وَلَكِنَّا لَا نَدْخُلُ بَيْتًا فِيهِ كَلْبٌ، قَالَ أَبُو رَافِعٍ: فَأَمَرَنِي أَنْ أَقْتُلَ كُلَّ كَلْبٍ بِالْمَدِينَةِ، فَقَتَلْتُ، حَتَّى انْتَهَيْتُ إِلَى امْرَأَةٍ عِنْدَهَا كَلْبٌ يَنْبَحُ عَلَيْهَا، فَتَرَكْتُهُ رَحْمَةً لَهَا، ثُمَّ جِئْتُ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَأَخْبَرَتْهُ فَأَمَرَنِي، فَرَجَعَتْ إِلَى الْكَلْبِ فَقَتَلْتُهُ، فَجَاءُوا فَقَالُوا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، مَا يَحِلُّ لنا من هذه الأمة التي أمرت بقتلها؟ قَالَ: فَسَكَتَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ، قَالَ: فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: ﴿يَسْأَلُونَكَ مَاذَا أُحِلَّ لَهُمْ قُلْ أُحِلَّ لَكُمُ الطَّيِّبَاتُ وَمَا عَلَّمْتُمْ مِنَ الْجَوَارِحِ مُكَلِّبِينَ﴾

وَرَوَاهُ الْحَاكِمُ فِي مُسْتَدْرَكِهِ مِنْ طَرِيقِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ أبَان بْنِ صَالِحٍ، بِهِ. وَقَالَ: صَحِيحٌ ولم يخرجاه. وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا الْقَاسِمُ، حَدَّثَنَا الْحُسَيْنُ، حَدَّثَنَا حَجَّاجٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْج، عَنْ عِكْرَمة؛ أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم بَعَثَ أَبَا رَافِعٍ فِي قَتْلِ الْكِلَابِ، حَتَّى بَلَغَ العَوالي فَدَخَلَ عَاصِمُ بْنُ عَدِيٍّ، وَسَعْدُ بْنُ خَيْثَمةَ، وعُوَيْم بْنُ سَاعِدَةَ، فَقَالُوا: مَاذَا أُحِلَّ لَنَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ؟ فَنَزَلَتْ: ﴿يَسْأَلُونَكَ مَاذَا أُحِلَّ لَهُمْ قُلْ أُحِلَّ لَكُمُ الطَّيِّبَاتُ وَمَا عَلَّمْتُمْ مِنَ الْجَوَارِحِ مُكَلِّبِينَ﴾ [الْآيَةَ] 

وَرَوَاهُ الْحَاكِمُ مِنْ طَرِيقِ سِمَاك، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ وَهَكَذَا قَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ كَعْبٍ القُرَظِيّ فِي سَبَبِ نُزُولِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: إِنَّهُ فِي قَتْلِ الْكِلَابِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿مُكَلِّبِينَ﴾ يُحْتَمَلُ أَنْ يَكُونَ حَالًا مِنَ الضَّمِيرِ فِي ﴿عَلَّمْتُمْ﴾ فَيَكُونُ حَالًا مِنَ الْفَاعِلِ، وَيَحْتَمِلُ أَنْ يَكُونَ حَالًا مِنَ الْمَفْعُولِ وَهُوَ ﴿الْجَوَارِحِ﴾ أَيْ: وَمَا عَلَّمْتُمْ مِنَ الْجَوَارِحِ فِي حَالِ كَوْنِهِنَّ مكلَّبات لِلصَّيْدِ، وَذَلِكَ أَنَّ تَقْتَنِصَهُ [الْجَوَارِحُ] بِمَخَالِبِهَا أَوْ أَظْفَارِهَا فَيُسْتَدَلُّ بِذَلِكَ -وَالْحَالَةُ هَذِهِ-عَلَى أَنَّ الْجَارِحَةَ إِذَا قَتَلَ الصَّيْدَ بِصَدْمَتِهِ أَوْ بِمِخْلَابِهِ وَظُفُرِهِ أَنَّهُ لَا يَحِلُّ، كَمَا هُوَ أَحَدُ قَوْلَيِ الشَّافِعِيِّ وَطَائِفَةٍ مِنَ الْعُلَمَاءِ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿تُعَلِّمُونَهُنَّ مِمَّا عَلَّمَكُمُ اللَّهُ﴾ وَهُوَ أَنَّهُ إِذَا أَرْسَلَهُ اسْتَرْسَلَ، وَإِذَا أَشْلَاهُ اسْتَشْلَى وَإِذَا أَخَذَ الصَّيْدَ أُمْسَكَهُ عَلَى صَاحِبِهِ حَتَّى يَجِيءَ إِلَيْهِ وَلَا يُمْسِكُهُ لِنَفْسِهِ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَكُلُوا مِمَّا أَمْسَكْنَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ﴾ فَمَتَى كَانَ الْجَارِحَةُ مُعَلَّمًا وَأَمْسَكَ عَلَى صَاحِبِهِ، وَكَانَ قَدْ ذَكَرَ اسْمَ اللَّهِ عِنْدَ إِرْسَالِهِ حَلَّ الصَّيْدُ، وَإِنْ قَتَلَهُ بِالْإِجْمَاعِ.

وَقَدْ وَرَدَتِ السُّنَّةُ بِمِثْلِ مَا دَلَّتْ عَلَيْهِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ الْكَرِيمَةُ، كَمَا ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ عَنْ عَدِيّ بْنِ حَاتِمٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنِّي أُرْسِلُ الْكِلَابَ المعلَّمة وَأَذْكُرُ اسْمَ اللَّهِ. فَقَالَ: "إِذَا أَرْسَلْتَ كَلْبَكَ المعلَّم وَذَكَرْتَ اسْمَ اللَّهِ، فَكُلْ مَا أَمْسَكَ عَلَيْكَ". قُلْتُ: وَإِنْ قَتَلْنَ؟ قَالَ: "وَإِنْ قَتَلْنَ مَا لَمْ يُشْرِكْهَا كَلْبٌ لَيْسَ مِنْهَا، فَإِنَّكَ إِنَّمَا سَمَّيْتَ عَلَى كَلْبِكَ وَلَمْ تُسَمِّ عَلَى غَيْرِهِ". قُلْتُ لَهُ: فَإِنِّي أَرْمِي بالمِعْرَاض الصَّيْدَ فَأُصِيبُ؟ فَقَالَ: "إِذَا رَمَيْتَ بِالْمِعْرَاضِ فَخَزق فَكُلْهُ، وَإِنْ أَصَابَهُ بعَرْض فَإِنَّهُ وَقِيذٌ، فَلَا تَأْكُلْهُ". وَفِي لَفْظٍ لَهُمَا: "إِذَا أَرْسَلْتَ كَلْبَكَ فَاذْكُرِ اسْمَ اللَّهِ، فَإِنْ أَمْسَكَ عَلَيْكَ فَأَدْرَكْتَهُ حَيًّا فَاذْبَحْهُ، وَإِنْ أَدْرَكْتَهُ قَدْ قُتِلَ وَلَمْ يَأْكُلْ مِنْهُ فَكُلْهُ، فَإِنَّ أخْذ الْكَلْبِ ذَكَاتُهُ". وَفِي رِوَايَةٍ لَهُمَا: "فَإِنْ أَكَلَ فَلَا تَأْكُلْ، فَإِنِّي أَخَافُ أَنْ يَكُونَ أَمْسَكَ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ." فَهَذَا دَلِيلٌ لِلْجُمْهُورِ وَهُوَ الصَّحِيحُ مِنْ مَذْهَبِ الشَّافِعِيِّ، وَهُوَ أَنَّهُ إِذَا أَكَلَ الْكَلْبُ مِنَ الصَّيْدِ يَحْرُمُ مُطْلَقًا، وَلَمْ يَسْتَفْصِلُوا كَمَا وَرَدَ بِذَلِكَ الْحَدِيثُ. وَحُكِيَ عَنْ طَائِفَةٍ مِنَ السَّلَفِ أنهم قالوا: لا يحرم مطلقا.

ذِكْرُ الْآثَارِ بِذَلِكَ:

قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا هَنَّاد، حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيع، عَنْ شُعْبَة، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ قَالَ: قَالَ سَلْمَانُ الْفَارِسِيُّ: كُلْ وَإِنْ أَكَلَ ثُلْثَيْهِ -يَعْنِي الصَّيْدَ-إِذَا أَكَلَ مِنْهُ الْكَلْبُ. وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ سَعِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَرُوبَة، وَعُمَرُ بْنُ عَامِرٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ. وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ، عَنْ سَلْمَانَ.

وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ أَيْضًا عَنْ مُجَاهِدِ بْنِ مُوسَى، عَنْ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ بَكْرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ المُزَنِيّ وَالْقَاسِمِ؛ أَنَّ سَلْمَانَ قَالَ: إِذَا أَكَلَ الْكَلْبُ فَكُلْ، وَإِنْ أَكَلَ ثُلْثَيْهِ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا يُونُسُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْأَعْلَى، أَخْبَرَنَا ابْنُ وَهْب، أَخْبَرَنِي مَخْرَمَة بْنُ بُكَيْر عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ حُمَيْدِ بْنِ مَالِكِ بْنِ خُثَيْم الدُّؤَلِيُّ؛ أَنَّهُ سَأَلَ سَعْدَ بْنَ أَبِي وَقَّاصٍ عَنِ الصَّيْدِ يَأْكُلُ مِنْهُ الْكَلْبُ، فَقَالَ: كُلْ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يَبْقَ مِنْهُ إِلَّا حِذْيَة -يَعْنِي: [إِلَّا] بِضْعَةٌ.

وَرَوَاهُ شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ عَبْدِ رَبِّهِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ بُكَيْرِ بْنِ الأشَجِّ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ، عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ أَبِي وَقَّاصٍ قَالَ: كُلْ وَإِنْ أَكَلَ ثُلْثَيْهِ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ المُثَنَّى، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، حَدَّثَنَا دَاوُدُ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ: لَوْ أَرْسَلْتَ كَلْبَكَ فَأَكَلَ مِنْهُ، فَإِنْ أَكَلَ ثُلْثَيْهِ وَبَقِيَ ثُلْثُهُ فَكُلْ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْأَعْلَى، حَدَّثَنَا المُعْتَمِر قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ عُبَيد اللَّهِ وَحَدَّثَنَا هَنَّادٌ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ-عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: إِذَا أَرْسَلْتَ كَلْبَكَ الْمُعَلَّمَ وَذَكَرْتَ اسْمَ اللَّهِ فَكُلْ مَا أَمْسَكَ عَلَيْكَ، أَكَلَ أَوْ لَمْ يَأْكُلْ.

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ عَبِيدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُمَرَ وَابْنُ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ وَغَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ.

فَهَذِهِ الْآثَارُ ثَابِتَةٌ عَنْ سَلْمَانَ، وَسَعْدِ بْنِ أَبِي وَقَّاصٍ، وَأَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، وَابْنِ عُمَرَ. وَهُوَ مَحْكِيٌّ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، وَابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ. وَاخْتُلِفَ فِيهِ عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، وَالْحَسَنِ الْبَصْرِيِّ. وَهُوَ قَوْلُ الزُّهْرِيِّ، وَرَبِيعَةَ، وَمَالِكٍ. وَإِلَيْهِ ذَهَبَ الشَّافِعِيُّ فِي الْقَدِيمِ، وَأَوْمَأَ إِلَيْهِ فِي الْجَدِيدِ.

وَقَدْ رُوِيَ مِنْ طَرِيقِ سَلْمَانَ الْفَارِسِيِّ مَرْفُوعًا، فَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا عِمْرَانُ بْنُ بَكَّار الكُلاعِيّ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْعَزِيزِ بْنُ مُوسَى اللَّاحُونِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ دِينَارٍ -هُوَ الطَّاحِيُّ-عَنْ أَبِي إِيَاسٍ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ قُرَّة، عَنْ سعيد بن المسيَّب، عَنْ سَلْمَانَ الْفَارِسِيِّ، عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "إِذَا أَرْسَلَ الرَّجُلُ كَلْبَهُ عَلَى الصَّيْدِ فَأَدْرَكَهُ، وَقَدْ أَكَلَ مِنْهُ، فَلْيَأْكُلْ مَا بَقِيَ ".

ثُمَّ قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: وَفِي إِسْنَادِ هَذَا الْحَدِيثِ نَظَرٌ، وَسَعِيدٌ غَيْرُ معلوم له سماع من سلمان، وَالثِّقَاتُ يَرْوُونَهُ مِنْ كَلَامِ سَلْمَانَ غَيْرَ مَرْفُوعٍ. 

وَهَذَا الَّذِي قَالَهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ صَحِيحٌ، لَكِنْ قَدْ رُوِيَ هَذَا الْمَعْنَى مَرْفُوعًا مِنْ وُجُوهٍ أُخَرَ، فَقَالَ أَبُو دَاوُدَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مِنْهال الضَّرِيرُ، حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ زُرَيْع، حَدَّثَنَا حَبِيبٌ الْمُعَلِّمُ، عَنْ عَمْرو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ؛ أَنَّ أَعْرَابِيًّا -يُقَالُ لَهُ: أَبُو ثَعْلَبَةَ-قَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنَّ لِي كِلَابًا مُكَلَّبة، فَأَفْتِنِي فِي صَيْدِهَا. فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "إِنَّ كَانَ لَكَ كِلَابٌ مُكَلَّبَةٌ، فَكُلْ مِمَّا أَمْسَكْنَ عَلَيْكَ". فَقَالَ: ذَكِيًّا وَغَيْرَ ذَكِيٍّ؟ قَالَ: "نَعَمْ". قَالَ: وَإِنْ أَكَلَ مِنْهُ؟ قَالَ: "نَعَمْ، وَإِنْ أَكَلَ مِنْهُ". قَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، أَفْتِنِي فِي قَوْسِي. فَقَالَ: "كُلْ مَا رَدَّتْ عَلَيْكَ قَوْسُكَ" قَالَ: ذَكِيًّا وَغَيْرَ ذَكِيٍّ؟ قَالَ: "وَإِنْ تَغَيَّبَ عَنْكَ مَا لَمْ يَصِلْ، أَوْ تَجِدْ فِيهِ أَثَرَ غَيْرِ سَهْمِكَ". قَالَ: أَفْتِنِي فِي آنِيَةِ الْمَجُوسِ إِذَا اضُّطُرِرْنَا إِلَيْهَا. قَالَ: "اغْسِلْهَا وَكُلْ فِيهَا". .

هَكَذَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ وَقَدْ أَخْرَجَهُ النَّسَائِيُّ. وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ، مِنْ طَرِيقِ بُسْر بْنِ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِي إِدْرِيسَ الْخَوْلَانِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي ثَعْلَبَةَ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "إِذَا أَرْسَلْتَ كَلْبَكَ وَذَكَرْتَ اسْمَ اللَّهِ فَكُلْ، وَإِنْ أَكَلَ مِنْهُ، وَكُلْ مَا رَدَّتْ عَلَيْكَ يَدُكَ" 

وَهَذَانَ إِسْنَادَانِ جَيِّدَانِ، وَقَدْ رَوَى الثَّوْرِيُّ، عَنْ سِماك بْنِ حَرْب، عَنْ عَدِيٍّ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَا كَانَ مِنْ كَلْبٍ ضَارٍّ أَمْسَكَ عَلَيْكَ، فَكُلْ". قُلْتُ: وَإِنْ أَكَلَ؟ قَالَ: "نَعَمْ".

وَرَوَى عَبْدُ الْمَلِكِ بْنُ حَبِيبٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَسَدُ بْنُ مُوسَى، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ عَدي مَثَلَهُ. 

فَهَذِهِ آثَارٌ دَالَّةٌ عَلَى أَنَّهُ يُغْتَفَرُ إِنْ أَكَلَ مِنْهُ الْكَلْبُ. وَقَدِ احْتَجَّ بِهَا مَنْ لَمْ يُحَرِّمِ الصَّيْدَ بِأَكْلِ الْكَلْبِ وَمَا أَشْبَهَهُ، كَمَا تَقَدَّمَ عَمَّنْ حَكَيْنَاهُ عَنْهُمْ، وَقَدْ تَوَسَّطَ آخَرُونَ فَقَالُوا: إِنْ أَكَلَ عَقِبَ مَا أَمْسَكَهُ فَإِنَّهُ يَحْرُمُ لِحَدِيثِ عَدِيِّ بْنِ حَاتِمٍ. وَلِلْعِلَّةِ الَّتِي أَشَارَ إِلَيْهَا النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "فَإِنْ أَكَلَ فَلَا تَأْكُلْ، فَإِنِّي أَخَافُ أَنْ يَكُونَ أَمْسَكَ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ" وَأَمَّا إِنْ أُمْسَكَهُ ثُمَّ انْتَظَرَ صَاحِبَهُ فَطَالَ عَلَيْهِ وَجَاعَ فَأَكَلَ مِنَ الصَّيْدِ لِجُوعِهِ، فَإِنَّهُ لَا يُؤَثِّرُ فِي التَّحْرِيمِ. وَحَمَلُوا عَلَى ذَلِكَ حَدِيثَ أَبِي ثَعْلَبَةَ الخُشَنِيّ، وَهَذَا تَفْرِيقٌ حَسَنٌ، وَجَمْعٌ بَيْنَ الْحَدِيثَيْنِ صَحِيحٌ. وَقَدْ تَمَنَّى الْأُسْتَاذُ أَبُو الْمَعَالِي الجُوَيني فِي كِتَابِهِ "النِّهَايَةِ" أَنْ لَوْ فَصَّلَ مُفَصِّلٌ هَذَا التَّفْصِيلَ، وَقَدْ حَقَّقَ اللَّهُ أُمْنِيَّتَهُ، وَقَالَ بِهَذَا الْقَوْلِ وَالتَّفْرِيقِ طَائِفَةٌ مِنَ الْأَصْحَابِ مِنْهُمْ، وَقَالَ آخَرُونَ قَوْلًا رَابِعًا فِي الْمَسْأَلَةِ، وَهُوَ التَّفْرِقَةُ بَيْنَ أَكْلِ الْكَلْبِ فَيَحْرُمُ لِحَدِيثِ عَدِيّ، وَبَيْنَ أَكْلِ الصُّقُورِ وَنَحْوِهَا فَلَا يَحْرُمُ؛ لِأَنَّهُ لَا يَقْبَلُ التَّعْلِيمَ إِلَّا بِالْأَكْلِ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو كُرَيْب، حَدَّثَنَا أَسْبَاطُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو إِسْحَاقَ الشَّيْبَانِيُّ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ؛ أَنَّهُ قَالَ فِي الطَّيْرِ: إِذَا أَرْسَلْتَهُ فَقَتَلَ فَكُلْ، فَإِنَّ الْكَلْبَ إِذَا ضَرَّبْتَهُ لَمْ يَعُدْ، وَإِنْ تَعَلّم الطَّيْرُ أَنْ يَرْجِعَ إِلَى صَاحِبِهِ وَلَيْسَ يَضْرِبُ، فَإِذَا أَكَلَ مِنَ الصَّيْدِ وَنَتَفَ الرِّيشَ فَكُلْ. 

وَكَذَا قَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ النَّخَعِي، وَالشَّعْبِيُّ، وَحَمَّادُ بْنُ أَبِي سُلَيْمَانَ.

وَقَدْ يُحْتَجُّ لِهَؤُلَاءِ بِمَا رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَعِيدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا الْمُحَارِبِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا مُجالد، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ عَدِيِّ بْنِ حَاتِمٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنَّا قَوْمٌ نَصِيدُ بِالْكِلَابِ وَالْبُزَاةِ، فَمَا يَحِلُّ لَنَا مِنْهَا؟ قَالَ: "يَحِلُّ لَكُمْ مَا عَلَّمْتُمْ مِنَ الْجَوَارِحِ مُكَلِّبِينَ تُعَلِّمُونَهُنَّ مِمَّا عَلَّمَكُمُ اللَّهُ، فَكُلُوا مِمَّا أَمْسَكْنَ عَلَيْكُمْ، وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ" ثُمَّ قَالَ: "مَا أَرْسَلْتَ مِنْ كَلْبٍ وَذَكَرْتَ اسْمَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ، فَكُلْ مِمَّا أَمْسَكَ عَلَيْكَ". قُلْتُ: وَإِنْ قَتَلَ؟ قَالَ: "وَإِنْ قَتَلَ، مَا لَمْ يَأْكُلْ". قُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، وَإِنْ خَالَطَتْ كِلَابُنَا كِلَابًا غَيْرَهَا؟ قَالَ: فَلَا تَأْكُلْ حَتَّى تَعْلَمَ أَنَّ كَلْبَكَ هُوَ الَّذِي أَمْسَكَ". قَالَ: قُلْتُ: إِنَّا قَوْمٌ نَرْمِي، فَمَا يَحِلُّ لَنَا؟ قَالَ: "مَا ذَكَرْتَ اسْمَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ وخزَقَتْ فَكُلْ ".

فَوَجْهُ الدَّلَالَةِ لَهُمْ أَنَّهُ اشْتَرَطَ فِي الْكَلْبِ أَلَّا يَأْكُلَ، وَلَمْ يَشْتَرِطْ ذَلِكَ فِي الْبُزَاةِ، فَدَلَّ عَلَى التَّفْرِقَةِ بَيْنَهُمَا فِي الْحَكَمِ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿فَكُلُوا مِمَّا أَمْسَكْنَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ﴾ أَيْ: عِنْدَ الْإِرْسَالِ، كَمَا قَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ لِعَدِيِّ بْنِ حَاتِمٍ: "إِذَا أَرْسَلْتَ كَلْبَكَ الْمُعَلَّمَ وَذَكَرْتَ اسْمَ اللَّهِ، فَكُلْ مَا أَمْسَكَ عَلَيْكَ". وَفِي حَدِيثِ أَبِي ثَعْلَبَةَ الْمُخَرَّجِ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ أَيْضًا: "إِذَا أَرْسَلْتَ كَلْبَكَ، فَاذْكُرِ اسْمَ اللَّهِ، وَإِذَا رَمَيْتَ بِسَهْمِكَ فَاذْكُرِ اسْمَ اللَّهِ"؛ وَلِهَذَا اشْتَرَطَ مَنِ اشْتَرَطَ مِنَ الْأَئِمَّةِ كَأَحْمَدَ [بْنِ حَنْبَلٍ] -فِي الْمَشْهُورِ عَنْهُ -التَّسْمِيَةَ -عِنْدَ إِرْسَالِ الْكَلْبِ وَالرَّمْيِ بِالسَّهْمِ لِهَذِهِ الْآيَةِ وَهَذَا الْحَدِيثِ، وَهَذَا الْقَوْلُ هُوَ الْمَشْهُورُ عَنِ الْجُمْهُورِ، أَنَّ الْمُرَادَ بِهَذِهِ الْآيَةِ الْأَمْرُ بِالتَّسْمِيَةِ عِنْدَ الْإِرْسَالِ، كَمَا قَالَ السُّدِّي وَغَيْرِ وَاحِدٍ.

وَقَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ﴾ يَقُولُ: إِذَا أَرْسَلْتَ جَارِحَكَ فَقُلْ: بِاسْمِ اللَّهِ، وَإِنْ نَسِيتَ فَلَا حَرَجَ.

وَقَالَ بَعْضُ النَّاسِ: الْمُرَادُ بِهَذِهِ الْآيَةِ الْأَمْرُ بِالتَّسْمِيَةِ عِنْدَ الْأَكْلِ كَمَا ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحِ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ عَلَّم رَبِيبه عُمَرَ بْنَ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ فَقَالَ: "سَمّ اللَّهَ، وكُل بِيَمِينِكَ، وَكُلْ مِمَّا يَلِيكَ". وَفِي صَحِيحِ الْبُخَارِيِّ: عَنْ عَائِشَةَ أَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنَّ قَوْمًا يَأْتُونَنَا -حَدِيثٌ عَهْدُهُمْ بِكُفْرٍ-بلُحْمانٍ لَا نَدْرِي أَذُكِرَ اسْمُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهَا أَمْ لَا؟ فَقَالَ: "سَمّوا اللَّهَ أَنْتُمْ وكلوا." حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ، حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامٌ، عَنْ بُدَيل، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُبَيد بْنِ عُمَير، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عليه وسلم كان يَأْكُلُ الطَّعَامَ فِي سِتَّةِ نَفَرٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ، فَجَاءَ أَعْرَابِيٌّ فَأَكَلَهُ بِلُقْمَتَيْنِ، فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "أَمَا إِنَّهُ لَوْ كَانَ ذَكَرَ اسْمَ اللَّهِ لَكَفَاكُمْ، فَإِذَا أَكَلَ أَحَدُكُمْ فَلْيَذْكُرِ اسْمَ اللَّهِ، فَإِنْ نَسِيَ أَنْ يَذْكُرَ اسْمَ اللَّهِ أَوَّلَهُ فَلْيَقُلْ: بِاسْمِ اللَّهِ أَوَّلَهُ وَآخِرَهُ ".

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَاجَهْ عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ هَارُونَ، بِهِ وَهَذَا مُنْقَطِعٌ بَيْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ عُمَيْرٍ وَعَائِشَةَ، فَإِنَّهُ لَمْ يَسْمَعْ مِنْهَا هَذَا الْحَدِيثَ، بِدَلِيلِ مَا رَوَاهُ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ:

حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ، أَخْبَرْنَا هِشَامٌ -يَعْنِي ابْنَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الدَّسْتَوائي-عَنْ بُدَيْلٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ عُمَيْرٍ؛ أَنَّ امْرَأَةً مِنْهُمْ -يُقَالُ لَهَا: أُمُّ كُلْثُومٍ-حَدَّثَتْهُ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ كَانَ يَأْكُلُ طَعَامًا فِي سِتَّةٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ، فَجَاءَ أَعْرَابِيٌّ جَائِعٌ فَأَكَلَهُ بِلُقْمَتَيْنِ، فَقَالَ: "أَمَا إِنَّهُ لَوْ ذَكَرَ اسْمَ اللَّهِ لَكَفَاكُمْ، فَإِذَا أَكَلَ أَحَدُكُمْ فَلْيَذْكُرِ اسْمَ اللَّهِ، فَإِنْ نَسِيَ اسْمَ اللَّهِ فِي أَوَّلِهِ فَلْيَقُلْ: بِاسْمِ اللَّهِ أَوَّلَهُ وَآخِرَهُ ".

[وَ] رَوَاهُ أَحْمَدُ أَيْضًا، وَأَبُو دَاوُدَ، وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ، وَالنَّسَائِيُّ مِنْ غَيْرِ وَجْهٍ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ الدَّسْتُوَائِيِّ، بِهِ وَقَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: حَسَنٌ صَحِيحٌ.

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: وَقَالَ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا جَابِرُ بْنُ صُبْحٍ حَدَّثَنِي الْمُثَنَّى بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْخُزَاعِيُّ، وَصَحِبْتُهُ إِلَى وَاسِطٍ، فَكَانَ يُسَمِّي فِي أَوَّلِ طَعَامِهِ وَفِي آخِرِ لُقْمَةٍ يَقُولُ: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ أَوَّلَهُ وَآخِرَهُ.

فَقُلْتُ لَهُ: إِنَّكَ تُسَمِّي فِي أَوَّلِ مَا تَأْكُلُ، أَرَأَيْتَ قَوْلَكَ فِي آخِرِ مَا تَأْكُلُ: بِاسْمِ اللَّهِ أَوَّلَهُ وَآخِرَهُ؟ فَقَالَ: أُخْبِرُكَ عَنْ ذَلِكَ إِنَّ جَدِّي أُمِّيَّةَ بْنَ مُخَشَّى -وَكَانَ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ-سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ: إِنَّ رَجُلًا كَانَ يَأْكُلُ، وَالنَّبِيُّ يَنْظُرُ، فَلَمْ يُسَمِّ، حَتَّى كَانَ فِي آخِرِ طَعَامِهِ لُقْمَةٌ، فَقَالَ: بِاسْمِ اللَّهِ أَوَّلَهُ وَآخِرَهُ. فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "وَاللَّهِ مَا زَالَ الشَّيْطَانُ يَأْكُلُ مَعَهُ حَتَّى سَمّى، فَلَمْ يَبْقَ شَيْءٌ فِي بَطْنِهِ حَتَّى قَاءَهُ ".

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ وَالنَّسَائِيُّ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ جَابِرِ بْنِ صُبْحٍ الرَّاسِبِيِّ أَبِي بِشْرٍ الْبَصْرِيِّ وَوَثَّقَهُ ابْنُ مَعِين وَالنَّسَائِيُّ، وَقَالَ أَبُو الْفَتْحِ الْأَزْدِيُّ: لَا تَقُومُ بِهِ الْحُجَّةُ. 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا الْأَعْمَشُ، عَنْ خَيْثَمَة، عَنْ أَبِي حُذَيْفَةَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ الْإِمَامِ أَحْمَدَ: وَاسْمُهُ سَلَمَةُ بْنُ الْهَيْثَمِ بْنِ صُهَيْبٍ -مِنْ أَصْحَابِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ-عَنْ حُذَيْفَةَ قَالَ: كُنَّا إِذَا حَضَرْنَا مَعَ النَّبِيِّ [ﷺ] عَلَى طَعَامٍ، لَمْ نَضَعْ أَيْدِيَنَا حَتَّى يَبْدَأَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ [ﷺ] فَيَضَعُ يَدَهُ، وَإِنَّا حَضَرْنَا مَعَهُ طَعَامًا فَجَاءَتْ جَارِيَةٌ، كَأَنَّمَا تُدفع، فَذَهَبَتْ تَضَعُ يَدَهَا فِي الطَّعَامِ، فَأَخَذَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ بِيَدِهَا، وَجَاءَ أَعْرَابِيٌّ كَأَنَّمَا يُدفع، فَذَهَبَ يَضَعُ يَدَهُ فِي الطَّعَامِ، فَأَخَذَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ [ﷺ] بِيَدِهِ، فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "إِنَّ الشَّيْطَانَ يَسْتَحِلُّ الطَّعَامَ إِذَا لَمْ يُذْكِرِ اسْمُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ، وَإِنَّهُ جَاءَ بِهَذِهِ الْجَارِيَةِ لِيَسْتَحِلَّ بِهَا، فَأَخَذْتُ بِيَدِهَا، وَجَاءَ بِهَذَا الْأَعْرَابِيِّ لِيَسْتَحِلَّ بِهِ، فَأَخَذْتُ بِيَدِهِ، وَالَّذِي نَفْسِي بِيَدِهِ، إِنَّ يَدَهُ فِي يَدِي مَعَ يَدِهِمَا يَعْنِي الشَّيْطَانَ. وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ وَأَبُو دَاوُدَ وَالنَّسَائِيُّ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ الْأَعْمَشِ بِهِ. 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: رَوَى مُسْلِمٌ وَأَهْلُ السُّنَنِ إِلَّا التِّرْمِذِيَّ مِنْ طَرِيقِ ابْنِ جُرَيْج، عَنْ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قَالَ: "إِذَا دَخَلَ الرَّجُلُ بَيْتَهُ، فَذَكَرَ اللَّهَ عِنْدَ دُخُولِهِ وَعِنْدَ طَعَامِهِ، قَالَ الشَّيْطَانُ: لَا مَبِيت لَكُمْ وَلَا عَشَاء، وَإِذَا دَخَلَ فَلَمْ يُذْكَرِ اسْمَ اللَّهِ عِنْدَ دُخُولِهِ قَالَ الشَّيْطَانُ: أَدْرَكْتُمُ الْمَبِيتَ، فَإِذَا لَمْ يَذْكُرِ اسْمَ اللَّهِ عِنْدَ طَعَامِهِ قَالَ: أَدْرَكْتُمُ الْمَبِيتَ وَالْعَشَاءَ". لَفْظُ أَبِي دَاوُدَ.

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ رَبِّهِ، حَدَّثَنَا الْوَلِيدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ، عَنْ وَحْشِيّ بْنِ حَرْب بْنِ وَحْشِي بْنِ حَرْب، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ؛ أَنَّ رَجُلًا قَالَ لِلنَّبِيِّ ﷺ: إِنَّا نَأْكُلُ وَمَا نَشْبَعُ؟ قَالَ: "فَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَأْكُلُونَ مُتَفَرِّقِينَ، اجْتَمِعُوا عَلَى طَعَامِكُمْ وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ، يُبَارَكْ لَكُمْ فِيهِ ".

وَرَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ، وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ، مِنْ طَرِيقِ الْوَلِيدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ.

---

## 5:5 — verse 5

### Translation

When the Exalted mentioned what He had forbidden His believing servants of foul things, and what He had made lawful for them of good things, He said after that: {Today the good things have been made lawful for you}.

Then He mentioned the ruling on the slaughtered animals of the People of the two Scriptures, the Jews and the Christians, saying: {and the food of those who were given the Book is lawful for you}. Ibn Abbas, Abu Umamah, Mujahid, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, Ikrimah, Ata, al-Hasan, Makhul, Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, al-Suddi, and Muqatil ibn Hayyan said: it means their slaughtered animals.

This is a matter upon which the scholars are agreed: that their slaughtered animals are lawful for Muslims, because they hold it forbidden to slaughter for anything other than Allah, and they do not invoke over their slaughtered animals any name but the name of Allah, even if they hold concerning Him, Exalted and Sanctified, beliefs from which He is free. It is established in the Sahih from Abdullah ibn Mughaffal, who said: A container of fat was lowered down on the day of Khaybar. [He said] So I embraced it and said, "I will not give anyone any of this today." Then I turned around and saw the Prophet ﷺ smiling.

The jurists have relied on this as evidence that it is permissible to take what one needs of food and similar things from the spoils of war before the division is made, and this is evident. The Hanafi, Shafi'i, and Hanbali jurists have relied on it as evidence against the followers of Malik, who prohibit eating what the Jews consider forbidden of their slaughtered animals, such as fats and similar things that are forbidden to them. The Malikis do not permit Muslims to eat it, citing the words of the Exalted: {and the food of those who were given the Book is lawful for you}, saying: this is not part of "their food." The majority relied against them on this hadith, but there is room for consideration in that, since it concerns a specific incident, and it is possible that it was a kind of fat which they held to be lawful, such as the fat of the back or the intestines and the like. Allah knows best.

Better evidence than this is what is established in the Sahih: that the people of Khaybar presented the Messenger of Allah ﷺ with a roasted sheep, having poisoned its foreleg, since the foreleg was something he liked. He took some of it and bit into it, and then the foreleg informed him that it was poisoned, so he spat it out. That poison had an effect on the front teeth of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and on his major vessel (abhar). Bishr ibn al-Bara ibn Ma'rur ate some of it with him and died. So he had the Jewish woman who had poisoned it killed; her name was Zaynab, and she was killed in retaliation for Bishr ibn al-Bara.

The point of evidence in this is that he intended to eat it, along with those with him, and did not ask them whether they had removed from it what they consider forbidden of its fat or not.

In another hadith: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was hosted by a Jew with barley bread and stale fat, meaning rancid grease.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: it was read to al-Abbas ibn al-Walid ibn Mazyad, [he said] Muhammad ibn Shu'ayb informed us, al-Nu'man ibn al-Mundhir informed me, from Makhul, who said: Allah revealed {and do not eat of that over which the name of Allah has not been mentioned} [al-An'am: 121], then the Lord, Mighty and Majestic, abrogated it, having mercy on the Muslims, and said: {Today the good things have been made lawful for you, and the food of those who were given the Book is lawful for you}, thereby abrogating it and making the food of the People of the Book lawful.

There is room for consideration in what Makhul said, may Allah have mercy on him, for it does not necessarily follow from the permission of the food of the People of the Book that eating what has not had the name of Allah mentioned over it is permitted, because they do mention the name of Allah over their slaughtered animals and offerings, and they are devoted to that practice. For this reason He did not permit the slaughtered animals of others among the polytheists and those who resemble them, because they do not mention the name of Allah over their slaughtered animals. Moreover, they do not even require proper slaughter for the meat they eat, but eat carrion, unlike the People of the two Scriptures and those who resemble them among the Samaritans and the Sabians, and those who adhere to the religion of Ibrahim (peace be upon him) and Shith (Seth, peace be upon him) and other than them among the prophets, according to one of the two opinions of the scholars, and the Christian Arabs such as the Banu Taghlib, Tanukh, Bahra, Judham, Lakhm, Amilah, and those who resemble them, whose slaughtered animals are not to be eaten according to the majority.

Abu Ja'far ibn Jarir said: [chain] Muhammad ibn Ubaydah said: Ali said: Do not eat the slaughtered animals of the Banu Taghlib, for they only hold to Christianity through their drinking of wine.

Others among the later and earlier generations said the same.

Sa'id ibn Abi Arubah said, from Qatadah, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab and al-Hasan, that they saw no harm in the slaughtered animals of the Christians of Banu Taghlib.

As for the Magians (Zoroastrians), even though jizyah was taken from them by way of following and attaching them to the People of the Book, their slaughtered animals are not eaten and their women are not married, contrary to Abu Thawr Ibrahim ibn Khalid al-Kalbi, one of the jurists among the companions of al-Shafi'i, and Ahmad ibn Hanbal. When he said this and it became known from him, the jurists rejected it, so much so that Imam Ahmad said of him: "Abu Thawr is like his name!" meaning, in this matter, and it is as though he relied on the generality of a hadith reported as mursal (with a broken chain) from the Prophet ﷺ, that he said: "Treat them according to the practice concerning the People of the Book," but this has not been established with this wording. Rather, what is in Sahih al-Bukhari is from Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf: that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ took jizyah from the Magians of Hajar. Even if the soundness of this hadith is granted, its generality is qualified by the implication of this verse: {and the food of those who were given the Book is lawful for you}, whose implication, by way of contrariety, indicates that the food of the followers of other religions besides them is not lawful.

As for His saying: {and your food is lawful for them}, meaning: it is lawful for you to feed them from your slaughtered animals. This is not a statement about the ruling as it stands with them, unless it is a statement about what they have been commanded regarding eating from any food over which the name of Allah has been mentioned, whether it be from among the people of their own faith or others. The first is clearer in meaning, that is: it is permitted for you to feed them from your slaughtered animals, just as you eat from theirs. This falls under the category of reciprocity, correspondence, and requital, just as the Prophet ﷺ clothed Abdullah ibn Ubayy ibn Salul in his own garment when he died and buried him in it. They said this was because Abdullah had clothed al-Abbas with his garment when he arrived in Medina, so the Prophet ﷺ repaid him in kind. As for the hadith which states: "Do not accompany anyone but a believer, and let no one eat your food but one who is righteous," it is to be understood as recommendation and encouragement, not obligation. Allah knows best.

As for His saying: {and chaste women among the believers}, meaning: I have made lawful for you marriage to free, chaste women among the believing women. Mentioning this prepares the way for what follows, namely His saying: {and chaste women among those who were given the Book before you}. It has been said that by "muhsanat" (chaste/protected women) He meant free women as opposed to slave women, as reported by Ibn Jarir from Mujahid. Mujahid said only "muhsanat means free women," so it is possible that he meant what has been reported from him, and it is possible that he meant by "free" the chaste woman, as Mujahid said in another report from him. This latter is the view of the majority here, and it is closer to the truth, so that it should not be the case that she is both a dhimmi woman and, in addition, unchaste, which would corrupt her situation entirely, and her husband would end up, as the proverb says, with "bad dates and a short measure besides." What is apparent from the verse is that "muhsanat" here means women chaste from fornication, as He said in another verse: {chaste, not committing fornication and not taking secret lovers} [al-Nisa: 25].

The exegetes and scholars then differed concerning His saying: {and chaste women among those who were given the Book before you}, as to whether it applies generally to every chaste woman of the People of the Book, whether free or a slave. Ibn Jarir reported this from a group of the earlier scholars among those who interpreted "muhsanah" as meaning chaste. It has also been said that "the People of the Book" here means Israelite women specifically, and this is the position of al-Shafi'i. It has also been said that what is meant by this is the dhimmi women, as opposed to women at war with the Muslims, based on His saying: {Fight those who do not believe in Allah nor in the Last Day, [nor forbid what Allah and His Messenger have forbidden, nor follow the religion of truth, among those who were given the Book, until they pay the jizyah with willing submission and feel themselves subdued]} [al-Tawbah: 29].

Abdullah ibn Umar used to disapprove of marrying a Christian woman, saying: I know of no greater shirk than for her to say that her Lord is Isa (Jesus), and Allah, Exalted, has said: {And do not marry polytheist women until they believe} [al-Baqarah: 221].

Ibn Abi Hatim said: my father narrated to us, Muhammad ibn Hatim ibn Sulayman al-Muaddib narrated to us, al-Qasim ibn Malik, meaning al-Muzani, narrated to us, Isma'il ibn Sami' narrated to us, from Abu Malik al-Ghifari, from Ibn Abbas, who said: when this verse was revealed, {And do not marry the polytheist women until they believe}, he said: the people held back from them until the verse after it was revealed, {And the chaste women from among those who were given the Book before you}, so the people married from the women of the People of the Book.

A group of the companions married Christian women and saw no harm in that, relying on this noble verse: {And the chaste women from among those who were given the Book before you}, taking this verse as a specification restricting the verse in Surat al-Baqarah, {And do not marry the polytheist women until they believe} [verse 221], if it is said that the women of the Book are included in its general wording. Otherwise there is no contradiction between the two verses at all, because the People of the Book are sometimes distinguished in mention from the polytheists in more than one place, as the Almighty said: {Those who disbelieved from among the People of the Book and the polytheists were not to separate until the clear proof came to them} [al-Bayyinah: 1], and as in His saying: {And say to those who were given the Book and to the unlettered ones, have you submitted? If they submit, then they have been guided} [Al Imran: 20]. And His saying, {when you have given them their rewards}, meaning: their dowries, that is: since they are chaste and virtuous, give them their dowries willingly and with a good heart.

Jabir ibn Abd Allah, Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, Amir al-Sha'bi, and al-Hasan al-Basri gave the legal opinion that if a man marries a woman and she commits fornication before he has consummated the marriage with her, then he and she are to be separated, and she must return to him what he had given her of the dowry. Ibn Jarir related this from them.

And His saying: {chaste, not committing fornication and not taking secret lovers}. Just as chastity, which is abstention from fornication, was made a condition for the women, so likewise it was made a condition for the men, that the man also be chaste and virtuous. That is why He said, {not committing fornication}, meaning those fornicators who do not restrain themselves from disobedience and do not turn away anyone who comes to them, and {nor taking secret lovers}, meaning those who have mistresses with whom alone they engage in such acts, exactly as was mentioned previously in Surat al-Nisa.

For this reason, the Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal, may God have mercy on him, held the view that the marriage of a promiscuous woman is not valid until she repents, and as long as she remains in that state, her marriage to a chaste man is not valid. Likewise, in his view, the marriage contract of a corrupt man to a chaste woman is not valid until he repents and abandons the fornication he was engaged in. This is based on this verse and on the other hadith: "The flogged fornicator marries none but his like."

Ibn Jarir said: Muhammad ibn Bashshar narrated to us, Sulayman ibn Harb narrated to us, Abu Hilal narrated to us, from Qatadah, from al-Hasan, who said: Umar ibn al-Khattab (may God be pleased with him) said: I had resolved not to let anyone who had committed an indecency in Islam marry a chaste woman. Ubayy ibn Ka'b said to him: O Commander of the Believers, polytheism is greater than that, and it is accepted from its perpetrator if he repents.

The discussion of this matter will come in full, God willing, at His saying: {The fornicator does not marry except a fornicatress or a polytheist woman, and the fornicatress is not married except by a fornicator or a polytheist, and that has been forbidden to the believers} [al-Nur: 3]. That is why the Almighty said here: {And whoever disbelieves in faith, his deeds have been rendered void, and in the Hereafter he will be among the losers}.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

لَمَّا ذَكَرَ تَعَالَى مَا حَرَّمَهُ عَلَى عِبَادِهِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنَ الْخَبَائِثِ، وَمَا أَحَلَّهُ لَهُمْ مِنَ الطَّيِّبَاتِ، قَالَ بَعْدَهُ: ﴿الْيَوْمَ أُحِلَّ لَكُمُ الطَّيِّبَاتُ﴾

ثُمَّ ذَكَرَ حُكْمَ ذَبَائِحِ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابَيْنِ مِنَ الْيَهُودِ وَالنَّصَارَى، فَقَالَ: ﴿وَطَعَامُ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَبَ حِلٌّ لَكُمْ﴾ قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ، وَأَبُو أُمَامَةَ، وَمُجَاهِدٌ، وَسَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْر، وعِكْرِمة، وعَطاء، وَالْحَسَنُ، ومَكْحول، وَإِبْرَاهِيمُ النَّخَعِي، والسُّدِّي، ومُقاتل بْنُ حيَّان: يَعْنِي ذَبَائِحَهُمْ.

وَهَذَا أَمْرٌ مُجْمَعٌ عَلَيْهِ بَيْنَ الْعُلَمَاءِ: أَنَّ ذَبَائِحَهُمْ حَلَالٌ لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ؛ لِأَنَّهُمْ يَعْتَقِدُونَ تَحْرِيمَ الذَّبْحِ لِغَيْرِ اللَّهِ، وَلَا يَذْكُرُونَ عَلَى ذَبَائِحِهِمْ إِلَّا اسْمَ اللَّهِ، وَإِنِ اعْتَقَدُوا فِيهِ تَعَالَى مَا هُوَ مُنَزَّهٌ عَنْ قَوْلِهِمْ، تَعَالَى وَتَقَدَّسَ. وَقَدْ ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُغَفَّل قَالَ: دُلِّي بِجِرَابٍ مِنْ شَحْمٍ يَوْمَ خَيْبَرَ. [قَالَ] فَاحْتَضَنْتُهُ وَقُلْتُ: لَا أُعْطِي الْيَوْمَ مِنْ هَذَا أَحَدًا، والتفتُّ فَإِذَا النَّبِيُّ ﷺ يَتَبَسَّمُ. 

فَاسْتَدَلَّ بِهِ الْفُقَهَاءُ عَلَى أَنَّهُ يَجُوزُ تناولُ مَا يَحْتَاجُ إِلَيْهِ مِنَ الْأَطْعِمَةِ وَنَحْوِهَا مِنَ الْغَنِيمَةِ قَبْلَ الْقِسْمَةِ، وَهَذَا ظَاهِرٌ. وَاسْتَدَلَّ بِهِ الْفُقَهَاءُ الْحَنَفِيَّةُ وَالشَّافِعِيَّةُ وَالْحَنَابِلَةُ عَلَى أَصْحَابِ مَالِكٍ فِي مَنْعِهِمْ أَكْلَ مَا يَعْتَقِدُ الْيَهُودُ تَحْرِيمَهُ مِنْ ذَبَائِحِهِمْ، كَالشُّحُومِ وَنَحْوِهَا مِمَّا حَرُمَ عَلَيْهِمْ. فَالْمَالِكِيَّةُ لَا يُجَوِّزُونَ لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ أَكْلَهُ؛ لِقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَطَعَامُ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ حِلٌّ لَكُمْ﴾ قَالُوا: وَهَذَا لَيْسَ مِنْ طَعَامِهِمْ. وَاسْتَدَلَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الْجُمْهُورُ بِهَذَا الْحَدِيثِ، وَفِي ذَلِكَ نَظَرٌ؛ لِأَنَّهُ قَضِيَّةُ عَيْنٍ، وَيُحْتَمَلُ أَنَّهُ كَانَ شَحْمًا يَعْتَقِدُونَ حِلَّهُ، كَشَحْمِ الظَّهْرِ وَالْحَوَايَا وَنَحْوِهِمَا، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَأَجْوَدُ مِنْهُ فِي الدَّلَالَةِ مَا ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحِ: أَنَّ أَهْلَ خَيْبَرَ أَهْدَوْا لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ شَاةٌ مَصْليَّة، وَقَدْ سَمّوا ذِرَاعَهَا، وَكَانَ يُعْجِبُهُ الذِّرَاعُ، فَتَنَاوَلَهُ فنَهَشَ مِنْهُ نَهْشةً، فَأَخْبَرَهُ الذِّرَاعُ أَنَّهُ مَسْمُومٌ، فلَفَظَه وَأَثَّرَ ذَلِكَ السُّمُّ فِي ثَنَايَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ وَفِي أبْهَرِه، وَأَكَلَ مَعَهُ مِنْهَا بِشْرُ بْنُ الْبَرَاءِ بْنِ مَعْرور؛ فَمَاتَ، فَقَتَلَ الْيَهُودِيَّةَ الَّتِي سَمَّتْهَا، وَكَانَ اسْمُهَا زَيْنَبَ، فَقُتِلَتْ بِبِشْرِ بْنِ الْبَرَاءِ. 

وَوَجْهُ الدَّلَالَةِ مِنْهُ أَنَّهُ عَزَمَ عَلَى أَكْلِهَا وَمَنْ مَعَهُ، وَلَمْ يَسْأَلْهُمْ هَلْ نَزَعُوا مِنْهَا مَا يَعْتَقِدُونَ تَحْرِيمَهُ مِنْ شَحْمِهَا أَمْ لَا.

وَفِي الْحَدِيثِ الْآخَرِ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ أَضَافَهُ يَهُودِيٌّ عَلَى خُبْزِ شَعِيرٍ وَإِهَالَةٍ سنَخَة، يَعْنِي: ودَكا زَنِخًا 

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: قُرِئَ عَلَى الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ الْوَلِيدِ بْنِ مَزْيَد، أَخْبَرْنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ شُعَيْبٍ، أَخْبَرَنِي النُّعْمَانُ بْنُ الْمُنْذِرِ، عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ قَالَ: أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿وَلا تَأْكُلُوا مِمَّا لَمْ يُذْكَرِ اسْمُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ﴾ [الْأَنْعَامِ: ١٢١] ثُمَّ نَسَخَهَا الرَّبُّ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، وَرَحِمَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ، فَقَالَ: ﴿الْيَوْمَ أُحِلَّ لَكُمُ الطَّيِّبَاتُ وَطَعَامُ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ حِلٌّ لَكُمْ﴾ فَنَسَخَهَا بِذَلِكَ، وَأَحَلَّ طَعَامَ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ.

وَفِي هَذَا الَّذِي قَالَهُ مَكْحُولٌ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ، نَظَرٌ، فَإِنَّهُ لَا يَلْزَمُ مِنْ إِبَاحَتِهِ طَعَامَ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ إباحةُ أَكْلِ مَا لَمْ يُذْكَرِ اسْمُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ؛ لِأَنَّهُمْ يَذْكُرُونَ اسْمَ اللَّهِ عَلَى ذَبَائِحِهِمْ وَقَرَابِينِهِمْ، وَهُمْ متعبدون بِذَلِكَ؛ وَلِهَذَا لَمْ يُبِحْ ذَبَائِحَ مَنْ عَدَاهُمْ مِنْ أَهْلِ الشِّرْكِ وَمَنْ شَابَهَهُمْ، لِأَنَّهُمْ لَمْ يَذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ عَلَى ذَبَائِحِهِمْ، بَلْ وَلَا يَتَوَقَّفُونَ فِيمَا يَأْكُلُونَهُ مِنَ اللَّحْمِ عَلَى ذَكَاةٍ، بَلْ يَأْكُلُونَ الْمَيْتَةَ، بِخِلَافِ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابَيْنِ وَمَنْ شَاكَلَهُمْ مِنَ السَّامِرَةِ وَالصَّابِئَةِ، وَمَنْ تَمَسّك بِدِينِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَشِيثَ وَغَيْرِهِمَا مِنَ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ، عَلَى أَحَدِ قَوْلَيِ الْعُلَمَاءِ، وَنَصَارَى الْعَرَبِ كَبَنِي تَغْلِب وتَنُوخ وبَهْرَاء وجُذام ولَخْم وعَاملة وَمَنْ أَشْبَهَهُمْ، لَا تُؤْكَلُ ذَبَائِحُهُمْ عِنْدَ الْجُمْهُورِ.

[وَ] قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرِ بْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّة، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبِيدة قَالَ: قَالَ عَلِيٌّ: لَا تَأْكُلُوا ذَبَائِحَ بَنِي تَغْلِبَ؛ لِأَنَّهُمْ إِنَّمَا يَتَمَسَّكُونَ مِنَ النَّصْرَانِيَّةِ بِشُرْبِ الْخَمْرِ.

وَكَذَا قَالَ غَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ مِنَ الْخَلَفِ وَالسَّلَفِ.

وَقَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَرُوبَة، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ، وَالْحَسَنِ؛ أَنَّهُمَا كَانَا لَا يَرَيَانِ بَأْسًا بِذَبِيحَةِ نَصَارَى بَنِي تَغْلِبَ.

وَأَمَّا الْمَجُوسُ، فَإِنَّهُمْ وَإِنْ أُخِذَتْ مِنْهُمُ الْجِزْيَةُ تَبَعًا وَإِلْحَاقًا لِأَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ فَإِنَّهُمْ لَا تُؤْكَلُ ذَبَائِحُهُمْ وَلَا تُنْكَحُ نِسَاؤُهُمْ، خِلَافًا لِأَبِي ثَوْرٍ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ الْكَلْبِيِّ، أَحَدِ الْفُقَهَاءِ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ الشَّافِعِيِّ، وَأَحْمَدَ بْنِ حَنْبَلٍ، وَلَمَّا قَالَ ذَلِكَ وَاشْتَهَرَ عَنْهُ أَنْكَرَ عَلَيْهِ الْفُقَهَاءُ ذَلِكَ، حَتَّى قَالَ عَنْهُ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: أَبُو ثَوْرٍ كَاسْمِهِ! يَعْنِي فِي هَذِهِ الْمَسْأَلَةِ، وَكَأَنَّهُ تَمَسَّكَ بِعُمُومِ حَدِيثٍ رُوِيَ مُرْسَلًا عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: "سُنوا بِهِمْ سُنَّةَ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ"، وَلَكِنْ لَمْ يَثْبُتْ بِهَذَا اللَّفْظِ، وَإِنَّمَا الَّذِي فِي صَحِيحِ الْبُخَارِيِّ: عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ عَوْفٍ؛ أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم أَخَذَ الْجِزْيَةَ مِنْ مَجوس هَجَر وَلَوْ سُلِّمَ صِحَّةُ هَذَا الْحَدِيثِ، فَعُمُومُهُ مَخْصُوصٌ بِمَفْهُومِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: ﴿وَطَعَامُ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ حِلٌّ لَكُمْ﴾ فَدَلَّ بِمَفْهُومِهِ -مَفْهُومِ الْمُخَالَفَةِ-عَلَى أَنَّ طَعَامَ مَنْ عَدَاهُمْ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْأَدْيَانِ لَا يَحِلُّ 

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَطَعَامُكُمْ حِلٌّ لَهُمْ﴾ أَيْ: وَيَحِلُّ لَكُمْ أَنَّ تُطْعِمُوهُمْ مِنْ ذَبَائِحِكُمْ، وَلَيْسَ هَذَا إِخْبَارًا عَنِ الْحُكْمِ عِنْدَهُمْ، اللَّهُمَّ إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ خَبَرًا عَمَّا أُمِرُوا بِهِ مِنَ الْأَكْلِ مِنْ كُلِّ طَعَامٍ ذُكِرَ اسْمُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ، سَوَاءٌ كَانَ مَنْ أَهْلِ مِلَّتِهِمْ أَوْ غَيْرِهَا. وَالْأَوَّلُ أَظْهَرُ فِي الْمَعْنَى، أَيْ: وَلَكُمْ أَنْ تُطْعِمُوهُمْ مِنْ ذَبَائِحِكُمْ كَمَا أَكَلْتُمْ مِنْ ذَبَائِحِهِمْ. وَهَذَا مِنْ بَابِ الْمُكَافَأَةِ وَالْمُقَابَلَةِ وَالْمُجَازَاةِ، كَمَا أَلْبَسَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ ثَوْبَهُ لِعَبْدِ الله بن أبيّ بن سَلُولَ حِينَ مَاتَ وَدَفَنَهُ فِيهِ، قَالُوا: لِأَنَّهُ كَانَ قَدْ كَسَا الْعَبَّاسَ حِينَ قَدِمَ الْمَدِينَةَ ثَوْبَهُ، فَجَازَاهُ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ ذَلِكَ بِذَلِكَ، فَأَمَّا الْحَدِيثُ الَّذِي فِيهِ: "لَا تَصْحَبْ إِلَّا مُؤْمِنا، وَلَا يَأْكُلْ طَعَامَكَ إِلَّا تَقِيٌّ" فَمَحْمُولٌ عَلَى النَّدْبِ وَالِاسْتِحْبَابِ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَالْمُحْصَنَاتُ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنَاتِ﴾ أَيْ: وَأَحِلُّ لَكُمْ نِكَاحُ الْحَرَائِرِ الْعَفَائِفِ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ الْمُؤْمِنَاتِ، وَذِكْرُ هَذَا تَوْطِئَةٌ لِمَا بَعْدَهُ، وَهُوَ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَالْمُحْصَنَاتُ مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُم﴾ فَقِيلَ: أَرَادَ بِالْمُحَصَّنَاتِ: الْحَرَائِرُ دُونَ الْإِمَاءِ، حَكَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ. وَإِنَّمَا قَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ: الْمُحْصَنَاتُ: الْحَرَائِرُ، فَيُحْتَمَلُ أَنْ يَكُونَ أَرَادَ مَا حَكَاهُ عَنْهُ، وَيُحْتَمَلُ أَنْ يَكُونَ أَرَادَ بِالْحُرَّةِ الْعَفِيفَةَ، كَمَا قَالَهُ مُجَاهِدٌ فِي الرِّوَايَةِ الْأُخْرَى عَنْهُ. وَهُوَ قَوْلُ الْجُمْهُورِ هَاهُنَا، وَهُوَ الْأَشْبَهُ؛ لِئَلَّا يَجْتَمِعَ فِيهَا أَنْ تَكُونَ ذِمِّيَّةً وَهِيَ مَعَ ذَلِكَ غَيْرُ عَفِيفَةٍ، فَيُفْسِدُ حَالَهَا بِالْكُلِّيَّةِ، وَيَتَحَصَّلُ زَوْجُهَا عَلَى مَا قِيلَ فِي الْمَثَلِ: "حَشفَا وسَوء كَيْلَةٍ". وَالظَّاهِرُ مِنَ الْآيَةِ أَنَّ الْمُرَادَ بِالْمُحْصَنَاتِ: الْعَفِيفَاتُ عَنِ الزِّنَا، كَمَا قَالَ فِي الْآيَةِ الْأُخْرَى: ﴿مُحْصَنَاتٍ غَيْرَ مُسَافِحَاتٍ وَلا مُتَّخِذَاتِ أَخْدَانٍ﴾ [النِّسَاءِ: ٢٥] .

ثُمَّ اخْتَلَفَ الْمُفَسِّرُونَ وَالْعُلَمَاءُ فِي قوله: ﴿وَالْمُحْصَنَاتُ مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ﴾ هَلْ يَعُمُّ كُلَّ كِتَابِيَّةٍ عَفِيفَةٍ، سَوَاءٌ كَانَتْ حُرَّةً أَوْ أَمَةً؟ حَكَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ عَنْ طَائِفَةٍ مِنَ السَّلَفِ، مِمَّنْ فَسَّرَ الْمُحْصَنَةَ بِالْعَفِيفَةِ. وَقِيلَ: الْمُرَادُ بِأَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ هَاهُنَا الْإِسْرَائِيلِيَّاتُ، وَهُوَ مَذْهَبُ الشَّافِعِيِّ. وَقِيلَ: الْمُرَادُ بِذَلِكَ: الذِّمِّيَّاتُ دُونَ الْحَرْبِيَّاتِ؛ لِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿قَاتِلُوا الَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَلا بِالْيَوْمِ الآخِرِ [وَلا يُحَرِّمُونَ مَا حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ وَلا يَدِينُونَ دِينَ الْحَقِّ مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ حَتَّى يُعْطُوا الْجِزْيَةَ عَنْ يَدٍ وَهُمْ صَاغِرُونَ] ﴾ [التَّوْبَةِ: ٢٩] 

وَقَدْ كَانَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُمَرَ لَا يَرَى التَّزْوِيجَ بِالنَّصْرَانِيَّةِ، وَيَقُولُ: لَا أَعْلَمَ شِرْكًا أَعْظَمَ مِنْ أَنْ تَقُولَ: إِنَّ رَبَّهَا عِيسَى، وَقَدْ قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَلا تَنْكِحُوا الْمُشْرِكَاتِ حَتَّى يُؤْمِنَّ﴾ الْآيَةَ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ٢٢١] .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ حَاتِمِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ الْمُؤَدِّبُ، حَدَّثَنَا الْقَاسِمُ بْنُ مَالِكٍ -يَعْنِي المُزَنِيّ-حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ سَمِيع، عَنْ أَبِي مَالِكٍ الْغِفَارِيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: لَمَّا نَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ: ﴿وَلا تَنْكِحُوا الْمُشْرِكَاتِ حَتَّى يُؤْمِنَّ﴾ قَالَ: فَحَجَزَ النَّاسُ عَنْهُنَّ حَتَّى نَزَلَتِ الَّتِي بَعْدَهَا: ﴿وَالْمُحْصَنَاتُ مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ﴾ فَنَكَحَ النَّاسُ [مِنْ] نِسَاءِ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ.

وَقَدْ تَزَوَّجَ جَمَاعَةٌ مِنَ الصَّحَابَةِ مِنْ نِسَاءِ النَّصَارَى وَلَمْ يَرَوْا بِذَلِكَ بَأْسًا، أَخْذًا بِهَذِهِ الْآيَةِ الْكَرِيمَةِ: ﴿وَالْمُحْصَنَاتُ مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ﴾ فَجَعَلُوا هَذِهِ مُخَصَّصَةً لِلْآيَةِ الَّتِي الْبَقَرَةِ: ﴿وَلا تَنْكِحُوا الْمُشْرِكَاتِ حَتَّى يُؤْمِنَّ﴾ [الْآيَةَ: ٢٢١] إِنْ قِيلَ بِدُخُولِ الْكِتَابِيَّاتِ فِي عُمُومِهَا، وَإِلَّا فَلَا مُعَارَضَةَ بَيْنَهَا وَبَيْنَهَا ؛ لِأَنَّ أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ قَدْ يُفْصَل فِي ذِكْرِهِمْ عَنِ الْمُشْرِكِينَ فِي غَيْرِ مَوْضِعٍ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿لَمْ يَكُنِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ وَالْمُشْرِكِينَ مُنْفَكِّينَ حَتَّى تَأْتِيَهُمُ الْبَيِّنَةُ﴾ [الْبَيِّنَةُ: ١] وَكَقَوْلِهِ ﴿وَقُلْ لِلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ وَالأمِّيِّينَ أَأَسْلَمْتُمْ فَإِنْ أَسْلَمُوا فَقَدِ اهْتَدَوْا﴾ الآية [آل عمران: ٢٠] ، وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿إِذَا آتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ﴾ أَيْ: مُهُورَهُنَّ، أَيْ: كَمَا هُنَّ مُحْصَنَاتٌ عَفَائِفُ، فَابْذُلُوا لَهُنَّ الْمُهُورَ عَنْ طِيبِ نَفْسٍ. وَقَدْ أَفْتَى جَابِرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، وَإِبْرَاهِيمُ النَّخَعِيُّ، وَعَامِرٌ الشَّعْبِيُّ، وَالْحَسَنُ الْبَصْرِيُّ بِأَنَّ الرَّجُلَ إِذَا نَكَحَ امْرَأَةً فَزَنَتْ قَبْلَ دُخُولِهِ بِهَا: أَنَّهُ يُفَرَّقُ بَيْنَهُ وَبَيْنَهَا، وتَرُدّ عَلَيْهِ مَا بَذَلَ لَهَا مِنَ الْمَهْرِ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ عَنْهُمْ..

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿مُحْصِنِينَ غَيْرَ مُسَافِحِينَ وَلا مُتَّخِذِي أَخْدَانٍ﴾ فَكَمَا شَرَطَ الْإِحْصَانَ فِي النِّسَاءِ -وَهِيَ الْعِفَّةُ-عَنِ الزِّنَا كَذَلِكَ شَرَطَهَا فِي الرِّجَالِ وَهُوَ أَنْ يَكُونَ الرَّجُلُ أَيْضًا مُحْصَنًا عَفِيفًا؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿غَيْرَ مُسَافِحِينَ﴾ وَهُمُ: الزُّنَاةُ الَّذِينَ لَا يَرْتَدِعُونَ عَنْ مَعْصِيَةٍ، وَلَا يَرُدُّونَ أَنْفُسَهُمْ عَمَّنْ جَاءَهُمْ، ﴿وَلا مُتَّخِذِي أَخْدَانٍ﴾ أَيْ: ذَوِي الْعَشِيقَاتِ الَّذِينَ لَا يَفْعَلُونَ إِلَّا مَعَهُنَّ، كَمَا تَقَدَّمَ فِي سُورَةِ النِّسَاءِ سَوَاءٌ؛ وَلِهَذَا ذَهَبَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ بْنُ حَنْبَلٍ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ، إِلَى أَنَّهُ لَا يَصِحُّ نِكَاحُ الْمَرْأَةِ البَغي حَتَّى تَتُوبَ، وَمَا دَامَتْ كَذَلِكَ لَا يَصِحُّ تَزْوِيجُهَا مِنْ رَجُلٍ عَفِيفٍ، وَكَذَلِكَ لَا يَصِحُّ عِنْدَهُ عَقْدُ الرَّجُلِ الْفَاجِرِ عَلَى عَفِيفَةٍ حَتَّى يَتُوبَ وَيُقْلِعَ عَمَّا هُوَ فِيهِ مِنَ الزِّنَا؛ لِهَذِهِ الْآيَةِ وَلِلْحَدِيثِ الْآخَرِ: "لَا يَنْكِحُ الزَّانِي الْمَجْلُودُ إِلَّا مِثْلَهُ." 

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَشّار، حَدَّثَنَا سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ حَرْب، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو هِلَالٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ: قَالَ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ [رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ] لَقَدْ هَمَمْتُ أَلَّا أَدَعَ أَحَدًا أَصَابَ فَاحِشَةً فِي الْإِسْلَامِ أَنْ يَتَزَوَّجَ مُحْصَنَةً. فَقَالَ لَهُ أُبِيُّ بْنُ كَعْبٍ: يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ، الشِّرْكُ أَعْظَمُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ، وَقَدْ يُقْبَلُ مِنْهُ إِذَا تَابَ. 

وَسَيَأْتِي الْكَلَامُ عَلَى هَذِهِ الْمَسْأَلَةِ مُسْتَقْصًى [إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى] عِنْدَ قَوْلِهِ: ﴿الزَّانِي لَا يَنْكِحُ إلَّا زَانِيَةً أَوْ مُشْرِكَةً وَالزَّانِيَةُ لَا يَنْكِحُهَا إِلا زَانٍ أَوْ مُشْرِكٌ وَحُرِّمَ ذَلِكَ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ﴾ [النُّورِ: ٣] ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى هَاهُنَا: ﴿وَمَنْ يَكْفُرْ بِالإيمَانِ فَقَدْ حَبِطَ عَمَلُهُ وَهُوَ فِي الآخِرَةِ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ﴾

---

## 5:6 — verse 6

### Translation
