Many of the salaf said: His statement, {when you rise up to prayer}, means "while you are in a state of hadath (ritual impurity requiring ablution)."

Others said: When you rise from sleep to prayer, and both of these are close in meaning.

Others said: Rather the meaning is more general than that. The verse is a command to perform wudu whenever one rises to prayer, but this command applies to the one in a state of hadath as an obligation, and to the one already in a state of purity as a matter of recommendation and desirability. It has also been said that the command to perform wudu for every prayer was obligatory at the beginning of Islam, then it was abrogated.

Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal said: [chain] Sulayman ibn Buraydah, from his father, who said: The Prophet ﷺ used to perform wudu for every prayer. When the day of the Conquest came, he performed wudu and wiped over his two leather socks, and prayed the prayers with a single wudu. Umar said to him: "O Messenger of Allah, you have done something you did not use to do." He said: "I did it intentionally, O Umar."

This is how Muslim and the compilers of the Sunan collections narrated it, from the hadith of Sufyan al-Thawri, from Alqamah ibn Marthad. It occurs in the Sunan of Ibn Majah, from Sufyan, from Muharib ibn Dithar, in place of Alqamah ibn Marthad, both of them narrating from Sulayman ibn Buraydah with the same content. Al-Tirmidhi said: hasan sahih (good, authentic).

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] al-Fadl ibn al-Mubashshir said: I saw Jabir ibn Abdullah pray the prayers with a single wudu. When he urinated or had a hadath, he would perform wudu and wipe over his leather socks with the remainder of his purifying water. I said: "Abu Abdullah, is this something you do by your own opinion?" He said: "Rather, I saw the Prophet ﷺ do it, so I do it, just as I saw the Messenger of Allah [ﷺ] do it."

Ibn Majah likewise narrated it from Isma'il ibn Tawbah, from Ziyad al-Bakka'i, with the same content. Ahmad said: [chain] Ubaydullah ibn Abdullah ibn Umar said: I said to him: "What do you think of Abdullah ibn Umar's practice of performing wudu for every prayer, whether he was in a state of purity or not, from whom does he take this?" He said: Asma bint Zayd ibn al-Khattab narrated to him that Abdullah ibn Hanzalah ibn Abi Amir al-Ghasil narrated to her that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ had been commanded to perform wudu for every prayer, whether in a state of purity or not. Then when that became difficult for the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, he was commanded to use the siwak (tooth-stick) before every prayer, and the requirement of wudu was lifted from him except in case of hadath. Abdullah (ibn Umar) held the view that he had the capacity to continue that practice, and he continued doing it until he died.

Abu Dawud likewise narrated it, [chain] from Abdullah ibn Abdullah ibn Umar. Then Abu Dawud said: Ibrahim ibn Sa'd also narrated it from Muhammad ibn Ishaq, saying "Ubaydullah ibn Abdullah ibn Umar," meaning as preceded in the narration of Imam Ahmad.

Whichever way it was, it is a sound chain of transmission. Ibn Ishaq explicitly stated that he heard and narrated directly from Muhammad ibn Yahya ibn Habban, so the concern about tadlis (concealed transmission) is removed. However, al-Hafiz Ibn Asakir said: Salamah ibn al-Fadl and Ali ibn Mujahid narrated it from Ibn Ishaq, from Muhammad ibn Talhah ibn Yazid ibn Rukanah, from Muhammad ibn Yahya ibn Habban, with the same content. And Allah knows best. In this action of Ibn Umar, and his persistence in performing complete wudu for every prayer, there is an indication of the desirability of that, as is the view of the majority.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Ibn Sirin said: The caliphs used to perform wudu for every prayer.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] I heard Ikrimah say: Ali, may Allah be pleased with him, used to perform wudu for every prayer, and he would recite this verse: {O you who believe, when you rise up to prayer} the verse.

He also narrated to us: [chain] al-Nazzal ibn Sabrah said: I saw Ali pray the noon prayer, then sit for the people in the courtyard. Then water was brought to him, and he washed his face and his hands, then wiped his head and his feet, and said: "This is the wudu of one who has not had a hadath."

He also narrated to me: [chain] from Ibrahim, that Ali dipped his hand into a jar of water and performed a light, abbreviated wudu, and said: "This is the wudu of one who has not had a hadath." These are good chains of transmission from Ali [may Allah be pleased with him], each strengthening the other.

Ibn Jarir also said: [chain] Anas said: Umar ibn al-Khattab performed a light, abbreviated wudu, and said: "This is the wudu of one who has not had a hadath." This is a sound chain of transmission. Muhammad ibn Sirin said: The caliphs used to perform wudu for every prayer.

As for what Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi narrated, [chain] from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, that he said: "Performing wudu without a hadath is transgression," this is gharib (a singular, isolated report) from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib. It is furthermore to be understood as referring to one who believes it to be obligatory, for such a person would indeed be transgressing. As for its being legislated as a matter of recommendation, the Sunnah has indicated that.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] I heard Anas ibn Malik say: The Prophet ﷺ used to perform wudu for every prayer. He (the narrator) said: I said, "And how did you (the Companions) used to do?" He said: "We used to pray the prayers with a single wudu as long as we did not have a hadath."

Al-Bukhari and the compilers of the Sunan narrated it through more than one route from Amr ibn Amir, with the same content.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Ibn Umar said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever performs wudu while already in a state of purity, ten good deeds are recorded for him."

He also narrated it through the hadith of Isa ibn Yunus, from al-Ifriqi, from Abu Ghutayf, from Ibn Umar, mentioning it, with an accompanying story.

Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, and Ibn Majah likewise narrated it through the hadith of al-Ifriqi, with similar content. Al-Tirmidhi said: this is a weak chain of transmission.

Ibn Jarir said: Some people have said that this verse was revealed to inform that wudu is not obligatory except when rising to prayer, and not for other actions. This is because he (peace be upon him), whenever he had a hadath, would refrain from all actions until he performed wudu.

[chain] Abdullah ibn Alqamah ibn al-Faghwa, from his father, said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ, when he relieved himself of urine, if we spoke to him he would not speak to us, and if we greeted him with salam he would not return the greeting, until the verse of concession was revealed: {O you who believe, when you rise up to prayer} the verse.

Ibn Abi Hatim narrated it also, [chain] with similar content. This is an extremely gharib (singular) hadith, and this Jabir is Jabir ibn Yazid al-Ju'fi, whom scholars have declared weak.

Abu Dawud said: [chain] Abdullah ibn Abbas said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ came out from the place of relieving himself, and food was brought to him. They said: "Shall we not bring you water for wudu?" He said: "I have only been commanded to perform wudu when I rise to prayer."

Al-Tirmidhi likewise narrated it [chain] with the same content, and al-Nasa'i [chain] with the same content. Al-Tirmidhi said: this is a hasan (good) hadith.

Muslim narrated, [chain] from Ibn Abbas, who said: We were with the Prophet ﷺ, and he went to relieve himself. Then he returned, and food was brought to him. It was said: "O Messenger of Allah, will you not perform wudu?" He said: "Why? Am I about to pray, that I should perform wudu?"

As for His saying, {then wash your faces}, a group of scholars have used His statement, {when you rise up to prayer, then wash your faces}, as evidence...

On the obligation of intention in ablution, because the sense of the words is: "when you rise for prayer, wash your faces for it," just as the Arabs say: "when you see the commander, stand," meaning: for him. It is established in the two Sahihs, the hadith: "Actions are by intentions, and every man shall have only what he intended."

It is recommended, before washing the face, to mention the name of Allah, exalted is He, over one's ablution, because of what has come in the hadith through good chains, from a group of the Companions, from the Prophet ﷺ, that he said: "There is no ablution for one who does not mention the name of Allah over it."

It is recommended to wash one's hands before putting them into the vessel, and this is emphasized upon rising from sleep, because of what is established in the two Sahihs from Abu Hurayrah, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "When one of you wakes from his sleep, let him not put his hand into the vessel before washing it three times, for none of you knows where his hand spent the night."

The boundary of the face according to the jurists is: what lies between the roots of the hair of the head, with no regard given to baldness or a low hairline, down to the end of the two jaws and the chin in length, and from ear to ear in width. Regarding the two temples (al-naz'atan) and the hairline edges (al-tahdhif), there is disagreement whether they belong to the head or the face. And regarding what hangs loose of the beard beyond the place obligated, there are two opinions. One of them is that it is obligatory to pour water over it, because it is what is faced in confrontation. It has been reported in a hadith that the Prophet ﷺ saw a man covering his beard and said: "Uncover it, for the beard is part of the face." Mujahid said: it is part of the face, do you not hear the saying of the Arabs about a boy when his beard grows: "his face has appeared."

It is recommended for the one performing ablution to comb through his beard with his fingers if it is thick. Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Abu Wa'il said: I saw Uthman perform ablution, and he mentioned the hadith, saying: he combed through his beard three times when he washed his face, then said: I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ do what you have seen me do.

Reported by al-Tirmidhi and Ibn Majah from the hadith of Abd al-Razzaq, and al-Tirmidhi said: hasan sahih (sound and authentic), and al-Bukhari deemed it hasan (sound).

Abu Dawud said: [chain] from Anas ibn Malik, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, when he performed ablution, would take a handful of water and put it under his chin, combing through his beard with it, and said: "Thus my Lord, mighty and majestic is He, commanded me."

Abu Dawud alone transmitted this, and it has been reported through other paths from Anas as well. Al-Bayhaqi said: we have reported concerning the combing of the beard from Ammar, Aisha, and Umm Salamah, from the Prophet ﷺ, then from Ali and others. And we have reported concerning the permission to omit it from Ibn Umar and al-Hasan ibn Ali, then from al-Nakha'i and a group of the Successors.

It is established from the Prophet ﷺ through more than one path in the authentic collections and elsewhere that when he performed ablution he would rinse his mouth and sniff water into his nose. The scholars differed on this: are the two of them obligatory in ablution and the ritual bath, as is the position of Ahmad ibn Hanbal, may Allah have mercy on him? Or are they recommended in both, as is the position of al-Shafi'i and Malik, because of what is established in the hadith transmitted by the compilers of the Sunan and deemed authentic by Ibn Khuzaymah, from Rifa'ah ibn Rafi' al-Zuraqi, that the Prophet ﷺ said to the man who had performed his prayer badly: "Perform ablution as Allah has commanded you." Or are they obligatory in the ritual bath but not in ablution, as is the position of Abu Hanifah? Or is sniffing water into the nose obligatory without the rinsing of the mouth, as is one report from Imam Ahmad, because of what is established in the two Sahihs, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever performs ablution, let him blow his nose," and in another version: "When one of you performs ablution, let him put water into his nostrils, then let him blow it out." Blowing out (al-intithar) is thoroughness in sniffing water into the nose.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] from Ibn Abbas, that he performed ablution: he washed his face, then took a scoop of water and rinsed his mouth with it and blew his nose, then took a scoop and did thus, meaning he joined it to his other hand, and washed his face with the two of them. Then he took a scoop of water and washed his right hand with it, then took a scoop of water and washed his left hand with it, then wiped his head, then took a scoop of water and sprinkled it on his right foot until he had washed it, then took another scoop and washed his left foot with it, then said: thus I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, meaning, perform ablution.

Al-Bukhari transmitted it from Muhammad ibn Abd al-Rahim, from Abu Salamah Mansur ibn Salamah al-Khuza'i, with this chain.

Regarding His saying, {and your hands to the elbows}: meaning, along with the elbows, just as He, exalted is He, said: {and do not consume their wealth into your wealth; indeed that is a great sin} [al-Nisa: 2].

Al-Hafiz al-Daraqutni and Abu Bakr al-Bayhaqi reported, through the path of al-Qasim ibn Muhammad, from Abdullah ibn Muhammad ibn Aqil, from his grandfather, from Jabir ibn Abdullah, who said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, when he performed ablution, would move the water around his two elbows. But this al-Qasim is a discarded narrator in hadith, and his grandfather is weak. And Allah knows best.

It is recommended for the one performing ablution to begin including part of the upper arm so as to wash it along with his forearms, because of what al-Bukhari and Muslim reported, from the hadith of Nu'aym al-Mujmir, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "My nation will be called on the Day of Resurrection with bright marks (ghurr) and radiant limbs (muhajjalin) from the traces of ablution. So whoever among you is able to lengthen his brightness, let him do so."

And in Sahih Muslim: from Qutaybah, from Khalaf ibn Khalifah, from Abu Malik al-Ashja'i, from Abu Hazim, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: I heard my beloved ﷺ say: "The adornment of the believer reaches to where the ablution reaches."

Regarding His saying, {and wipe your heads}: they differed concerning this "ba" (the preposition "with/by"), whether it indicates attachment (which is the more apparent meaning) or partition. There is room for consideration here, on two opinions. Some of the specialists in legal theory said: this is unspecified (mujmal), so one must turn to the Sunnah for its clarification. It is established in the two Sahihs, through the path of Malik, from Amr ibn Yahya al-Mazini, from his father, that a man said to Abdullah ibn Zayd ibn Asim, who is the grandfather of Amr ibn Yahya and was one of the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ: can you show me how the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to perform ablution? Abdullah ibn Zayd said: yes. So he called for water for ablution and poured it over his hands, and washed his hands twice each, then rinsed his mouth and sniffed water into his nose three times, and washed his face three times, then washed his hands twice each up to the elbows, then wiped his head with his two hands, moving them forward and backward, beginning from the front of his head, then taking them back to the nape of his neck, then bringing them back until he returned to the place from which he had begun, then he washed his feet.

In the hadith of Abd Khayr, from Ali, describing the ablution of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, there is something similar to this. Abu Dawud reported from Mu'awiyah and al-Miqdam ibn Ma'd Yakrib, describing the ablution of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, something similar as well.

In these hadiths there is evidence for those who hold that completing the wiping of the entire head is obligatory, as is the position of Imam Malik and Ahmad ibn Hanbal, especially according to the view of those who claim that these reports came in the manner of clarification for what was left unspecified in the Qur'an.

The Hanafis held that wiping a quarter of the head is obligatory, which is the measure of the forelock (al-nasiyah).

Our companions (the Shafi'is) held that what is obligatory is merely whatever the term "wiping" applies to, without a fixed limit; indeed, even if one wiped only some of the hair of his head, it would suffice him.

Both groups cited as evidence the hadith of al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah, who said: the Prophet ﷺ fell behind, so I fell behind with him, and when he had finished his need he said: "Do you have water with you?" So I brought him a vessel of water, and he washed his hands and his face, then went to uncover his forearms, but the sleeve of the cloak was too tight, so he brought his hands out from beneath the cloak and threw the cloak over his shoulders, and washed his forearms and wiped his forelock, and over the turban, and over his two leather socks... and he mentioned the rest of the hadith, which is found in Sahih Muslim and elsewhere.

The companions of Imam Ahmad said to them: He limited himself to wiping the forelock only because he completed the wiping of the rest of the head over the turban. We say the same, that it fulfills the requirement, as many hadiths have come reporting this, and that he used to wipe over the turban and over the leather socks. This is more fitting, and there is nothing in it for you as evidence for the permissibility of limiting oneself to wiping the forelock or part of the head without completing it over the turban. And God knows best.

Then they differed over whether it is recommended to repeat the wiping of the head three times, as is well known from the Shafi'i school, or whether only a single wipe is recommended, as is the school of Ahmad ibn Hanbal and those who followed him, there being two views. Abd al-Razzaq said: from Ma'mar, from al-Zuhri, from Ata ibn Yazid al-Laythi, from Humran ibn Aban, who said: I saw Uthman ibn Affan perform ablution. He poured water over his hands three times and washed them, then rinsed his mouth and nose, then washed his face three times, then washed his right hand up to the elbow three times, then washed the left in the same way, then wiped his head, then washed his right foot three times, then the left three times likewise. Then he said: I saw the Messenger of God ﷺ perform ablution similar to this ablution of mine, then he said, "Whoever performs ablution like this ablution of mine, then prays two rak'ahs without letting his mind wander during them, will have his previous sins forgiven."

Al-Bukhari and Muslim reported it in the two Sahihs from the route of al-Zuhri with something similar to this. And in the Sunan of Abu Dawud, from the narration of Abdullah ibn Ubaydullah ibn Abi Mulaykah, from Uthman, describing the ablution: "and he wiped his head once only." Likewise from the narration of Abd Khayr, from Ali, similarly.

Those who recommended repeating the wiping of the head argued from the general wording of the hadith reported by Muslim in his Sahih, from Uthman, may God be pleased with him, that the Messenger of God ﷺ performed ablution washing each part three times.

Abu Dawud said: Muhammad ibn al-Muthanna narrated to us, al-Dahhak ibn Makhlad narrated to us, Abd al-Rahman ibn Wardan narrated to us, Abu Salamah ibn Abd al-Rahman narrated to me, Humran narrated to me, saying: I saw Uthman ibn Affan perform ablution, and he mentioned something similar, but did not mention rinsing the mouth and nose. He said in it: "then he wiped his head three times, then washed his feet three times," then he said: "I saw the Messenger of God ﷺ perform ablution like this," and he said, "Whoever performs ablution less than this, it suffices him."

Abu Dawud narrated this alone (tafarrada bihi), then said: The sound hadiths of Uthman indicate that he wiped the head only once.

Concerning His saying, {and your feet up to the ankles}: it is read as {wa arjulakum} in the accusative case, in conjunction with {so wash your faces and your hands}.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: Abu Zur'ah narrated to us, Abu Salamah narrated to us, Wuhayb narrated to us, from Khalid, from Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas, that he read it as {wa arjulakum}, saying: it reverts to washing.

It has been reported similarly from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, Urwah, Ata, Ikrimah, al-Hasan, Mujahid, Ibrahim, al-Dahhak, al-Suddi, Muqatil ibn Hayyan, al-Zuhri, and Ibrahim al-Taymi.

This reading is apparent in indicating the obligation of washing, as the Salaf stated. From this, those who held the obligation of sequential order (tartib) derived their view, as is the school of the majority, contrary to Abu Hanifah, who did not require sequential order. Rather, according to him, if one washed his feet, then wiped his head, then washed his hands, then his face, this would suffice, because the verse commanded the washing of these body parts, and "the waw" (conjunction "and") does not indicate sequence. The majority pursued various approaches in responding to this issue. Some of them said: the verse indicates the obligation of washing the face first upon rising to pray, because it is commanded with the fa of sequence (fa al-ta'qib), which necessitates order, and no one among the people held that washing the face is obligatory first but that sequence is not obligatory after that. Rather, there are two positions: one holds that sequence is obligatory, as occurs in the verse; the other holds that sequence is not obligatory at all, while the verse indicates the obligation of washing the face first, so that sequence became obligatory for what follows it by consensus (ijma), there being no distinguishing factor.

Others said: We do not concede that "the waw" does not indicate sequence; rather it does indicate it, as is the position of a group of grammarians, linguists, and some jurists. Then we say, even granting that it does not indicate sequence linguistically, it indicates sequence legally (shar'an) in matters whose nature is to be ordered. The evidence for this is that when he ﷺ circled the House (the Ka'bah), he went out from the gate of al-Safa reciting His saying, Most High: {Indeed al-Safa and al-Marwah are among the symbols of God} [al-Baqarah: 158], then he said, "I begin with what God began with," this being Muslim's wording, while al-Nasa'i's wording is, "Begin with what God began with." This is a command form, and its chain is sound (sahih), indicating the obligation of beginning with what God began with, which is the meaning of its indicating sequence legally. And God knows best.

Others said: since God, Most High, mentioned this description in this verse in this order, separating the like from its like and inserting the wiped item between the two washed items, this indicates the intent of sequence.

Others said: there is no doubt that Abu Dawud and others reported, from the route of Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather, that the Messenger of God ﷺ performed ablution washing each part once, then said, "This is an ablution that God does not accept prayer without." They said: it cannot be but that either he performed ablution in sequence, in which case sequence is obligatory, or he performed it without sequence, in which case the absence of sequence would be obligatory, and no one holds this. So what was mentioned [regarding the obligation of sequence] must be the case.

As for the other reading, that of those who read {wa arjulikum} in the genitive case, the Shi'ah used it as evidence for their view of the obligation of wiping the feet, because according to them it is in conjunction with the wiping of the head. It has been reported from a group of the Salaf something that gives the impression of holding the view of wiping. Ibn Jarir said:

Ya'qub ibn Ibrahim narrated to me, Ibn Ulayyah narrated to us, Humayd narrated to us, saying: Musa ibn Anas said to Anas, while we were with him: O Abu Hamzah, al-Hajjaj gave us a sermon at al-Ahwaz while we were with him, and he mentioned purification, and said: "Wash your faces and your hands, and wipe your heads and your feet, and there is nothing on the son of Adam closer to filth than his two feet, so wash their soles and their backs and their heels." Anas said: God spoke the truth and al-Hajjaj lied. God, Most High, said {and wipe your heads and your feet}. He said: and Anas, when he wiped his feet, would wet them. A sound chain (isnad sahih) to him.

Ibn Jarir said: Ali ibn Sahl narrated to us, Mu'ammal narrated to us, Hammad narrated to us, Asim al-Ahwal narrated to us, from Anas, who said: The Qur'an came down with wiping, but the sunnah is washing. This is also a sound chain.

Ibn Jarir said: Abu Kurayb narrated to us, Muhammad ibn Qays al-Khurasani narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from Amr ibn Dinar, from Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas, who said: Ablution consists of two washings and two wipings.

Sa'id ibn Abi Arubah likewise reported this from Qatadah.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: my father narrated to me, Abu Ma'mar al-Minqari narrated to us, Abd al-Wahhab narrated to us, Ali ibn Zayd narrated to us, from Yusuf ibn Mihran, from Ibn Abbas, concerning {and wipe your heads and your feet up to the ankles}: he said, it is wiping. Then he said: it has been reported similarly from Ibn Umar, Alqamah, Abu Ja'far, Muhammad ibn Ali, al-Hasan (in one of the narrations), Jabir ibn Zayd, and Mujahid (in one of the narrations).

Ibn Jarir said: Ya'qub narrated to us, Ibn Ulayyah narrated to us, Ayyub narrated to us, who said: I saw Ikrimah wiping his feet, and he said: he used to hold this view.

Ibn Jarir said: Abu al-Sa'ib narrated to me, Ibn Idris narrated to us, from Dawud ibn Abi Hind, from al-Sha'bi, who said: Gabriel came down with wiping. Then al-Sha'bi said: Do you not see that tayammum consists in wiping what would otherwise be washed, and dispensing with what would otherwise be wiped?

Ibn Abi Ziyad narrated to us, Yazid narrated to us, Isma'il informed us: I said to Amir: some people say that Gabriel came down with washing the feet? He said: Gabriel came down with wiping.

These are very strange (gharib) reports, and they are to be understood as meaning that what is intended by "wiping" is light washing, on account of what we shall mention of the established sunnah regarding the obligation of washing the feet.

This reading with the genitive case came about either through proximity and grammatical assimilation of neighboring words, as in the saying of the Arabs, "juhru dabbin kharibin" (the burrow of a corrupt lizard), and as in His statement, {Upon them will be garments of green fine silk and brocade} [al-Insan: 21]. This is a common and widespread usage, prevalent in the language of the Arabs. Some said: it is to be understood as referring to the wiping of the two feet when khuffs (leather socks) are upon them, this was said by Abu Abdillah al-Shafi'i, may Allah have mercy on him. Others said: it indicates the wiping of the two feet, but what is meant by that is light washing, as has come in the Sunnah. In any case, the obligation is the washing of the two feet as a duty, which is unavoidable due to the verse and the hadiths which we will bring forth.

Among the best evidences for the fact that "wiping" (mash) is used to mean light washing is what the hafiz al-Bayhaqi narrated, where he said: [chain] Ali ibn Abi Talib, that he prayed the Dhuhr, then sat attending to the needs of the people in the courtyard of Kufa until the time of the Asr prayer arrived, then a jug of water was brought to him, and he took from it a single handful, and wiped with it his face, his hands, his head, and his feet, then he stood and drank what remained of it while standing, then he said: some people dislike drinking while standing, but the Messenger of Allah ﷺ did what I have done. And he said: "This is the ablution of one who has not invalidated his state of purity."

Al-Bukhari narrated it in the Sahih, from Adam, with part of its meaning.

Whoever among the Shia holds it obligatory to wipe the two feet as one wipes over the khuff has gone astray and led others astray. Likewise, whoever permits wiping them while also permitting washing them has erred as well. And whoever transmits from Abu Ja'far ibn Jarir that he held washing them obligatory on account of the hadiths, and held wiping them obligatory on account of the verse, has not verified his actual position on the matter. For his statement in his tafsir indicates only that he intended that rubbing (dalk) of the two feet is obligatory, apart from the rest of the limbs of ablution, because they come into contact with the ground, mud, and other such things, so he made rubbing them obligatory so that whatever is upon them would be removed. But he expressed this rubbing with the term "wiping" (mash), so whoever did not reflect carefully on his statement believed that he intended the obligation of combining both the washing of the feet and the wiping of them, and so it was transmitted by whoever transmitted it in that manner. This is why many jurists find it problematic, though he is excused, for there is no sense in combining wiping and washing, whether one precedes the other or follows it, since it would be subsumed within it. Rather, the man intended what I have mentioned, and Allah knows best. Then I reflected on his statement again, and found that he was attempting to reconcile the two readings in His statement, {wa arjulakum}, with the genitive case referring to wiping, which is rubbing, and the accusative case referring to washing, so he held both obligatory, taking a combination of this reading and that one.

Mention of the hadiths reported concerning the washing of the two feet, and that it is indispensable:

It has already preceded in the hadith of the two commanders of the faithful, Uthman and Ali, and of Ibn Abbas, Mu'awiyah, Abdullah ibn Zayd ibn Asim, and al-Miqdad ibn Ma'dikarib, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ washed the two feet in his ablution, either once, twice, or three times, according to the variance in their reports.

And in the hadith of Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed ablution and washed his feet, then said: "This is an ablution without which Allah does not accept the prayer."

And in the Sahihayn, from the narration of Abu Awanah, from Abu Bishr, from Yusuf ibn Mahak, from Abdullah ibn Amr, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ fell behind us on a journey we were making, and he caught up with us when the time of prayer, the Asr prayer, was pressing upon us, and we were performing ablution, and we began to wipe over our feet, so he called out at the top of his voice: "Complete the ablution thoroughly, woe to the heels from the Fire."

And likewise it is in the Sahihayn from Abu Hurayrah, and in Sahih Muslim from Aishah, from the Prophet ﷺ, that he said: "Complete the ablution thoroughly, woe to the heels from the Fire."

And al-Layth ibn Sa'd narrated, from Haywah ibn Shurayh, from Uqbah ibn Muslim, from Abdullah ibn al-Harith ibn Juz', that he heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "Woe to the heels and the soles of the feet from the Fire." Al-Bayhaqi and al-Hakim narrated it, and this chain is sound.

And Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Sa'id ibn Abi Karb, or Shu'ayb ibn Abi Karb, said: I heard Jabir ibn Abdullah, while he was upon a camel, saying: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "Woe to the backs of the heels from the Fire."

And he narrated to us [chain] Sa'id ibn Abi Karb, from Jabir ibn Abdullah, who said: The Prophet ﷺ saw on the foot of a man among us something the size of a dirham that he had not washed, so he said: "Woe to the heel from the Fire."

Ibn Majah narrated it, from Abu Bakr ibn Abi Shaybah, from al-Ahwas, from Abu Ishaq, from Sa'id, similarly. Likewise, Ibn Jarir narrated it from the hadith of Sufyan al-Thawri and Shu'bah ibn al-Hajjaj and more than one other, from Abu Ishaq al-Sabi'i, from Sa'id ibn Abi Karb, from Jabir, from the Prophet ﷺ, similarly. Then he said:

[chain] Jabir, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saw a group performing ablution, and the water had not reached their heels, so he said: "Woe to the backs of the heels from the Fire."

And Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Mu'ayqib, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Woe to the heels from the Fire." Ahmad alone narrated it.

And Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Abu Umamah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Woe to the heels from the Fire, woe to the heels from the Fire." He said: There remained no one in the mosque, of high or low standing, but that I saw him turning over his heels, looking at them.

And he narrated to us: [chain] Abdur-Rahman ibn Sabit, from Abu Umamah, or from the brother of Abu Umamah, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saw a group performing ablution, and on the heel of one of them, or his ankle bone, was something the size of a dirham, or the size of a fingernail, that the water had not touched, so he said: "Woe to the heels from the Fire." He said: So the man began, whenever he saw something on his heel that the water had not reached, to repeat his ablution.

The aspect of evidence from these hadiths is clear, in that if the obligation regarding the two feet were merely wiping them, or if that were permissible for them, he would not have threatened punishment for neglecting it, because wiping does not cover the entire foot, but rather follows the same pattern as wiping over the khuff. This is likewise the aspect of evidence used against the Shia by the Imam Abu Ja'far ibn Jarir, may Allah have mercy on him.

Muslim narrated in his Sahih, through the route of Abu al-Zubayr, from Jabir, from Umar ibn al-Khattab, that a man performed ablution and left the space the size of a fingernail on his foot unwashed, and the Prophet ﷺ saw him and said: "Go back and perfect your ablution."

And the hafiz Abu Bakr al-Bayhaqi said: [chain] Anas ibn Malik, that a man came to the Prophet ﷺ having performed ablution, and had left on his foot something the size of a fingernail's spot, so the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to him: "Go back and perfect your ablution."

And likewise Abu Dawud narrated it from Harun ibn Ma'ruf, and Ibn Majah, from Harmalah ibn Yahya, both of them from Ibn Wahb with it, and this is a good chain, all of its men being trustworthy, but Abu Dawud said: this hadith is not well known, no one narrated it except Ibn Wahb.

And he narrated to us: [chain] al-Hasan, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ... with the meaning of the hadith of Qatadah.

Imam Ahmad said: Ibrahim ibn Abi al-Abbas told us, Baqiyyah told us, Bahir ibn Sa'd told me, from Khalid ibn Ma'dan, from one of the wives of the Prophet ﷺ, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saw a man praying while there was a spot the size of a dirham on the top of his foot that the water had not touched, so the Messenger of Allah ﷺ commanded him to repeat the ablution.

Abu Dawud narrated it from the hadith of Baqiyyah and added: "and the prayer." This is a good, strong, sound chain, and Allah knows best.

In the hadith of Humran, from Uthman, describing the ablution of the Prophet ﷺ: that he ran his fingers between his fingers. The compilers of the Sunan narrated from the hadith of Isma'il ibn Kathir, from Asim ibn Laqit ibn Sabrah, from his father, who said: I said, "O Messenger of Allah, tell me about ablution." He said: "Perform the ablution thoroughly, run your fingers between your fingers, and be thorough in sniffing water into the nose, except when you are fasting."

Imam Ahmad said: Abdullah ibn Yazid, Abu Abd al-Rahman al-Muqri, told us, Ikrimah ibn Ammar told us, Shaddad ibn Abdullah al-Dimashqi said, Abu Umamah said: Amr ibn Absah told us, he said: I said, "O Prophet of Allah, tell me about ablution." He said: "There is not one of you who brings his ablution water near, then rinses his mouth, sniffs water into his nose and blows it out, but that his sins fall from his mouth and nostrils along with the water when he blows it out. Then he washes his face as Allah commanded him, and his sins fall from his face, from the ends of his beard, along with the water. Then he washes his hands up to the elbows, and his sins fall from his hands, from the tips of his fingers. Then he wipes his head, and his sins fall from his head, from the ends of his hair, along with the water. Then he washes his feet up to the ankles as Allah commanded him, and his sins fall from his feet, from the tips of his toes, along with the water. Then he stands and praises Allah and extols Him as He deserves, then he prays two rak'ahs, and he emerges from his sins as on the day his mother bore him." Abu Umamah said: "O Amr, watch what you say. Did you hear this from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ? Is this man given all of that in his one standing?" Amr ibn Absah said: "O Abu Umamah, my age has grown great, my bones have grown frail, and my appointed time has drawn near, and I have no need to lie against Allah and against the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Even if I had heard it from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ only once or twice or three times, I actually heard it from him seven times or more than that."

This is a sound chain, and it is in Sahih Muslim from another route, in which it says: "Then he washes his feet as Allah commanded him." This indicates that the Qur'an commands washing.

Likewise Abu Ishaq al-Sabi'i narrated, from al-Harith, from Ali ibn Abi Talib, may Allah be pleased with him, that he said: "Wash the feet up to the ankles as you have been commanded."

From this it becomes clear to you what is meant by the hadith of Abd Khayr, from Ali, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sprinkled water on his feet while they were in his sandals and rubbed them. He only intended a light washing while they were in the sandals, and there is nothing preventing washing from being achieved while the foot is in its sandal. But in this there is a refutation of those who go to excessive lengths and those afflicted with obsessive doubt who exaggerate in these matters.

Likewise the hadith which Ibn Jarir brought forward against himself, which is from his own narration, from al-A'mash, from Abu Wa'il, from Hudhayfah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to a rubbish-heap belonging to some people and urinated standing, then called for water and performed ablution, and wiped over his sandals. This is a sound hadith. Ibn Jarir answered concerning it that the trustworthy memorizers narrated it from al-A'mash, from Abu Wa'il, from Hudhayfah, who said: he urinated standing, then performed ablution and wiped over his leather socks (khuffayn).

I say: it is possible to reconcile the two reports by supposing that he had leather socks on his feet, and over them were sandals.

Likewise the hadith narrated by Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal: Yahya told us, from Shu'bah, Ya'la told me, from his father, from Aws ibn Abi Aws, who said: I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ perform ablution and wipe over his sandals, then he stood up for prayer. Abu Dawud narrated it from Musaddad and Abbad ibn Musa, both from Hushaym, from Ya'la ibn Ata', from his father, from Aws ibn Abi Aws, who said: I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ come to a rubbish-heap belonging to some people and urinate, then perform ablution and wipe over his sandals and his feet.

Ibn Jarir narrated it via the route of Shu'bah and via the route of Hushaym, then said: This is to be understood as meaning that he performed ablution in that manner while he was not in a state requiring purification, since it is not permissible for the obligations of Allah and the practices of His Messenger to be mutually contradictory and conflicting. It has been established as sound from him ﷺ that he commanded the general washing of the feet in ablution with water, through widely disseminated transmission that cuts off the excuse of anyone who receives it and to whom it reaches.

Since the Qur'an commands the washing of the feet, as is indicated by the reading with a fatha (nasb), and as is obligatory when interpreting the reading with a kasra (khafd) in light of it, some of the early generations mistakenly supposed that this verse abrogates the concession of wiping over the leather socks. This has been reported from Ali ibn Abi Talib, but its chain is not sound; rather, what is established from him is the opposite of that, and it is not as they claimed, for it has been established that the Prophet ﷺ wiped over the leather socks after the revelation of this noble verse.

Imam Ahmad said: Hashim ibn al-Qasim told us, Ziyad ibn Abdullah ibn Ulathah told us, from Abd al-Karim ibn Malik al-Jazari, from Mujahid, from Jarir ibn Abdullah al-Bajali, who said: I embraced Islam after the revelation of Surat al-Ma'idah, and I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ wiping over his socks after I had embraced Islam. Ahmad alone narrated this.

In the two Sahihs, from the hadith of al-A'mash, from Ibrahim, from Hammam, who said: Jarir urinated, then performed ablution and wiped over his leather socks. It was said to him: "Do you do this?" He said: "Yes, I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ urinate, then perform ablution and wipe over his leather socks." Al-A'mash said: Ibrahim said: "This hadith used to please them greatly, because Jarir's embracing of Islam was after the revelation of Surat al-Ma'idah." This is the wording of Muslim.

It has been established through mass-transmitted reports (tawatur) from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ that the legitimacy of wiping over the leather socks is established both by his word and his deed, as is established in the book "al-Ahkam al-Kabir," and what needs to be mentioned there, regarding the time-limit of wiping or the absence thereof, or the detailed rulings concerning it, is elaborated in its proper place. The Rafidah have opposed all of this without any basis, but rather out of ignorance and misguidance, even though it is established in Sahih Muslim from the narration of the Commander of the Faithful, Ali ibn Abi Talib, may Allah be pleased with him. Just as it is established in the two Sahihs from him, from the Prophet ﷺ, the prohibition of mut'ah marriage, while they consider it permissible. Likewise this noble verse indicates the obligation of washing the feet, along with what has been established through mass-transmitted reports of the deed of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in accordance with what the noble verse indicates, and they oppose all of that, and they have no sound proof in the reality of the matter. Praise be to Allah.

Likewise they have opposed the leading scholars and the early generations regarding the two ankles that are in the feet. According to them, the ankles are on the top of the foot, so according to them there is one ankle in each foot, while according to the majority the two ankles are the two protruding bones at the joint of the shin and the foot. Al-Rabi' said: al-Shafi'i said: "I do not know of anyone who disputes that the two ankles which Allah mentioned in His Book regarding ablution are the two protruding bones, and they are the meeting point of the joint of the shin and the foot." This is his wording. So according to the leading scholars, may Allah have mercy on them, there are two ankles in each foot, as is well known among people, and as the Sunnah indicates. In the two Sahihs, from the route of Humran, from Uthman, that he performed ablution and washed his right foot up to the two ankles, and likewise the left.

Al-Bukhari narrated it as a decisive mu'allaq (suspended report), and Abu Dawud and Ibn Khuzaymah in his Sahih, from the narration of Abu al-Qasim al-Husayn ibn al-Harith al-Jadali, from al-Nu'man ibn Bashir, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ turned his face toward us and said: "Straighten your rows," three times, "By Allah, you must straighten your rows or Allah will surely cause discord among your hearts." He said: So I saw a man pressing his ankle against the ankle of his companion, and his knee against the knee of his companion, and his shoulder against his shoulder. This is the wording of Ibn Khuzaymah.

It is not possible for a man to press his ankle against the ankle of his companion unless what is meant by it is the protruding bone in the shin, until it aligns with the ankle of the other. This indicates what we have mentioned, that the two ankles are the two protruding bones at the joint of the shin and the foot, as is the position of Ahl al-Sunnah.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: my father told us, Isma'il ibn Musa told us, Sharik informed us, from Yahya ibn Abdullah ibn al-Harith al-Taymi (that is, al-Jabir), who said: I examined the slain among the companions of Zayd, and I found the ankle bone above the top of the foot. This was a punishment inflicted upon the Shi'a after their killing, as a deterrent example for their opposition to the truth and their persistence in it.

Regarding His saying: {And if you are ill or on a journey, or one of you has come from the toilet, or you have touched women, and you find no water, then seek clean earth and wipe your faces and hands with it}, all of this has already been discussed in the commentary on the verse of Surat al-Nisa, so there is no need to repeat it here, lest the discussion become too long. We have already mentioned there the occasion of revelation of the verse of tayammum (dry ablution). However, al-Bukhari related here a hadith specific to this noble verse, saying:

Yahya ibn Sulayman told us, Ibn Wahb told us, Amr ibn al-Harith informed me, that Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Qasim told him, from his father, from Aisha: A necklace of mine fell at al-Bayda while we were entering Medina. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ made his mount kneel and dismounted, and he laid his head in my lap, sleeping. Abu Bakr came forward and struck me sharply, saying, "You have detained the people for a necklace!" Death nearly came upon me because of the position of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and he had hurt me. Then the Prophet ﷺ woke up and the dawn had come, and water was sought but none was found. So there was revealed: {O you who believe, when you rise for prayer, wash your faces} this verse. Then Usayd ibn al-Hudayr said, "Allah has indeed blessed the people through you, O family of Abu Bakr! You are nothing but a blessing for them."

Regarding His saying: {Allah does not intend to place upon you any hardship}, that is: for this reason He made things easy and simple for you and did not make them difficult. Rather, He permitted tayammum in the case of illness and in the case of lack of water, as an expansion for you and a mercy toward you, and He made it, within the rights established by Allah's law, stand in place of water except in certain respects, as has already been explained, and as is established in detail in the book "al-Ahkam al-Kabir".

Regarding His saying: {But He intends to purify you and to complete His favor upon you, that you might be grateful}, that is: so that you may be grateful for His favors upon you in what He legislated for you of expansion, kindness, mercy, ease, and leniency. The Sunna has come urging supplication after ablution, that its performer be made among the purified who enter into compliance with this noble verse, as related by Imam Ahmad, Muslim, and the compilers of the Sunan, from Uqba ibn Amir, who said: We had the duty of tending the camels, and my turn came, so I brought them back in the evening, and I caught up with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ standing and speaking to the people. I caught from his words: "There is no Muslim who performs ablution and does it well, then stands and prays two units of prayer, turning to them fully with his heart and his face, except that Paradise becomes obligatory for him." He said: I said, "How excellent this is!" Then someone before me said, "What came before it is even more excellent than it." I looked and it was Umar, may Allah be pleased with him, who said, "Indeed I saw you come just now." He said: "There is none among you who performs ablution and completes it, or perfects it, thoroughly, saying: I bear witness that there is no god but Allah and that Muhammad is His servant and Messenger, except that the eight gates of Paradise are opened for him, and he may enter through whichever of them he wishes." This is the wording of Muslim.

Malik said: from Suhayl ibn Abi Salih, from his father, from Abu Hurayra, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "When the Muslim servant, or the believer, performs ablution and washes his face, every sin he looked upon with his eyes comes out from his face along with the water, or with the last drop of water. When he washes his hands, every sin his hands committed comes out from his hands along with the water, or with the last drop of water. When he washes his feet, every sin his feet walked toward comes out along with the water, or with the last drop of water, until he emerges cleansed of sins."

Muslim related this from Abu al-Tahir, from Ibn Wahb, from Malik, with this chain.

Ibn Jarir said: Abu Kurayb told us, Mu'awiya ibn Hisham told us, from Sufyan, from Mansur, from Salim ibn Abi al-Ja'd, from Ka'b ibn Murra, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "There is no man who performs ablution and washes his hands, or his forearms, except that his sins come out from them. When he washes his face, his sins come out from his face. When he wipes his head, his sins come out from his head. When he washes his feet, his sins come out from his feet."

This is his wording. Imam Ahmad related it from Muhammad ibn Ja'far, from Shu'ba, from Mansur, from Salim, from Murra ibn Ka'b, or Ka'b ibn Murra al-Sulami, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "When the servant performs ablution and washes his hands, his sins come out from between his hands. When he washes his face, his sins come out from his face. When he washes his forearms, his sins come out from his forearms. When he washes his feet, his sins come out from his feet." Shu'ba said: he did not mention the wiping of the head. This chain is sound (sahih).

Ibn Jarir also related, through the path of Shamir ibn Atiyya, from Shahr ibn Hawshab, from Abu Umama, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever performs ablution and does it well, then rises for prayer, his sins come out from his hearing, his sight, his hands, and his feet."

Muslim related in his Sahih, from the hadith of Yahya ibn Abi Kathir, from Zayd ibn Sallam, from his grandfather Mamtur, from Abu Malik al-Ashari, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Purification is half of faith. 'Praise be to Allah' fills the scale. 'Glory be to Allah' and 'Praise be to Allah' fill up what is between heaven and earth. Prayer is light, charity is proof, patience is illumination, and the Qur'an is an argument either for you or against you. Every person goes forth in the morning, and either sells himself and thereby frees it, or ruins it."

In the Sahih of Muslim, from the narration of Simak ibn Harb, from Musab ibn Sa'd, from Ibn Umar, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Allah does not accept charity from ill-gotten gain, nor prayer without purification."

Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi said: Shu'ba told us, from Qatada: I heard Abu al-Malih al-Hudhali relate from his father, who said: I was with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in a house, and I heard him say: "Allah does not accept a prayer without purification, nor charity from ill-gotten gain."

Ahmad, Abu Dawud, al-Nasa'i, and Ibn Majah related it likewise, from the hadith of Shu'ba.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

قَالَ كَثِيرُونَ مِنَ السَّلَفِ: قَوْلُهُ: ﴿إِذَا قُمْتُمْ إِلَى الصَّلاةِ﴾ مَعْنَاهُ وَأَنْتُمْ مُحْدِثون.

وَقَالَ آخَرُونَ: إِذَا قُمْتُمْ مِنَ النَّوْمِ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ، وَكِلَاهُمَا قَرِيبٌ.

وَقَالَ آخَرُونَ: بَلِ الْمَعْنَى أَعَمُّ مِنْ ذَلِكَ، فَالْآيَةُ آمِرَةٌ بِالْوُضُوءِ عِنْدَ الْقِيَامِ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ، وَلَكِنْ هُوَ فِي حَقِّ الْمُحْدِثِ عَلَى سَبِيلِ الْإِيجَابِ، وَفِي حَقِّ الْمُتَطَهِّرِ عَلَى سَبِيلِ النَّدْبِ وَالِاسْتِحْبَابِ. وَقَدْ قِيلَ: إِنَّ الْأَمْرَ بِالْوُضُوءِ لِكُلِّ صَلَاةٍ كَانَ وَاجِبًا فِي ابْتِدَاءِ الْإِسْلَامِ، ثُمَّ نُسِخَ.

قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ بْنُ حَنْبَلٍ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ، حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ عَلْقَمَة بْنِ مَرْثَدٍ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ بُرَيْدة عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ: كَانَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ يَتَوَضَّأُ عِنْدَ كُلِّ صَلَاةٍ، فَلَمَّا كَانَ يَوْمُ الْفَتْحِ تَوَضَّأَ وَمَسَحَ عَلَى خُفَّيْهِ، وَصَلَّى الصَّلَوَاتِ بِوُضُوءٍ وَاحِدٍ. فَقَالَ لَهُ عُمَرُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنَّكَ فَعَلْتَ شَيْئًا لَمْ تَكُنْ تَفْعَلُهُ؟ قَالَ: "إِنِّي عَمْدًا فَعَلْتُهُ يَا عُمَرُ.

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ وَأَهْلُ السُّنَنِ مِنْ حَدِيثِ سُفْيَانَ الثَّوْرِيِّ، عَنْ عَلْقَمَةَ بْنِ مَرْثَدٍ وَوَقَعَ فِي سُنَنِ ابْنِ مَاجَهْ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ عَنْ مُحَارِبِ بْنِ دِثَار -بَدَلَ عَلْقَمَةَ بْنِ مَرْثَدٍ-كِلَاهُمَا عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ بُريدة بِهِ وَقَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: حَسَنٌ صَحِيحٌ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبَّادِ بْنِ مُوسَى، أَخْبَرْنَا زِيَادُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الطُّفَيْلِ الْبَكَّائِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ المُبَشِّر قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ جَابِرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ يُصَلِّي الصَّلَوَاتِ بِوُضُوءٍ وَاحِدٍ، فَإِذَا بَالَ أَوْ أَحْدَثَ، تَوَضَّأَ وَمَسَحَ بِفَضْلِ طَهُوره الْخُفَّيْنِ. فَقُلْتُ: أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، شَيْءٌ تَصْنَعُهُ بِرَأْيِكَ؟ قَالَ: بَلْ رَأَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ يَصْنَعُهُ، فَأَنَا أَصْنَعُهُ، كَمَا رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ [ﷺ] يَصْنَعُ. 

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَاجَهْ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ تَوْبة، عَنْ زِيَادٍ الْبَكَّائِيِّ، بِهِ وَقَالَ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، عَنِ ابْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، حَدَّثَنِي مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى بْنِ حَبَّان الْأَنْصَارِيُّ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لَهُ: أَرَأَيْتَ وُضُوءَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ لِكُلِّ صَلَاةٍ طَاهِرًا كَانَ أَوْ غَيْرَ طَاهِرٍ، عَمَّن هُوَ؟ قَالَ: حَدَّثَتْهُ أَسْمَاءُ بِنْتُ زَيْدِ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ؛ أَنَّ عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ حَنْظَلَةَ بْنِ أَبِي عَامِرِ بْنِ الْغَسِيلِ حَدَّثَهَا، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ كَانَ أُمِرَ بِالْوُضُوءِ لِكُلِّ صَلَاةٍ طَاهِرًا كَانَ أَوْ غَيْرَ طَاهِرٍ، فَلَمَّا شَقَّ ذَلِكَ عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ أُمِرَ بِالسِّوَاكِ عِنْدَ كُلِّ صَلَاةٍ وَوُضِع عَنْهُ الْوُضُوءَ، إِلَّا مِنْ حَدَثٍ. فَكَانَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ يَرَى أَنَّ بِهِ قُوَّةً عَلَى ذَلِكَ، كَانَ يَفْعَلُهُ حَتَّى مَاتَ. 

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَوْف الحِمْصِيّ، عن أحمد بن خالد الذهني، عن محمد بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ يَحْيَى بْنِ حَبَّان، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ ثُمَّ قَالَ أَبُو دَاوُدَ: وَرَوَاهُ إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ فَقَالَ: عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، يَعْنِي كَمَا تَقَدَّمَ فِي رِوَايَةِ الْإِمَامِ أَحْمَدَ.

وَأَيًّا مَا كَانَ فَهُوَ إِسْنَادٌ صَحِيحٌ، وَقَدْ صَرَّحَ ابْنُ إِسْحَاقَ فِيهِ بِالتَّحْدِيثِ وَالسَّمَاعِ مِنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ يَحْيَى بْنِ حَبَّان، فَزَالَ مَحْذُورُ التَّدْلِيسِ. لَكِنْ قَالَ الْحَافِظُ ابْنُ عَسَاكِرَ: رَوَاهُ سَلَمَةُ بْنُ الْفَضْلِ وَعَلِيُّ بْنُ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ طَلْحَةَ بْنِ يَزِيدَ بْنِ رُكَانة، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ يَحْيَى بْنِ حَبَّان، بِهِ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ. وَفِي فِعْلِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ هَذَا، وَمُدَاوَمَتِهِ عَلَى إِسْبَاغِ الْوُضُوءِ لِكُلِّ صَلَاةٍ، دَلَالَةٌ عَلَى اسْتِحْبَابِ ذَلِكَ، كَمَا هُوَ مَذْهَبُ الْجُمْهُورِ. .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا زَكَرِيَّا بْنُ يَحْيَى بْنِ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا أزْهَر، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْن، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرين: أَنَّ الْخُلَفَاءَ كَانُوا يَتَوَضَّئُونَ لِكُلِّ صَلَاةٍ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْمُثَنَّى حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَة، سَمِعْتُ مَسْعُودَ بْنَ عَلِيِّ الشَّيْبَانِيَّ، سَمِعْتُ عِكْرِمة يَقُولُ: كَانَ عَلِيٌّ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، يَتَوَضَّأُ عِنْدَ كُلِّ صَلَاةٍ، وَيَقْرَأُ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا قُمْتُمْ إِلَى الصَّلاةِ﴾ الْآيَةَ.

وَحَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ الْمُثَنَّى، حَدَّثَنِي وَهْب بْنُ جَرِيرٍ، أَخْبَرْنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ مَيْسَرَةَ، عَنِ النَّزَّالِ بْنِ سَبْرَةَ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عَلِيًّا صَلَّى الظُّهْرَ، ثُمَّ قَعَدَ لِلنَّاسِ فِي الرّحْبة، ثُمَّ أُتِيَ بِمَاءٍ فَغَسَلَ وَجْهَهُ وَيَدَيْهِ، ثُمَّ مَسَحَ بِرَأْسِهِ وَرِجْلَيْهِ، وَقَالَ هَذَا وُضُوءُ مَنْ لَمْ يُحْدث.

وَحَدَّثَنِي يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْم عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ؛ أَنَّ عَلِيًّا اكماز مِنْ حُبٍّ، فَتَوَضَّأَ وُضُوءًا فِيهِ تَجَوُّزٌ فَقَالَ: هَذَا وُضُوءُ مَنْ لَمْ يُحْدِثْ ". وَهَذِهِ طُرُقٌ جَيِّدَةٌ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ [رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ] يُقَوِّي بَعْضُهَا بَعْضًا.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ أَيْضًا: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ بَشَّار، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنْ حُمَيْد، عَنْ أَنَسٍ قَالَ: تَوَضَّأَ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ وَضَوْءًا فِيهِ تَجَوّز، خَفِيفًا، فَقَالَ هَذَا وُضُوءُ مَنْ لَمْ يُحْدِثْ. وَهَذَا إِسْنَادٌ صَحِيحٌ. وَقَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سِيرِينَ: كَانَ الْخُلَفَاءُ يَتَوَضَّئُونَ لِكُلِّ صَلَاةٍ..

وَأَمَّا مَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ الطَّيَالِسِيُّ، عَنْ أَبِي هِلَالٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةُ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: الْوُضُوءُ مِنْ غَيْرِ حَدَثٍ اعْتِدَاءٌ. فَهُوَ غَرِيبٌ عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ، ثُمَّ هُوَ مَحْمُولٌ عَلَى أَنَّ مَنِ اعْتَقَدَ وَجُوبَهَ فَهُوَ مُعْتَدٍ، وَأَمَّا مَشْرُوعِيَّتُهُ اسْتِحْبَابًا فَقَدْ دَلَّتِ السُّنَّةُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ.

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ عَامِرٍ الْأَنْصَارِيِّ، سَمِعْتُ أَنَسَ بْنَ مَالِكٍ يَقُولُ: كَانَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ يَتَوَضَّأُ عِنْدَ كُلِّ صَلَاةٍ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ فَأَنْتُمْ كَيْفَ كُنْتُمْ تَصْنَعُونَ؟ قَالَ: كُنَّا نُصَلِّي الصَّلَوَاتِ بِوُضُوءٍ وَاحِدٍ مَا لَمْ نُحْدِثْ.

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ وَأَهْلُ السُّنَنِ مِنْ غَيْرِ وَجْهٍ عَنْ عَمْرو بْنِ عَامِرٍ، بِهِ. 

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو سَعِيدٍ الْبَغْدَادِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ هُرَيم، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ -هُوَ الْإِفْرِيقِيُّ-عَنْ أَبِي غُطَيف، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَنْ تَوَضَّأَ عَلَى طُهْر كُتِبَ لَهُ عَشْرُ حَسَنَاتٍ ".

وَرَوَاهُ أَيْضًا مِنْ حَدِيثِ عِيسَى بْنِ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْإِفْرِيقِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي غُطَيْفٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، فَذَكَرَهُ، وَفِيهِ قِصَّةٌ. 

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ، وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ، وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ مِنْ حَدِيثِ الْإِفْرِيقِيِّ، بِهِ نَحْوَهُ وَقَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: وَهُوَ إِسْنَادٌ ضَعِيفٌ.

قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: وَقَدْ قَالَ قَوْمٌ: إِنَّ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ نَزَلَتْ إِعْلَامًا مِنَ اللَّهِ أَنَّ الْوُضُوءَ لَا يَجِبُ إِلَّا عِنْدَ الْقِيَامِ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ، دُونَ غَيْرِهَا مِنَ الْأَعْمَالِ؛ وَذَلِكَ لِأَنَّهُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ كَانَ إِذَا أَحْدَثَ امْتَنَعَ مِنَ الْأَعْمَالِ كُلِّهَا حَتَّى يَتَوَضَّأَ.

حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو كُرَيْب، حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ عَمْرو بْنِ حَزْمٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَلْقَمَة بْنِ الفَغَواء، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ إِذَا أَرَاقَ الْبَوْلَ نُكَلِّمُهُ فَلَا يُكَلِّمُنَا، وَنُسَلِّمُ عَلَيْهِ فَلَا يَرُدُّ عَلَيْنَا، حَتَّى نَزَلَتْ آيَةُ الرُّخْصَةِ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا قُمْتُمْ إِلَى الصَّلاةِ﴾ الْآيَةَ.

وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي كُرَيْب، به نحوه. وهو حديث غريب جِدًّا، وَجَابِرُ هَذَا هُوَ ابْنُ يَزِيدَ الْجُعْفِيُّ، ضَعَّفُوهُ.

وَقَالَ أَبُو دَاوُدَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُسَدَّد، حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ، حَدَّثَنَا أَيُّوبُ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي مُلَيكة، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم خَرَجَ مِنَ الْخَلَاءِ، فَقُدِّم إِلَيْهِ طَعَامٌ، فَقَالُوا: أَلَا نَأْتِيكَ بوَضُوء فَقَالَ: "إِنَّمَا أُمِرْتُ بِالْوُضُوءِ إِذَا قُمْتُ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ.

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مَنِيع وَالنَّسَائِيِّ عَنْ زِيَادِ بْنِ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ -وَهُوَ ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ-بِهِ وَقَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: هَذَا حَدِيثٌ حَسَنٌ.

وَرَوَى مُسْلِمٌ عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ بْنِ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ دِينَارٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْحُوَيْرِثِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: كُنَّا عِنْدَ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ فَأَتَى الْخَلَاءَ، ثُمَّ إِنَّهُ رَجَعَ فَأُتِيَ بِطَعَامٍ، فَقِيلَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، أَلَا تَتَوَضَّأُ؟ فَقَالَ: "لِمَ؟ أَأُصْلِي فَأَتَوَضَّأُ؟ ". 

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿فَاغْسِلُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ﴾ قَدِ اسْتَدَلَّ طَائِفَةٌ مِنَ الْعُلَمَاءِ بِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿إِذَا قُمْتُمْ إِلَى الصَّلاةِ فَاغْسِلُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ﴾ عَلَى وُجُوبِ النِّيَّةِ فِي الْوُضُوءِ؛ لِأَنَّ تَقْدِيرَ الْكَلَامِ: "إِذَا قُمْتُمْ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ فَاغْسِلُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ لَهَا"، كَمَا تَقُولُ الْعَرَبُ: "إِذَا رَأَيْتَ الْأَمِيرَ فَقُمْ" أَيْ: لَهُ. وَقَدْ ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ حَدِيثُ: "الْأَعْمَالُ بِالنِّيَّاتِ، وَإِنَّمَا لِكُلِّ امْرِئٍ مَا نَوَى". وَيُسْتَحَبُّ قَبْلَ غَسْلِ الْوَجْهِ أَنْ يُذْكَرَ اسْمُ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى عَلَى وُضُوئِهِ؛ لِمَا وَرَدَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ مِنْ طُرُقٍ جَيِّدَةٍ، عَنْ جَمَاعَةٍ مِنَ الصَّحَابَةِ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: "لَا وُضُوءَ لِمَنْ لَمْ يَذْكُرِ اسْمَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ". 

وَيُسْتَحَبُّ أَنْ يَغْسِلَ كَفَّيْهِ قَبْلَ إِدْخَالِهِمَا فِي الْإِنَاءِ وَيَتَأَكَّدُ ذَلِكَ عِنْدَ الْقِيَامِ مِنَ النَّوْمِ؛ لِمَا ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "إِذَا اسْتَيْقَظَ أَحَدُكُمْ مِنْ نَوْمِه، فَلَا يُدخل يَدَهُ فِي الْإِنَاءِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَغْسِلَهَا ثَلَاثًا، فَإِنَّ أحدَكم لَا يَدْرِي أَيْنَ بَاتَتْ يَدُهُ". 

وحَدُّ الْوَجْهِ عِنْدَ الْفُقَهَاءِ: مَا بَيْنَ مَنَابِتِ شَعْرِ الرَّأْسِ -وَلَا اعْتِبَارَ بالصَّلع وَلَا بالغَمَم-إِلَى مُنْتَهَى اللَّحْيَيْنِ وَالذَّقْنِ طُولًا وَمِنَ الْأُذُنِ إِلَى الْأُذُنِ عَرْضًا، وفي النزعتين والتحذيف خلاف، هل هما مِنَ الرَّأْسِ أَوِ الْوَجْهِ، وَفِي الْمُسْتَرْسِلِ مِنَ اللِّحْيَةِ عَنْ مَحَلِّ الْفَرْضِ قَوْلَانِ، أَحَدُهُمَا: أَنَّهُ يَجِبُ إِفَاضَةُ الْمَاءِ عَلَيْهِ لِأَنَّهُ تَقَعُ بِهِ الْمُوَاجَهَةُ. وَرُوِيَ فِي حَدِيثٍ: أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ رَأَى رَجُلًا مُغَطِّيًا لِحْيَتَهُ، فَقَالَ: "اكْشِفْهَا، فَإِنَّ اللِّحْيَةَ مِنَ الْوَجْهِ" وَقَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ: هِيَ مِنَ الْوَجْهِ، أَلَا تَسْمَعُ إِلَى قَوْلِ الْعَرَبِ فِي الْغُلَامِ إِذَا نَبَتَتْ لِحْيَتُهُ: طَلَعَ وَجْهُهُ.

وَيُسْتَحَبُّ لِلْمُتَوَضِّئِ أَنْ يُخَلِّلَ لِحْيَتَهُ إِذَا كَانَتْ كَثَّة، قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، حَدَّثَنَا إِسْرَائِيلُ، عَنْ عَامِرِ بْنِ شَقِيقِ بْنِ جَمْرَة، عَنْ أَبِي وَائِلٍ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عُثْمَانَ تَوَضَّأَ -فَذَكَرَ الْحَدِيثَ-قَالَ: وَخَلَّلَ اللِّحْيَةَ ثَلَاثًا حِينَ غَسَلَ وَجْهَهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَعَلَ الَّذِي رَأَيْتُمُونِي فَعَلْتُ.

رَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ، وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ مِنْ حَدِيثِ عَبْدِ الرَّزَّاقِ وَقَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: حَسَنٌ صَحِيحٌ، وَحَسَّنَهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ.

وَقَالَ أَبُو دَاوُدَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو تَوْبَة الرَّبِيعُ بْنُ نَافِعٍ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو المَلِيح، حَدَّثَنَا الْوَلِيدُ بْنُ زَوْرَانَ عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ كَانَ إِذَا تَوَضَّأَ أَخَذَ كَفًّا مِنْ مَاءٍ فَأَدْخَلَهُ تَحْتَ حَنَكِهِ، يُخَلِّلُ بِهِ لِحْيَتَهُ، وَقَالَ: "هَكَذَا أَمَرَنِي بِهِ رَبِّي عَزَّ وَجَلَّ.

تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ أَبُو دَاوُدَ وَقَدْ رُوي هَذَا مِنْ غَيْرِ وَجْهٍ عَنْ أَنَسٍ. قَالَ الْبَيْهَقِيُّ: وَرُوِّينَا فِي تَخْلِيلِ اللِّحْيَةِ عَنْ عَمَّارٍ، وَعَائِشَةَ، وَأُمِّ سَلَمَةَ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، ثُمَّ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ وَغَيْرِهِ، وَرُوِّينَا فِي الرُّخْصَةِ فِي تَرْكِهِ عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، وَالْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ، ثُمَّ عَنِ النَّخَعِيِّ، وَجَمَاعَةٍ مِنَ التَّابِعَيْنِ. 

وَقَدْ ثَبَتَ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ مِنْ غَيْرِ وَجْهٍ فِي الصِّحَاحِ وَغَيْرِهَا: أَنَّهُ كَانَ إِذَا تَوَضَّأَ تَمَضْمَضَ وَاسْتَنْشَقَ، فَاخْتَلَفَ الْأَئِمَّةُ فِي ذَلِكَ: هَلْ هُمَا وَاجِبَانِ فِي الْوُضُوءِ وَالْغُسْلِ، كَمَا هُوَ مَذْهَبُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ حَنْبَلٍ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ؟ أَوْ مُسْتَحَبَّانِ فِيهِمَا، كَمَا هُوَ مَذْهَبُ الشَّافِعِيِّ وَمَالِكٍ؟ لِمَا ثَبَتَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ الَّذِي رَوَاهُ أَهْلُ السُّنَنِ وَصَحَّحَهُ ابْنُ خُزَيمة، عَنْ رَفَاعَةَ بْنِ رَافِعٍ الزُّرْقِيِّ؛ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ قَالَ لِلْمُسِيءِ فِي صِلَاتِهِ: "تَوَضَّأْ كَمَا أَمَرَكَ اللَّهُ" أَوْ يَجِبَانِ فِي الْغَسْلِ دُونَ الْوُضُوءِ، كَمَا هُوَ مَذْهَبُ أَبِي حَنِيفَةَ؟ أَوْ يجب الِاسْتِنْشَاقُ دُونَ الْمَضْمَضَةِ كَمَا هُوَ رِوَايَةٌ عَنِ الْإِمَامِ أَحْمَدَ لِمَا ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى الله عليه وسلم قال: "مَنْ تَوَضَّأَ فَلْيَسْتَنْثِرْ" وَفِي رِوَايَةٍ: "إِذَا تَوَضَّأَ أَحَدُكُمْ فَلْيَجْعَلْ فِي مَنْخِرَيْهِ مِنَ الْمَاءِ ثُمَّ لِيَنْتَثِرْ" وَالِانْتِثَارُ: هُوَ الْمُبَالَغَةُ فِي الِاسْتِنْشَاقِ.

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَلَمَةَ الْخُزَاعِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ بِلَالٍ، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ، عَنْ عَطَاءِ بْنِ يَسَارٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ؛ أَنَّهُ تَوَضَّأَ فَغَسَلَ وَجْهَهُ، ثُمَّ أَخَذَ غَرْفَةً مِنْ مَاءٍ فَتَمَضْمَضَ بِهَا وَاسْتَنْثَرَ، ثُمَّ أَخَذَ غَرْفَةً فَجَعَلَ بِهَا هَكَذَا، يَعْنِي أَضَافَهَا إِلَى يَدِهِ الْأُخْرَى، فَغَسَلَ بِهِمَا وَجْهَهُ. ثُمَّ أَخَذَ غَرْفَةً مِنْ مَاءٍ، فَغَسَلَ بِهَا يَدَهُ الْيُمْنَى، ثُمَّ أَخَذَ غَرْفَةً مِنْ مَاءٍ فَغَسَلَ بِهَا يَدَهُ الْيُسْرَى، ثُمَّ مَسَحَ رَأْسَهُ، ثُمَّ أَخَذَ غَرْفَةً مِنْ مَاءٍ، ثُمَّ رَشَّ عَلَى رِجْلِهِ الْيُمْنَى حَتَّى غَسْلَهَا، ثُمَّ أَخَذَ غَرْفَةً أُخْرَى فَغَسَلَ بِهَا رِجْلَهُ الْيُسْرَى، ثُمَّ قَالَ: هَكَذَا رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ، يَعْنِي يَتَوَضَّأُ.

وَرَوَاهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحِيمِ، عَنْ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ الْخُزَاعِيِّ، بِهِ 

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ إِلَى الْمَرَافِقِ﴾ أَيْ: مَعَ الْمَرَافِقِ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَلا تَأْكُلُوا أَمْوَالَهُمْ إِلَى أَمْوَالِكُمْ إِنَّهُ كَانَ حُوبًا كَبِيرًا﴾ [النِّسَاءِ: ٢]

وَقَدْ رَوَى الْحَافِظُ الدَّارَقُطْنِيُّ وَأَبُو بَكْرٍ الْبَيْهَقِيُّ، مِنْ طَرِيقِ الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَقِيلٍ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ إِذَا تَوَضَّأَ أَدَارَ الْمَاءَ عَلَى مِرْفَقَيْهِ. وَلَكِنِ الْقَاسِمُ هَذَا مَتْرُوكُ الْحَدِيثِ، وَجَدُّهُ ضَعِيفٌ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَيُسْتَحَبُّ لِلْمُتَوَضِّئِ أَنْ يَشْرَعَ فِي الْعَضُدِ لِيَغْسِلَهُ مَعَ ذِرَاعَيْهِ؛ لِمَا رَوَى الْبُخَارِيُّ وَمُسْلِمٌ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ نُعَيم المُجْمِر، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "إِنَّ أُمَّتِي يُدْعَوْن يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ غُرًّا مُحَجَّلين مِنْ آثَارِ الْوُضُوءِ، فَمَنِ اسْتَطَاعَ مِنْكُمْ أَنْ يُطِيلَ غُرَّته فَلْيَفْعَلْ". 

وَفِي صَحِيحِ مُسْلِمٍ: عَنْ قُتَيْبَة، عَنْ خَلَف بْنِ خَلِيفَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي مَالِكٍ الْأَشْجَعِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي حَازِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ خَلِيلِي ﷺ يَقُولُ: "تَبْلُغُ الحِلْية مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِ حَيْثُ يَبْلُغُ الْوُضُوءُ". 

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَامْسَحُوا بِرُءُوسِكُمْ﴾ اخْتَلَفُوا فِي هَذِهِ "الْبَاءِ" هَلْ هِيَ لِلْإِلْصَاقِ، وَهُوَ الْأَظْهَرُ أَوْ لِلتَّبْعِيضِ؟ وَفِيهِ نَظَرٌ، عَلَى قَوْلَيْنِ. وَمِنَ الْأُصُولِيِّينَ مَنْ قَالَ: هَذَا مُجْمَلٌ فَلْيُرْجَعْ فِي بَيَانِهِ إِلَى السُّنَّةِ، وَقَدْ ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ مِنْ طَرِيقِ مَالِكٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ يَحْيَى الْمَازِنِيِّ عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّ رَجُلًا قَالَ لِعَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ زَيْدِ بْنِ عَاصِمٍ -وَهُوَ جَدُّ عَمْرِو بْنِ يَحْيَى، وَكَانَ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ-: هَلْ تَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ تُرِيَنِي كَيْفَ كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَتَوَضَّأُ؟ فَقَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ زَيْدٍ: نَعَمْ، فَدَعَا بِوُضُوءٍ، فَأَفْرَغَ عَلَى يَدَيْهِ، فَغَسَلَ يَدَيْهِ مَرَّتَيْنِ مَرَّتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ مَضْمَضَ وَاسْتَنْشَقَ ثَلَاثًا، وَغَسَلَ وَجْهَهُ ثَلَاثًا، ثُمَّ غسل يديه مَرَّتَيْنِ مَرَّتَيْنِ إِلَى الْمِرْفَقَيْنِ، ثُمَّ مَسَحَ بِيَدَيْهِ، فَأَقْبَلَ بِهِمَا وَأَدْبَرَ، بَدَأَ بِمُقَدَّمِ رَأْسِهِ ثُمَّ ذَهَبَ بِهِمَا إِلَى قَفَاهُ، ثُمَّ رَدَّهُمَا حَتَّى رَجَعَ إِلَى الْمَكَانِ الَّذِي بَدَأَ مِنْهُ، ثُمَّ غَسَلَ رِجْلَيْهِ. 

وَفِي حَدِيثِ عَبْدِ خَيْرٍ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ فِي صِفَةِ وَضَوْءِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ نَحْوُ هَذَا، وَرَوَى أَبُو دَاوُدَ، عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ وَالْمِقْدَامِ بْنِ مَعْدِ يَكْرِبَ، فِي صِفَةِ وَضَوْءِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ مِثْلَهُ. 

فَفِي هَذِهِ الْأَحَادِيثِ دَلَالَةٌ لِمَنْ ذَهَبَ إِلَى وُجُوبِ تَكْمِيلِ مَسْحِ جَمِيعِ الرَّأْسِ، كَمَا هُوَ مَذْهَبُ الْإِمَامِ مَالِكٍ وَأَحْمَدَ بْنِ حَنْبَلٍ، لَا سِيَّمَا عَلَى قَوْلِ مَنْ زَعَمَ أَنَّهَا خَرَجَتْ مَخْرَجَ الْبَيَانِ لِمَا أُجْمِلَ فِي الْقُرْآنِ.

وَقَدْ ذَهَبَ الْحَنَفِيَّةُ إِلَى وُجُوبِ مَسْحِ رُبْعِ الرَّأْسِ، وَهُوَ مِقْدَارُ النَّاصِيَةِ.

وَذَهَبَ أَصْحَابُنَا إِلَى أَنَّهُ إِنَّمَا يَجِبُ مَا يُطْلَقُ عَلَيْهِ اسْمُ مَسْحٍ، لَا يَتَقَدَّرُ ذَلِكَ بحدٍّ، بَلْ لَوْ مَسَحَ بَعْضَ شَعْرِهِ مِنْ رَأْسِهِ أَجْزَأَهُ.

وَاحْتَجَّ الْفَرِيقَانِ بِحَدِيثِ الْمُغِيرَةِ بْنِ شُعْبَةَ، قَالَ: تَخَلَّفَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ فَتَخَلَّفْتُ مَعَهُ، فَلَمَّا قَضَى حَاجَتَهُ قَالَ: "هَلْ مَعَكَ مَاءٌ؟ " فَأَتَيْتُهُ بِمِطْهَرَةٍ فَغَسَلَ كَفَّيْهِ وَوَجْهَهُ، ثُمَّ ذَهَبَ يَحْسِرُ عَنْ ذِرَاعَيْهِ فَضَاقَ كُمُّ الْجُبَّةِ، فَأَخْرَجَ يَدَيْهِ مِنْ تَحْتِ الْجُبَّةِ وَأَلْقَى الْجُبَّةَ عَلَى مَنْكِبَيْهِ فَغَسَلَ ذِرَاعَيْهِ وَمَسَحَ بِنَاصِيَتِهِ، وَعَلَى الْعِمَامَةِ وَعَلَى خُفَّيْهِ ... وَذَكَرَ بَاقِيَ الْحَدِيثِ، وَهُوَ فِي صَحِيحِ مُسْلِمٍ، وَغَيْرِهِ. 

فَقَالَ لَهُمْ أَصْحَابُ الْإِمَامِ أَحْمَدَ: إِنَّمَا اقْتَصَرَ عَلَى مَسْحِ النَّاصِيَةِ لِأَنَّهُ كَمَّلَ مَسْحَ بَقِيَّةِ الرَّأْسِ عَلَى الْعِمَامَةِ، وَنَحْنُ نَقُولُ بِذَلِكَ، وَأَنَّهُ يَقَعُ عَنِ الْمَوْقِعِ كَمَا وَرَدَتْ بِذَلِكَ أَحَادِيثُ كَثِيرَةٌ، وَأَنَّهُ كَانَ يَمْسَحُ عَلَى الْعِمَامَةِ وَعَلَى الْخُفَّيْنِ، فَهَذَا أَوْلَى، وَلَيْسَ لَكُمْ فِيهِ دَلَالَةٌ عَلَى جَوَازِ الِاقْتِصَارِ عَلَى مَسْحِ النَّاصِيَةِ أَوْ بَعْضِ الرَّأْسِ مِنْ غَيْرِ تَكْمِيلٍ عَلَى الْعِمَامَةِ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

ثُمَّ اخْتَلَفُوا فِي أَنَّهُ: هَلْ يُسْتَحَبُّ تَكْرَارُ مَسْحِ الرَّأْسِ ثَلَاثًا، كَمَا هُوَ الْمَشْهُورُ مِنْ مَذْهَبِ الشَّافِعِيِّ، أَوْ إِنَّمَا يُسْتَحَبُّ مَسْحَةٌ وَاحِدَةٌ، كَمَا هُوَ مَذْهَبُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ حَنْبَلٍ وَمَنْ تَابَعَهُ، عَلَى قَوْلَيْنِ. فَقَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ: عَنْ مَعْمَر، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ عَطَاءِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ اللَّيْثِيِّ، عَنْ حُمْران بْنِ أَبَانٍ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عُثْمَانَ بْنَ عَفَّانَ تَوَضَّأَ فَأَفْرَغَ عَلَى يَدَيْهِ ثَلَاثًا فَغَسَلَهُمَا، ثُمَّ مَضْمَضَ وَاسْتَنْشَقَ، ثُمَّ غَسَلَ وَجْهَهُ ثَلَاثًا، ثُمَّ غَسَلَ يَدَهُ الْيُمْنَى إِلَى الْمِرْفَقِ ثَلَاثًا، ثُمَّ غَسَلَ الْيُسْرَى مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ، ثُمَّ مَسَحَ بِرَأْسِهِ، ثُمَّ غَسَلَ قَدَمَهُ الْيُمْنَى ثَلَاثًا، ثُمَّ الْيُسْرَى ثَلَاثًا مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ ثُمَّ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ تَوَضَّأَ نَحْوَ وُضُوئِي هَذَا، ثُمَّ قَالَ: "مَنْ تَوَضَّأ نَحْوَ وُضُوئِي هَذَا، ثُمَّ صلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ لَا يُحدِّث فِيْهِمَا نَفْسَهُ، غُفِرَ لَهُ ما تقدم من ذنبه ".

أَخْرَجَهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ وَمُسْلِمٌ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ مِنْ طَرِيقِ الزُّهْرِيِّ بِهِ نَحْوَ هَذَا وُفِي سُنَنِ أَبِي دَاوُدَ مِنْ رِوَايَةِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي مُلَيْكَة، عَنْ عُثْمَانَ فِي صِفَةِ الْوُضُوءِ: وَمَسَحَ بِرَأْسِهِ مَرَّةً وَاحِدَةً وَكَذَا مِنْ رِوَايَةِ عَبْدِ خَيْرٍ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ مِثْلُهُ.

وَاحْتَجَّ مَنِ اسْتَحَبَّ تَكْرَارَ مَسْحِ الرَّأْسِ بِعُمُومِ الْحَدِيثِ الَّذِي رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ فِي صَحِيحِهِ، عَنْ عُثْمَانَ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ: تَوَضَّأَ ثَلَاثًا ثَلَاثًا.

وَقَالَ أَبُو دَاوُدَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْمُثَنَّى، حَدَّثَنَا الضَّحَّاكُ بْنُ مَخْلَد، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ وَرْدَان، حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو سَلَمَةَ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، حَدَّثَنِي حُمْرَانُ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عُثْمَانَ بْنَ عَفَّانَ تَوَضَّأَ. فَذَكَرَ نَحْوَهُ، وَلَمْ يَذْكُرِ الْمَضْمَضَةَ وَالِاسْتِنْشَاقَ، قَالَ فِيهِ: ثُمَّ مَسَحَ رَأْسَهُ ثَلَاثًا، ثُمَّ غَسَلَ رِجْلَيْهِ ثَلَاثًا، ثُمَّ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ توضأ هَكَذَا وَقَالَ: "مَنْ تَوَضَّأَ دُونَ هَذَا كَفَاهُ.

تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ أَبُو دَاوُدَ ثُمَّ قَالَ: وَأَحَادِيثُ عُثْمَانَ الصِّحَاحُ تَدُلُّ عَلَى أَنَّهُ مَسَحَ الرَّأْسَ مَرَّةً وَاحِدَةً.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَأَرْجُلَكُمْ إِلَى الْكَعْبَيْنِ﴾ قُرئ: ﴿وَأَرْجُلَكُمْ﴾ بِالنَّصْبِ عَطْفًا عَلَى ﴿فَاغْسِلُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ﴾

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو زُرْعَة، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَلَمَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا وُهَيْب، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ عِكَرِمة، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ؛ أَنَّهُ قَرَأَهَا: ﴿وَأَرْجُلَكُمْ﴾ يَقُولُ: رَجَعَتْ إِلَى الْغَسْلِ.

وَرُوِيَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، وعُرْوَة، وَعَطَاءٍ، وَعِكْرِمَةَ، وَالْحَسَنِ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، وَإِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَالضِّحَاكِ، والسُّدِّي، ومُقاتل بْنِ حَيَّانَ، وَالزُّهْرِيِّ، وَإِبْرَاهِيمَ التَّيْمِيِّ، نَحْوُ ذَلِكَ.

وَهَذِهِ قِرَاءَةٌ ظَاهِرَةٌ فِي وُجُوبِ الْغَسْلِ، كَمَا قَالَهُ السَّلَفُ، وَمِنْ هَاهُنَا ذَهَبَ مَنْ ذَهَبَ إِلَى وُجُوبِ التَّرْتِيبِ كَمَا هُوَ مَذْهَبُ الْجُمْهُورِ، خِلَافًا لِأَبِي حَنِيفَةَ حَيْثُ لَمْ يَشْتَرِطِ التَّرْتِيبَ، بَلْ لَوْ غَسَلَ قَدَمَيْهِ ثُمَّ مَسَحَ رَأْسَهُ وَغَسَلَ يَدَيْهِ ثُمَّ وَجْهَهُ أَجَزْأَهُ ذَلِكَ؛ لَأَنَّ الْآيَةَ أَمَرَتْ بِغَسْلِ هَذِهِ الْأَعْضَاءِ، وَ"الْوَاوُ" لَا تَدُلُّ عَلَى التَّرْتِيبِ. وَقَدْ سَلَكَ الْجُمْهُورُ فِي الْجَوَابِ عَنْ هَذَا الْبَحْثِ طُرُقًا، فَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ قَالَ: الْآيَةُ دَلَّتْ عَلَى وُجُوبِ غَسْلِ الْوَجْهِ ابْتِدَاءً عِنْدَ الْقِيَامِ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ؛ لِأَنَّهُ مَأْمُورٌ بِهِ بِفَاءِ التَّعْقِيبِ، وَهِيَ مُقْتَضِيَةٌ لِلتَّرْتِيبِ، وَلَمْ يُقِلْ أَحَدٌ مِنَ النَّاسِ بِوُجُوبِ غَسْلِ الْوَجْهِ أَوَّلًا ثُمَّ لَا يَجِبُ التَّرْتِيبُ بَعْدَهُ، بَلِ الْقَائِلُ اثْنَانِ، أَحَدُهُمَا: يُوجِبُ التَّرْتِيبَ، كَمَا هُوَ وَاقِعٌ فِي الْآيَةِ. وَالْآخَرُ يَقُولُ: لَا يَجِبُ التَّرْتِيبُ مُطْلَقًا، وَالْآيَةُ دَلَّتْ عَلَى وُجُوبِ غَسْلِ الْوَجْهِ ابْتِدَاءً، فَوَجَبَ التَّرْتِيبُ فِيمَا بَعْدَهُ بِالْإِجْمَاعِ، حَيْثُ لَا فَارِقَ. وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ قَالَ: لَا نُسَلِّمُ أَنْ "الْوَاوَ" لَا تَدُلُّ عَلَى التَّرْتِيبِ، بَلْ هِيَ دَالَّةٌ -كَمَا هُوَ مَذْهَبُ طَائِفَةٍ مِنَ النُّحَاةِ وَأَهْلِ اللُّغَةِ وَبَعْضِ الْفُقَهَاءِ. ثُمَّ نَقُولُ -بِتَقْدِيرِ تَسْلِيمِ كَوْنِهَا لَا تَدُلُّ عَلَى الترتيب اللغوي-: هي دَالَّةٌ عَلَى التَّرْتِيبِ شَرْعًا فِيمَا مِنْ شَأْنِهِ أَنْ يُرَتَّبَ، وَالدَّلِيلُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ أَنَّهُ ﷺ لَمَّا طَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ، خَرَجَ مِنْ بَابِ الصَّفَا وَهُوَ يَتْلُو قَوْلَهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿إِنَّ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةَ مِنْ شَعَائِرِ اللَّهِ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ١٥٨] ثُمَّ قَالَ: "أَبْدَأُ بِمَا بَدَأَ اللَّهُ بِهِ" لَفْظُ مُسْلِمٍ، وَلَفْظُ النَّسَائِيِّ: "ابْدَءُوا بِمَا بَدَأَ اللَّهُ بِهِ". وَهَذَا لَفْظُ أَمْرٍ، وَإِسْنَادُهُ صَحِيحٌ، فَدَلَّ عَلَى وُجُوبِ الْبُدَاءَةِ بِمَا بَدَأَ اللَّهُ بِهِ، وَهُوَ مَعْنَى كَوْنِهَا تَدُلُّ عَلَى التَّرْتِيبِ شَرْعًا، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ قَالَ: لَمَّا ذَكَرَ تَعَالَى هَذِهِ الصِّفَةَ فِي هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ عَلَى هَذَا التَّرْتِيبِ، فَقَطَعَ النَّظِيرَ عَنِ النَّظِيرِ، وَأَدْخَلَ الْمَمْسُوحَ بَيْنَ الْمَغْسُولَيْنِ، دَلَّ ذَلِكَ عَلَى إِرَادَةِ التَّرْتِيبِ.

وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ قَالَ: لَا شَكَّ أَنَّهُ قَدْ رَوَى أَبُو دَاوُدَ وَغَيْرُهُ مِنْ طَرِيقِ عَمْرو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ تَوَضَّأَ مَرَّةً مَرَّةً، ثُمَّ قَالَ: "هَذَا وُضُوءٌ لَا يَقْبَلُ اللَّهُ الصَّلَاةَ إِلَّا بِهِ" قَالُوا: فَلَا يَخْلُو إِمَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ تَوَضَّأْ مُرَتِّبًا فَيَجِبُ التَّرْتِيبُ، أَوْ يَكُونَ تَوَضَّأْ غَيْرَ مُرَتِّبٍ فَيَجِبُ عَدَمُ التَّرْتِيبِ، وَلَا قَائِلَ بِهِ، فَوَجَبَ مَا ذَكَرَهُ. 

وَأَمَّا الْقِرَاءَةُ الْأُخْرَى، وَهِيَ قِرَاءَةُ مَنْ قَرَأَ: ﴿وَأَرْجُلِكُمْ﴾ بِالْخَفْضِ. فَقَدِ احْتَجَّ بِهَا الشِّيعَةُ فِي قَوْلِهِمْ بِوُجُوبِ مَسْحِ الرِّجْلَيْنِ؛ لِأَنَّهَا عِنْدَهُمْ مَعْطُوفَةٌ عَلَى مَسْحِ الرَّأْسِ. وَقَدْ رُوي عَنْ طَائِفَةٍ مِنَ السَّلَفِ مَا يُوهِمُ الْقَوْلَ بِالْمَسْحِ، فَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ:

حَدَّثَنِي يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْد قَالَ: قَالَ مُوسَى بْنُ أَنَسٍ لِأَنَسٍ وَنَحْنُ عِنْدَهُ: يَا أَبَا حَمْزَةَ، إِنَّ الْحَجَّاجَ خَطَبَنا بِالْأَهْوَازِ وَنَحْنُ مَعَهُ، فَذَكَرَ الطَّهُورَ فَقَالَ: اغْسِلُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ، وَامْسَحُوا بِرُءُوسِكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُمْ، وَأَنَّهُ لَيْسَ شَيْءٌ مِنَ ابْنِ آدَمَ أَقْرَبَ مِنْ خَبَثِهِ مِنْ قَدَمَيْهِ، فَاغْسِلُوا بُطُونَهُمَا وَظُهُورَهُمَا عَرَاقيبهما فَقَالَ أَنَسٌ: صَدَقَ اللَّهُ وَكَذَبَ الْحَجَّاجُ، قَالَ اللَّهُ [تَعَالَى] ﴿وَامْسَحُوا بِرُءُوسِكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُمْ﴾ قَالَ: وَكَانَ أَنَسٌ إِذَا مَسَحَ قَدَمَيْهِ بَلَّهما إِسْنَادٌ صَحِيحٌ إِلَيْهِ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ سَهْل، حَدَّثَنَا مُؤَمَّل، حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادٌ، حَدَّثَنَا عَاصِمٌ الْأَحْوَلُ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ قَالَ: نَزَلَ الْقُرْآنُ بِالْمَسْحِ، وَالسُّنَّةُ الْغَسْلُ وَهَذَا أَيْضًا إِسْنَادٌ صَحِيحٌ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو كُرَيْب، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ قَيْس الْخُرَاسَانِيُّ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْج، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ دِينَارٍ، عَنْ عِكرِمة، عَنِ ابن عباس قَالَ: الْوُضُوءُ غَسْلتَان وَمَسْحَتَانِ. 

وَكَذَا رَوَى سَعِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَرُوبَة، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنِي أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مَعْمَر المِنْقَريّ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ، حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيِّ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ يُوسُفَ بْنِ مِهْران، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: ﴿وَامْسَحُوا بِرُءُوسِكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُمْ إِلَى الْكَعْبَيْنِ﴾ قَالَ: هُوَ الْمَسْحُ. ثُمَّ قَالَ: وَرُوِيَ عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ وَعَلْقَمَةَ، وَأَبِي جَعْفَرٍ، [وَ] مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ، وَالْحَسَنِ -فِي إِحْدَى الرِّوَايَاتِ-وَجَابِرِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ -فِي إِحْدَى الرِّوَايَاتِ-نَحْوُهُ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، حَدَّثَنَا أَيُّوبُ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عِكْرِمَةَ يَمْسَحُ عَلَى رِجْلَيْهِ، قَالَ: وَكَانَ يَقُولُهُ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو السَّائِبِ، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ أَبِي هِنْدٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ قَالَ: نَزَلَ جِبْرِيلُ بِالْمَسْحِ. ثُمَّ قَالَ الشَّعْبِيُّ: أَلَّا تَرَى أَنَّ "التَّيَمُّمَ" أنْ يَمْسَحَ مَا كَانَ غَسْلًا وَيُلْغِيَ مَا كَانَ مَسْحًا؟

وَحَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي زِيَادٍ، حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ، أَخْبَرْنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ، قُلْتُ لِعَامِرٍ: إِنَّ نَاسًا يَقُولُونَ: إِنَّ جِبْرِيلَ نَزَلَ بِغَسْلِ الرِّجْلَيْنِ؟ فَقَالَ: نَزَلَ جِبْرِيلُ بِالْمَسْحِ.

فَهَذِهِ آثَارٌ غَرِيبَةٌ جِدًّا، وَهِيَ مَحْمُولَةٌ عَلَى أَنَّ الْمُرَادَ بِالْمَسْحِ هُوَ الْغَسْلُ الْخَفِيفُ، لِمَا سَنَذْكُرُهُ مِنَ السُّنَّةِ الثَّابِتَةِ فِي وُجُوبِ غَسْلِ الرِّجْلَيْنِ. وَإِنَّمَا جَاءَتْ هَذِهِ الْقِرَاءَةُ بِالْخَفْضِ إِمَّا عَلَى الْمُجَاوَرَةِ وَتَنَاسُبِ الْكَلَامِ، كَمَا فِي قَوْلِ الْعَرَبِ: "جُحْرُ ضَب خربٍ"، وَكَقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿عَالِيَهُمْ ثِيَابُ سُنْدُسٍ خُضْرٌ وَإِسْتَبْرَقٌ﴾ [الْإِنْسَانِ: ٢١] وَهَذَا سَائِغٌ ذَائِعٌ، فِي لُغَةِ الْعَرَبِ شَائِعٌ. وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ قَالَ: هِيَ مَحْمُولَةٌ عَلَى مَسْحِ الْقَدَمَيْنِ إِذَا كَانَ عَلَيْهِمَا الْخُفَّانِ، قَالَهُ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الشَّافِعِيُّ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ. وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ قَالَ: هِيَ دَالَّةٌ عَلَى مَسْحِ الرِّجْلَيْنِ، وَلَكِنَّ الْمُرَادَ بِذَلِكَ الْغَسْلُ الْخَفِيفُ، كَمَا وَرَدَتْ بِهِ السُّنَّةُ. وَعَلَى كُلِّ تَقْدِيرٍ فَالْوَاجِبُ غَسْلُ الرِّجْلَيْنِ فَرْضًا، لَا بُدَّ مِنْهُ لِلْآيَةِ وَالْأَحَادِيثِ الَّتِي سَنُورِدُهَا.

وَمِنْ أَحْسَنِ مَا يُسْتَدَلُّ بِهِ عَلَى أَنَّ الْمَسْحَ يُطْلَقُ عَلَى الْغَسْلِ الْخَفِيفِ مَا رَوَاهُ الْحَافِظُ الْبَيْهَقِيُّ، حَيْثُ قَالَ: أَخْبَرْنَا أَبُو عَلِيٍّ الرُّوذَبَارِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مَحْمَوَيْهِ الْعَسْكَرِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا جَعْفَرُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ الْقَلَانِسِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا آدَمُ، حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْمَلِكِ بْنُ مَيْسَرَة، سَمِعْتُ النَّزَّالَ بْنَ سَبْرَة يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ، أَنَّهُ صَلَّى الظُّهْرَ، ثُمَّ قَعَدَ فِي حَوَائِجِ النَّاسِ فِي رَحَبَة الْكُوفَةِ حَتَّى حَضَرَتْ صَلَاةُ الْعَصْرِ، ثُمَّ أُتِيَ بِكُوزٍ مِنْ مَاءٍ، فَأَخَذَ مِنْهُ حَفْنَةً وَاحِدَةً، فَمَسَحَ بِهَا وَجْهَهُ وَيَدَيْهِ وَرَأْسَهُ وَرِجْلَيْهِ، ثُمَّ قَامَ فَشَرِبَ فَضْلَهُ وَهُوَ قَائِمٌ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: إِنَّ نَاسًا يَكْرَهُونَ الشُّرْبَ قَائِمًا، وإن رسول الله [صلى الله عليه وسلم] صَنَعَ مَا صنعتُ. وَقَالَ: "هَذَا وُضُوءُ مَنْ لَمْ يُحْدِثْ ".

رَوَاهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ فِي الصَّحِيحِ، عَنْ آدَمَ، بِبَعْضِ مَعْنَاهُ.
