He said, "A people who feared associating partners with God and the worship of idols, and made their worship sincere; so they will pass on to Paradise."

Regarding His statement: {If you stretch out your hand against me to kill me, I will not stretch out my hand against you to kill you. Indeed, I fear God, the Lord of all the worlds}. His brother, the righteous man whose sacrifice God had accepted because of his piety, says this to him when his brother threatened him with killing over no offense committed against him: {If you stretch out your hand against me to kill me, I will not stretch out my hand against you to kill you}, meaning: I will not respond to your corrupt act with the like of it, so that I and you would be equal in the sin. {Indeed, I fear God, the Lord of all the worlds}, meaning: from doing as you wish to do; rather, I will be patient and seek my reward with God.

Abdullah ibn Amr said, "By God, he was in fact the stronger of the two men, but restraint held him back," meaning scrupulousness.

For this reason it is established in the two Sahihs, from the Prophet ﷺ, that he said: "When two Muslims face each other with their swords, both the killer and the killed are in the Fire." They said, "O Messenger of God, this is the killer, but what of the one killed?" He said, "He was eager to kill his companion."

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas said, at the time of the trial concerning Uthman: "I bear witness that the Messenger of God ﷺ said: 'There will be a trial in which the one sitting is better than the one standing, and the one standing is better than the one walking, and the one walking is better than the one running towards it.'" He said, "What do you think if someone enters my house and stretches out his hand against me to kill me?" He said, "Be like the son of Adam."

Al-Tirmidhi likewise related this from Qutaybah ibn Sa'id, and said: This hadith is hasan (good), and there are corroborating reports on this subject from Abu Hurayrah, Khabbab ibn al-Aratt, Abu Bakrah, Ibn Mas'ud, Abu Waqid, Abu Musa, and Kharashah. Some related it from al-Layth ibn Sa'd with an added man in the chain.

Al-Hafiz Ibn Asakir said: The man is Husayn al-Ashja'i.

I say: Abu Dawud related it through his chain, saying: [chain] Husayn ibn Abd al-Rahman al-Ashja'i heard Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas narrate from the Prophet ﷺ this hadith. He said: I said, "O Messenger of God, what do you think if someone enters my house and stretches out his hand to kill me?" The Messenger of God ﷺ said, "Be like the son of Adam," and Yazid recited: {If you stretch out your hand against me to kill me, I will not stretch out my hand against you to kill you. Indeed, I fear God, the Lord of all the worlds.}

Ayyub al-Sakhtiyani said: The first of this community to act upon this verse, {If you stretch out your hand against me to kill me, I will not stretch out my hand against you to kill you. Indeed, I fear God, the Lord of all the worlds}, was Uthman ibn Affan, may God be pleased with him. Ibn Abi Hatim related this.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Abu Dharr said: The Prophet ﷺ rode a donkey and seated me behind him, and said, "O Abu Dharr, what do you think if a severe famine were to strike the people such that you could not rise from your bed to your mosque, what would you do?" He said, "God and His Messenger know best." He said, "Restrain yourself." He said, "O Abu Dharr, what do you think if a severe death were to strike the people, such that a house would be filled with a single slave," meaning the grave, "what would you do?" I said, "God and His Messenger know best." He said, "Be patient." He said, "O Abu Dharr, what do you think if people were to kill one another," meaning until the stones of al-Zayt were drowned in blood, "what would you do?" He said, "God and His Messenger know best." He said, "Sit in your house and close your door upon yourself." He said, "What if I am not left alone?" He said, "Then go to those to whom you belong and be among them." He said, "Shall I take up my weapon?" He said, "Then you would be sharing with them in what they are engaged in. But if you fear that the gleam of the sword might terrify you, then throw the edge of your garment over your face, so that he returns with his sin and yours."

Muslim and the compilers of the Sunan, other than al-Nasa'i, related it through various chains from Abu Imran al-Jawni, from Abd Allah ibn al-Samit, in this wording. Abu Dawud and Ibn Majah related it through the route of Hammad ibn Zayd, from Abu Imran, from al-Mush'ath ibn Tarif, from Abd Allah ibn al-Samit, from Abu Dharr, similarly.

Abu Dawud said: no one but Hammad ibn Zayd mentioned al-Mush'ath in this hadith.

Ibn Mardawayh said: [chain] Rib'i said: we were at the funeral of Hudhayfah, and I heard a man say, I heard this man say concerning some people, among what I heard from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ: "If you fight one another, I will surely look to the farthest room in my house and enter it, and if so-and-so comes in upon me, I will surely say: take upon yourself my sin and your sin, so that I may be like the better of the two sons of Adam."

Concerning His saying, {Indeed I wish that you bear my sin and your sin and be among the companions of the Fire, and that is the recompense of the wrongdoers}: Ibn Abbas, Mujahid, al-Dahhak, Qatadah, and al-Suddi said, regarding His saying {Indeed I wish that you bear my sin and your sin}: meaning, the sin of killing me, and your sin that was already upon you before that.

Ibn Jarir said: others said that this means: I wish that you bear my wrongdoing and carry its burden, along with your sin in killing me. This is a saying I found attributed to Mujahid, but I fear it may be a mistake, because the sound narration from him is the opposite of it. That is, what Sufyan al-Thawri related from Mansur, from Mujahid: {Indeed I wish that you bear my sin} he said, by your killing me, {and your sin} he said, by what was from you before that.

Isa likewise related from Ibn Abi Najih, from Mujahid, the same. Shibl related from Ibn Abi Najih, from Mujahid: {Indeed I wish that you bear my sin and your sin}, meaning: I wish that my wrongdoing and my blood be upon you, so that you bear both of them together.

I say: many people fall into this misunderstanding, and mention in this regard a hadith that has no basis: "The killer leaves the victim with no sin remaining upon him."

The hafiz Abu Bakr al-Bazzar related a hadith resembling this, but it is not the same: [chain] Aishah said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Killing by patient endurance does not pass over any sin without erasing it."

This chain for this wording is not sound, and even if it were sound, its meaning would be that Allah expiates the sins of the one killed through the pain of being killed, not that these sins are transferred onto the killer. However, this may occur, and often does, in the case of certain individuals, for the one killed will demand redress from the killer on the plains of the Resurrection, and from the killer's good deeds an amount equal to the wrong done will be taken for him. If these are exhausted and his right is not fully satisfied, some of the sins of the one killed will be taken and cast upon the killer, so that it may happen that no sin remains on the one killed, all of it having been placed upon the killer. This is confirmed by an authentic hadith from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ concerning all wrongs, and killing is among the greatest and most severe of them. And Allah knows best.

As for Ibn Jarir, he said: the correct view in this matter is that its meaning is: I wish that you go away bearing your own wrongdoing in killing me, and that is the meaning of His saying {Indeed I wish that you bear my sin}. As for the meaning of {and your sin}, it is his sin apart from the killing, that is, his disobedience to Allah, mighty and majestic, in other deeds besides this one.

We have said that this is correct because the people of tafsir agree upon it, and because Allah, mighty and majestic, informed us that every doer bears the recompense of his deed, for or against himself. Since this is His ruling concerning His creation, it is not permissible that the sins of the one killed be taken and placed upon this killer; rather, the killer is held accountable only for his own sin in the unlawful killing and for the rest of the sins of disobedience which he himself committed, not for what his victim committed.

This is his wording. He then raised a question, the gist of which is: how did Habil wish that the sin of killing him, along with his own sin, be placed upon his brother Qabil, given that killing him was forbidden? He answered, in substance, that Habil was informing about himself, that he would not fight his brother even if his brother fought him, but would restrain his hand from him, desiring that if a killing occurred, it be attributed to his brother and not to himself.

I say: this speech contains an admonition for him, had he taken heed, and a deterrent for him, had he been deterred. Hence He said: {Indeed I wish that you bear my sin and your sin}, that is, you carry my sin and your sin, {and be among the companions of the Fire, and that is the recompense of the wrongdoers}.

Ibn Abbas said: he warned him of the Fire, but he did not desist and was not deterred.

Concerning His saying, Most High: {But his soul made it attractive for him to kill his brother, so he killed him, and became among the losers}: meaning, his soul made it seem pleasant and enticing to him, and emboldened him to kill his brother, so he killed him, that is, after this admonition and this deterrent.

It was mentioned earlier, in the narration from Abu Ja'far al-Baqir, that is, Muhammad ibn Ali ibn al-Husayn, that he killed him with an iron implement in his hand.

Al-Suddi related, from Abu Malik and from Abu Salih, from Ibn Abbas, and from Murrah, from Abd Allah, and from some of the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ, concerning {But his soul made it attractive for him to kill his brother}: he sought him out to kill him, and the young man fled from him to the mountaintops. One day he came upon him while he was tending his sheep, asleep, so he lifted a rock and crushed his head with it, and he died, and he left him lying exposed on the ground. Ibn Jarir related it.

It is reported from some of the People of the Book that he killed him by strangling and biting, as beasts of prey kill. Ibn Jarir said: when he wished to kill him, he began twisting his neck, whereupon Iblis took an animal and placed its head on a stone, then took another stone and struck its head with it until he killed it, while the son of Adam was watching, and he then did the same to his brother. Ibn Abi Hatim related it.

Abd Allah ibn Wahb related, from Abd al-Rahman ibn Zayd ibn Aslam, from his father, who said: he took hold of his head to kill him, and lay him down, and began pressing his head and bones, not knowing how to kill him. Then Iblis came to him and said: do you wish to kill him? He said: yes. He said: take this rock and throw it upon his head. So he took it and threw it upon him, crushing his head. Then Iblis went hurriedly to Hawwa' (Eve) and said: O Hawwa', Qabil has killed Habil. She said to him: woe to you, what is this killing? He said: he does not eat, does not drink, and does not move. She said: that is death. He said: yes, it is death. She then began screaming, until Adam came in upon her while she was screaming, and he said: what is wrong with you? But she did not speak to him. He returned to her twice, and she did not speak to him. So he said: upon you be the screaming, and upon your daughters, but I and my sons are free of it. Ibn Abi Hatim related it.

Concerning His saying, {and became among the losers}: that is, in this world and the Hereafter, and what loss could be greater than this? Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Abd Allah ibn Mas'ud said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "No soul is killed wrongfully but a share of its blood falls upon the first son of Adam, because he was the first to introduce killing."

The group of hadith compilers, other than Abu Dawud, related it through various chains from al-A'mash, in this wording.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Mujahid said: one of the killer's legs was tied by its shin to its thigh, from that day until the Day of Resurrection, and his face is turned toward the sun wherever it moves, and over him in summer is an enclosure of fire, and over him in winter an enclosure of snow. He said: Abd Allah ibn Amr said: we indeed find that the son of Adam who is the killer shares with the people of the Fire an exact portion of punishment, having upon him half of their punishment.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Abd Allah ibn Amr used to say: the most wretched person among the people of the Fire is the son of Adam who killed his brother, for no blood has been shed on the earth, from the time he killed his brother until the Day of Resurrection, but some of its evil attaches to him, because he was the first to introduce killing.

Ibrahim al-Nakha'i said: no one is killed wrongfully but a share of it falls upon the first son of Adam and upon Satan.

Ibn Jarir also related this.

And His statement, exalted is He: {So Allah sent a crow which scratched in the ground to show him how he might cover the corpse of his brother. He said: "Woe to me! Am I unable to be like this crow and cover the corpse of my brother?" So he became one of those who regret.}

Al-Suddi said, with his previously mentioned chain going back to the Companions: when the young man died, his brother left him lying out in the open, not knowing how to bury him. So Allah sent two crows, brothers to each other, and they fought and one of them killed the other. Then it dug for its companion and heaped earth over him. When he saw that, he said: {He said: "Woe to me! Am I unable to be like this crow and cover the corpse of my brother?"}

Ali ibn Abi Talha, from Ibn Abbas, said: a crow came upon a dead crow and scratched dirt over it until it had covered it. Then the one who had killed his brother said: {He said: "Woe to me! Am I unable to be like this crow and cover the corpse of my brother?"}

Al-Dahhak, from Ibn Abbas, said: he continued carrying his brother in a sack on his shoulder for a year, until Allah sent the two crows, and he saw them scratching, and he said: {"Am I unable to be like this crow?"} So he buried his brother.

Layth ibn Abi Sulaym, from Mujahid, said: he carried him dead upon his shoulder for a hundred years, not knowing what to do with him, carrying him and setting him down on the ground, until he saw the crow burying the crow. Then he said: {"Woe to me! Am I unable to be like this crow and cover the corpse of my brother? So he became one of those who regret."} This was narrated by Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim.

Atiyyah al-Awfi said: when he killed him he felt regret, so he held him close until he began to smell, and the birds and beasts of prey gathered around, waiting for the moment he would cast him away so that they could eat him. Narrated by Ibn Jarir.

Muhammad ibn Ishaq said, from some of the people of knowledge of the earlier Scripture: when he killed him, he was overcome with dismay, and did not know how to cover him. This was because, as they claim, he was the first person killed among the children of Adam and the first dead person. {So Allah sent a crow which scratched in the ground to show him how he might cover the corpse of his brother. He said: "Woe to me! Am I unable to be like this crow and cover the corpse of my brother?" So he became one of those who regret.} He said: the people of the Torah claim that when Qayn (Cain) killed his brother Habil (Abel), Allah, mighty and majestic is He, said to him: "O Qayn, where is your brother Habil?" He said: "I do not know, I was not a watcher over him." Then Allah said: "The voice of your brother's blood calls out to Me from the earth, and now you are cursed from the earth which opened its mouth and swallowed your brother's blood from your hand. If you work the earth, it will never again give you its harvest, until you become one who wanders in fear, lost upon the earth."

And His statement: {So he became one of those who regret} Al-Hasan al-Basri said: Allah heaped regret upon him after loss.

These are the sayings of the commentators regarding this story, and they all agree that these two were the sons of Adam (peace be upon him) by direct descent, as is apparent from the Qur'an, and as is stated in the hadith in the words: "No soul is killed unjustly except that a share of its blood falls upon the first son of Adam, because he was the first to set the practice of killing." This is clear and evident. But Ibn Jarir said:

[chain] Ibn Wakee, from Sahl ibn Yusuf, from Amr, from al-Hasan, meaning al-Basri, who said: the two men mentioned in the Qur'an, those whom Allah spoke of: {And relate to them the story of the two sons of Adam in truth}, were from the Children of Israel, and were not the sons of Adam by direct descent. Rather, the offering (qurban) was practiced among the Children of Israel, and Adam was the first to die. This is extremely gharib (singular/rare), and there is doubt regarding its chain.

Abd al-Razzaq said, from Ma'mar, from al-Hasan, who said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The two sons of Adam, upon him be peace, were set forth as an example for this nation, so take the good from the two of them."

Ibn al-Mubarak narrated it from Asim al-Ahwal, from al-Hasan, who said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Allah has set forth for you the two sons of Adam as an example, so take from their good and leave the evil."

Bakr ibn Abdullah al-Muzani likewise transmitted this hadith as a mursal (report with a missing link at the end). Ibn Jarir narrated all of this.

Salim ibn Abi al-Ja'd said: when the son of Adam killed his brother, Adam remained grieving for a hundred years, not laughing, until it was said to him: "May Allah greet you and make you laugh," meaning, may He make you laugh again.

Narrated by Ibn Jarir, who then said: [chain] Ibn Humayd, from Salamah, from Ghiyath ibn Ibrahim, from Abu Ishaq al-Hamdani, who said: Ali ibn Abi Talib said: when the son of Adam killed his brother, Adam wept for him and said:

"The lands have changed, and those upon them, and the color of the earth has become dusty and ugly.

Everything of color and taste has changed, and the brightness of the fair face has diminished."

Then Adam, upon him be peace, was answered:

"O father of Habil, both have been slain together, and the living one has become like the slaughtered dead.

He came in a state he had feared, and came crying out."

It appears that Qabil (Cain) was punished swiftly, as Mujahid ibn Jabr mentioned, that his leg was attached to his thigh on the day he killed his brother, and Allah made his face follow the sun wherever it turned, as a punishment and disgrace for him. It has been related in a hadith from the Prophet ﷺ that he said: "There is no sin more deserving that Allah hasten its punishment in this world, along with what He stores up for its doer in the Hereafter, than tyranny and severing family ties." Both of these came together in the deed of Qabil. Truly to Allah we belong and to Him we return.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى مُبَيِّنًا وَخِيمَ عَاقِبَةِ الْبَغْيِ وَالْحَسَدِ وَالظُّلْمِ فِي خَبَرِ ابْنَيْ آدَمَ لِصُلْبِهِ -فِي قَوْلِ الْجُمْهُورِ-وَهُمَا هَابِيلُ وَقَابِيلُ كَيْفَ عَدَا أَحَدُهُمَا عَلَى الْآخَرِ، فَقَتَلَهُ بَغْيًا عَلَيْهِ وَحَسَدًا لَهُ، فِيمَا وَهَبَهُ اللَّهُ مِنَ النِّعْمَةِ وتَقَبّل الْقُرْبَانِ الَّذِي أَخْلَصَ فِيهِ لِلَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، فَفَازَ الْمَقْتُولُ بِوَضْعِ الْآثَامِ وَالدُّخُولِ إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ، وَخَابَ الْقَاتِلُ وَرَجَعَ بِالصَّفْقَةِ الْخَاسِرَةِ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ، فَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَاتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ ابْنَيْ آدَمَ بِالْحَقِّ﴾ أَيْ: وَاقْصُصْ عَلَى هَؤُلَاءِ الْبُغَاةِ الْحَسَدَةِ، إِخْوَانِ الْخَنَازِيرِ وَالْقِرَدَةِ مِنَ الْيَهُودِ وَأَمْثَالِهِمْ وَأَشْبَاهِهِمْ-خَبَرَ ابْنَيْ آدَمَ، وَهُمَا هَابِيلُ وَقَابِيلُ فِيمَا ذَكَرَهُ غَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ مِنَ السَّلَفِ وَالْخَلْفِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿بِالْحَقِّ﴾ أَيْ: عَلَى الْجَلِيَّةِ وَالْأَمْرِ الَّذِي لَا لَبْسَ فِيهِ وَلَا كَذِبَ، وَلَا وَهْم وَلَا تَبْدِيلَ، وَلَا زِيَادَةَ وَلَا نُقْصَانَ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿إِنَّ هَذَا لَهُوَ الْقَصَصُ الْحَقّ﴾ [آلِ عِمْرَانَ:٦٢] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿نَحْنُ نَقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ نَبَأَهُمْ بِالْحَقِّ﴾ [الْكَهْفِ:١٣] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿ذَلِكَ عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ قَوْلَ الْحَقِّ الَّذِي فِيهِ يَمْتَرُونَ] ﴾ [مَرْيَمَ: ٣٤]

وَكَانَ مِنْ خَبَرِهِمَا فِيمَا ذَكَرَهُ غَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ مِنَ السَّلَفِ وَالْخَلَفِ، أَنَّ اللَّهَ تَعَالَى قَدْ شَرَعَ لِآدَمَ، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، أَنْ يُزَوِّجَ بَنَاتِهِ مِنْ بَنِيهِ لِضَرُورَةِ الْحَالِ، وَلَكِنْ قَالُوا: كَانَ يُولَد لَهُ فِي كُلِّ بَطْنِ ذَكَرٌ وَأُنْثَى، فَكَانَ يُزَوِّجُ أُنْثَى هَذَا الْبَطْنِ لِذِكْرِ الْبَطْنِ الْآخَرِ، وَكَانَتْ أُخْتُ هَابِيلَ دَميمةً، وَأُخْتُ قَابِيلَ وَضِيئَةً، فَأَرَادَ أَنْ يَسْتَأْثِرَ بِهَا عَلَى أَخِيهِ، فَأَبَى آدَمُ ذَلِكَ إِلَّا أَنْ يُقَرِّبَا قُرْبَانًا، فَمَنْ تُقُبِّلَ مِنْهُ فَهِيَ لَهُ، فَقَرَّبَا فَتُقُبِّل مِنْ هَابِيلَ وَلَمْ يتَقَبَّل مِنْ قَابِيلَ، فَكَانَ مِنْ أَمْرِهِمَا مَا قَصَّ اللَّهُ فِي كِتَابِهِ.

ذِكْرُ أَقْوَالِ الْمُفَسِّرِينَ هَاهُنَا:

قَالَ السُّدي -فِيمَا ذَكَرَ-عَنْ أَبِي مَالِكٍ، وَعَنْ أَبِي صَالِحٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ -وَعَنْ مُرَّة، عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ-وَعَنْ نَاسٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ؛ أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يُولَدُ لِآدَمَ مَوْلُودٌ إِلَّا وَلِدَ مَعَهُ جَارِيَةٌ، فَكَانَ يُزَوِّجُ غُلَامَ هَذَا الْبَطْنِ جَارِيَةَ هَذَا الْبَطْنِ الْآخَرِ، وَيُزَوِّجُ جَارِيَةَ هَذَا الْبَطْنِ غُلَامَ هَذَا الْبَطْنِ الْآخَرِ، حَتَّى وُلِدَ لَهُ ابْنَانِ يُقَالُ لَهُمَا: قَابِيلُ وَهَابِيلُ وَكَانَ قَابِيلُ صَاحِبَ زَرْعٍ، وَكَانَ هَابِيلُ صَاحِبَ ضَرع، وَكَانَ قَابِيلُ أَكْبَرَهُمَا، وَكَانَ لَهُ أُخْتٌ أَحْسَنَ مِنْ أُخْتِ هَابِيلَ، وَإِنَّ هَابِيلَ طَلَبَ أَنْ يَنْكِحَ أُخْتَ قَابِيلَ، فَأَبَى عَلَيْهِ وَقَالَ: هِيَ أُخْتِي، وُلِدَتْ مَعِي، وَهِيَ أَحْسَنُ مِنْ أُخْتِكَ، وَأَنَا أَحَقُّ أَنْ أَتَزَوَّجَ بِهَا. فَأَمَرَهُ أَبُوهُ أَنْ يُزَوِّجَهَا هَابِيلَ، فَأَبَى، وَأَنَّهُمَا قَرَّبَا قُرْبَانًا إِلَى اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ أَيُّهُمَا أَحَقُّ بِالْجَارِيَةِ، وَكَانَ آدَمُ، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، قَدْ غَابَ عَنْهُمَا، أَتَى مَكَّةَ يَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهَا، قَالَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: هَلْ تَعْلَمُ أَنَّ لِي بَيْتًا فِي الْأَرْضِ؟ قَالَ: اللَّهُمَّ لَا قَالَ: إِنَّ لِي بَيْتًا فِي مَكَّةَ فَأْتِهِ. فَقَالَ آدَمُ لِلسَّمَاءِ: احْفَظِي وَلَدِي بِالْأَمَانَةِ، فَأَبَتْ. وَقَالَ لِلْأَرْضِ، فَأَبَتْ. وَقَالَ لِلْجِبَالِ، فَأَبَتْ. فَقَالَ لِقَابِيلَ، فَقَالَ: نَعَمْ، تَذْهَبُ وَتَرْجِعُ وَتَجِدُ أَهْلَكَ كَمَا يَسُرُّكَ فَلَمَّا انْطَلَقَ آدَمُ قَربا قُرْبَانًا، وَكَانَ قَابِيلُ يَفْخَرُ عَلَيْهِ، فَقَالَ: أَنَا أَحَقُّ بِهَا مِنْكَ، هِيَ أُخْتِي، وَأَنَا أَكْبَرُ مِنْكَ، وَأَنَا وَصِيُّ وَالِدِي. فَلَمَّا قَربا، قَرَّبَ هَابِيلُ جَذعَة سِمْنَةً، وَقَرَّبَ قَابِيلُ حَزْمَة سُنْبُلٍ، فَوَجَدَ فِيهَا سُنْبُلَةً عَظِيمَةً، فَفَرَكَهَا فَأَكَلَهَا. فَنَزَلَتِ النَّارُ فَأَكَلَتْ قُرْبَانَ هَابِيلَ، وَتَرَكَتْ قُرْبَانَ قَابِيلَ، فَغَضِبَ وَقَالَ: لِأَقْتُلُنَّكَ حَتَّى لَا تَنْكِحَ أُخْتِي. فَقَالَ هَابِيلُ: إِنَّمَا يَتَقَبَّلُ اللَّهُ من المتقين. رواه ابن جرير. وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الصَّبَّاحِ، حَدَّثَنَا حَجَّاجٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، أَخْبَرَنِي ابْنُ خُثَيْم قَالَ: أَقْبَلْتُ مَعَ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ فَحَدَّثَنِي عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: نُهِيَ أَنْ تَنْكِحَ الْمَرْأَةُ أَخَاهَا تَوْأمها، وَأُمِرَ أَنْ يَنْكِحَهَا غَيْرُهُ مِنْ إِخْوَتِهَا، وَكَانَ يُولَدُ لَهُ فِي كُلِّ بَطْنٍ رَجُلٌ وَامْرَأَةٌ، فَبَيْنَمَا هُمْ كَذَلِكَ وُلِدَ لَهُ امْرَأَةٌ وَضِيئَةٌ، وَوُلِدَ لَهُ أُخْرَى قَبِيحَةٌ دَمِيمَةٌ، فَقَالَ أَخُو الدَّمِيمَةِ: أَنْكِحْنِي أُخْتَكَ وَأُنْكِحُكَ أُخْتِي. قَالَ: لَا أَنَا أَحَقُّ بِأُخْتِي فَقَرَّبَا قُرْبَانًا، فَتُقُبِّلَ مَنْ صَاحِبِ الْكَبْشِ، وَلَمْ يُتَقَبَّلْ مِنْ صَاحِبِ الزَّرْعِ، فَقَتَلَهُ. إِسْنَادٌ جَيِّدٌ.

وَحَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَلَمَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ خُثَيْم، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَوْلُهُ ﴿إِذْ قَرَّبَا قُرْبَانًا﴾ فَقَرَّبَا قُرْبَانَهُمَا، فَجَاءَ صَاحِبُ الْغَنَمِ بِكَبْشٍ أعْين أَقْرَنَ أَبْيَضَ، وَصَاحِبُ الْحَرْثِ بصَبرة مِنْ طَعَامٍ، فَقَبِلَ اللَّهُ الْكَبْشَ فَخَزَنَهُ فِي الْجَنَّةِ أَرْبَعِينَ خَرِيفًا، وَهُوَ الْكَبْشُ الَّذِي ذَبَحَهُ إِبْرَاهِيمُ ﷺ إِسْنَادٌ جَيِّدٌ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ بَشَّار، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، حَدَّثَنَا عَوْف، عَنْ أَبِي الْمُغِيرَةِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو قَالَ: إِنَّ ابْنَيْ آدَمَ اللَّذَيْنِ قَرَّبَا قُرْبَانًا فَتُقَبِّلَ مِنْ أَحَدِهِمَا وَلَمْ يُتَقَبَّلْ مِنَ الْآخَرِ، كَانَ أَحَدُهُمَا صَاحِبَ حَرْثٍ وَالْآخِرُ صَاحِبَ غَنَمٍ، وَإِنَّهُمَا أُمِرَا أَنْ يُقَرِّبَا قُرْبَانًا، وَإِنَّ صَاحِبَ الْغَنَمِ قَرَّبَ أَكْرَمَ غَنَمِهِ وأسمنَها وَأَحْسَنَهَا، طَيِّبَةً بِهَا نَفْسُهُ، وَإِنَّ صَاحِبَ الْحَرْثِ قَرَّبَ أشَرَّ حَرْثِهِ الْكَوْدَنِ والزُّوان غَيْرَ طَيِّبَةٍ بِهَا نَفْسُهُ، وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، تَقَبَّلَ قُرْبَانَ صَاحِبِ الْغَنَمِ، وَلَمْ يَتَقَبَّلْ قُرْبَانَ صَاحِبِ الْحَرْثِ، وَكَانَ مِنْ قِصَّتِهِمَا مَا قَصَّ اللَّهُ فِي كِتَابِهِ، قَالَ: وَايْمُ اللَّهِ، إِنْ كَانَ الْمَقْتُولُ لَأَشَدَّ الرَّجُلَيْنِ، وَلَكِنْ مَنْعَهُ التَّحَرُّجُ أَنْ يَبْسُطَ [يَدَهُ] إِلَى أَخِيهِ.

وَقَالَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ رَافِعٍ الْمَدَنِيُّ الْقَاصُّ: بَلَغَنِي أَنَّ ابْنَيْ آدَمَ لَمَّا أُمِرَا بِالْقُرْبَانِ، كَانَ أَحَدُهُمَا صَاحِبَ غَنَم، وَكَانَ أنْتج لَهُ حَمَل فِي غَنَمِهِ، فَأَحَبَّهُ حَتَّى كَانَ يُؤْثِرُهُ بِاللَّيْلِ، وَكَانَ يَحْمِلُهُ عَلَى ظَهْرِهِ مِنْ حُبِّهِ، حَتَّى لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ مَالٌ أَحَبَّ إِلَيْهِ مِنْهُ. فَلَمَّا أُمِرَ بِالْقُرْبَانِ قَرَّبَهُ لِلَّهِ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، فَقَبِلَهُ اللَّهُ مِنْهُ، فَمَا زَالَ يَرْتَعُ فِي الْجَنَّةِ حَتَّى فَدى بِهِ ابْنَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا الْأَنْصَارِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا الْقَاسِمُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ قَالَ: قَالَ آدَمُ، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، لِهَابِيلَ وَقَابِيلَ: إِنَّ رَبِّي عَهِدَ إِلَيَّ أَنَّهُ كَائِنٌ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِي مَنْ يُقَرِّب الْقُرْبَانَ، فَقَرِّبَا قُرْبَانًا حَتَّى تَقَر عَيْنِي إِذَا تُقُبّل قُرْبَانُكُمَا، فَقَرَّبَا. وَكَانَ هَابِيلُ صَاحِبَ غَنَمٍ فَقَرَّبَ أكُولة غَنَمِهِ، خَيْر مَالِهِ، وَكَانَ قَابِيلُ صَاحِبَ زَرْعٍ، فَقَرَّبَ مَشَاقَّةً مِنْ زَرْعِهِ، فَانْطَلَقَ آدَمُ مَعَهُمَا، وَمَعَهُمَا قُرْبَانُهُمَا، فَصَعِدَا الْجَبَلَ فَوَضَعَا قُرْبَانَهُمَا، ثُمَّ جَلَسُوا ثَلَاثَتُهُمْ: آدَمُ وَهُمَا، يَنْظُرَانِ إِلَى الْقُرْبَانِ، فَبَعَثَ اللَّهُ نَارًا حَتَّى إِذَا كَانَتْ فَوْقَهُمَا دَنَا مِنْهَا عُنُقٌ، فَاحْتَمَلَ قُرْبَانَ هَابِيلَ وَتَرَكَ قُرْبَانَ قَابِيلَ، فَانْصَرَفُوا. وَعَلِمَ آدَمُ أَنَّ قَابِيلَ مَسْخُوطٌ عَلَيْهِ، فَقَالَ: وَيْلَكَ يَا قَابِيلُ رُدَّ عَلَيْكَ قُرْبَانُكَ. فَقَالَ قَابِيلُ: أحببتَه فصليتَ عَلَى قُرْبَانِهِ وَدَعَوْتَ لَهُ فتُقُبل قُرْبَانُهُ، وَرُدَّ عليَّ قرباني. وقال قابيل لهابيل: لأقتلنك فَأَسْتَرِيحُ مِنْكَ، دَعَا لَكَ أَبُوكَ فَصَلَّى عَلَى قُرْبَانِكَ، فَتُقُبِّلَ مِنْكَ. وَكَانَ يَتَوَاعَدُهُ بِالْقَتْلِ، إِلَى أَنِ احْتَبَسَ هَابِيلَ ذَاتَ عَشِيَّةٍ فِي غَنَمِهِ، فَقَالَ آدَمُ: يَا قَابِيلُ، أَيْنَ أَخُوكَ؟ [قَالَ] قَالَ: وبَعثتني لَهُ رَاعِيًا؟ لَا أَدْرِي. فَقَالَ [لَهُ] آدَمُ: وَيْلَكَ يَا قَابِيلُ. انْطَلِقْ فَاطْلُبْ أَخَاكَ. فَقَالَ قَابِيلُ فِي نَفْسِهِ: اللَّيْلَةَ أَقْتُلُهُ. وَأَخَذَ مَعَهُ حَدِيدَةً فَاسْتَقْبَلَهُ وَهُوَ مُنْقَلِبٌ، فَقَالَ: يَا هَابِيلُ، تُقُبِّلَ قُرْبَانُكَ وَرُدَّ عَلَيَّ قُرْبَانِي، لَأَقْتُلَنَّكَ. فَقَالَ هَابِيلُ: قربتُ أَطْيَبَ مَالِي، وقربتَ أَنْتَ أَخْبَثَ مَالِكَ، وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَقْبَلُ إِلَّا الطَّيِّبَ، إِنَّمَا يَتَقَبَّلُ اللَّهُ مِنَ الْمُتَّقِينَ، فَلَمَّا قَالَهَا غَضِبَ قَابِيلُ فَرَفَعَ الْحَدِيدَةَ وَضَرَبَهُ بِهَا، فَقَالَ: وَيْلَكَ يَا قَابِيلُ أَيْنَ أَنْتَ مِنَ اللَّهِ؟ كَيْفَ يَجْزِيكَ بِعَمَلِكَ؟ فَقَتَلَهُ فَطَرَحَهُ فِي جَوْبة مِنَ الْأَرْضِ، وَحَثَى عَلَيْهِ شَيْئًا مِنَ التُّرَابِ. 

وَقَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ بَعْضِ أَهْلِ الْعِلْمِ بِالْكِتَابِ الْأَوَّلِ: إِنَّ آدَمَ أَمَرَ ابْنَهُ قَيْنًا أَنْ يَنْكِحَ أُخْتَهُ تَوأمة هَابِيلَ، وَأَمَرَ هَابِيلَ أَنْ يَنْكِحَ أُخْتَهُ تَوْأَمَةَ قَيْنٍ، فَسَلَّمَ لِذَلِكَ هَابِيلُ وَرَضِيَ، وَأَبَى ذَلِكَ قَيْنٌ وَكَرِهَ، تَكَرُّمًا عَنْ أُخْتِ هَابِيلَ، وَرَغِبَ بِأُخْتِهِ عَنْ هَابِيلَ، وَقَالَ: نَحْنُ وِلَادَةُ الْجَنَّةِ، وَهُمَا مِنْ وِلَادَةِ الْأَرْضِ، وَأَنَا أَحَقُّ بِأُخْتِي -وَيَقُولُ بَعْضُ أَهْلِ الْعِلْمِ بِالْكِتَابِ الْأَوَّلِ: كَانَتْ أُخْتُ قَيْنٍ مِنْ أَحْسَنِ النَّاسِ، فَضَنّ بِهَا عَنْ أَخِيهِ وَأَرَادَهَا لِنَفْسِهِ، فَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ أَيَّ ذَلِكَ كَانَ-فَقَالَ لَهُ أَبُوهُ: يَا بُنَيَّ، إِنَّهَا لَا تَحِلُّ لَكَ، فَأَبَى قَابِيلُ أَنْ يَقْبَلَ ذَلِكَ مِنْ قَوْلِ أَبِيهِ. فَقَالَ لَهُ أَبُوهُ: يَا بُنَيَّ، قَرِّبْ قُرْبَانًا، وَيُقَرِّبْ أَخُوكَ هَابِيلُ قُرْبَانًا، فَأَيُّكُمَا تُقُبِّل قُرْبَانُهُ فَهُوَ أَحَقُّ بِهَا، وَكَانَ قَيْنٌ عَلَى بَذْر الْأَرْضِ، وَكَانَ هَابِيلُ عَلَى رِعَايَةِ الْمَاشِيَةِ، فَقَرَّبَ قَيْنٌ قَمْحًا، وَقَرَّبَ هَابِيلُ أَبْكَارًا مِنْ أَبْكَارِ غَنَمِهِ -وَبَعْضُهُمْ يَقُولُ: قَرَّبَ بَقَرَةً-فَأَرْسَلَ اللَّهُ نَارًا بَيْضَاءَ، فَأَكَلَتْ قُرْبَانَ هَابِيلَ، وَتَرَكَتْ قُرْبَانَ قَيْنٍ، وَبِذَلِكَ كَانَ يُقْبَل الْقُرْبَانُ إِذَا قَبِلَهُ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ.

وَقَالَ العَوْفِيُّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: كَانَ مِنْ شَأْنِهِمَا أَنَّهُ لَمْ يَكُنْ مِسْكِينٌ يُتَصَدّق عَلَيْهِ، وَإِنَّمَا كَانَ الْقُرْبَانُ يُقَرِّبُهُ الرَّجُلُ. فَبَيْنَا ابْنَا آدَمَ قَاعِدَانِ إِذْ قَالَا لَوْ قَرَّبْنَا قُرْبَانًا وَكَانَ الرَّجُلُ إِذَا قَرَّبَ قُرْبَانًا فَرَضِيَهُ اللَّهُ، أَرْسَلَ إِلَيْهِ نَارًا فَتَأْكُلَهُ وَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ رَضِيَهُ اللَّهُ خَبَت النَّارُ، فَقَرَّبَا قُرْبَانًا، وَكَانَ أَحَدُهُمَا رَاعِيًا، وَكَانَ الْآخَرُ حَرّاثا، وَإِنَّ صَاحِبَ الْغَنَمِ قَرَّبَ خَيْرَ غَنِمِهِ وَأَسْمَنَهَا، وَقَرَّبَ الْآخَرُ بَعْضَ زَرْعِهِ، فَجَاءَتِ النَّارُ فَنَزَلَتْ بَيْنَهُمَا، فَأَكَلَتِ الشَّاةَ وَتَرَكَتِ الزَّرْعَ، وَإِنَّ ابْنَ آدَمَ قَالَ لِأَخِيهِ: أَتَمْشِي فِي النَّاسِ وَقَدْ عَلِمُوا أَنَّكَ قَرّبت قُرْبَانًا فَتُقُبِّل مِنْكَ وَرُدّ عليَّ؟ فَلَا وَاللَّهِ لَا يَنْظُرُ النَّاسُ إِلَيْكَ وإليَّ وأنت خَيْرٌ مِنِّي. فَقَالَ: لَأَقْتُلَنَّكَ. فَقَالَ لَهُ أَخُوهُ: مَا ذَنْبِي؟ إِنَّمَا يَتَقَبَّلُ اللَّهُ مِنَ الْمُتَّقِينَ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ.

فَهَذَا الْأَثَرُ يَقْتَضِي أَنَّ تَقْرِيبَ الْقُرْبَانِ كَانَ لَا عَنْ سَبَبٍ وَلَا عَنْ تَدَارُئٍ فِي امْرَأَةٍ، كَمَا تَقَدَّمَ عَنْ جَمَاعَةٍ مَنْ تَقُدُّمِ ذِكْرِهِمْ، وَهُوَ ظَاهِرُ الْقُرْآنِ: ﴿إِذْ قَرَّبَا قُرْبَانًا فَتُقُبِّلَ مِنْ أَحَدِهِمَا وَلَمْ يُتَقَبَّلْ مِنَ الآخَرِ قَالَ لأقْتُلَنَّكَ قَالَ إِنَّمَا يَتَقَبَّلُ اللَّهُ مِنَ الْمُتَّقِينَ﴾ فَالسِّيَاقُ يَقْتَضِي أَنَّهُ إِنَّمَا غَضَبُ عَلَيْهِ وَحَسَدُهُ لِقَبُولِ قُرْبَانِهِ دُونَهُ.

ثُمَّ الْمَشْهُورُ عِنْدَ الْجُمْهُورِ أَنَّ الَّذِي قَرَّبَ الشَّاةَ هُوَ هَابِيلُ، وَأَنَّ الَّذِي قَرَّبَ الطَّعَامَ هُوَ قَابِيلُ، وَأَنَّهُ تُقُبل مِنْ هَابِيلَ شَاتُهُ، حَتَّى قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ وَغَيْرُهُ: إِنَّهُ الْكَبْشُ الَّذِي فُدِيَ بِهِ الذَّبِيحُ، وَهُوَ مُنَاسِبٌ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ، وَلَمْ يُتَقَبَّلْ مِنْ قَابِيلَ. كَذَلِكَ نَصَّ عَلَيْهِ غَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ مِنَ السَّلَفِ وَالْخَلَفِ، وَهُوَ الْمَشْهُورُ عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ أَيْضًا، وَلَكِنْ رَوَى ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ، عَنْهُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: الَّذِي قَرَّبَ الزَّرْعَ قَابِيلُ، وَهُوَ الْمُتَقَبَّلُ مِنْهُ، وَهَذَا خِلَافَ الْمَشْهُورِ، وَلَعَلَّهُ لَمْ يُحْفَظْ عَنْهُ جَيِّدًا وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَمَعْنَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿إِنَّمَا يَتَقَبَّلُ اللَّهُ مِنَ الْمُتَّقِينَ﴾ أَيْ: مِمَّنِ اتَّقَى اللَّهَ فِي فِعْلِهِ ذَلِكَ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ الْعَلَاءِ بْنِ زِبْرِيقٍ، حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ عَيَّاش، حَدَّثَنِي صَفْوان بْنُ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ تَمِيم، يَعْنِي ابْنَ مَالِكٍ الْمَقْرِيَّ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا الدَّرْدَاءِ يَقُولُ: لِأَنْ أَسْتَيْقِنَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ قَدْ تَقَبَّلَ مِنِّي صَلَاةً وَاحِدَةً أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنَ الدُّنْيَا وَمَا فِيهَا، إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَقُولُ: ﴿إِنَّمَا يَتَقَبَّلُ اللَّهُ مِنَ الْمُتَّقِينَ﴾

وَحَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عِمْرَانَ، حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ -يَعْنِي الرَّازِيَّ-عَنِ الْمُغِيرَةِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ، عَنْ مَيْمُونِ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ قَالَ: كُنْتُ جَالِسًا عِنْدَ أَبِي وَائِلٍ، فَدَخَلَ عَلَيْنَا رَجُلٌ -يُقَالُ لَهُ: أَبُو عَفِيفٍ، مِنْ أَصْحَابِ مُعَاذٍ-فَقَالَ لَهُ شَقِيقُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ: يَا أَبَا عَفِيفٍ، أَلَا تُحَدِّثُنَا عَنْ مُعَاذِ بْنِ جَبَلٍ؟ قَالَ: بَلَى، سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ: يُحْبَسُ النَّاسُ فِي بَقِيعٍ وَاحِدٍ، فَيُنَادِي مُنَادٍ: أَيْنَ الْمُتَّقُونَ؟ فَيَقُومُونَ فِي كَنَف مِنَ الرَّحْمَنِ، لَا يَحْتَجِبُ اللَّهُ مِنْهُمْ وَلَا يَسْتَتِرُ. قُلْتُ: مَنِ الْمُتَّقُونَ؟ قَالَ: قَوْمٌ اتَّقَوُا الشِّرْكَ وَعِبَادَةَ الْأَوْثَانِ، وَأَخْلَصُوا الْعِبَادَةَ، فَيَمُرُّونَ إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿لَئِنْ بَسَطْتَ إِلَيَّ يَدَكَ لِتَقْتُلَنِي مَا أَنَا بِبَاسِطٍ يَدِيَ إِلَيْكَ لأقْتُلَكَ إِنِّي أَخَافُ اللَّهَ رَبَّ الْعَالَمِينَ﴾ يَقُولُ لَهُ أَخُوهُ الرَّجُلُ الصَّالِحُ، الَّذِي تَقَبَّلَ اللَّهُ قُرْبَانَهُ لِتَقْوَاهُ حِينَ تَوَاعَدَهُ أَخُوهُ بِالْقَتْلِ عَلَى غَيْرِ مَا ذَنْبٍ مِنْهُ إِلَيْهِ: ﴿لَئِنْ بَسَطْتَ إِلَيَّ يَدَكَ لِتَقْتُلَنِي مَا أَنَا بِبَاسِطٍ يَدِيَ إِلَيْكَ لأقْتُلَكَ﴾ أَيْ: لَا أُقَابِلُكَ عَلَى صَنِيعِكَ الْفَاسِدِ بِمِثْلِهِ، فَأَكُونُ أَنَا وَأَنْتَ سَوَاءٌ فِي الْخَطِيئَةِ، ﴿إِنِّي أَخَافُ اللَّهَ رَبَّ الْعَالَمِينَ﴾ أَيْ: مِنْ أَنْ أَصْنَعَ كَمَا تُرِيدُ أَنَّ تَصْنَعَ، بَلْ أَصْبِرُ وَأَحْتَسِبُ.

قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَمْرٍو: وَايْمُ اللَّهِ، إِنْ كَانَ لَأَشَدَّ الرجلين ولكن منعه التحرج، يعني الورع.

وَلِهَذَا ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: "إِذَا تَوَاجَهَ الْمُسْلِمَانِ بِسَيْفَيْهِمَا، فَالْقَاتِلُ وَالْمَقْتُولُ فِي النَّارِ". قَالُوا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، هَذَا الْقَاتِلُ فَمَا بَالُ الْمَقْتُولِ؟ قَالَ: "إِنَّهُ كَانَ حَرِيصًا عَلَى قَتْلِ صَاحِبِهِ". 

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا لَيْثُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ عَيَّاش بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، عَنْ بُكَيْرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ بُسْر بْنِ سَعِيدٍ ؛ أَنَّ سَعْدَ بْنَ أَبِي وَقَّاصٍ قَالَ عِنْدَ فِتْنَةِ عُثْمَانَ: أَشْهَدُ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم قَالَ: "إِنَّهَا سَتَكُونُ فِتْنَةٌ، الْقَاعِدُ فِيهَا خَيْرٌ مِنَ الْقَائِمِ، وَالْقَائِمُ خَيْرٌ مِنَ الْمَاشِي، وَالْمَاشِي خَيْرٌ مِنَ السَّاعِي". قَالَ: أَفَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ دَخَلَ عَلَيَّ بَيْتِي فَبَسَطَ يَدَهُ إليَّ لِيَقْتُلَنِي قَالَ: "كُنْ كَابْنِ آدَمَ".

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ عَنْ قُتَيْبَة بْنِ سَعِيدٍ وَقَالَ: هَذَا الْحَدِيثُ حَسَنٌ، وَفِي الْبَابِ عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، وخَباب بْنِ الْأَرَتِّ، وَأَبِي بَكْرَة وَابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، وَأَبِي وَاقِدٍ، وَأَبِي مُوسَى، وخَرَشَة. وَرَوَاهُ بَعْضُهُمْ عَنِ اللَّيْثِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ، وَزَادَ فِي الْإِسْنَادِ رَجُلًا.

قَالَ الْحَافِظُ ابْنُ عَسَاكِرَ: الرَّجُلُ هُوَ حُسَيْنٌ الْأَشْجَعِيُّ.

قُلْتُ: وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ مِنْ طَرِيقِهِ فَقَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ الرَّمْلِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا الْمُفَضَّلُ، عَنْ عَيَّاشِ بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ عَنْ بُكَيْر، عَنْ بُسْر بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ حُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْأَشْجَعِيِّ؛ أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ سَعْدَ بْنَ أَبِي وَقَّاصٍ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ فِي هَذَا الْحَدِيثِ قَالَ: فَقُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، أَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ دَخَلَ عَلَيَّ بَيْتِي وَبَسَطَ يَدَهُ لِيَقْتُلَنِي؟ قَالَ: فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "كُنْ كَابْنِ آدَم" وَتَلَا يَزِيدُ: ﴿لَئِنْ بَسَطْتَ إِلَيَّ يَدَكَ لِتَقْتُلَنِي مَا أَنَا بِبَاسِطٍ يَدِيَ إِلَيْكَ لأقْتُلَكَ إِنِّي أَخَافُ اللَّهَ رَبَّ الْعَالَمِينَ﴾ 

قَالَ أَيُّوبُ السَّخْتَياني: إِنَّ أَوَّلَ مَنْ أَخَذَ بِهَذِهِ الْآيَةِ مِنْ هَذِهِ الْأُمَّةِ: ﴿لَئِنْ بَسَطْتَ إِلَيَّ يَدَكَ لِتَقْتُلَنِي مَا أَنَا بِبَاسِطٍ يَدِيَ إِلَيْكَ لأقْتُلَكَ إِنِّي أَخَافُ اللَّهَ رَبَّ الْعَالَمِينَ﴾ لَعُثْمان بْنِ عَفَّانَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ.

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا مَرْحوم، حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو عِمْرَانَ الجَوْني، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الصَّامِتِ، عَنْ أَبِي ذَرٍّ قَالَ: رَكِبَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ حِمَارًا وَأَرْدَفَنِي خَلْفَهُ، وَقَالَ: "يَا أَبَا ذَرٍّ، أَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ أَصَابَ النَّاسَ جوعٌ شَدِيدٌ لَا تَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ تَقُومَ مِنْ فِرَاشِكَ إِلَى مَسْجِدِكَ، كَيْفَ تَصْنَعُ؟ ". قَالَ: قَالَ اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ أَعْلَمُ. قَالَ: "تَعَفَّفْ" قَالَ: "يَا أَبَا ذَرٍّ، أَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ أَصَابَ النَّاسَ موتٌ شَدِيدٌ، وَيَكُونُ الْبَيْتُ فِيهِ بِالْعَبْدِ، يَعْنِي الْقَبْرَ، كَيْفَ تَصْنَعُ؟ " قُلْتُ: اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ أَعْلَمُ. قَالَ: "اصْبِرْ". قَالَ: "يَا أَبَا ذَرٍّ، أَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ قَتَلَ النَّاسُ بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضًا، يَعْنِي حَتَّى تَغْرَقَ حِجَارَةُ الزَّيْتِ مِنَ الدِّمَاءِ، كَيْفَ تَصْنَعُ؟ ". قَالَ: اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ أَعْلَمُ. قَالَ: "اقْعُدْ فِي بَيْتِكَ وَأَغْلِقْ عَلَيْكَ بَابَكَ". قَالَ: فَإِنْ لَمْ أتْرَك؟ قال: "فأت من أنت منهم، فكن فِيهِمْ قَالَ: فَآخُذُ سِلَاحِي؟ قَالَ: "إذًا تُشَارِكُهُمْ فِيمَا هُمْ فِيهِ، وَلَكِنْ إِنْ خَشِيتَ أَنْ يُرَوِّعَكَ شُعَاعُ السَّيْفِ، فَأَلْقِ طَرْفَ رِدَائِكَ عَلَى وَجْهِكَ حَتَّى يَبُوءَ بِإِثْمِهِ وَإِثْمِكَ". 

رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ وَأَهْلُ السُّنَنِ سِوَى النَّسَائِيِّ، مِنْ طُرُقٍ عَنْ أَبِي عِمْرَانَ الْجَوْنَيِّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الصَّامِتِ بِهِ وَرَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ، مِنْ طَرِيقِ حَمَّادِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي عِمْرَانَ، عَنِ المُشَعَّث بْنِ طَرِيفٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الصَّامِتِ عَنْ أَبِي ذَرٍّ بِنَحْوِهِ. 

قَالَ أَبُو دَاوُدَ: وَلَمْ يَذْكُرِ الْمُشَعَّثُ فِي هَذَا الْحَدِيثِ غَيْرَ حَمَّادِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ مَرْدُويَه: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ دُحَيْم، حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ حَازِمٍ، حَدَّثَنَا قَبِيصَة بْنُ عُقْبَة، حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ رِبْعِيّ قَالَ: كُنَّا فِي جِنَازَةِ حُذَيفة، فَسَمِعْتُ رَجُلًا يَقُولُ: سَمِعْتُ هَذَا يَقُولُ فِي نَاسٍ: مِمَّا سَمِعْتُ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "لَئِنِ اقْتَتَلْتُمْ لَأَنْظُرَنَّ إِلَى أَقْصَى بَيْتٍ فِي دَارِي، فلألجنَّه، فَلَئِنْ دَخَلَ عَليّ فُلَانٌ لَأَقُولُنَّ: هَا بُؤْ بِإِثْمِي وَإِثْمِكَ، فَأَكُونُ كَخَيْرِ ابْنَيْ آدَمَ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿إِنِّي أُرِيدُ أَنْ تَبُوءَ بِإِثْمِي وَإِثْمِكَ فَتَكُونَ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّارِ وَذَلِكَ جَزَاءُ الظَّالِمِينَ﴾ قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ، وَمُجَاهِدٌ، وَالضَّحَّاكُ، وَقَتَادَةُ، والسُّدِّي، فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿إِنِّي أُرِيدُ أَنْ تَبُوءَ بِإِثْمِي وَإِثْمِكَ﴾ أَيْ: بِإِثْمِ قَتْلِي وَإِثْمِكَ الَّذِي عَلَيْكَ قَبْلَ ذَلِكَ.

قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: وَقَالَ آخَرُونَ: يَعْنِي ذَلِكَ أَنِّي أُرِيدُ أَنْ تَبُوءَ بِخَطِيئَتِي، فَتَتَحَمَّلَ وِزْرَهَا، وَإِثْمَكَ فِي قَتْلِكَ إِيَّايَ. وَهَذَا قَوْلٌ وَجَدْتُهُ عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، وَأَخْشَى أَنْ يَكُونَ غَلَطًا؛ لِأَنَّ الصَّحِيحَ مِنَ الرِّوَايَةِ عَنْهُ خِلَافُهُ. يَعْنِي: مَا رَوَاهُ سُفْيَانُ الثَّوْرِيُّ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ: ﴿إِنِّي أُرِيدُ أَنْ تَبُوءَ بِإِثْمِي﴾ قَالَ: بِقَتْلِكَ إِيَّايَ، ﴿وَإِثْمِكَ﴾ قَالَ: بِمَا كَانَ مِنْكَ قَبْلَ ذَلِكَ.

وَكَذَا رَوَى عِيسَى عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، مثْله، وَرَوَى شِبْل عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيح، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ: ﴿إِنِّي أُرِيدُ أَنْ تَبُوءَ بِإِثْمِي وَإِثْمِكَ﴾ يَقُولُ: إِنِّي أُرِيدُ أَنْ يَكُونَ عَلَيْكَ خَطِيئَتِي وَدَمِي، فَتَبُوءَ بِهِمَا جَمِيعًا.

قُلْتُ: وَقَدْ يَتَوَهَّمُ كَثِيرٌ مِنَ النَّاسِ هَذَا الْقَوْلَ، وَيَذْكُرُونَ فِي ذَلِكَ حَدِيثًا لَا أَصْلَ لَهُ: مَا تَرَكَ الْقَاتِلُ عَلَى الْمَقْتُولِ مِنْ ذَنْبٍ.

وَقَدْ رَوَى الْحَافِظُ أَبُو بَكْرٍ الْبَزَّارُ حَدِيثًا يُشْبِهُ هَذَا، وَلَكِنْ لَيْسَ بِهِ، فَقَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَمْرُو بْنُ عَلِيٍّ، حَدَّثَنَا عَامِرُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ الْأَصْبَهَانِيُّ، حدثنَا يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، حَدَّثَنَا عتبة بن سعيد، عن هشام بْنِ عُرْوَة، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ قَالَتْ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "قَتْلُ الصَّبْر لَا يَمُرُّ بِذَنْبٍ إِلَّا مَحَاهُ".

وَهَذَا بِهَذَا لَا يَصِحُّ وَلَوْ صَحَّ فَمَعْنَاهُ أَنَّ اللَّهَ يُكَفِّرُ عَنِ الْمَقْتُولِ بِأَلَمِ الْقَتْلِ ذُنُوبَهُ، فَأَمَّا أَنْ تُحْمَلَ عَلَى الْقَاتِلِ فَلَا. وَلَكِنْ قَدْ يَتَّفِقُ هَذَا فِي بَعْضِ الْأَشْخَاصِ، وَهُوَ الْغَالِبُ، فَإِنَّ الْمَقْتُولَ يُطَالِبُ الْقَاتِلَ فِي العَرَصات فَيُؤْخَذُ لَهُ مِنْ حَسَنَاتِهِ بِقَدْرِ مَظْلِمَتِهِ، فَإِنْ نَفِدَتْ وَلَمْ يَسْتَوْفِ حَقَّهُ أَخَذَ مِنْ سَيِّئَاتِ الْمَقْتُولِ فطُرِحَت عَلَى الْقَاتِلِ، فَرُبَّمَا لَا يَبْقَى عَلَى الْمَقْتُولِ خَطِيئَةٌ إِلَّا وُضِعَتْ عَلَى الْقَاتِلِ. وَقَدْ صَحَّ الْحَدِيثُ بِذَلِكَ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ فِي الْمَظَالِمِ كُلِّهَا، وَالْقَتْلُ مِنْ أَعْظَمِهَا وَأَشَدَّهَا، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَأَمَّا ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ فَقَالَ وَالصَّوَابُ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ فِي ذَلِكَ أَنْ يُقَالَ: إِنَّ تَأْوِيلَهُ: إِنِّي أُرِيدُ أَنْ تَنْصَرِفَ بِخَطِيئَتِكَ فِي قَتْلِكَ إِيَّايَ -وَذَلِكَ هُوَ مَعْنَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿إِنِّي أُرِيدُ أَنْ تَبُوءَ بِإِثْمِي﴾ وَأَمَّا مَعْنَى ﴿وَإِثْمِكَ﴾ فَهُوَ إِثْمُهُ بِغَيْرِ قَتْلِهِ، وَذَلِكَ مَعْصِيَتُهُ اللَّهَ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، فِي أَعْمَالٍ سِوَاهُ.

وَإِنَّمَا قُلْنَا هُوَ الصَّوَابُ، لِإِجْمَاعِ أَهْلِ التَّأْوِيلِ عَلَيْهِ، وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، أَخْبَرَنَا أَنَّ كُلَّ عَامِلٍ فَجَزَاءُ عَمَلِهِ لَهُ أَوْ عَلَيْهِ وَإِذَا كَانَ هَذَا حُكْمُهُ فِي خَلْقِهِ، فَغَيْرُ جَائِزٍ أَنْ تَكُونَ آثَامُ الْمَقْتُولِ مَأْخُوذًا بِهَذَا الْقَاتِلِ، وَإِنَّمَا يُؤْخَذُ الْقَاتِلُ بِإِثْمِهِ بِالْقَتْلِ الْمُحَرَّمِ وَسَائِرِ آثَامِ مَعَاصِيهِ الَّتِي ارْتَكَبَهَا بِنَفْسِهِ دُونَ مَا رَكِبَهُ قَتِيلُهُ.

هَذَا لَفْظُهُ ثُمَّ أَوْرَدَ سُؤَالًا حَاصِلُهُ: كَيْفَ أَرَادَ هَابِيلُ أَنْ يَكُونَ عَلَى أَخِيهِ قَابِيلَ إِثْمُ قَتْلِهِ، وَإِثْمُ نَفْسِهِ، مَعَ أَنَّ قَتْلَهُ لَهُ مُحَرَّمٌ؟ وَأَجَابَ بِمَا حَاصِلُهُ أَنَّ هَابِيلَ أَخْبَرَ عَنْ نَفْسِهِ بِأَنَّهُ لَا يُقَاتِلُ أَخَاهُ إِنْ قَاتَلَهُ، بَلْ يَكُفُّ يَدَهُ عَنْهُ، طَالِبًا -إنْ وَقَعَ قَتْلٌ-أَنْ يَكُونَ مِنْ أَخِيهِ لَا مِنْهُ.

قُلْتُ: وَهَذَا الْكَلَامُ مُتَضَمِّنٌ مَوْعِظَةً لَهُ لَوِ اتَّعَظَ، وَزَجْرًا لَهُ لَوِ انْزَجَرَ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿إِنِّي أُرِيدُ أَنْ تَبُوءَ بِإِثْمِي وَإِثْمِكَ﴾ أَيْ: تَتَحَمَّلُ إِثْمِي وَإِثْمَكَ ﴿فَتَكُونَ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّارِ وَذَلِكَ جَزَاءُ الظَّالِمِينَ﴾

وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: خَوَّفَهُ النَّارَ فَلَمْ يَنْتَهِ وَلَمْ يَنْزَجِرْ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَطَوَّعَتْ لَهُ نَفْسُهُ قَتْلَ أَخِيهِ فَقَتَلَهُ فَأَصْبَحَ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ﴾ أَيْ: فَحَسُنَتْ وَسَوَّلَتْ لَهُ نَفْسُهُ، وَشَجَّعَتْهُ عَلَى قَتْلِ أَخِيهِ فَقَتَلَهُ، أَيْ: بَعْدَ هَذِهِ الْمَوْعِظَةِ وَهَذَا الزَّجْرِ.

وَقَدْ تَقَدَّمَ فِي الرِّوَايَةِ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ الْبَاقِرِ، وَهُوَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ: أَنَّهُ قَتَلَهُ بِحَدِيدَةٍ فِي يَدِهِ.

وَقَالَ السُّدِّي، عَنْ أَبِي مَالِكٍ وَعَنْ أَبِي صَالِحٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ -وَعَنْ مُرَّةَ، عَنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، وَعَنْ نَاسٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ: ﴿فَطَوَّعَتْ لَهُ نَفْسُهُ قَتْلَ أَخِيهِ﴾ فَطَلَبَهُ لِيَقْتُلَهُ، فَرَاغَ الْغُلَامُ مِنْهُ فِي رُءُوسِ الْجِبَالِ، فَأَتَاهُ يَوْمًا مِنَ الْأَيَّامِ وَهُوَ يَرْعَى غَنَمًا لَهُ، وَهُوَ نَائِمٌ فَرَفَعَ صَخْرَةً، فَشَدَخَ بِهَا رَأْسَهُ فمات، فَتَرَكَهُ بالعَرَاء. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ.

وَعَنْ بَعْضِ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ: أَنَّهُ قَتَلَهُ خَنْقًا وَعَضًّا، كَمَا تَقْتُل السِّبَاعُ، وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ لَمَّا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَقْتُلَهُ جَعَلَ يَلْوِي عُنُقَهُ، فَأَخَذَ إِبْلِيسُ دَابَّةً وَوَضَعَ رَأْسَهَا عَلَى حَجَرٍ، ثُمَّ أَخَذَ حَجَرًا آخَرَ فَضَرَبَ بِهِ رَأْسَهَا حَتَّى قَتَلَهَا، وَابْنُ آدَمَ يَنْظُرُ، فَفَعَلَ بِأَخِيهِ مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ.

وَقَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ وَهْب، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ: أَخَذَ بِرَأْسِهِ لِيَقْتُلَهُ، فَاضْطَجَعَ لَهُ، وَجَعَلَ يَغْمِزُ رَأْسَهُ وَعِظَامَهُ وَلَا يَدْرِي كَيْفَ يَقْتُلُهُ، فَجَاءَهُ إِبْلِيسُ فَقَالَ: أَتُرِيدُ أَنْ تَقْتُلَهُ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ. قَالَ: فَخُذْ هَذِهِ الصَّخْرَةَ فَاطْرَحْهَا عَلَى رَأْسِهِ. قَالَ: فَأَخَذَهَا، فَأَلْقَاهَا عَلَيْهِ، فشَدَخ رَأْسَهُ. ثُمَّ جَاءَ إِبْلِيسُ إِلَى حَوَّاءَ مُسْرِعًا، فَقَالَ: يَا حَوَّاءُ، إِنَّ قَابِيلَ قَتَلَ هَابِيلَ. فَقَالَتْ لَهُ: وَيْحَكَ. أَيُّ شَيْءٍ يَكُونُ الْقَتْلُ؟ قَالَ: لَا يَأْكُلُ وَلَا يَشْرَبُ وَلَا يَتَحَرَّكُ. قَالَتْ: ذَلِكَ الْمَوْتُ. قَالَ: فَهُوَ الْمَوْتُ. فَجَعَلَتْ تَصِيحُ حَتَّى دَخَلَ عَلَيْهَا آدَمُ وَهِيَ تَصِيحُ، فَقَالَ: مَا لَكِ؟ فَلَمْ تُكَلِّمْهُ، فَرَجَعَ إِلَيْهَا مَرَّتَيْنِ، فَلَمْ تُكَلِّمْهُ. فَقَالَ: عَلَيْكِ الصَّيْحَةُ وَعَلَى بَنَاتِكِ، وَأَنَا وَبَنِيَّ مِنْهَا بُرَآءُ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿فَأَصْبَحَ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ﴾ أَيْ: فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ، وَأَيُّ خسارة أعظم من هذه؟ . وقد قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ ووَكِيع قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا الْأَعْمَشُ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُرّة، عَنْ مَسْرُوقٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "لَا تُقتَل نَفْسٌ ظُلْمًا، إِلَّا كَانَ عَلَى ابْنِ آدَمَ الْأَوَّلِ كِفْلٌ مِنْ دَمِهَا، لِأَنَّهُ كَانَ أَوَّلَ مَنْ سَنَّ الْقَتْلَ".

وَقَدْ أَخْرَجَهُ الْجَمَاعَةُ سِوَى أَبِي دَاوُدَ مِنْ طُرُقٍ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، بِهِ. 

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا الْقَاسِمُ، حَدَّثَنَا الْحُسَيْنُ، حَدَّثَنِي حَجَّاجٌ قَالَ: قَالَ ابْنُ جُرَيْج: قَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ: عُلّقت إِحْدَى رِجْلَيِ الْقَاتِلِ بِسَاقِهَا إِلَى فَخِذِهَا مِنْ يَوْمِئِذٍ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ، وَوَجْهُهُ فِي الشَّمْسِ حَيْثُمَا دَارَتْ دَارَ، عَلَيْهِ فِي الصَّيْفِ حَظِيرَةٌ مِنْ نَارٍ، وَعَلَيْهِ فِي الشِّتَاءِ حَظِيرَةٌ مِنْ ثَلْجٍ -قَالَ: وَقَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَمْرٍو: إِنَّا لِنَجِدُ ابْنَ آدَمَ الْقَاتِلَ يُقَاسِمُ أَهْلَ النَّارِ قِسْمَةً صَحِيحَةَ العذابِ، عَلَيْهِ شَطْرُ عَذَابِهِمْ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ حُمَيْد، حَدَّثَنَا سَلَمَةُ، عَنِ ابْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ حَكِيمِ بْنِ حَكِيمٍ، أَنَّهُ حَدَّثَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ: إِنَّ أَشْقَى أَهْلِ النَّارِ رَجُلًا ابْنُ آدَمَ الَّذِي قَتَلَ أَخَاهُ، مَا سُفِك دَمٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ مُنْذُ قَتَل أَخَاهُ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ، إِلَّا لَحِقَ بِهِ مِنْهُ شَرٌّ، وَذَلِكَ أَنَّهُ أَوَّلُ مَنْ سَنّ الْقَتْلَ. 

وَقَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ النَّخَعِيُّ: مَا مِنْ مَقْتُولٍ يُقْتَلُ ظُلْمًا، إِلَّا كَانَ عَلَى ابْنِ آدَمَ الْأَوَّلِ والشيطان كِفْل منه.

رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ أَيْضًا.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَبَعَثَ اللَّهُ غُرَابًا يَبْحَثُ فِي الأرْضِ لِيُرِيَهُ كَيْفَ يُوَارِي سَوْأَةَ أَخِيهِ قَالَ يَا وَيْلَتَى أَعَجَزْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِثْلَ هَذَا الْغُرَابِ فَأُوَارِيَ سَوْأَةَ أَخِي فَأَصْبَحَ مِنَ النَّادِمِينَ﴾ قَالَ السُّدِّيُّ بِإِسْنَادِهِ الْمُتَقَدِّمِ إِلَى الصَّحَابَةِ: لَمَّا مَاتَ الْغُلَامُ تَرَكَهُ بالعَرَاء، وَلَا يَعْلَمُ كَيْفَ يُدْفَنُ، فَبَعَثَ اللَّهُ غُرَابَيْنِ أَخَوَيْنِ، فَاقْتَتَلَا فَقَتَلَ أَحَدُهُمَا صَاحِبَهُ، فَحَفَرَ لَهُ ثُمَّ حَثَى عَلَيْهِ. فَلَمَّا رَآهُ قَالَ: ﴿قَالَ يَا وَيْلَتَى أَعَجَزْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِثْلَ هَذَا الْغُرَابِ فَأُوَارِيَ سَوْأَةَ أَخِي﴾ .

وَقَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عباس قال: جَاءَ غُرَابٌ إِلَى غُرَابٍ مَيِّتٍ، فبَحَث عَلَيْهِ مِنَ التُّرَابِ حَتَّى وَارَاهُ، فَقَالَ الَّذِي قَتَلَ أَخَاهُ: ﴿قَالَ يَا وَيْلَتَى أَعَجَزْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِثْلَ هَذَا الْغُرَابِ فَأُوَارِيَ سَوْأَةَ أَخِي﴾

وَقَالَ الضَّحَّاكُ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: مَكَثَ يَحْمِلُ أَخَاهُ فِي جِرَابٍ عَلَى عَاتِقِهِ سَنَةً، حَتَّى بَعَثَ اللَّهُ الغُرابين، فَرَآهُمَا يَبْحَثَانِ، فَقَالَ: ﴿أَعَجَزْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِثْلَ هَذَا الْغُرَابِ﴾ فَدَفَنَ أَخَاهُ.

وَقَالَ لَيْثُ بْنُ أَبِي سُلَيْمٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ: وَكَانَ يَحْمِلُهُ عَلَى عَاتِقِهِ مِائَةَ سَنَةٍ مَيِّتًا، لَا يَدْرِي مَا يَصْنَعُ بِهِ يَحْمِلُهُ، وَيَضَعُهُ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ حَتَّى رَأَى الْغُرَابَ يَدْفِنُ الْغُرَابَ، فَقَالَ: ﴿يَا وَيْلَتَى أَعَجَزْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِثْلَ هَذَا الْغُرَابِ فَأُوَارِيَ سَوْأَةَ أَخِي فَأَصْبَحَ مِنَ النَّادِمِينَ﴾ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ وَابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ.

وَقَالَ عَطِيَّةُ الْعَوْفِيُّ: لَمَّا قَتَلَهُ نَدِمَ. فَضَمَّهُ إِلَيْهِ حَتَّى أَرْوَحَ، وَعَكَفَتْ عَلَيْهِ الطُّيُورُ وَالسِّبَاعُ تَنْتَظِرُ مَتَى يَرْمِي بِهِ فَتَأْكُلُهُ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ.

وَقَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ بَعْضِ أَهْلِ الْعِلْمِ بِالْكِتَابِ الْأَوَّلِ: لَمَّا قَتَلَهُ سُقِط فِي يَدَيْهِ، وَلَمْ يَدْرِ كَيْفَ يُوَارِيهِ. وَذَلِكَ أَنَّهُ كَانَ، فِيمَا يَزْعُمُونَ، أَوَّلَ قَتِيلٍ فِي بَنِي آدَمَ وَأَوَّلَ مَيِّتٍ ﴿فَبَعَثَ اللَّهُ غُرَابًا يَبْحَثُ فِي الأرْضِ لِيُرِيَهُ كَيْفَ يُوَارِي سَوْأَةَ أَخِيهِ قَالَ يَا وَيْلَتَى أَعَجَزْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِثْلَ هَذَا الْغُرَابِ فَأُوَارِيَ سَوْأَةَ أَخِي فَأَصْبَحَ مِنَ النَّادِمِينَ﴾ قَالَ: وَزَعَمَ أَهْلُ التَّوْرَاةِ أَنَّ قَيْنًا لَمَّا قَتَلَ أَخَاهُ هَابِيلَ، قَالَ لَهُ اللَّهُ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: يَا قَيْنُ، أَيْنَ أَخُوكَ هَابِيلُ؟ قَالَ: قَالَ: مَا أَدْرِي، مَا كُنْتُ عَلَيْهِ رَقِيبًا. فَقَالَ اللَّهُ: إِنَّ صَوْتَ دَمِ أَخِيكَ لَيُنَادِيَنِي مِنَ الْأَرْضِ، وَالْآنَ أَنْتَ مَلْعُونٌ مِنَ الْأَرْضِ الَّتِي فَتَحَتْ فَاهَا فَبَلَعَتْ دَمَ أَخِيكَ مِنْ يَدِكَ، فَإِنْ أَنْتَ عَمِلْتَ فِي الْأَرْضِ، فَإِنَّهَا لَا تَعُودُ تُعْطِيكَ حَرْثَهَا حَتَّى تَكُونَ فَزِعًا تَائِهًا فِي الْأَرْضِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿فَأَصْبَحَ مِنَ النَّادِمِينَ﴾ قَالَ الْحَسَنُ الْبَصْرِيُّ: عَلَاهُ اللَّهُ بِنَدَامَةٍ بَعْدَ خُسْرَانٍ.

فَهَذِهِ أَقْوَالُ الْمُفَسِّرِينَ فِي هَذِهِ الْقِصَّةِ، وَكُلُّهُمْ مُتَّفِقُونَ عَلَى أَنَّ هَذَيْنَ ابْنَا آدَمَ لِصُلْبِهِ، كَمَا هُوَ ظَاهِرُ الْقُرْآنِ، وَكَمَا نَطَقَ بِهِ الْحَدِيثُ فِي قَوْلِهِ: "إِلَّا كَانَ عَلَى ابْنِ آدَمَ الْأَوَّلِ كِفْل مِنْ دَمِهَا؛ لِأَنَّهُ أَوَّلُ مَنْ سَنَّ الْقَتْلَ". وَهَذَا ظَاهِرٌ جَليّ، وَلَكِنْ قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ:

حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ وَكِيع، حَدَّثَنَا سَهْل بْنُ يُوسُفَ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عن الحسن -هو البصري-قال: كان الرَّجُلَانِ اللَّذَانِ فِي الْقُرْآنِ، اللَّذَانِ قَالَ اللَّهُ: ﴿وَاتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ ابْنَيْ آدَمَ بِالْحَقِّ﴾ مِنْ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ، وَلَمْ يَكُونَا ابْنِي آدَمَ لِصُلْبِهِ، وَإِنَّمَا كَانَ القُربان فِي بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ، وَكَانَ آدَمُ أَوَّلَ مَنْ مَاتَ. وَهَذَا غَرِيبٌ جِدًّا، وَفِي إِسْنَادِهِ نَظَرٌ.

وَقَدْ قَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، عَنْ مَعْمَر، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "إِنَّ ابْنَيْ آدَمَ، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، ضُربا لِهَذِهِ الْأُمَّةِ مَثَلًا فَخُذُوا بِالْخَيْرِ مِنْهُمَا 

وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ الْمُبَارَكِ عَنْ عَاصِمٍ الْأَحْوَلِ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "إِنَّ اللَّهَ ضَرَبَ لَكُمُ ابْنَيْ آدَمَ مَثَلًا فَخُذُوا مِنْ خَيْرِهِمْ وَدَعُوا الشَّرَّ".

وَكَذَا أَرْسَلَ هَذَا الْحَدِيثَ بَكْرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْمُزَنِيُّ، رَوَى ذَلِكَ كُلَّهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ. 

وَقَالَ سَالِمُ بْنُ أَبِي الجَعْد: لَمَّا قَتَلَ ابْنُ آدَمَ أَخَاهُ، مَكَثَ آدَمُ مِائَةَ سَنَةٍ حَزِينًا لَا يَضْحَكُ، ثُمَّ أُتِيَ فَقِيلَ لَهُ: حَيَّاكَ اللَّهُ وَبَيَّاكَ. أَيْ: أَضْحَكَكَ.

رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ حُمَيْد، حَدَّثَنَا سَلَمَةُ، عَنْ غِياث بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ الهمْداني قَالَ: قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَالِبٍ: لَمَّا قَتَلَ ابْنُ آدَمَ أَخَاهُ، بَكَاهُ آدَمُ فَقَالَ:

تَغيَّرت الْبِلَادُ ومَنْ عَلَيها ... فَلَوْنُ الْأَرْضِ مُغْبر قَبيح ...

تغيَّر كُلُّ ذِي لَوْنٍ وَطَعْمٍ ... وقلَّ بَشَاشَة الوجْه الْمَلِيحُ ...

فَأُجِيبَ آدَمُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ:

أبَا هَابيل قَدْ قُتلا جَميعًا ... وَصَارَ الْحَيُّ كالميْت الذَّبِيحِ

وجَاء بشرةٍ قَدْ كَانَ مِنْها عَلى خَوف فجاء بها يَصيح وَالظَّاهِرُ أَنَّ قَابِيلَ عُوجل بِالْعُقُوبَةِ، كَمَا ذَكَرَهُ مُجَاهِدُ بْنُ جَبْر أَنَّهُ عُلِّقَتْ سَاقُهُ بِفَخِذِهِ يَوْمَ قَتْلِهِ، وَجَعَلَ اللَّهُ وَجْهَهُ إِلَى الشَّمْسِ حَيْثُ دَارَتْ عُقُوبَةً لَهُ وَتَنْكِيلًا بِهِ. وَقَدْ وَرَدَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ [أَنَّهُ] قَالَ: "مَا مِنْ ذَنْبٍ أَجْدَرَ أَنْ يُعَجَّل اللَّهُ عُقُوبَتَهُ فِي الدُّنْيَا مَعَ مَا يَدَّخر لِصَاحِبِهِ فِي الْآخِرَةِ، مِنَ البَغْي وَقَطِيعَةِ الرَّحِمِ". وَقَدِ اجْتَمَعَ فِي فِعْلِ قَابِيلَ هَذَا وَهَذَا، فَإِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ راجعون.

---

## 5:32-34 — verses 32-34 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Almighty says: {From that reason} that Ibn Adam killed his brother wrongfully and aggressively: {We decreed for the Children of Israel} meaning: We legislated for them and made known to them {that whoever kills a soul for other than a soul or corruption in the land, it is as if he had killed all of mankind, and whoever saves a life, it is as if he had saved the lives of all of mankind}. That is: whoever kills a soul without a cause of retaliation, or corruption in the land, and deems its killing permissible without cause or crime, it is as if he had killed all of mankind, because in his view there is no difference between one soul and another. {And whoever saves it}, meaning forbids its killing and holds that belief, then all of mankind is safe from him in this sense. This is why He said: {it is as if he had saved the lives of all of mankind}.

Al-Amash and others said, from Abu Salih, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: I came to Uthman on the Day of the House [when he was besieged] and said: I have come to support you, and the striking has become good, O Commander of the Faithful. He said: O Abu Hurayrah, would it please you to kill all of mankind, myself included among them? I said: No. He said: If you kill a single man, it is as if you had killed all of mankind. So withdraw, with my permission, rewarded and not burdened with sin. He said: So I withdrew and did not fight.

Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas: It is as Allah the Almighty said: {Whoever kills a soul for other than a soul or corruption in the land, it is as if he had killed all of mankind, and whoever saves it, it is as if he had saved the lives of all of mankind}. And saving it means not killing a soul that Allah has made forbidden; that is the one who has saved all of mankind, meaning: whoever forbids its killing except by right, all mankind lives on account of him.

Similarly Mujahid said: {And whoever saves it} means: refrains from killing it.

Al-Awfi said, from Ibn Abbas, regarding His saying: {it is as if he had killed all of mankind}: he means whoever kills a single soul that Allah has made forbidden is like one who has killed all of mankind. Sa'id ibn Jubayr said: whoever deems the blood of a Muslim permissible, it is as if he deemed the blood of all mankind permissible, and whoever forbids the blood of a Muslim, it is as if he forbade the blood of all mankind.

This is one view, and it is the more apparent one. Ikrimah and al-Awfi said, from Ibn Abbas [regarding His saying: {it is as if he had killed all of mankind}, he means]: whoever kills a prophet or a just leader, it is as if he had killed all of mankind, and whoever supports the cause of a prophet or a just leader, it is as if he had saved all of mankind. Ibn Jarir related it.

Mujahid said, in another report from him: whoever kills a soul without cause, it is as if he had killed all of mankind; that is because whoever kills a soul, the Fire is his due, so it is as if he had killed all of mankind.

Ibn Jurayj said, from al-A'raj, from Mujahid, regarding His saying: {it is as if he had killed all of mankind}: whoever kills a believing soul deliberately, Allah has made his recompense Hell, and Allah is angry with him and curses him, and has prepared for him a great punishment. He means: even if he had killed all of mankind, it would not add to that punishment.

Ibn Jurayj said: Mujahid said regarding {and whoever saves it, it is as if he had saved the lives of all of mankind}: whoever does not kill anyone, then mankind lives on account of him.

Abd al-Rahman ibn Zayd ibn Aslam said: whoever kills a soul, it is as if he had killed all of mankind, meaning: retaliation becomes obligatory upon him, for there is no difference between one person and a group. {And whoever saves it}, meaning: pardons the killer of his relative, it is as if he had saved all of mankind. This was also related from his father. Ibn Jarir related it.

Mujahid said, in one report: {And whoever saves it} means: rescues it from drowning, burning, or destruction.

Al-Hasan and Qatadah said, regarding His saying: {that whoever kills a soul for other than a soul or corruption in the land, it is as if he had killed all of mankind}: this is a magnification of the gravity of committing killing. Qatadah said: By Allah, its sin is immense, and by Allah, its reward [for refraining] is immense.

Ibn al-Mubarak said, from Salam ibn Miskin, from Sulayman ibn Ali al-Rib'i, who said: I said to al-Hasan: Is this verse for us, O Abu Sa'id, just as it was for the Children of Israel? He said: Yes, by the One besides whom there is no god, just as it was for the Children of Israel. The blood of the Children of Israel was not made more honorable before Allah than our blood.

Al-Hasan al-Basri said: {it is as if he had killed all of mankind}, he said: in sin. {And whoever saves it, it is as if he had saved the lives of all of mankind}, he said: in reward.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Abd Allah ibn Amr said: Hamzah ibn Abd al-Muttalib came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said: O Messenger of Allah, appoint me to something by which I may live. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "O Hamzah, is a soul you give life to dearer to you, or a soul you cause to die?" He said: Rather, a soul I give life to. He said: "Then hold to your own soul."

His saying: {And indeed Our messengers came to them with clear proofs} meaning: with arguments, demonstrations, and clear evidences, {yet even after that, many of them commit excesses in the land}. This is a rebuke and reproach to them for committing forbidden acts after having known of them, just as Banu Qurayzah, al-Nadir, and others of Banu Qaynuqa among the Jews around Medina used to fight alongside the Aws and the Khazraj whenever wars broke out between them in the Jahiliyyah, then when the wars laid down their burdens, they would ransom whomever they had captured and pay blood-money for whomever they had killed. Allah condemned this of them in Surat al-Baqarah, where He says: {And [recall] when We took your covenant, [saying], "You shall not shed your own blood or evict one another from your homes." Then you ratified [this] and were witnesses thereto. Then, you are those who kill one another and evict a party of your people from their homes, cooperating against them in sin and aggression. And if they come to you as captives, you ransom them, although their eviction was forbidden to you. So do you believe in part of the Scripture and disbelieve in part? Then what is the recompense for those who do that among you except disgrace in worldly life; and on the Day of Resurrection they will be sent back to the severest of punishment. And Allah is not unaware of what you do} [al-Baqarah: 84, 85].

His saying: {Indeed, the recompense of those who wage war against Allah and His Messenger and strive upon the earth to cause corruption is that they be killed or crucified or that their hands and feet be cut off from opposite sides or that they be exiled from the land}, to the end of the verse. Waging war (muharabah) is opposition and defiance, and it truly applies both to disbelief and to highway robbery and terrorizing the road; likewise, causing corruption in the land is applied to various kinds of evil, to the point that many of the Salaf, among them Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, said: clipping coins of silver and gold is among the corruption in the land. Allah the Almighty has said: {And when he turns away, he strives throughout the land to cause corruption therein and destroy crops and animals. And Allah does not like corruption} [al-Baqarah: 205].

Then some of them said: this noble verse was revealed regarding the polytheists, as Ibn Jarir said:

[chain] Ikrimah and al-Hasan al-Basri said [Allah the Almighty said]: {Indeed, the recompense of those who wage war against Allah and His Messenger}, up to {indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful}: this verse was revealed regarding the polytheists. Whoever among them repents before you gain power over him, there is no way [of punishment] against him. This verse does not shield the Muslim man from the legal penalty (hadd) if he kills or causes corruption in the land or wages war against Allah and His Messenger, then joins the disbelievers before being apprehended; that does not prevent the penalty he has incurred from being carried out upon him.

Abu Dawud and al-Nasa'i related it, through the route of Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas: {Indeed, the recompense of those who wage war against Allah and His Messenger and strive upon the earth to cause corruption} was revealed regarding the polytheists. Whoever among them repents before being apprehended, that does not prevent the legal penalty he has incurred from being carried out upon him.

Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas, regarding His saying: {Indeed, the recompense of those who wage war against Allah and His Messenger and strive upon the earth to cause corruption}: there was a group of the People of the Book who had a covenant and pact with the Prophet ﷺ, but they broke the covenant and caused corruption in the land. So Allah gave His Messenger the choice: if he wished, he could kill them, and if he wished, their hands and feet could be cut off from opposite sides. Ibn Jarir related it.

Shu'bah related, from Mansur, from Hilal ibn Yasaf, from Mus'ab ibn Sa'd, from his father, who said: it was revealed regarding the Haruriyyah (Khawarij): {Indeed, the recompense of those who wage war against Allah and His Messenger and strive upon the earth to cause corruption}. Ibn Mardawayh related it.

The correct view is that this verse is general, applying to the polytheists and to others besides them who commit these acts, as al-Bukhari and Muslim narrated in the hadith of Abu Qilaba, whose name was Abdullah ibn Zayd al-Jarmi al-Basri, from Anas ibn Malik: that a group of eight men from Ukl came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and pledged allegiance to him upon Islam. Then they found the land unhealthy and their bodies became sick, so they complained of this to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He said, "Will you not go out with our herdsman among his camels and drink from their urine and milk?" They said, "Yes." So they went out and drank from the camels' urine and milk, and they recovered. Then they killed the herdsman and drove off the camels. This reached the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, so he sent men after them, and they were caught and brought back. He ordered that their hands and feet be cut off, and their eyes be branded (blinded with a heated iron), and then they were left in the sun until they died.

This is the wording of Muslim. In another wording found in both collections: "from Ukl or Urayna." In another wording: "and they were thrown in the harra (lava field) and began asking for water but were not given any." In a wording of Muslim: "and he did not cauterize them." And in al-Bukhari, Abu Qilaba said: "These people stole, killed, disbelieved after their belief, and waged war against Allah and His Messenger." Muslim also narrated it through Hushaym, from Abd al-Aziz ibn Suhayb and Humayd, from Anas, mentioning something similar, and in his version: "and they apostatized." Both al-Bukhari and Muslim narrated it also through Qatada from Anas, with similar wording. Sa'id said, from Qatada: "from Ukl and Urayna." Muslim also narrated it through Sulayman al-Taymi, from Anas, who said: "The Prophet ﷺ only blinded the eyes of those men because they had blinded the eyes of the herdsmen." Muslim also narrated it from the hadith of Mu'awiya ibn Qurra, from Anas, who said: A group from Urayna came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, accepted Islam, and pledged allegiance to him. At that time an epidemic called al-mum (which is al-birsam, a kind of fever/delirium illness) had struck Madina. Then he mentioned a similar account, adding: "With him were some young men of the Ansar, about twenty horsemen, whom he sent, and he sent with them a tracker to follow their trail." All of these are wordings of Muslim, may Allah have mercy on him.

Hammad ibn Salama said: Qatada, Thabit al-Bunani, and Humayd al-Tawil narrated to us, from Anas ibn Malik: that some people from Urayna came to Madina and found it unhealthy for them, so the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent them out with the camels of the zakat, and instructed them to drink from their urine and milk. They did so, and recovered, then apostatized from Islam, killed the herdsman, and drove off the camels. So the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent men after them, and they were brought back. He cut off their hands and feet on opposite sides, branded their eyes, and threw them in the harra. Anas said: "I saw one of them biting the ground with his mouth out of thirst, until they died." And it was then that this was revealed: {Indeed, the recompense of those who wage war against Allah and His Messenger} the verse.

This was narrated by Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasa'i, and Ibn Marduyah, this last being the wording given, and al-Tirmidhi said, "Hasan, sahih (sound)."

Ibn Marduyah narrated it through many chains from Anas ibn Malik. Among them is what he narrated through two paths, from Sallam ibn Abi al-Sahba, from Thabit, from Anas ibn Malik, who said: "I have never regretted any hadith as much as I regretted a hadith al-Hajjaj asked me about. He said, 'Tell me about the harshest punishment the Messenger of Allah ﷺ ever inflicted.'" He said, "I said: A people from Urayna, from al-Bahrayn, came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and complained to him of what they suffered in their bellies, their complexions having turned yellow and their bellies swollen. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ ordered them to go to the camels of the zakat and drink from their urine and milk, until, when their complexions returned to normal and their bellies had gone down, they attacked the herdsman and killed him, and drove off the camels. So the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent men after them, and he cut off their hands and feet and branded their eyes, then threw them onto the scorching sand until they died." Al-Hajjaj, whenever he ascended the pulpit, used to say: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ cut off the hands and feet of a group of people and threw them on the scorching sand until they died, on account of a few camels," and he used to cite this hadith as an argument to the people.

Ibn Jarir said: Ali ibn Sahl narrated to us, al-Walid, meaning ibn Muslim, narrated to us, Sa'id narrated to me, from Qatada, from Anas, who said: "They were four men from Urayna and three men from Ukl. When they were brought, he cut off their hands and feet, branded their eyes, and did not cauterize them, and left them to gnaw at the stones in the harra." Allah then revealed concerning that: {Indeed, the recompense of those who wage war against Allah and His Messenger} the verse.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: Ali ibn Harb al-Mawsili narrated to us, Abu Mas'ud, meaning Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hasan al-Zajjaj, narrated to us, Abu Sa'd, meaning al-Baqqal, narrated to us, from Anas ibn Malik, who said: "There was a group from Urayna who came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, suffering from exhaustion, their complexions yellow and their bellies swollen. He ordered them to go join the camels and drink from their urine and milk. They did so, and their complexions cleared, their bellies went down, and they gained weight. Then they killed the herdsman and drove off the camels. The Prophet ﷺ sent men in pursuit of them, and they were brought back. He killed some of them, branded the eyes of some of them, and cut off the hands and feet of others." And it was revealed: {Indeed, the recompense of those who wage war against Allah and His Messenger} to the end of the verse.

Abu Ja'far ibn Jarir said: Ali ibn Sahl narrated to us, al-Walid ibn Muslim narrated to us, Ibn Lahi'a narrated to us, from Yazid ibn Abi Habib, that Abd al-Malik ibn Marwan wrote to Anas asking him about this verse, and Anas wrote back informing him that this verse was revealed concerning that group from Urayna, who were from Bajila. Anas said: "They apostatized from Islam, killed the herdsman, drove off the camels, terrorized the road, and committed unlawful sexual acts (al-farj al-haram)."

He also said: Yunus narrated to me, Ibn Wahb informed us, Amr ibn al-Harith informed me, from Sa'id ibn Abi Hilal, from Abu al-Zinad, from Abdullah ibn Ubaydullah, from Abdullah ibn Umar, or Amr, Yunus being uncertain, from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ regarding this, meaning the story of the people of Urayna, and that the verse concerning waging war (al-muharaba) was revealed about them. Abu Dawud and al-Nasa'i also narrated it through Abu al-Zinad, and in it: "from Ibn Umar" without any doubt.

Ibn Jarir said: Muhammad ibn Khalaf narrated to us, al-Hasan ibn Hammad narrated to us, from Amr ibn Hashim, from Musa ibn Ubayda, from Muhammad ibn Ibrahim, from Jarir, who said: "A people from Urayna came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, barefoot and afflicted. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ gave orders concerning them, and when they had recovered and regained their strength, they killed the herdsmen of the milk-camels, then set out with the camels heading toward the land of their own people." Jarir said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent me with a group of Muslims, and we caught up with them after they had come within sight of the land of their own people. We brought them back to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and he cut off their hands and feet on opposite sides and blinded their eyes. They began to cry out, 'Water!' while the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, 'Fire!' until they perished." He said: "Allah, Mighty and Majestic, disliked the blinding of eyes, so Allah revealed this verse: {Indeed, the recompense of those who wage war against Allah and His Messenger} to the end of the verse."

This is a gharib (singular) hadith, and in its chain is al-Rabadhi, who is weak. It contains a useful point, namely the mention of the commander of this expedition, who was Jarir ibn Abdullah al-Bajali. It was already mentioned in the Sahih of Muslim that the expedition consisted of twenty horsemen from the Ansar. As for the statement, "Allah disliked the blinding of eyes, so Allah revealed this verse," this is munkar (objectionable/rejected), for it was already established in the Sahih of Muslim that they themselves had blinded the eyes of the herdsmen, so what was done to them was in retribution (qisas). And Allah knows best.

Abd al-Razzaq said, from Ibrahim ibn Muhammad al-Aslami, from Salih the freed slave of al-Taw'ama, from Abu Hurayra, who said: "Men from Banu Fazara came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, having nearly died of emaciation. The Prophet ﷺ directed them to his milk-camels, and they drank from them until they recovered. Then they proceeded to his milk-camels and stole them. They were pursued and brought to the Prophet ﷺ, who cut off their hands and feet and branded their eyes." Abu Hurayra said: "It was concerning them that this verse was revealed: {Indeed, the recompense of those who wage war against Allah and His Messenger}."

The Prophet ﷺ then abandoned the practice of branding the eyes after that.

It was also narrated through another route from Abu Hurayra.

Abu Bakr ibn Mardawayh said: Ahmad ibn Ishaq narrated to us, al-Husayn ibn Ishaq al-Tustari narrated to us, Abu al-Qasim Muhammad ibn al-Walid narrated to us, from Amr ibn Muhammad al-Madini, Muhammad ibn Talhah narrated to us, from Musa ibn Muhammad ibn Ibrahim al-Taymi, from his father, from Abu Salamah ibn Abd al-Rahman, from Salamah ibn al-Akwa who said: The Prophet ﷺ had a servant called "Yasar." He observed that Yasar prayed well, so he freed him and sent him out to tend some milk camels of his at al-Harrah, and he stayed there. He said: some people from Urayna outwardly professed Islam, and they came suffering from fever, their bellies swollen. The Prophet ﷺ sent them to Yasar, and they drank from the milk of the camels until their bellies subsided. Then they attacked Yasar and slaughtered him, and put thorns in his eyes, then drove off the camels. The Prophet ﷺ sent horsemen from the Muslims after them, commanded by Kurz ibn Jabir al-Fihri. He caught up with them and brought them back to him, so he cut off their hands and feet and gouged out their eyes. This is gharib (singular) in the extreme.

The story of the people of Urayna has been narrated from the hadith of a group of the Companions, among them Jabir, Aisha, and others besides them. The eminent hadith master Abu Bakr ibn Mardawayh took great care in tracing this hadith through very many transmission paths. May God have mercy on him and reward him.

Ibn Jarir said: Muhammad ibn Ali ibn al-Hasan ibn Shaqiq narrated to us, saying: I heard my father say: I heard Abu Hamzah say, from Abd al-Karim, who was asked about the urine of camels, and he said: Sa'id ibn Jubayr narrated to me concerning the muharibin (those who wage war), saying: Some people came to the Messenger of God ﷺ and said, "We pledge allegiance to you upon Islam." So they gave him their pledge, but they were lying and did not want Islam at all. Then they said, "We find the climate of Medina unhealthy for us." The Prophet ﷺ said, "These milk camels go out in the morning and return in the evening for your benefit, so drink from their urine and their milk." He said: while they were doing that, a cry for help came to them, and someone cried out to the Messenger of God ﷺ, saying: they have killed the shepherd and driven off the herd. So the Prophet ﷺ ordered that it be called out among the people: "O horsemen of God, ride out!" He said: they mounted up, no horseman waiting for another. He said: the Messenger of God ﷺ rode out in their tracks, and they kept pursuing them until they drove them into their place of safety. The Companions of the Messenger of God ﷺ returned having captured some of them, and they brought them to the Prophet ﷺ. Then God sent down: {The only recompense of those who wage war against God and His Messenger} [the verse]. He said: their banishment consisted in exiling them until they were driven into their place of refuge and their own land, banishing them from the land of the Muslims. The Prophet of God ﷺ killed some of them, crucified, cut off limbs, and burned out their eyes with a heated iron. He said: the Messenger of God ﷺ never mutilated anyone before that nor after it. He said: and he forbade mutilation, saying, "Do not mutilate anything." He said: Anas used to say this too, except that he said: he burned them with fire after having killed them.

He said: some say they were people from Banu Sulaym, and that among them the people of Urayna were from Bajilah.

The scholars have differed concerning the ruling regarding these people of Urayna: is it abrogated or is it a standing ruling? Some said it is abrogated by this verse, claiming that it contains a rebuke of the Prophet ﷺ, as in His statement [Most High]: {God has pardoned you. Why did you grant them leave} [al-Tawbah: 43]. Others said it is abrogated by the Prophet's ﷺ prohibition of mutilation. This view is open to question, and moreover its proponent is required to demonstrate that the alleged abrogating text came later than the abrogated one. Some said this incident occurred before the legal punishments (hudud) were revealed, this was said by Muhammad ibn Sirin, but this too is open to question, for their story is a later one. In the narration of Jarir ibn Abd Allah concerning their story there is an indication that it occurred later, for he accepted Islam after the revelation of Surat al-Ma'idah. Others said the Prophet ﷺ did not actually gouge out their eyes, but only resolved to do so, until the Qur'an came down and clarified the ruling regarding those who wage war. This view too is open to question, for it has already been established in the hadith agreed upon [by Bukhari and Muslim] that he did gouge out, in one version, burned out, their eyes.

Ibn Jarir said: Ali ibn Sahl narrated to us, al-Walid ibn Muslim narrated to us, saying: I discussed with al-Layth ibn Sa'd the matter of the Prophet's ﷺ gouging out their eyes and leaving them without cauterization until they died. He said: I heard Muhammad ibn Ajlan say: this verse was sent down to the Messenger of God ﷺ as a rebuke concerning that, teaching him the punishment for the likes of them, namely killing, cutting off limbs, and banishment, and after them he never gouged out anyone else's eyes. He said: this view was mentioned to Abu Amr, meaning al-Awza'i, and he denied that it was sent down as a rebuke, saying rather: it was the punishment specifically for those particular individuals, then this verse was sent down concerning the punishment of others who waged war after them, lifting the practice of eye-gouging from them.

Then the majority of the scholars have argued from the general applicability of this verse in holding that waging war is the same whether it occurs within cities or on the roads, based on His saying: {and strive upon earth to cause corruption}. This is the position of Malik, al-Awza'i, al-Layth ibn Sa'd, al-Shafi'i, and Ahmad ibn Hanbal, to the point that Malik said, regarding one who lures a man by deceiving him until he brings him into a house and kills him and takes what he has with him: this is waging war, and his blood is a matter for the ruler, not for the guardian of the one killed, and the guardian's pardon of him carries no weight in preventing the execution of the death penalty.

Abu Hanifah and his companions said: waging war can only occur on the roads; as for within cities, it does not apply, because help can reach the victim there if he cries out for it, unlike on the road, due to its distance from anyone who could help or aid him. [And God knows best.]

As for His saying: {that they be killed or crucified or that their hands and feet be cut off from opposite sides or that they be banished from the land} [the verse]: Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas, concerning [His saying: {The only recompense of those who wage war against God and His Messenger}] [the verse, he said]: whoever brandishes a weapon within the domain of Islam and terrorizes the road, then is caught and overpowered, the leader (imam) of the Muslims has the choice regarding him: if he wishes he may kill him, if he wishes he may crucify him, and if he wishes he may cut off his hand and his foot.

Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, Mujahid, Ata, al-Hasan al-Basri, Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, and al-Dahhak said likewise. Abu Ja'far ibn Jarir narrated all of this, and the like of it has been related from Malik ibn Anas, may God have mercy on him. The basis for this view is that the apparent meaning of "or" indicates a choice, as in comparable instances in the Qur'an, such as His saying regarding the recompense for hunting: {then a recompense the equivalent of what he killed of grazing livestock, as judged by two just men among you, brought as an offering to the Ka'bah, or an expiation of feeding the needy, or its equivalent in fasting} [al-Ma'idah: 95], and His saying regarding the expiation for relief from restrictions: {and whoever among you is ill or has an ailment of the head, let there be a ransom of fasting or charity or sacrifice} [al-Baqarah: 196], and His saying regarding the expiation for an oath: {feeding ten needy people from the average of what you feed your own families, or clothing them, or freeing a slave} [al-Ma'idah: 89]. All of these indicate a choice, so this verse should likewise be understood in that way. The majority said: this verse applies according to different circumstances, as Abu Abd Allah al-Shafi'i [may God have mercy on him] said: Ibrahim, that is, ibn Abi Yahya, informed us, from Salih the freedman of al-Taw'amah, from Ibn Abbas, concerning highway robbers: if they killed and took property, they are to be killed and crucified; if they killed but did not take property, they are to be killed but not crucified; if they took property but did not kill, their hands and feet are to be cut off from opposite sides; and if they terrorized the road but took no property, they are to be banished from the land.

Ibn Abi Shaybah narrated this from Abd al-Rahim ibn Sulayman, from Hajjaj, from Atiyyah, from Ibn Abbas, in similar wording. And from Abu Mijlaz, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, al-Hasan, Qatadah, al-Suddi, and Ata al-Khurasani, similar statements have been transmitted. Thus said not just one of the predecessors and leading scholars.
