وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ عَنْ هَنَّاد وَابْنِ وَكِيع كِلَاهُمَا عَنْ أَبِي مُعَاوِيَةَ. ثُمَّ رَوَى ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ عَنْ عُبَيدة وَالْأَسْوَدِ، وشُرَيح الْقَاضِي، وَمُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِيرِين، وَالْحَسَنِ، وَالضَّحَّاكِ، وَأَبِي رَزِين: أَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا نَحْوَ ذَلِكَ، وَحَكَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ أَيْضًا.

وَاخْتَارَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ أَنَّ الْمُرَادَ بِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿مِنْ أَوْسَطِ مَا تُطْعِمُونَ أَهْلِيكُمْ﴾ أَيْ: فِي الْقِلَّةِ وَالْكَثْرَةِ.

ثُمَّ اخْتَلَفَ الْعُلَمَاءُ فِي مِقْدَارِ مَا يُطْعِمُهُمْ، فَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ:

حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَعِيدٍ حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ حُصَيْن الْحَارِثِيِّ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ [رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ] فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿مِنْ أَوْسَطِ مَا تُطْعِمُونَ أَهْلِيكُمْ﴾ قَالَ: يُغَذِّيهِمْ وَيُعَشِّيهِمْ.

وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ وَمُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سِيرِينَ: يَكْفِيهِ أَنْ يُطْعِمَ عَشَرَةَ مَسَاكِينَ أَكْلَةً وَاحِدَةً خُبْزًا وَلَحْمًا، زَادَ الْحَسَنُ: فَإِنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ فَخُبْزًا وَسَمْنًا وَلَبَنًا، فَإِنْ لَمْ يَجُدْ فَخُبْزًا وَزَيْتًا وَخَلًّا حَتَّى يَشْبَعُوا.

وَقَالَ آخَرُونَ: يُطْعِمُ كُلَّ وَاحِدٍ مِنَ الْعَشْرَةِ نِصْفَ صَاعٍ مِنْ بُرّ أَوْ تَمْرٍ، وَنَحْوِهِمَا. هَذَا قَوْلُ عُمَرَ، وَعَلِيٍّ، وَعَائِشَةَ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، وَالشَّعْبِيِّ، وَسَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، وَإِبْرَاهِيمَ النَّخَعي، وَمَيْمُونِ بْنِ مِهْران، وَأَبِي مَالِكٍ، وَالضَّحَّاكِ، وَالْحَاكِمِ وَمَكْحُولٍ، وَأَبِي قِلَابَةَ، ومُقَاتِل بْنِ حَيَّان.

وَقَالَ أَبُو حَنِيفَةَ: نِصْفُ صَاعٍ [مِنْ] بُرٍّ، وَصَاعٌ مِمَّا عَدَاهُ.

وَقَدْ قَالَ أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ مَرْدُويه: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ الثَّقَفِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبِيدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ يُوسُفَ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُعَاوِيَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا زِيَادُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الله بن الطُّفَيل بن سَخْبَرَة بن أَخِي عَائِشَةَ لِأُمِّهِ، حَدَّثَنَا عُمَرُ بْنُ يَعْلَى، عَنِ الْمِنْهَالِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: كفَّر رسولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ بِصَاعٍ مِنْ تَمْرٍ، وَأَمَرَ النَّاسَ بِهِ، وَمَنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ فَنِصْفُ صَاعٍ مِنْ بُرٍّ.

وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَاجَهْ، عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ زِيَادِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَكَّائِيِّ، عَنْ عُمر بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ يَعْلَى الثَّقَفِيِّ، عَنِ الْمُنْهَالِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، بِهِ. 

لَا يَصِحُّ هَذَا الْحَدِيثُ لِحَالِ عُمر بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ هَذَا فَإِنَّهُ مُجْمَعٌ عَلَى ضَعْفِهِ، وَذَكَرُوا أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَشْرَبُ الْخَمْرَ. وقال الدارقطني: متروك.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَعِيدٍ الْأَشَجُّ، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ -يَعْنِي ابْنُ أَبِي هِنْدٍ-عَنْ عِكْرِمة، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: مُدٌّ مِنْ بُرٍّ -يَعْنِي لِكُلِّ مِسْكِين-وَمَعَهُ إِدَامُهُ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ورُوِي عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، وَزَيْدِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ، وَسَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، وَعَطَاءٍ، وَعِكْرِمَةَ، وَأَبِي الشَّعْثَاءِ، وَالْقَاسِمِ وَسَالِمٍ، وَأَبِي سَلَمَةَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، وَسُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ يَسَارٍ، وَالْحَسَنِ، وَمُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِيرِينَ، وَالزُّهْرِيِّ، نَحْوُ ذَلِكَ.

وَقَالَ الشَّافِعِيُّ: الْوَاجِبُ فِي كَفَّارَةِ الْيَمِينِ مُدٌّ بمُدِّ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ لِكُلِّ مِسْكِينٍ. وَلَمْ يَتَعَرَّضْ لِلْأُدُمِ -وَاحْتَجَّ بِأَمْرِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ لِلَّذِي جَامَعَ فِي رَمَضَانَ بِأَنْ يُطْعِمَ سِتِّينَ مِسْكِينًا مِنْ مِكْيَلٍ يَسَعُ خَمْسَةَ عَشَرَ صَاعًا لِكُلِّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمْ مُدٌّ.

وَقَدْ وَرَدَ حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ صَرِيحٌ فِي ذَلِكَ، فَقَالَ أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ مَرْدَوَيْهِ: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ الْمُقْرِي، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ السَّرَّاجُ، حَدَّثَنَا قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا النضْر بْنُ زُرَارة الْكُوفِيُّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَر العُمَري، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ كَانَ يُقِيمُ كَفَّارَةَ الْيَمِينِ مُدًّا مِنْ حِنْطَةٍ بِالْمَدِّ الْأَوَّلِ.

إِسْنَادُهُ ضَعِيفٌ، لِحَالِ النَّضِرِ بْنِ زُرَارَةَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْأَكْرَمِ الذُّهْلِيِّ الْكُوفِيِّ نَزِيلِ بَلْخ، قَالَ فِيهِ أَبُو حَاتِمٍ الرَّازِيُّ: هُوَ مَجْهُولٌ مَعَ أَنَّهُ قَدْ رَوَى عَنْهُ غَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ. وَذَكَرَهُ ابْنُ حِبَّانَ فِي الثِّقَاتِ وَقَالَ: رَوَى عَنْهُ قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ أَشْيَاءَ مُسْتَقِيمَةً، فَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ. ثُمَّ إِنَّ شَيْخَهُ العُمَري ضَعِيفٌ أَيْضًا.

وَقَالَ أَحْمَدُ بْنُ حَنْبَلٍ: الْوَاجِبُ مُدّ مِنْ بُرٍّ، أَوْ مُدَّانِ مِنْ غَيْرِهِ. وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿أَوْ كِسْوَتُهُمْ﴾ قَالَ الشَّافِعِيُّ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ: لَوْ دُفِعَ إِلَى كُلِّ وَاحِدٍ مِنَ الْعَشَرَةِ مَا يَصْدُقُ عَلَيْهِ اسْمُ الْكِسْوَةِ مِنْ قَمِيصٍ أَوْ سَرَاوِيلَ أَوْ إِزَارٍ أَوْ عِمَامَةٍ أَوْ مقْنَعَة أَجْزَأَهُ ذَلِكَ. وَاخْتَلَفَ أَصْحَابُهُ فِي الْقُلُنْسُوَةِ: هَلْ تُجْزِئُ أَمْ لَا؟ عَلَى وَجْهَيْنِ، فَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ ذَهَبَ إِلَى الْجَوَازِ، احْتِجَاجًا بِمَا رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ:

حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَعِيدٍ الْأَشَجُّ، وَعَمَّارُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ الْوَاسِطِيُّ قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا الْقَاسِمُ بْنُ مَالِكٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ عِمْرَانَ بْنَ حُصَيْنٍ عَنْ قَوْلِهِ: ﴿أَوْ كِسْوَتُهُمْ﴾ قَالَ: لَوْ أَنَّ وَفْدًا قَدِمُوا عَلَى أَمِيرِكُمْ وَكَسَاهُمْ قَلَنْسُوَةً قَلَنْسُوَةً، قُلْتُمْ: قَدْ كُسُوا.

وَلَكِنَّ هَذَا إِسْنَادٌ ضَعِيفٌ؛ لِحَالِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ هَذَا، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ. وَهَكَذَا حَكَى الشَّيْخُ أَبُو حَامِدٍ الِاسْفَرَايِنِيُّ فِي الْخُفِّ وَجْهَيْنِ أَيْضًا، وَالصَّحِيحُ عَدَمُ الْإِجْزَاءِ.

وَقَالَ مَالِكٌ وَأَحْمَدُ بْنُ حَنْبَلٍ: لَا بُدَّ أَنْ يَدْفَعَ إِلَى كُلٍّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمْ مَنِ الْكُسْوَةِ مَا يَصِحُّ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ فِيهِ، إِنْ كَانَ رَجُلًا أَوِ امْرَأَةً، كلٌّ بِحَسْبِهِ. وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَقَالَ العَوْفي عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: عَبَاءَةٌ لِكُلِّ مِسْكِينٍ، أَوْ ثَمْلَة.

وَقَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ: أَدْنَاهُ ثَوْبٌ، وَأَعْلَاهُ مَا شِئْتَ.

وَقَالَ لَيْث، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ: يُجْزِئُ فِي كَفَّارَةِ الْيَمِينِ كُلُّ شَيْءٍ إِلَّا التُّبَّان.

وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ، وَأَبُو جَعْفَرٍ الْبَاقِرُ، وَعَطَاءٌ، وَطَاوُسٌ، وَإِبْرَاهِيمُ النَّخَعي، وَحَمَّادُ بْنُ أَبَى سُلَيْمَانَ، وَأَبُو مَالِكٍ: ثَوْبٌ ثَوْبٌ.

وَعَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ النَّخَعِيِّ أَيْضًا: ثَوْبٌ جَامِعٌ كَالْمِلْحَفَةِ وَالرِّدَاءِ، وَلَا يُرَى الدِّرْعُ وَالْقَمِيصُ وَالْخِمَارُ وَنَحْوُهُ جَامِعًا.

وَقَالَ الْأَنْصَارِيُّ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، وَالْحَسَنِ: ثَوْبَانِ. 

وَقَالَ الثَّوْرِيُّ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ أَبِي هِنْدٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ: عِمَامَةٌ يَلُفُّ بِهَا رَأْسَهُ، وَعَبَاءَةٌ يَلْتَحِفُ بِهَا.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا هَنَّاد، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ الْمُبَارَكِ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ الْأَحْوَلِ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، عَنْ أَبِي مُوسَى؛ أَنَّهُ حَلَفَ عَلَى يَمِينٍ، فَكَسَا ثَوْبَيْنِ مِنْ مُعقَّدة الْبَحْرَيْنِ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ مَرْدَوَيْهِ: حَدَّثَنَا سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ، حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ الْمُعَلَّى، حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ، حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ مُقَاتِلِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ أَبِي عُثْمَانَ، عَنْ أَبِي عِيَاضٍ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿أَوْ كِسْوَتُهُمْ﴾ قَالَ: "عَبَاءَةٌ لِكُلِّ مِسْكِينٍ". حَدِيثٌ غَرِيبٌ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿أَوْ تَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ﴾ أَخَذَ أَبُو حَنِيفَةَ بِإِطْلَاقِهَا، فَقَالَ: تُجْزِئُ الْكَافِرَةُ كَمَا تُجْزِئُ الْمُؤْمِنَةُ. وَقَالَ الشَّافِعِيُّ وَآخَرُونَ: لَا بُدَّ أَنْ تَكُونَ مُؤْمِنَةً. وَأَخَذَ تَقْيِيدَهَا بِالْإِيمَانِ مِنْ كَفَّارَةِ الْقَتْلِ؛ لِاتِّحَادِ الْمُوجَبِ وَإِنِ اخْتَلَفَ السَّبَبُ وَلِحَدِيثِ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ السُّلَمِيِّ، الَّذِي هُوَ فِي مُوَطَّأِ مَالِكٍ وَمُسْنَدِ الشَّافِعِيِّ وَصَحِيحِ مُسْلِمٍ: أَنَّهُ ذَكَرَ أَنَّ عَلَيْهِ عِتْقُ رَقَبَةٍ، وَجَاءَ مَعَهُ بِجَارِيَةٍ سَوْدَاءَ، فَقَالَ لَهَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "أَيْنَ اللَّهُ؟ " قَالَتْ: فِي السَّمَاءِ. قَالَ: "مَنْ أَنَا؟ " قَالَتْ: رَسُولُ اللَّهِ. قَالَ: "أَعْتِقْهَا فَإِنَّهَا مُؤْمِنَةٌ". الْحَدِيثَ بِطُولِهِ. 

فَهَذِهِ خِصَالٌ ثَلَاثٌ فِي كَفَّارَةِ الْيَمِينِ، أيُّها فَعَلَ الحانثُ أَجْزَأَ عَنْهُ بِالْإِجْمَاعِ. وَقَدْ بَدَأَ بِالْأَسْهَلِ فَالْأَسْهَلِ، فَالْإِطْعَامُ أَيْسَرُ مِنَ الْكِسْوَةِ، كَمَا أَنَّ الْكِسْوَةَ أَيْسَرُ مِنَ الْعِتْقِ، فَرُقىَ فِيهَا مِنَ الْأَدْنَى إِلَى الْأَعْلَى. فَإِنْ لَمْ يَقْدِرِ الْمُكَلَّفُ عَلَى وَاحِدَةٍ مِنْ هَذِهِ الْخِصَالِ الثَّلَاثِ كَفَّرَ بِصِيَامِ ثَلَاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَمَنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ فَصِيَامُ ثَلاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ﴾

وَرَوَى ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ وَالْحَسَنِ الْبَصْرِيِّ أَنَّهُمَا قَالَا مَنْ وَجَدَ ثَلَاثَةَ دَرَاهِمَ لَزِمَهُ الْإِطْعَامُ وَإِلَّا صَامَ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ، حَاكِيًا عَنْ بَعْضِ مُتَأَخَّرِي مُتَفَقِّهَةِ زَمَانِهِ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: جَائِزٌ لِمَنْ لَمْ يكن له فضل عن رَأْسِ مَالٍ يَتَصَرَّفُ بِهِ لِمَعَاشِهِ مَا يُكَفِّرُ بِهِ بِالْإِطْعَامِ، أَنْ يَصُومَ إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ لَهُ كِفَايَةٌ، وَمِنَ الْمَالِ مَا يَتَصَرَّفُ بِهِ لِمَعَاشِهِ، وَمِنَ الْفَضْلِ عَنْ ذَلِكَ مَا يُكَفِّرُ بِهِ عَنْ يَمِينِهِ.

ثُمَّ اخْتَارَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: أَنَّهُ الَّذِي لَا يَفْضُلُ عَنْ قُوتِهِ وَقُوتِ عِيَالِهِ فِي يَوْمِهِ ذَلِكَ مَا يُخْرِجُ بِهِ كَفَّارَةَ الْيَمِينِ. 

وَاخْتَلَفَ الْعُلَمَاءُ: هَلْ يَجِبُ فِيهَا التَّتَابُعُ، أَوْ يُسْتَحَبُّ وَلَا يَجِبُ وَيُجْزِئُ التَّفْرِيقُ؟ عَلَى قَوْلَيْنِ: أَحَدُهُمَا أَنَّهُ لَا يَجِبُ التَّتَابُعُ، هَذَا مَنْصُوصُ الشَّافِعِيِّ فِي كِتَابِ "الْأَيْمَانِ"، وَهُوَ قَوْلُ مَالِكٍ، لِإِطْلَاقِ قَوْلِهِ: ﴿فَصِيَامُ ثَلاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ﴾ وَهُوَ صَادِقٌ عَلَى الْمَجْمُوعَةِ وَالْمُفَرَّقَةِ، كَمَا فِي قَضَاءِ رَمَضَانَ؛ لِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿فَعِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَيَّامٍ أُخَرَ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ١٨٤] .

وَنَصَّ الشَّافِعِيُّ فِي مَوْضِعٍ آخَرَ فِي "الْأُمِّ" عَلَى وُجُوبِ التَّتَابُعِ، كَمَا هُوَ قَوْلُ الْحَنَفِيَّةِ وَالْحَنَابِلَةِ؛ لِأَنَّهُ قَدْ رُوِيَ عَنْ أُبَيِّ بْنِ كَعْبٍ وَغَيْرِهِمْ أَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا يَقْرَءُونَهَا: "فَصِيَامُ ثَلَاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ مُتَتَابِعَاتٍ".

قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ الرَّازِيُّ، عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ، عَنْ أَبِي الْعَالِيَةِ، عَنْ أُبَيِّ بْنِ كَعْبٍ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقْرَؤُهَا: "فَصِيَامُ ثَلَاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ مُتَتَابِعَاتٍ".

وَحَكَاهَا مُجَاهِدٌ، وَالشَّعْبِيُّ، وَأَبُو إِسْحَاقَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ.

وَقَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ: فِي قِرَاءَةِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ: "فَصِيَامُ ثَلَاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ مُتَتَابِعَاتٍ".

وَقَالَ الْأَعْمَشُ: كَانَ أَصْحَابُ ابن مسعود يقرؤونها كَذَلِكَ.

وَهَذِهِ إِذَا لَمْ يَثْبُتْ كَوْنُهَا قُرْآنًا مُتَوَاتِرًا، فَلَا أَقَلَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ خَبَرًا وَاحِدًا، أَوْ تَفْسِيرًا مِنَ الصَّحَابِيِّ، وَهُوَ فِي حُكْمِ الْمَرْفُوعِ.

وَقَالَ أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ مَرْدَوَيْهِ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ الْأَشْعَرِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا الْهَيْثَمُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ الْقُرَشِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ قَيْسٍ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ يَحْيَى، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: لَمَّا نَزَلَتْ آيَةُ الْكَفَّارَاتِ قَالَ حُذَيْفَةُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، نَحْنُ بِالْخِيَارِ؟ قَالَ: "أَنْتَ بِالْخِيَارِ، إِنْ شِئْتَ أَعْتَقْتَ، وَإِنْ شِئْتَ كَسَوْتَ، وَإِنْ شِئْتَ أَطْعَمْتَ، فَمَنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ فَصِيَامُ ثَلَاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ مُتَتَابِعَاتٍ".

وَهَذَا حَدِيثٌ غَرِيبٌ جِدًّا. 

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿ذَلِكَ كَفَّارَةُ أَيْمَانِكُمْ إِذَا حَلَفْتُمْ وَاحْفَظُوا أَيْمَانَكُمْ﴾ قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: مَعْنَاهُ لَا تَتْرُكُوهَا بِغَيْرِ تَكْفِيرٍ. ﴿كَذَلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ آيَاتِهِ﴾ أَيْ: يُوَضِّحُهَا وَيَنْشُرُهَا ﴿لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ﴾

---

## 5:90-93 — verses 90-93 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Exalted says, forbidding His believing servants from taking wine and gambling (al-maysir).

It has been related from the Commander of the Faithful, Ali ibn Abi Talib, that he said: "Chess is a form of gambling." Ibn Abi Hatim related this from his father, from Ubays ibn Marhum, from Hatim, from Ja'far ibn Muhammad, from his father, from Ali.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: Muhammad ibn Isma'il al-Ahmasi narrated to us, Waki' narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Layth, from Ata', Mujahid, and Tawus (Sufyan said: or two of them), who said: "Everything involving wagering is a form of gambling, even children's play with walnuts."

It was related from Rashid ibn Sa'd and Hamzah ibn Habib, and they said: "Even knucklebones, walnuts, and eggs which children play with." Musa ibn Uqbah said, from Nafi', from Ibn Umar, who said: "Al-maysir is gambling."

Al-Dahhak said, from Ibn Abbas: "Al-maysir is gambling. They used to gamble against one another in the Jahiliyyah (pre-Islamic era) up until the coming of Islam, and Allah forbade them these vile practices."

Malik said, from Dawud ibn al-Husayn, that he heard Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab say: "The maysir of the people of the Jahiliyyah was the selling of meat for one or two sheep."

Al-Zuhri said, from al-A'raj: "Al-maysir is the casting of arrows for wealth and produce."

Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad said: "Everything that distracts from the remembrance of Allah and from prayer is a form of maysir."

Ibn Abi Hatim related all of these.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: Ahmad ibn Mansur al-Ramadi narrated to us, Hisham ibn Ammar narrated to us, Sadaqah narrated to us, Uthman ibn Abi al-Atikah narrated to us, from Ali ibn Yazid, from al-Qasim, from Abu Umamah, from Abu Musa al-Ash'ari, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "Avoid these marked dice which are used for divination and casting lots, for they are a form of maysir." This is a gharib (singular) hadith. It seems what is meant here is backgammon (nard), which is mentioned in a hadith in Sahih Muslim, from Buraydah ibn al-Husayb al-Aslami, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever plays nardashir has as it were dipped his hand in the flesh and blood of a pig." And in the Muwatta of Malik, the Musnad of Ahmad, and the Sunans of Abu Dawud and Ibn Majah, from Abu Musa al-Ash'ari, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever plays backgammon has disobeyed Allah and His Messenger." It has also been related as a mawquf (halted report) statement of Abu Musa himself, and Allah knows best.

Imam Ahmad said: Makki ibn Ibrahim narrated to us, al-Ju'ayd narrated to us, from Musa ibn Abd al-Rahman al-Khatmi, that he heard Muhammad ibn Ka'b asking Abd al-Rahman, saying: "Tell me, what did you hear your father say about the Messenger of Allah ﷺ?" Abd al-Rahman said: "I heard my father say: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'The likeness of one who plays backgammon and then rises to pray is like the likeness of one who performs ablution with pus and the blood of a pig and then rises to pray.'"

As for chess, Abdullah ibn Umar said: "It is worse than backgammon." It was mentioned earlier that Ali said: "It is a form of maysir." Malik, Abu Hanifah, and Ahmad explicitly declared it forbidden, while al-Shafi'i disliked it, may Allah have mercy on them all.

As for al-ansab (sacrificial stones), Ibn Abbas, Mujahid, Ata', Sa'id ibn Jubayr, al-Hasan, and others said: "They were stones at which they used to slaughter their offerings."

As for al-azlam (divining arrows), they likewise said: "They were arrows with which they sought to determine their fortune."

Concerning His saying, {an abomination of Satan's handiwork}: Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas: "That is, a cause of Allah's wrath, from Satan's handiwork." Sa'id ibn Jubayr said: "A sin." Zayd ibn Aslam said: "That is, an evil, from Satan's handiwork."

{So avoid it}: the pronoun refers back to "the abomination," meaning: leave it. {That you may succeed}: this is an inducement.

Then the Exalted says: {Satan only wants to cause enmity and hatred between you through wine and gambling, and to hinder you from the remembrance of Allah and from prayer. So will you not desist?} This is a warning and a threat.

Mention of the hadiths reported concerning the prohibition of wine:

Imam Ahmad said: Surayj narrated to us, Abu Ma'shar narrated to us, from Abu Wahb, the freedman of Abu Hurayrah, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: "Wine was forbidden in three stages. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to Medina while they were drinking wine and consuming the proceeds of gambling. They asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ about the two of them, so Allah revealed: {They ask you about wine and gambling. Say: In both of them is great sin and some benefits for people} to the end of the verse [al-Baqarah: 219]. The people said: 'It has not been forbidden to us, He only said: {In both of them is great sin}.' So they continued drinking wine, until one day a man from the Muhajirun led his companions in the Maghrib prayer and confused his recitation, so Allah, Mighty and Majestic, revealed a verse more severe than the first: {O you who believe, do not approach prayer while you are intoxicated until you know what you are saying} [al-Nisa: 43]. The people continued to drink until one of them would come to prayer sober. Then a verse even more severe than that was revealed: {O you who believe, wine, gambling, sacrificial stones, and divining arrows are but an abomination of Satan's handiwork, so avoid it that you may succeed}. They said: 'We have desisted, our Lord.' Then the people said: 'O Messenger of Allah, there are people who were killed in the path of Allah, and people who died in their excess, who used to drink wine and consume the proceeds of gambling, and Allah has made it an abomination of Satan's handiwork.' So Allah, the Exalted, revealed: {There is no blame upon those who believe and do righteous deeds for what they have eaten} to the end of the verse. And the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Had it been forbidden to them, they would have left it as you have left it.'" Ahmad alone related this.

Imam Ahmad said: Khalaf ibn al-Walid narrated to us, Isra'il narrated to us, from Abu Ishaq, from Abu Maysarah, from Umar ibn al-Khattab, may Allah be pleased with him, that he said: "When the prohibition of wine was revealed, he said: 'O Allah, give us a clear and complete explanation concerning wine.' Then this verse in al-Baqarah was revealed: {They ask you about wine and gambling. Say: In both of them is great sin}. Umar was called and it was recited to him, and he said: 'O Allah, give us a clear and complete explanation concerning wine.' Then the verse in Surat al-Nisa was revealed: {O you who believe, do not approach prayer while you are intoxicated}. So the caller of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, when he called the people to prayer, would announce: 'Let no intoxicated person approach the prayer.' Umar was called and it was recited to him, and he said: 'O Allah, give us a clear and complete explanation concerning wine.' Then the verse in al-Ma'idah was revealed. Umar was called and it was recited to him, and when it reached {So will you not desist?}, Umar said: 'We have desisted.'"

Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, and al-Nasa'i likewise related this through various chains, from Isra'il, from Abu Ishaq Amr ibn Abdullah al-Sabi'i, and from Abu Maysarah (whose name was Amr ibn Shurahbil al-Hamdani), from Umar. He has no other narration from him besides this. Abu Zur'ah said: "He did not hear from him." Ali ibn al-Madini and al-Tirmidhi authenticated this hadith.

It is established in the two Sahihs, from Umar ibn al-Khattab, that he said in a sermon on the pulpit of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ: "O people, the prohibition of wine has been revealed, and it is made from five things: grapes, dates, honey, wheat, and barley, and wine is whatever befogs the mind."

Al-Bukhari said: Ishaq ibn Ibrahim narrated to us, Muhammad ibn Bishr narrated to us, Abd al-Aziz ibn Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz narrated to us, Nafi' narrated to me, from Ibn Umar, who said: "The prohibition of wine was revealed while there were five kinds of drinks in Medina at that time, none of which was made from grapes."

Another hadith: Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi said: Muhammad ibn Abi Humayd narrated to us, from al-Misri, meaning Abu Tu'mah, the reciter of Egypt, who said: "I heard Ibn Umar say: Three verses were revealed concerning wine. The first thing revealed was: {They ask you about wine and gambling} [al-Baqarah: 219]. It was said: 'Wine has been forbidden.' They said: 'O Messenger of Allah, we benefit from it as Allah has said.' He remained silent about them. Then this verse was revealed: {Do not approach prayer while you are intoxicated} [al-Nisa: 43]. It was said: 'Wine has been forbidden.' They said: 'O Messenger of Allah, we do not drink it near the time of prayer.' He remained silent about them. Then it was revealed: {O you who believe, wine, gambling, sacrificial stones, and divining arrows are but an abomination of Satan's handiwork, so avoid it that you may succeed}. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ then said: 'Wine has been forbidden.'"

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: Ya'la narrated to us, Muhammad ibn Ishaq narrated to us, from al-Qa'qa' ibn Hakim, that Abd al-Rahman ibn Wa'la said: I asked Ibn Abbas about the sale of wine, and he said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ had a friend from Thaqif, or from Daws, and he met him on the day of the Conquest carrying a large vessel of wine as a gift for him. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "O so-and-so, did you not know that Allah has forbidden it?" The man turned to his young servant and said, "Go and sell it." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "O so-and-so, what did you order him to do?" He said, "I ordered him to sell it." He said, "The One who forbade drinking it also forbade selling it." So he ordered it to be dealt with, and it was poured out in the valley of Batha.

Muslim narrated it through the route of Ibn Wahb, from Malik, from Zayd ibn Aslam, and also through the route of Ibn Wahb, from Sulayman ibn Bilal, from Yahya ibn Sa'id, both of them from Abd al-Rahman ibn Wa'la, from Ibn Abbas, with this account. Al-Nasa'i narrated it from Qutayba, from Malik, with the same account.

Another hadith: The hafiz Abu Ya'la al-Mawsili said: Muhammad ibn Abi Bakr al-Muqaddami narrated to us, Abu Bakr al-Hanafi narrated to us, Abd al-Hamid ibn Ja'far narrated to us, from Shahr ibn Hawshab, from Tamim al-Dari, that he used to give the Messenger of Allah ﷺ a gift of a large vessel of wine. When Allah revealed the prohibition of wine, he brought it to him. When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saw it, he laughed and said, "It has been forbidden after you brought it." He said, "O Messenger of Allah, shall I sell it and benefit from its price?" The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "May Allah curse the Jews. The fat of cattle and sheep was forbidden to them, so they melted it down and sold it. Allah has forbidden wine and its price."

Imam Ahmad also narrated it, saying: Rawh narrated to us, Abd al-Hamid ibn Bahram narrated to us, saying: I heard Shahr ibn Hawshab say: Abd al-Rahman ibn Ghanm narrated to me: that al-Dari used to give the Messenger of Allah ﷺ a gift every year of a large vessel of wine. In the year it was forbidden, he brought a vessel. When he looked at it, he laughed and said, "Are you aware that it has been forbidden after you brought it?" He said, "O Messenger of Allah, shall I not sell it and benefit from its price?" The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "May Allah curse the Jews. They went to what had been forbidden to them, the fat of cattle and sheep, and melted it down, then sold it and ate its price with what they earned. Wine is forbidden and its price is forbidden. Wine is forbidden and its price is forbidden. Wine is forbidden and its price is forbidden."

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: Qutayba ibn Sa'id narrated to us, Ibn Lahi'a narrated to us, from Sulayman ibn Abd al-Rahman, from Nafi' ibn Kaysan, that his father informed him that he used to trade in wine during the time of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and that he came from Syria with wine in leather skins, intending to trade with it. He brought it to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said, "O Messenger of Allah, I have brought you a good drink." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "O Kaysan, it has been forbidden after you brought it." He said, "Shall I sell it, O Messenger of Allah?" The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "It has been forbidden, and its price has been forbidden." So Kaysan went to the leather skins, took them by their ends, and poured them out.

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: Yahya ibn Sa'id narrated to us, from Humayd, from Anas, who said: I used to serve drinks to Abu Ubayda ibn al-Jarrah, Ubayy ibn Ka'b, Suhayl ibn Bayda, and a group of his companions at the house of Abu Talha, and I was serving them, until the drink was nearly taking effect on them. Then someone came from among the Muslims and said, "Are you not aware that wine has been forbidden?" They did not respond until they could look into the matter and ask. Then they said, "O Anas, pour out what remains in your vessel." By Allah, they never returned to it. It was nothing but dates and unripe dates, and that was their wine at that time.

Both al-Bukhari and Muslim narrated it in their Sahihs through more than one route from Anas. In the narration of Hammad ibn Zayd, from Thabit, from Anas, he said: I was the one serving the people drinks on the day wine was forbidden, in the house of Abu Talha, and their drink was nothing but fadikh made from unripe dates and dried dates. Then suddenly a caller was calling out. He said: Go out and look. There was a caller calling out: Indeed, wine has been forbidden! So it flowed through the streets of Medina. He said: Abu Talha said to me, "Go out and pour it away." So I poured it away. Then some of them said, or one of them said, "So-and-so and so-and-so were killed while it was still in their bellies." He said: Then Allah revealed: {There is no blame upon those who believe and do righteous deeds for what they have eaten} the verse.

Ibn Jarir said: Muhammad ibn Bashshar narrated to us, Abd al-Kabir ibn Abd al-Majid narrated to me, Abbad ibn Rashid narrated to us, from Qatada, from Anas ibn Malik, who said: While I was passing the cup around to Abu Talha, Abu Ubayda ibn al-Jarrah, Mu'adh ibn Jabal, Suhayl ibn Bayda, and Abu Dujana, until their heads were leaning from a mixture of unripe dates and dried dates, I heard a caller calling out: Indeed, wine has been forbidden! He said: No one entered upon us and no one left from among us until we had poured out the drink and broken the jars. Some of us performed ablution and some of us took a full bath, and we took some perfume of Umm Sulaym. Then we went out to the mosque, and there was the Messenger of Allah ﷺ reciting: {O you who believe, indeed intoxicants, gambling, sacrificial stones, and divining arrows are an abomination from the work of Satan, so avoid it that you may succeed}

up to His saying: {So will you not desist?} A man said, "O Messenger of Allah, what is the status of one who died while drinking it?" So Allah revealed: {There is no blame upon those who believe and do righteous deeds for what they have eaten, if they fear Allah and believe and do righteous deeds} the verse. A man said to Qatada, "Did you hear this from Anas ibn Malik yourself?" He said, "Yes." A man said to Anas ibn Malik, "Did you hear this from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ yourself?" He said, "Yes," or "Someone who did not lie narrated it to me. We did not lie, and we did not know what lying was."

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: Yahya ibn Ishaq narrated to us, Yahya ibn Ayyub informed me, from Ubayd Allah ibn Zahr, from Bakr ibn Sawada, from Qays ibn Sa'd ibn Ubada, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Indeed my Lord, blessed and exalted is He, has forbidden me wine, al-kuba (a drum-like instrument), and al-qinnin (stringed instruments). And beware of al-ghubayra, for it is a third of the wine of the world."

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: Yazid narrated to us, Faraj ibn Fadala narrated to us, from Ibrahim ibn Abd al-Rahman ibn Rafi', from his father, from Abd Allah ibn Amr, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Indeed Allah has forbidden my nation wine, gambling, al-mizr (a fermented drink), al-kuba, and al-qinnin. And He has given me additionally the prayer of witr." Yazid said: al-qinnin means stringed instruments. Ahmad alone narrated it.

Ahmad also said: Abu Asim, that is al-Nabil, narrated to us, Abd al-Hamid ibn Ja'far informed us, Yazid ibn Abi Habib narrated to us, from Amr ibn al-Walid, from Abd Allah ibn Amr, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Whoever attributes to me what I did not say, let him take his seat in Hellfire." He said: And I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say, "Indeed Allah has forbidden wine, gambling, al-kuba, and al-ghubayra, and every intoxicant is forbidden." Ahmad also narrated this alone.

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: Waki' narrated to us, Abd al-Aziz ibn Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz narrated to us, from Abu Tu'ma, their freed client, and from Abd al-Rahman ibn Abd Allah al-Ghafiqi, that both of them heard Ibn Umar say: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Wine has been cursed with respect to ten characteristics: wine itself has been cursed, and the one who drinks it, the one who serves it, the one who sells it, the one who buys it, the one who presses it, the one for whom it is pressed, the one who carries it, the one to whom it is carried, and the one who consumes its price."

Abu Dawud and Ibn Majah narrated it from the hadith of Waki', with the same account.

Ahmad said: Hasan narrated to us, Ibn Lahi'a narrated to us, Abu Tu'ma narrated to us: I heard Ibn Umar say: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ went out to al-Mirbad, and I went out with him and was on his right. Then Abu Bakr came forward, so I moved back for him, and he was on his right while I was on his left. Then Umar came forward, so I moved aside for him, and he was on his left. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to al-Mirbad, and there were leather skins there containing wine. Ibn Umar said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ called for a knife. Ibn Umar said: I had not known what a knife was until that day. He ordered the skins to be slashed, and they were slashed. Then he said, "Wine has been cursed, and the one who drinks it, the one who serves it, the one who sells it, the one who buys it, the one who carries it, the one to whom it is carried, the one who presses it, the one for whom it is pressed, and the one who consumes its price."

Ahmad said: al-Hakam ibn Nafi' related to us, Abu Bakr ibn Abi Maryam related to us, from Damrah ibn Habib, who said: Abdullah ibn Umar said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ ordered me to bring him a mudyah," which is a large knife, "so I brought it to him, and he sent for it to be sharpened, so I sharpened it, then he gave it back to me and said: 'Bring it to me in the morning.'" So I did, and he went out with his companions to the markets of Madinah, and in them were skins of wine that had been brought from Syria. He took the knife from me and cut open, in his presence, whatever skins were there, then gave it back to me and ordered his companions who were with him to go with me and help me, and he ordered me to go to all the markets and not leave any wine-skin I found without cutting it open. So I did that, and I did not leave a single skin in their markets without cutting it open.

Another hadith: Abdullah ibn Wahb said: Abd al-Rahman ibn Shurayh, Ibn Lahi'ah, and al-Layth ibn Sa'd informed me, from Khalid ibn Yazid, from Thabit ibn Yazid al-Khawlani, who reported that he had an uncle who used to sell wine and also used to give charity. "I forbade him from it, but he did not stop. So I came to Madinah and met Ibn Abbas, and I asked him about wine and its price. He said: 'It is forbidden, and its price is forbidden.'" Then Ibn Abbas, may Allah be pleased with him, said: "O community of the ummah of Muhammad, if there had been a scripture after your scripture, and a prophet after your prophet, it would have been revealed concerning you just as it was revealed concerning those before you. But that matter has been deferred for you until the Day of Resurrection, and by my life, that is harsher upon you."

Thabit said: "Then I met Abdullah ibn Umar and asked him about the price of wine. He said: 'I will tell you about wine. I was with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in the mosque, and while he was sitting with his knees drawn up and wrapped in his garment, he loosened his wrap and said: "Whoever has any of this wine, let him bring it to us." So they began bringing it to him, one of them saying, "I have a large skin," another saying, "I have a skin," or whatever Allah willed that he had. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Gather it at such and such a place in al-Baqi', then inform me." So they did, then they informed him, and he stood up, and I stood with him, and I walked on his right side while he leaned on me. Then Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, caught up with us, so the Messenger of Allah ﷺ moved me back and put me on his left, placing Abu Bakr in my place. Then Umar ibn al-Khattab, may Allah be pleased with him, caught up with us, so he moved me back again and placed Umar on his left, and he walked between the two of them.

When he stopped at the wine, he said to the people: "Do you recognize this?" They said: "Yes, O Messenger of Allah, this is wine." He said: "You have spoken truly." He said: "Indeed Allah has cursed wine, the one who presses it, the one for whom it is pressed, its drinker, the one who serves it, the one who carries it, the one to whom it is carried, its seller, its buyer, and the one who consumes its price." Then he called for a knife and said: "Sharpen it." So they did, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ took it and began tearing open the skins with it.'"

He said: "The people said: 'There is benefit in these skins.' He said: 'Indeed, but I am only doing this out of anger for the sake of Allah, mighty and majestic, because of what it contains of His wrath.'" Umar said: "Shall I do it for you, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "No."

Ibn Wahb said: "Some of them add to what others narrate in the story of this hadith." Narrated by al-Bayhaqi.

Another hadith: The hafiz Abu Bakr al-Bayhaqi said: Abu al-Husayn ibn Bishran informed us, Isma'il ibn Muhammad al-Saffar informed us, Muhammad ibn Ubaydullah al-Munadi related to us, Wahb ibn Jarir related to us, Shu'bah related to us, from Simak, from Mus'ab ibn Sa'd, from Sa'd, who said: "Four verses were revealed concerning wine." Then he related the hadith: "A man from the Ansar prepared a meal and invited us, and we drank wine before it was forbidden, until we became intoxicated. We then boasted against one another, and the Ansar said: 'We are better,' and the Quraysh said: 'We are better.' Then a man from the Ansar took the jawbone of a slaughtered camel and struck Sa'd's nose with it, breaking it. Sa'd's nose remained broken thereafter." Then the verse of wine was revealed: {Indeed, intoxicants and gambling [and idols and divining arrows]} up to His saying: {then will you not desist?}. Reported by Muslim from the hadith of Shu'bah.

Another hadith: Al-Bayhaqi said: Abu Nasr ibn Qatadah informed us, Abu Ali al-Raffa' informed us, Ali ibn Abd al-Aziz related to us, Hajjaj ibn Minhal related to us, Rabi'ah ibn Kulthum related to us, my father related to me, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn Abbas, who said: "The prohibition of wine was revealed concerning two tribes of the Ansar tribes. They drank, and when one of them became intoxicated, some of them assaulted others. When they sobered up, a man would see the mark on his face, head, and beard, and would say: 'My brother, so and so, did this to me,' though they were brothers with no rancor in their hearts. 'By Allah, if he had been kind and merciful to me he would not have done this to me,' until rancor settled in their hearts. So Allah revealed this verse: {O you who believe, indeed intoxicants, gambling, idols, and divining arrows are filth from the work of Satan; [avoid it that you may succeed. Satan only wants to cast among you enmity and hatred through intoxicants and gambling, and to keep you from the remembrance of Allah and from prayer], so will you not desist?}"

Then some among those who took on affectation said: "It is filth, and it is in the belly of so and so," who had been killed on the day of Uhud. So Allah revealed: {There is no blame upon those who believe and do righteous deeds for what they have eaten, [as long as they are conscious of Allah and believe and do righteous deeds, then are conscious of Allah and believe, then are conscious of Allah and do good; and Allah loves those who do good]}.

Al-Nasa'i also narrated it in his Tafsir, from Muhammad ibn Abd al-Rahim Sa'iqah, from Hajjaj ibn Minhal.

Another hadith: Ibn Jarir said: Muhammad ibn Khalaf related to me, Sa'id ibn Muhammad al-Jarmi related to us, from Abu Tumaylah, from Salam, the freedman of Hafs Abu al-Qasim, from Abu Buraydah, from his father, who said: "While we were sitting at a drinking gathering of ours, and we were at Ramlah, and we were three or four, with a large vessel among us, drinking wine, which was still lawful, I got up to go to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ to greet him with peace, when the prohibition of wine was revealed: {O you who believe, indeed intoxicants and gambling} to the end of the two verses, {so will you not desist?}. So I came to my companions and recited it to them up to His saying: {so will you not desist?}." He said: "One of the group had his drink in his hand, having drunk part of it with some remaining in the vessel, so he held the vessel beneath his upper lip, as a cupper does, then they poured out what remained in their large vessel and said: 'We have desisted, our Lord.'"

Another hadith: Al-Bukhari said: Sadaqah ibn al-Fadl related to us, Ibn Uyaynah informed us, from Amr, from Jabir, who said: "Some people drank wine in the morning on the day of Uhud, and they were killed that same day, all of them as martyrs, and that was before it was forbidden."

Thus al-Bukhari narrated it in his Tafsir within his Sahih. Al-hafiz Abu Bakr al-Bazzar also narrated it in his Musnad: Ahmad ibn Abdah related to us, Sufyan related to us, from Amr ibn Dinar, who heard Jabir ibn Abdullah say: "Some people among the companions of the Prophet ﷺ drank wine in the morning, then were killed as martyrs on the day of Uhud. The Jews said: 'Indeed some of those who were killed died with it in their bellies.'" So Allah revealed: {There is no blame upon those who believe and do righteous deeds for what they have eaten}. Then he said: "This is a sound chain (sahih)." And it is as he said, though there is something unusual (gharabah) in its wording.

Another hadith: Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi said: Shu'bah related to us, from Abu Ishaq, from al-Bara' ibn Azib, who said: "When the prohibition of wine was revealed, they said: 'What of those who drank it before it was forbidden?' So it was revealed: {There is no blame upon those who believe and do righteous deeds for what they have eaten}, the verse."

Al-Tirmidhi also narrated it, from Bundar, from Ghundar, from Shu'bah, similarly, and he said: "Hasan sahih (good and sound)."

Another hadith: The hafiz Abu Ya'la al-Mawsili said: Ja'far ibn Humayd al-Kufi related to us, Ya'qub al-Qummi related to us, from Isa ibn Jariyah, from Jabir ibn Abdullah, who said: "There was a man who used to carry wine from Khaybar to Madinah and sell it to the Muslims. He loaded a quantity of it worth much money and brought it to Madinah. A man from the Muslims met him and said: 'O so and so, wine has been forbidden.' So he set it down where he had reached, on a hill, and covered it with cloaks. Then he came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, it has reached me that wine has been forbidden.' He said: 'Indeed.' He said: 'May I return it to the one I bought it from?' He said: 'It is not permissible to return it.' He said: 'May I give it as a gift to someone who will reward me for it?' He said: 'No.' He said: 'It contains wealth belonging to orphans under my guardianship.' He said: 'When the wealth of Bahrain comes to us, come to us, and we will compensate your orphans from their wealth.'"

Then he called out in Medina, and a man said, "O Messenger of God, may we make use of the containers?" He said, "Loosen their tying cords." So it poured out until it settled at the bottom of the valley. This is a gharib (singular) hadith.

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: Waki' told us, Sufyan told us, from al-Suddi, from Abu Hubayrah, that is, Yahya ibn 'Abbad al-Ansari, from Anas ibn Malik, that Abu Talhah asked the Prophet ﷺ about orphans in his care who had inherited wine. He said, "Pour it out." He said, "Shall we not make it into vinegar?" He said, "No."

Muslim, Abu Dawud, and al-Tirmidhi related it from the hadith of al-Thawri, with the same content.

Another hadith: Ibn Abi Hatim said: My father told us, 'Abdullah ibn Raja' told us, 'Abd al-'Aziz ibn Abi Salamah told us, Hilal ibn Abi Hilal told us, from 'Ata' ibn Yasar, from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr, who said: This verse which is in the Qur'an, {O you who believe, wine, gambling, idols, and divining arrows are but filth from the work of Satan, so avoid it that you may prosper}, he said: it is in the Torah: "God has sent down the truth to remove falsehood by it, and to nullify by it games, flutes, dancing, the kibarat (meaning the barbat, a stringed instrument), the zammarat (meaning the tambourine), the tanabir (a stringed instrument), poetry, and wine once and for all for whoever tastes it. God has sworn by His oath and by the might of His power that whoever drinks it after I have forbidden it, I will surely make him thirst on the Day of Resurrection, and whoever abandons it after I have forbidden it, I will surely give him to drink of it in the enclosure of holiness (hazirat al-quds)."

This is a sound chain of transmission.

Another hadith: 'Abdullah ibn Wahb said: 'Amr ibn al-Harith informed me that 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb told them, from his father, from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As, from the Messenger of God ﷺ, who said: "Whoever abandons the prayer due to intoxication once, it is as if the world and all that is in it had been his and then stripped from him. And whoever abandons the prayer due to intoxication four times, it becomes incumbent upon God to give him to drink from tinat al-khabal (the mire of corruption)." It was asked, "What is tinat al-khabal?" He said, "The extract [pressed filth] of the people of Hell."

Ahmad related it by the route of 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb.

Another hadith: Abu Dawud said: Muhammad ibn Rafi' told us, Ibrahim ibn 'Umar al-San'ani told us, he said: I heard al-Nu'man, that is, ibn Abi Shaybah al-Janadi, say from Tawus, from Ibn 'Abbas, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "Every intoxicating drink is wine, and every intoxicant is forbidden. Whoever drinks an intoxicant, his prayer is diminished for forty mornings. If he repents, God accepts his repentance. But if he returns to it a fourth time, it becomes incumbent upon God to give him to drink from tinat al-khabal." It was asked, "What is tinat al-khabal, O Messenger of God?" He said, "The pus of the people of the Fire. And whoever gives it to drink to a young child who does not know its lawful from its unlawful nature, it becomes incumbent upon God to give him to drink from tinat al-khabal."

Abu Dawud alone transmitted this.

Another hadith: al-Shafi'i, may God have mercy on him, said: Malik informed us, from Nafi', from Ibn 'Umar, that the Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Whoever drinks wine in this world and does not repent from it will be deprived of it in the Hereafter."

Al-Bukhari and Muslim both recorded it from the hadith of Malik, with the same content.

Muslim related from Abu al-Rabi', from Hammad ibn Zayd, from Ayyub, from Nafi', from Ibn 'Umar, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Every intoxicant is wine, and every intoxicant is forbidden. Whoever drinks wine and dies while persisting in it and without repenting from it will not drink it in the Hereafter."

Another hadith: Ibn Wahb said: 'Umar ibn Muhammad informed me, from 'Abdullah ibn Yasar, that he heard Salim ibn 'Abdullah say: 'Abdullah ibn 'Umar said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "There are three whom God will not look upon on the Day of Resurrection: the one who is undutiful to his parents, the one persistently addicted to wine, and the one who reminds others of favors he has given."

Al-Nasa'i related it from 'Umar ibn 'Ali, from Yazid ibn Zuray', from 'Umar ibn Muhammad al-'Umari, with the same content.

Ahmad related from Ghundar, from Shu'bah, from Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad, from Mujahid, from Abu Sa'id, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "No one who reminds others of favors, nor one undutiful to parents, nor one persistently addicted to wine will enter Paradise."

Ahmad also related it from 'Abd al-Samad, from 'Abd al-'Aziz ibn Muslim, from Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad, from Mujahid, with the same content. And from Marwan ibn Shuja', from Khasif, from Mujahid, with the same content. Al-Nasa'i related it from al-Qasim ibn Zakariyya, from al-Husayn al-Ju'fi, from Za'idah, from Ibn Abi Ziyad, from Salim ibn Abi al-Ja'd and Mujahid, both from Abu Sa'id, with the same content.

Another hadith: Ahmad said: 'Abd al-Razzaq told us, Sufyan told us, from Mansur, from Salim ibn Abi al-Ja'd, from Jaban, from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "No one undutiful to his parents, nor one persistently addicted to wine, nor one who reminds others of favors, nor a child born of fornication will enter Paradise."

Likewise he related it from Yazid, from Hammam, from Mansur, from Salim, from Jaban, from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr, with the same content. He also related it from Ghundar and others, from Shu'bah, from Mansur, from Salim, from Nubayt ibn Shurayt, from Jaban, from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "No one who reminds others of favors, nor one undutiful to his parents, nor one persistently addicted to wine will enter Paradise."

Al-Nasa'i related it from the hadith of Shu'bah likewise, then said: We do not know anyone who corroborated Shu'bah's report from Nubayt ibn Shurayt.

Al-Bukhari said: It is not known that Jaban heard directly from 'Abdullah, nor that Salim heard directly from Jaban or from Nubayt.

This hadith has also been related by the route of Mujahid, from Ibn 'Abbas, and also by his route from Abu Hurayrah. God knows best.

Al-Zuhri said: Abu Bakr ibn 'Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Harith ibn Hisham told me, that his father said: I heard 'Uthman ibn 'Affan say: Avoid wine, for it is the mother of all vices. There was a man among those who came before you who devoted himself to worship and withdrew from people. A wanton woman became infatuated with him, and sent her slave girl to him, saying, "We summon you to bear witness." He went with her, and she kept closing every door behind him as he passed through it, until he came upon a beautiful woman who had with her a young boy and a jar of wine. She said, "By God, I did not summon you to bear witness, but I summoned you so that you would either lie with me, or kill this boy, or drink this wine." She gave him a cup to drink, and he said, "Give me more," and he did not stop until he had lain with her and killed the person. So avoid wine, for it and faith never coexist for long, but one of them will soon drive the other out.

Al-Bayhaqi related it, and this is a sound chain of transmission. Abu Bakr ibn Abi al-Dunya also related it in his book "The Condemnation of Intoxicants," from Muhammad ibn 'Abdullah ibn Bazi', from al-Fudayl ibn Sulayman al-Numayri, from 'Umar ibn Sa'id, from al-Zuhri, with the same content, as a raised (marfu') report, though the halted (mawquf) version is more sound. God knows best.

It has a corroborating witness in the two Sahihs, from the Messenger of God ﷺ, who said: "The fornicator does not fornicate while he is a believer at the moment he fornicates, nor does the thief steal while he is a believer at the moment he steals, nor does the wine drinker drink wine while he is a believer at the moment he drinks it."

Ahmad ibn Hanbal said: Aswad ibn 'Amir told us, Isra'il informed us, from Simak, from 'Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas, who said: When wine was forbidden, people said, "O Messenger of God, what of our companions who died while they were drinking it?" So God revealed: {There is no blame upon those who believe and do righteous deeds for what they have eaten [previously]} the verse. He said: And when the direction of prayer was changed, people said, "O Messenger of God, what of our companions who died while praying toward Jerusalem?" So God revealed: {And God would not let your faith be wasted} [al-Baqarah: 143].

Imam Ahmad said: Dawud ibn Mihran al-Dabbagh told us, Dawud, that is al-'Attar, told us, from Ibn Khuthaym, from Shahr ibn Hawshab, from Asma' bint Yazid, that she heard the Prophet ﷺ say: "Whoever drinks wine, God will not be pleased with him for forty nights. If he dies, he dies an unbeliever. If he repents, God accepts his repentance. But if he returns to it, it becomes incumbent upon God to give him to drink from tinat al-khabal." She said: I said, "O Messenger of God, what is tinat al-khabal?" He said, "The pus of the people of the Fire."

Al-A'mash said, from Ibrahim, from 'Alqamah, from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, that the Prophet ﷺ said, when {There is no blame upon those who believe and do righteous deeds for what they have eaten, provided they are mindful of God and believe} was revealed, the Prophet ﷺ said: "It was said to me: you are among them."

Muslim, al-Tirmidhi, and al-Nasa'i related it likewise, by his route.

'Abdullah ibn al-Imam Ahmad said: I read to my father, [chain] Ali ibn Asim told us, Ibrahim al-Hajari told us, from Abu al-Ahwas, from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Beware of these two marked cubes that are thrown with a throwing motion, for they are the maysir (gambling) of the non-Arabs."

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى نَاهِيًا عِبَادَهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَنْ تَعَاطِي الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ، وَهُوَ الْقِمَارُ.

وَقَدْ وَرَدَ عَنْ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَالِبٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: الشَطْرَنج مِنَ الْمَيْسِرِ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عُبَيس بْنِ مَرْحُومٍ، عَنْ حَاتِمٍ، عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، بِهِ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ الْأَحْمَسِيُّ حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيع، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ لَيْث، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ وَمُجَاهِدٍ وَطَاوُسٍ -قَالَ سُفْيَانُ: أَوِ اثْنَيْنِ مِنْهُمْ-قَالُوا: كُلُّ شَيْءٍ مِنَ الْقِمَارِ فَهُوَ مِنَ الْمَيْسِرِ، حَتَّى لَعِبِ الصِّبْيَانِ بِالْجَوْزِ.

ورُوي عَنْ رَاشِدِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ وَحَمْزَةَ بْنِ حَبِيبٍ وَقَالَا حَتَّى الْكَعَابِ، وَالْجَوْزِ، وَالْبَيْضِ الَّتِي تَلْعَبُ بِهَا الصِّبْيَانُ، وَقَالَ مُوسَى بْنِ عُقْبَةَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: الْمَيْسِرُ هُوَ الْقِمَارُ.

وَقَالَ الضَّحَّاكُ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: الْمَيْسِرُ هُوَ الْقِمَارُ، كَانُوا يَتَقَامَرُونَ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ إِلَى مَجِيءِ الْإِسْلَامِ، فَنَهَاهُمُ اللَّهُ عَنْ هَذِهِ الْأَخْلَاقِ الْقَبِيحَةِ.

وَقَالَ مَالِكٌ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ الحُصَيْن: أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ سَعِيدَ بْنَ الْمُسَيَّبِ يَقُولُ: كَانَ مَيْسِرُ أَهْلِ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ بَيْعَ اللَّحْمِ بِالشَّاةِ وَالشَّاتَيْنِ.

وَقَالَ الزُّهْرِيُّ، عَنِ الْأَعْرَجِ قَالَ: الْمَيْسِرُ وَالضَّرْبُ بِالْقِدَاحِ عَلَى الْأَمْوَالِ وَالثِّمَارِ.

وَقَالَ الْقَاسِمُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ: كُلُّ مَا أَلْهَى عَنْ ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ وَعَنِ الصَّلَاةِ، فَهُوَ مِنَ الْمَيْسِرِ.

رَوَاهُنَّ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ الرَّمَادِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ، حَدَّثَنَا صَدَقَةُ، حَدَّثَنَا عُثْمَانُ بْنُ أَبِي الْعَاتِكَةِ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ يَزِيدَ، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ، عَنْ أَبِي أُمَامَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي مُوسَى الْأَشْعَرِيِّ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قَالَ: "اجْتَنِبُوا هَذِهِ الكِعَاب الْمَوْسُومَةَ الَّتِي يُزْجَرُ بِهَا زَجْرًا فَإِنَّهَا مِنَ الْمَيْسِرِ". حديث غريب. وَكَأَنَّ الْمُرَادَ بِهَذَا هُوَ النَّرْدُ، الَّذِي وَرَدَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ بِهِ فِي صَحِيحِ مُسْلِمٍ، عَنْ بُرَيدة بْنِ الحُصَيب الْأَسْلَمِيِّ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَنْ لَعِبَ بالنَّرْدَشير فَكَأَنَّمَا صَبَغ يَدَهُ فِي لَحْمِ خِنْزِيرٍ ودَمه". وفي موطأ مالك ومسند أحمد، وسنني أَبِي دَاوُدَ وَابْنِ مَاجَهْ، عَنْ أَبِي مُوسَى الْأَشْعَرِيِّ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَنْ لَعِبَ بِالنَّرْدِ فَقَدْ عَصَى اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ". وَرُوِيَ مَوْقُوفًا عَنْ أَبِي مُوسَى مِنْ قَوْلِهِ، فَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا مَكِّيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَدَّثَنَا الجُعَيْد، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْخَطْمِيِّ، أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ مُحَمَّدَ بْنَ كَعْبٍ وَهُوَ يَسْأَلُ عَبْدَ الرَّحْمَنِ يَقُولُ: أَخْبِرْنِي، مَا سَمِعْتَ أَبَاكَ يَقُولُ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ؟ فقال عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ: سَمِعْتُ أَبِي يَقُولُ: سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَقُولُ: "مَثَلُ الَّذِي يَلْعَبُ بِالنَّرْدِ، ثُمَّ يَقُومُ فَيُصَلِّي، مَثَلُ الَّذِي يَتَوَضَّأُ بالقَيْح وَدَمِ الْخِنْزِيرِ ثُمَّ يَقُومُ فَيُصَلِّي". 

وَأَمَّا الشِّطْرَنْجُ فَقَدْ قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُمَرَ: إِنَّهُ شَرٌّ مِنَ النَّرْدِ. وَتَقَدَّمَ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: هُوَ مِنَ الْمَيْسِرِ، وَنَصَّ عَلَى تَحْرِيمِهِ مَالِكٌ، وَأَبُو حَنِيفَةَ، وَأَحْمَدُ، وَكَرِهَهُ الشَّافِعِيُّ، رَحِمَهُمُ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى.

وَأَمَّا الْأَنْصَابُ، فَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ، وَمُجَاهِدٌ، وَعَطَاءٌ، وَسَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ، وَالْحَسَنُ، وَغَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ: هِيَ حِجَارَةٌ كَانُوا يَذْبَحُونَ قَرَابِينَهُمْ عِنْدَهَا.

وَأَمَّا الْأَزْلَامُ فَقَالُوا أَيْضًا: هِيَ قِدَاحٌ كَانُوا يَسْتَقْسِمُونَ بِهَا.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿رِجْسٌ مِنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطَانِ﴾ قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: أَيْ سَخَط مِنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطَانِ. وَقَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ: إِثْمٌ. وَقَالَ زَيْدُ بْنُ أَسْلَمَ: أَيْ شَرٌّ مِنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطَانِ.

﴿فَاجْتَنِبُوهُ﴾ الضَّمِيرُ عَائِدٌ عَلَى الرِّجْسِ، أَيِ اتْرُكُوهُ ﴿لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ﴾ وَهَذَا تَرْغِيبٌ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿إِنَّمَا يُرِيدُ الشَّيْطَانُ أَنْ يُوقِعَ بَيْنَكُمُ الْعَدَاوَةَ وَالْبَغْضَاءَ فِي الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ وَيَصُدَّكُمْ عَنْ ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ وَعَنِ الصَّلاةِ فَهَلْ أَنْتُمْ مُنْتَهُونَ﴾ وَهَذَا تَهْدِيدٌ وَتَرْهِيبٌ.

ذِكْرُ الْأَحَادِيثِ الْوَارِدَةِ فِي [بَيَانِ] تَحْرِيمِ الْخَمْرِ:

قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا سُرَيج حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مَعْشَر، عَنْ أَبِي وَهْب مَوْلَى أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ: حُرِّمَتِ الْخَمْرُ ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ، قَدِمَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ الْمَدِينَةَ، وَهُمْ يَشْرَبُونَ الْخَمْرَ وَيَأْكُلُونَ الْمَيْسِرَ، فَسَأَلُوا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ عَنْهُمَا، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿يَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ قُلْ فِيهِمَا إِثْمٌ كَبِيرٌ وَمَنَافِعُ لِلنَّاسِ﴾ إِلَى آخَرَ الْآيَةِ [الْبَقَرَةِ:٢١٩] . فَقَالَ النَّاسُ: مَا حَرُمَ عَلَيْنَا، إِنَّمَا قَالَ: ﴿فِيهِمَا إِثْمٌ كَبِيرٌ﴾ وَكَانُوا يَشْرَبُونَ الْخَمْرَ، حَتَّى كَانَ يَوْمًا مِنَ الْأَيَّامِ صَلَّى رَجُلٌ مِنَ المهاجرين، أمَّ أصحابه في الْمَغْرِبِ، خَلَطَ فِي قِرَاءَتِهِ، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ [عَزَّ وَجَلَّ] آيَةً أَغْلَظَ مِنْهَا: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَقْرَبُوا الصَّلاةَ وَأَنْتُمْ سُكَارَى حَتَّى تَعْلَمُوا مَا تَقُولُونَ﴾ [النِّسَاءِ: ٤٣] وَكَانَ النَّاسُ يَشْرَبُونَ، حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ أَحَدُهُمُ الصَّلَاةَ وَهُوَ مُفِيقٌ. ثُمَّ أُنْزِلَتْ آيَةٌ أَغْلَظُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّمَا الْخَمْرُ وَالْمَيْسِرُ وَالأنْصَابُ وَالأزْلامُ رِجْسٌ مِنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطَانِ فَاجْتَنِبُوهُ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ﴾ قَالُوا: انْتَهَيْنَا رَبَّنَا. وَقَالَ النَّاسُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، نَاسٌ قُتِلُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ، [وَنَاسٌ] مَاتُوا عَلَى سَرَفِهِمْ كَانُوا يَشْرَبُونَ الْخَمْرَ وَيَأْكُلُونَ الْمُيْسِرَ، وَقَدْ جَعَلَهُ اللَّهُ رِجْسًا مِنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطَانِ؟ فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿لَيْسَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ جُنَاحٌ فِيمَا طَعِمُوا﴾ إِلَى آخِرِ الْآيَةِ، وَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "لَوْ حَرُمَ عَلَيْهِمْ لَتَرَكُوهُ كَمَا تَرَكْتُمْ". انْفَرَدَ بِهِ أَحْمَدُ. 

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا خَلَف بْنُ الْوَلِيدِ، حَدَّثَنَا إِسْرَائِيلُ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ أَبِي مَيْسَرة، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ [رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ] أَنَّهُ قَالَ: لَمَّا نَزَلَ تَحْرِيمُ الْخَمْرِ قَالَ: اللَّهُمَّ بَيّن لَنَا فِي الْخَمْرِ بَيَانًا شَافِيًا. فَنَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ التِي فِي الْبَقَرَةِ: ﴿يَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ قُلْ فِيهِمَا إِثْمٌ كَبِيرٌ﴾ فَدُعي عُمَرُ فَقُرِئَتْ عَلَيْهِ، فَقَالَ: اللَّهُمَّ بَيِّنْ لَنَا فِي الْخَمْرِ بَيَانًا شَافِيًا. فَنَزَلَتِ الْآيَةُ الَّتِي فِي سُورَةِ النِّسَاءِ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَقْرَبُوا الصَّلاةَ وَأَنْتُمْ سُكَارَى﴾ فَكَانَ مُنَادِي رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ إِذَا أَقَامَ الصَّلَاةَ نَادَى: أَلَّا يَقْرَبَنَّ الصَّلَاةَ سَكْرَانُ. فَدُعِيَ عُمَرُ فَقُرِئَتْ عَلَيْهِ، فَقَالَ: اللَّهُمَّ بَيِّنْ لَنَا فِي الْخَمْرِ بَيَانًا شَافِيًا. فَنَزَلَتِ الْآيَةُ التِي فِي الْمَائِدَةِ، فَدَعِي عُمَرُ فَقُرِئَتْ عَلَيْهِ فَلَمَّا بَلَغَ: ﴿فَهَلْ أَنْتُمْ مُنْتَهُونَ﴾ قَالَ عُمَرُ: انْتَهَيْنَا. 

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ، وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ، وَالنَّسَائِيُّ مِنْ طُرُقٍ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ عَمْرو بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ السَّبِيعي وَعَنْ أَبِي مَيْسَرَةَ -وَاسْمُهُ عَمْرُو بْنُ شُرَحبيل الْهَمْدَانِيُّ-عَنْ عُمَر، بِهِ. وَلَيْسَ لَهُ عَنْهُ سِوَاهُ، قَالَ أَبُو زُرْعَة: وَلَمْ يَسْمَعْ مِنْهُ. وَصَحَّحَ هَذَا الْحَدِيثَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْمَدِينِيِّ وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ. 

وَقَدْ ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ أَنَّهُ قَالَ فِي خُطْبَتِهِ عَلَى مِنْبَرِ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ، إِنَّهُ نَزَلَ تَحْرِيمُ الْخَمْرِ وَهِيَ مِنْ خَمْسَةٍ: مِنَ الْعِنَبِ، وَالتَّمْرِ، وَالْعَسَلِ، والحِنْطَة، وَالشَّعِيرِ، وَالْخَمْرُ مَا خَامَرَ الْعَقْلَ. 

وَقَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بِشْر، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْعَزِيزِ بْنُ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، حَدَّثَنِي نَافِعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: نَزَلَ تَحْرِيمُ الْخَمْرِ وَإِنَّ بِالْمَدِينَةِ يَوْمَئِذَ لَخَمْسَةُ أَشْرِبَةٍ مَا فِيهَا شَرَابُ الْعِنَبِ. 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ أَبُو دَاوُدَ الطَّيَالِسِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي حُمَيْدٍ، عَنِ الْمِصْرِيِّ -يعني أبا طعمة قَارِئَ مِصْرَ -قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ ابْنَ عُمَرَ يَقُولُ: نَزَلَتْ فِي الْخَمْرِ ثَلَاثُ آيَاتٍ، فَأَوَّلُ شَيْءٍ نَزَلَ: ﴿يَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ﴾ الْآيَةَ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ٢١٩] فَقِيلَ: حُرِّمَتِ الْخَمْرُ. فَقَالُوا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، نَنْتَفِعُ بِهَا كَمَا قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى. قَالَ: فَسَكَتَ عَنْهُمْ ثُمَّ نَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ: ﴿لَا تَقْرَبُوا الصَّلاةَ وَأَنْتُمْ سُكَارَى﴾ [النِّسَاءِ: ٤٣] . فَقِيلَ: حُرِّمَتِ الْخَمْرُ، فَقَالُوا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنَّا لَا نَشْرَبُهَا قُرْبَ الصَّلَاةِ، فَسَكَتَ عَنْهُمْ ثُمَّ نَزَلَتْ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّمَا الْخَمْرُ وَالْمَيْسِرُ وَالأنْصَابُ وَالأزْلامُ رِجْسٌ مِنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطَانِ فَاجْتَنِبُوهُ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ﴾ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "حُرِّمَتِ الْخَمْرُ". 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا يَعْلَى، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الْقَعْقَاعِ بْنِ حَكِيمٍ؛ أَنَّ عَبْدَ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنَ وَعْلَة قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ عَنْ بَيْعِ الْخَمْرِ، فَقَالَ: كَانَ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ صَدِيقٌ مِنْ ثَقِيفٍ -أَوْ: مِنْ دَوْسٍ-فَلَقِيَهُ يَوْمَ الْفَتْحِ بِرَاوِيَةِ خَمْرٍ يُهْدِيهَا إِلَيْهِ، فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "يَا فُلَانُ، أَمَا عَلِمْتَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ حَرَّمَهَا؟ " فَأَقْبَلَ الرَّجُلُ عَلَى غُلَامِهِ فَقَالَ: اذْهَبْ فَبِعْهَا. فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "يَا فُلَانُ، بِمَاذَا أَمَرْتَهُ؟ " فَقَالَ: أَمَرْتُهُ أَنْ يَبِيعَهَا. قَالَ: "إِنَّ الَّذِي حَرَّمَ شُرْبَهَا حَرَّمَ بَيْعَهَا". فَأَمَرَ بِهَا فَأُفْرِغَتْ فِي الْبَطْحَاءِ.

رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ مِنْ طَرِيقِ ابْنِ وَهْب، عَنْ مَالِكٍ، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ. وَمِنْ طَرِيقِ ابْنِ وَهْبٍ أَيْضًا، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ بِلَالٍ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ كِلَاهُمَا -عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ وَعْلَة، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، بِهِ. وَرَوَاهُ النَّسَائِيُّ، عَنْ قُتَيْبَةَ، عَنْ مَالِكٍ، بِهِ. 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ الْحَافِظُ أَبُو يَعْلَى الْمَوْصِلِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي بَكْرٍ الْمُقَدِّمِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ الْحَنَفِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْحَمِيدِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، عَنْ شَهْر بْنِ حَوْشَب، عَنْ تَمِيمٍ الدَّارِيِّ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَهْدِي لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ رَاوِيَةً مِنْ خَمْرٍ، فَلَمَّا أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ تَحْرِيمَ الْخَمْرِ جَاءَ بِهَا، فَلَمَّا رَآهَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ ضَحِكَ وَقَالَ: "إِنَّهَا قَدْ حُرِّمَتْ بَعْدَكَ". قَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، فَأَبِيعُهَا وَأَنْتَفِعُ بِثَمَنِهَا؟ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "لَعَنَ اللَّهُ الْيَهُودَ، حَرُمَ عَلَيْهِمْ شُحُوم الْبَقَرِ وَالْغَنَمِ، فَأَذَابُوهُ، وَبَاعُوهُ، وَاللَّهُ حَرّم الْخَمْرَ وَثَمَنَهَا". 

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ أَيْضًا الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ فَقَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا رَوْح، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْحَمِيدِ بْنُ بَهْرام قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ شَهْرَ بْنَ حَوْشَبٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ غَنْم: أَنَّ الدَّارِيَّ كَانَ يهدي لرسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم كُلَّ عَامٍ رَاوِيَةً مِنْ خَمْرٍ، فَلَمَّا كَانَ عَامُ حُرّمت جَاءَ بِرَاوِيَةٍ، فَلَمَّا نَظَرَ إِلَيْهِ ضَحِكَ فَقَالَ أَشْعَرْتَ أَنَّهَا حُرِّمَتْ بَعْدَكَ؟ " فَقَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، أَلَّا أَبِيعُهَا وَأَنْتَفِعُ بِثَمَنِهَا؟ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "لَعَنَ اللَّهُ الْيَهُودَ، انْطَلَقُوا إِلَى مَا حُرّم عليهم من شحم البقر والغنم فأذابوه، فباعوه به ما يأكلون، وإن الخمر حرام وَثَمَنُهَا حَرَامٌ، وَإِنَّ الْخَمْرَ حَرَامٌ وَثَمَنُهَا حَرَامٌ، وَإِنَّ الْخَمْرَ حَرَامٌ وَثَمَنُهَا حَرَامٌ". 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ لَهِيعَة، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ نَافِعِ بْنِ كَيسان أَنَّ أَبَاهُ أَخْبَرَهُ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَتَّجِرُ فِي الْخَمْرِ فِي زَمَنِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ، وَأَنَّهُ أَقْبَلَ مِنَ الشَّامِ وَمَعَهُ خَمْرٌ فِي الزُّقَاقِ، يُرِيدُ بِهَا التِّجَارَةَ، فَأَتَى بِهَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَقَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنِّي جِئْتُكَ بِشَرَابٍ طَيِّبٍ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "يَا كَيْسَانُ، إِنَّهَا قَدْ حُرِّمَتْ بَعْدَكَ". قَالَ: فَأَبِيعُهَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ؟ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "إِنَّهَا قَدْ حُرِّمَتْ وَحَرُمَ ثَمَنُهَا". فَانْطَلَقَ كَيْسَانُ إِلَى الزُّقَاقِ، فَأَخَذَ بِأَرْجُلِهَا ثُمَّ هَرَاقَهَا. 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ قَالَ: كُنْتُ أَسْقِي أَبَا عُبَيْدَةَ بْنَ الْجَرَّاحِ، وَأُبَيَّ بْنَ كَعْب، وسُهَيْل بْنَ بَيْضَاءَ، وَنَفَرًا مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ عِنْدَ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ وَأَنَا أَسْقِيهِمْ، حَتَّى كَادَ الشَّرَابُ يَأْخُذُ مِنْهُمْ، فَأَتَى آتٍ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ فَقَالَ: أَمَا شَعَرْتُمْ أَنَّ الْخَمْرَ قَدْ حُرِّمَتْ؟ فَمَا قَالُوا: حَتَّى نَنْظُرَ وَنَسْأَلَ، فَقَالُوا: يَا أَنَسُ اكْفِ مَا بَقِيَ فِي إِنَائِكَ، فَوَاللَّهِ مَا عَادُوا فِيهَا، وَمَا هِيَ إِلَّا التَّمْرُ وَالْبُسْرُ، وَهِيَ خَمْرُهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ. 

أَخْرَجَاهُ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ -مِنْ غَيْرِ وَجْهٍ-عَنْ أَنَسٍ وَفِي رِوَايَةِ حَمَّادُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ ثَابِتٍ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ قَالَ: كنتُ سَاقِيَ الْقَوْمِ يَوْمَ حُرّمت الْخَمْرُ فِي بَيْتِ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، وَمَا شَرَابُهُمْ إِلَّا الفَضيخ البسرُ والتمرُ، فَإِذَا مُنَادٍ يُنَادِي، قَالَ: اخْرُجْ فَانْظُرْ. فَإِذَا مُنَادٍ يُنَادِي: أَلَا إِنَّ الْخَمْرَ قَدْ حُرّمت، فَجرت فِي سِكَكِ الْمَدِينَةِ، قَالَ: فَقَالَ لِي أَبُو طَلْحَةَ: اخْرُجْ فَأهْرقها. فَهَرَقْتُهَا، فَقَالُوا -أَوْ: قَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ: قُتل فُلَانٌ وَفُلَانٌ وَهِيَ فِي بُطُونِهِمْ. قَالَ: فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿لَيْسَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ جُنَاحٌ فِيمَا طَعِمُوا﴾ الْآيَةَ. 

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَشَّار، حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ الْكَبِيرِ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْمَجِيدِ حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ رَاشِدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ قَالَ: بَيْنَمَا أَنَا أُدِيرُ الْكَأْسَ عَلَى أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، وَأَبِي عُبَيْدَةَ بْنِ الْجَرَّاحِ، وَمُعَاذِ بْنِ جَبَلٍ، وَسُهَيْلِ بْنِ بَيْضَاءَ، وَأَبِي دُجَانة، حَتَّى مَالَتْ رُؤُوسُهُمْ مِنْ خَليط بُسْر وَتَمْرٍ. فَسَمِعْتُ مُنَادِيًا يُنَادِي: أَلَا إِنَّ الْخَمْرَ قَدْ حُرّمت! قَالَ: فَمَا دَخَلَ عَلَيْنَا دَاخِلٌ وَلَا خَرَجَ مِنَّا خَارِجٌ، حَتَّى أَهْرَقْنَا الشَّرَابَ، وَكَسَرْنَا الْقِلَالَ، وَتَوَضَّأَ بَعْضُنَا وَاغْتَسَلَ بَعْضُنَا، وَأَصَبْنَا مِنْ طِيبِ أُمِّ سُلَيْمٍ، ثُمَّ خَرَجْنَا إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ، فَإِذَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَقْرَأُ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّمَا الْخَمْرُ وَالْمَيْسِرُ وَالأنْصَابُ وَالأزْلامُ رِجْسٌ مِنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطَانِ فَاجْتَنِبُوهُ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ﴾

إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿فَهَلْ أَنْتُمْ مُنْتَهُونَ﴾ فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، فَمَا مَنْزِلَةُ مَنْ مَاتَ وَهُوَ يَشْرَبُهَا؟ فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿لَيْسَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ جُنَاحٌ فِيمَا طَعِمُوا [إِذَا مَا اتَّقَوْا وَآمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ] ﴾ الْآيَةَ، فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ لِقَتَادَةَ: أَنْتَ سَمعتَه مَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ. وَقَالَ رَجُلٌ لِأَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ: أَنْتَ سَمِعْتَهُ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ -أَوْ: حَدَّثَنِي مَنْ لَمْ يَكْذِبْ، مَا كُنَّا نَكْذِبُ، وَلَا نَدْرِي مَا الْكَذِبُ. 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، أَخْبَرَنِي يَحْيَى بْنُ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ زَحر، عَنْ بَكْرِ بْنِ سَوَادَةَ، عَنْ قَيْسِ بْنِ سَعْدِ بْنِ عُبَادَةَ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "إِنَّ رَبِّي تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى حَرَّمَ عَلَيّ الْخَمْرَ، والكُوبَة، وَالْقِنِّينَ. وَإِيَّاكُمْ والغُبيراء فَإِنَّهَا ثُلُثُ خَمْرِ الْعَالَمِ". 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ، حَدَّثَنَا فَرَجُ بْنُ فَضَالَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ رَافِعٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرو قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "إِنَّ اللَّهَ حَرَّمَ عَلَى أُمَّتِي الْخَمْرَ وَالْمَيْسِرَ والمزْر، والكُوبة والقِنّين. وَزَادَنِي صَلَاةَ الْوَتْرِ". قَالَ يَزِيدُ: الْقِنِّينُ: الْبَرَابِطُ. تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ أَحْمَدُ. 

وَقَالَ أَحْمَدُ أَيْضًا: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَاصِمٍ -وَهُوَ النَّبِيلُ-أَخْبَرْنَا عَبْدُ الْحَمِيدِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي حَبِيبٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْوَلِيدِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "مَنْ قَالَ عَلَيَّ مَا لَمْ أَقُلْ فَلْيَتَبَوَّأْ مَقْعَدَهُ مِنْ جَهَنَّمَ". قَالَ: وَسَمِعَتْ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم يقول: "إِنَّ اللَّهَ حَرَّمَ الْخَمْرَ وَالْمَيْسِرَ وَالْكُوبَةَ وَالْغُبَيْرَاءَ، وَكُلُّ مُسْكِرٍ حَرَامٌ". تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ أَحْمَدُ أَيْضًا 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيع، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْعَزِيزِ بْنُ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، عَنْ أَبِي طُعْمَةَ -مَوْلَاهُمْ-وَعَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْغَافِقِيِّ أَنَّهُمَا سَمِعَا ابْنَ عُمَرَ يَقُولُ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "لُعِنَتِ الْخَمْرُ عَلَى عَشَرَةِ وُجُوهٍ: لُعِنَتِ الْخَمْرُ بِعَيْنِهَا وَشَارِبِهَا، وَسَاقِيهَا، وَبَائِعِهَا، ومُبتاعها، وَعَاصِرِهَا، ومُعتصرها، وَحَامِلِهَا، وَالْمَحْمُولَةِ إِلَيْهِ، وَآكُلِ ثَمَنِهَا".

وَرَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ وَكِيعٍ، بِهِ. 

وَقَالَ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا حَسَنٌ، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ لَهِيعة، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو طِعْمة، سَمِعْتُ ابْنَ عُمَرَ يَقُولُ: خَرَجَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ إِلَى الْمِرْبَدِ، فَخَرَجْتُ مَعَهُ فَكُنْتُ عَنْ يَمِينِهِ، وَأَقْبَلَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ فَتَأَخَّرْتُ عَنْهُ، فَكَانَ عَنْ يَمِينِهِ وَكُنْتُ عَنْ يَسَارِهِ. ثُمَّ أَقْبَلَ عُمَرُ فَتَنَحَّيْتُ لَهُ، فَكَانَ عَنْ يَسَارِهِ. فَأَتَى رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ الْمِرْبَدَ، فَإِذَا بِزُقَاقٍ عَلَى الْمِرْبَدِ فِيهَا خَمْرٌ -قَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ-: فَدَعَانِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ بِالْمُدْيَةِ -قَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ: وَمَا عَرَفْتُ الْمُدْيَةَ إِلَّا يَوْمَئِذٍ -فَأَمَرَ بِالزِّقَاقِ فَشُقَّتْ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: "لُعِنَتِ الْخَمْرُ وَشَارِبُهَا، وساقيها، وبائعها، ومبتاعها، وَحَامِلُهَا، وَالْمَحْمُولَةُ إِلَيْهِ، وَعَاصِرُهَا، وَمُعْتَصِرُهَا، وَآكِلُ ثَمَنِهَا". 

وَقَالَ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا الْحَكَمُ بْنُ نَافِعٍ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنِ أَبِي مَرْيَمَ، عَنْ ضَمْرة بْنِ حَبِيبٍ قَالَ: قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُمَرَ: أَمَرَنِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ أَنْ آتِيَهُ بِمُدْيَةٍ وَهِيَ الشَّفْرَةُ، فَأَتَيْتُهُ بِهَا فَأَرْسَلَ بِهَا فَأَرْهَفْتُ ثُمَّ أَعْطَانِيهَا وَقَالَ: "اغْدُ عليَّ بِهَا". فَفَعَلْتُ فَخَرَجَ بِأَصْحَابِهِ إِلَى أَسْوَاقِ الْمَدِينَةِ، وَفِيهَا زِقَاقُ الْخَمْرِ قَدْ جُلِبَتْ مِنَ الشَّامِ، فَأَخَذَ الْمُدْيَةَ مِنِّي فَشَقَّ مَا كَانَ مِنْ تِلْكَ الزِّقَاقِ بِحَضْرَتِهِ، ثُمَّ أَعْطَانِيهَا وَأَمَرَ أَصْحَابَهَ الَّذِينَ كَانُوا مَعَهُ أَنْ يَمْضُوا مَعِي وَأَنْ يُعَاوِنُونِي، وَأَمَرَنِي أَنْ آتِيَ الْأَسْوَاقَ كُلَّهَا فَلَا أَجِدُ فِيهَا زِقَّ خَمْرٍ إِلَّا شَقَقْتُهُ، فَفَعَلْتُ، فَلَمْ أَتْرُكْ فِي أَسْوَاقِهَا زِقًّا إِلَّا شَقَقْتُهُ. 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ وَهب: أَخْبَرَنِي عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ شُرَيْح، وَابْنُ لَهِيعة، وَاللَّيْثُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ خَالِدِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ ثَابِتِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ الْخَوْلَانِيِّ أَخْبَرَهُ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَهُ عَمٌّ يَبِيعُ الْخَمْرَ، وَكَانَ يَتَصَدَّقُ، فَنَهَيْتُهُ عَنْهَا فَلَمْ يَنْتَهِ، فَقَدِمْتُ الْمَدِينَةَ فَتَلَقَّيْتُ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ، فَسَأَلَتْهُ عَنِ الْخَمْرِ وَثَمَنِهَا، فَقَالَ: هِيَ حَرَامٌ وَثَمَنُهَا حَرَامٌ. ثُمَّ قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ: يَا مَعْشَرَ أُمَّةِ مُحَمَّدٍ، إِنَّهُ لَوْ كَانَ كِتَابٌ بَعْدَ كِتَابِكُمْ، وَنَبِيٌّ بَعْدَ نَبِيِّكُمْ، لَأُنْزِلَ فِيكُمْ كَمَا أُنْزِلَ فِيمَنْ قَبْلَكُمْ، وَلَكِنْ أَخَّرَ ذَلِكَ مِنْ أَمْرِكُمْ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ، ولَعمري لَهُوَ أَشُدُّ عَلَيْكُمْ، قَالَ ثَابِتٌ: فَلَقِيتُ عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ عُمَرَ فَسَأَلَتْهُ عَنْ ثَمَنِ الْخَمْرِ، فَقَالَ: سَأُخْبِرُكَ عَنِ الْخَمْرِ، إِنِّي كُنْتُ عِنْدَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ، فَبَيْنَمَا هُوَ مُحْتَبٍ حَلّ حُبْوَته ثُمَّ قَالَ: "مَنْ كَانَ عِنْدَهُ مِنْ هَذِهِ الْخَمْرِ فَلْيَأْتِنَا بِهَا". فَجَعَلُوا يَأْتُونَهُ، فَيَقُولُ أَحَدُهُمْ: عِنْدِي رَاوِيَةٌ. وَيَقُولُ الْآخَرُ: عِنْدِي زقٌّ أَوْ: مَا شَاءَ اللَّهُ أَنْ يَكُونَ عِنْدَهُ، فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "اجْمَعُوهُ بِبَقِيعِ كَذَا وَكَذَا ثُمَّ آذِنُونِي". فَفَعَلُوا، ثُمَّ آذَنُوهُ فَقَامَ وَقُمْتُ مَعَهُ، فَمَشَيْتُ عَنْ يَمِينِهِ وَهُوَ مُتَّكِئٌ عَلَيَّ، فَأَلْحَقَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، فَأَخَّرَنِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ، فَجَعَلَنِي عَنْ شِمَالِهِ، وَجَعَلَ أَبَا بَكْرٍ فِي مَكَانِي. ثُمَّ لَحِقَنَا عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ فَأَخَّرَنِي، وَجَعَلَهُ عَنْ يَسَارِهِ، فَمَشَى بَيْنَهُمَا. حَتَّى إِذَا وَقَفَ عَلَى الْخَمْرِ قَالَ لِلنَّاسِ: "أَتَعْرِفُونَ هَذِهِ قَالُوا: نَعَمْ، يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، هَذِهِ الْخَمْرُ. قَالَ: "صَدَقْتُمْ". قَالَ: "فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَعَنَ الْخَمْرَ وَعَاصِرَهَا وَمُعْتَصِرَهَا، وَشَارِبَهَا وَسَاقِيَهَا، وَحَامِلَهَا وَالْمَحْمُولَةَ إِلَيْهِ، وَبَائِعَهَا وَمُشْتَرِيَهَا وَآكِلَ ثَمَنِهَا". ثُمَّ دَعَا بِسِكِّينٍ فَقَالَ: "اشْحَذُوهَا". فَفَعَلُوا، ثُمَّ أَخَذَهَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَخْرِقُ بِهَا الزِّقَاقَ، قَالَ: فَقَالَ النَّاسُ: فِي هَذِهِ الزِّقَاقِ مَنْفَعَةٌ، قَالَ: "أَجَلْ، وَلَكِنِّي إِنَّمَا أَفْعَلُ ذَلِكَ غَضَبًا لِلَّهِ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، لِمَا فِيهَا مِنْ سَخَطِهِ". فَقَالَ عُمَرُ: أَنَا أَكْفِيكَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ؟ قَالَ: "لَا".

قَالَ ابْنُ وَهْب: وَبَعْضُهُمْ يَزِيدُ عَلَى بَعْضٍ فِي قِصَّةِ الْحَدِيثِ. رَوَاهُ الْبَيْهَقِيُّ. 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ الْحَافِظُ أَبُو بَكْرٍ الْبَيْهَقِيُّ: أَنْبَأَنَا أَبُو الْحُسَيْنِ بْنُ بِشْران، أنبأنا إسماعيل بن محمد الصَّفَّارُ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبِيدِ اللَّهِ الْمُنَادِي، حَدَّثَنَا وَهْبُ بْنُ جَرِيرٍ، حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَة، عَنْ سِماك، عَنْ مُصْعَبِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ سَعْدٍ، قَالَ: أُنْزِلَتْ فِي الْخَمْرِ أَرْبَعُ آيَاتٍ، فَذَكَرَ الْحَدِيثَ. قَالَ: وَضَعَ رَجُلٌ مِنَ الْأَنْصَارِ طَعَامًا، فَدَعَانَا فَشَرِبْنَا الْخَمْرَ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَحْرُمَ حَتَّى انْتَشَيْنَا، فَتَفَاخَرْنَا، فَقَالَتِ الْأَنْصَارُ: نَحْنُ أَفْضَلُ. وَقَالَتْ قُرَيْشٌ: نَحْنُ أَفْضَلُ. فَأَخَذَ رَجُلٌ مِنَ الْأَنْصَارِ لَحْي جَزُور، فَضَرَبَ بِهِ أَنْفَ سَعْدٍ فَفَزَرَهُ، وَكَانَ أَنْفُ سَعْدٍ مَفْزُورًا. فَنَزَلَتْ آيَةُ الْخَمْرِ: ﴿إِنَّمَا الْخَمْرُ وَالْمَيْسِرُ [وَالأنْصَابُ وَالأزْلامُ] ﴾ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَهَلْ أَنْتُمْ مُنْتَهُونَ﴾ أَخْرَجَهُ مُسْلِمٌ مِنْ حَدِيثِ شُعْبَةَ. 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ الْبَيْهَقِيُّ: وَأَخْبَرَنَا أَبُو نَصْرِ بْنُ قَتَادَةَ، أَنْبَأَنَا أَبُو عَلِيٍّ الرَّفَّاءُ، حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، حَدَّثَنَا حَجَّاجُ بْنُ مِنْهال، حَدَّثَنَا رَبِيعَةُ بْنُ كُلْثُومٍ، حَدَّثَنِي أَبِي، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: إِنَّمَا نَزَلَ تَحْرِيمُ الْخَمْرِ فِي قَبِيلَتَيْنِ مِنْ قَبَائِلِ الْأَنْصَارِ، شَرِبُوا فَلَمَّا أن ثمل عَبِثَ بَعْضُهُمْ بِبَعْضٍ، فَلَمَّا أَنَّ صَحُوا جَعَلَ الرَّجُلُ يُرَى الْأَثَرَ بِوَجْهِهِ وَرَأْسِهِ وَلِحْيَتِهِ، فَيَقُولُ: صَنَعَ هَذَا بِي، أَخِي فُلَانٌ -وَكَانُوا إِخْوَةً لَيْسَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ ضَغَائِنُ وَاللَّهِ لَوْ كَانَ بِي رَؤُوفًا رَحِيمًا مَا صَنَعَ هَذَا بِي، حَتَّى وَقَعَتِ الضَّغَائِنُ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّمَا الْخَمْرُ وَالْمَيْسِرُ وَالأنْصَابُ وَالأزْلامُ رِجْسٌ مِنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطَانِ [فَاجْتَنِبُوهُ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ * إِنَّمَا يُرِيدُ الشَّيْطَانُ أَنْ يُوقِعَ بَيْنَكُمُ الْعَدَاوَةَ وَالْبَغْضَاءَ فِي الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ وَيَصُدَّكُمْ عَنْ ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ وَعَنِ الصَّلاةِ] فَهَلْ أَنْتُمْ مُنْتَهُونَ﴾ فَقَالَ نَاسٌ مِنَ الْمُتَكَلِّفِينَ: هِيَ رِجْسٌ، وَهِيَ فِي بَطْنِ فُلَانٍ، وَقَدْ قُتِلَ يَوْمَ أُحُدٍ، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿لَيْسَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ جُنَاحٌ فِيمَا طَعِمُوا [إِذَا مَا اتَّقَوْا وَآمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ ثُمَّ اتَّقَوْا وَآمَنُوا ثُمَّ اتَّقَوْا وَأَحْسَنُوا وَاللَّهُ يُحِبُّ الْمُحْسِنِينَ] ﴾ 

وَرَوَاهُ النَّسَائِيُّ فِي التَّفْسِيرِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحِيمِ صَاعِقَةَ، عَنْ حَجَّاجِ بْنِ مِنْهَالٍ. 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنِي مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ خَلَف، حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ الجرْمي، عَنْ أَبِي تُمَيْلَة، عَنْ سَلَامٍ مَوْلَى حَفْصٍ أَبِي الْقَاسِمِ، عَنْ أَبِي بُرَيْدَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ: بَيْنَا نَحْنُ قُعُود عَلَى شَرَابٍ لَنَا، وَنَحْنُ رَمْلة، وَنَحْنُ ثَلَاثَةٌ أَوْ أَرْبَعَةٌ، وَعِنْدَنَا بَاطِيَةٌ لَنَا، وَنَحْنُ نَشْرَبُ الْخَمْرَ حِلًّا إِذْ قُمْتُ حَتَّى آتِيَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَأُسَلِّمَ عَلَيْهِ، إِذْ نَزْلَ تَحْرِيمُ الْخَمْرِ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّمَا الْخَمْرُ وَالْمَيْسِرُ﴾ إِلَى آخِرِ الْآيَتَيْنِ ﴿فَهَلْ أَنْتُمْ مُنْتَهُونَ﴾ ؟ فَجِئْتُ إِلَى أَصْحَابِي فَقَرَأْتُهَا إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿فَهَلْ أَنْتُمْ مُنْتَهُون﴾ ؟ قَالَ: وَبَعْضُ الْقَوْمِ شَربته فِي يَدِهِ، قَدْ شَرِبَ بَعْضَهَا وَبَقِيَ بَعْضٌ فِي الْإِنَاءِ، فَقَالَ بِالْإِنَاءِ تَحْتَ شَفَتِهِ الْعُلْيَا، كَمَا يَفْعَلُ الْحَجَّامُ، ثُمَّ صَبُّوا مَا فِي بَاطِيَتِهِمْ فَقَالُوا: انْتَهَيْنَا رَبَّنَا. 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا صَدَقة بْنُ الْفَضْلِ، أَخْبَرْنَا ابْنُ عُيَينة، عن عمرو، عن جابر قَالَ: صَبَّح نَاسٌ غَدَاةَ أُحُدٍ الْخَمْرَ، فَقُتلوا مِنْ يَوْمِهِمْ جَمِيعًا شُهَدَاءَ، وَذَلِكَ قَبْلَ تَحْرِيمِهَا.

هَكَذَا رَوَاهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ فِي تَفْسِيرِهِ مِنْ صَحِيحِهِ وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ الْحَافِظُ أَبُو بَكْرٍ الْبَزَّارُ فِي مُسْنَدِهِ: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ عَبْدة، حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ دِينَارٍ سَمِعَ جَابِرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ يَقُولُ: اصْطَبَحَ نَاسٌ الْخَمْرَ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ ثم قُتِلُوا شُهَدَاءَ يَوْمَ أُحُدٍ، فَقَالَتِ الْيَهُودُ: فَقَدْ مَاتَ بَعْضُ الَّذِينَ قُتِلُوا وَهِيَ فِي بُطُونِهِمْ. فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿لَيْسَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ جُنَاحٌ فِيمَا طَعِمُوا﴾ ثُمَّ قَالَ: وَهَذَا إِسْنَادٌ صَحِيحٌ. وَهُوَ كَمَا قَالَ، وَلَكِنْ فِي سِيَاقَتِهِ غَرَابَةٌ.

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ أَبُو دَاوُدَ الطَّيَالِسِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الْبَرَاءِ بْنِ عَازِبٍ قَالَ: لَمَّا نَزَلَ تَحْرِيمُ الْخَمْرِ قَالُوا: كَيْفَ بِمَنْ كَانَ يَشْرَبُهَا قَبْلَ أَنْ تَحْرُمَ؟ فَنَزَلَتْ: ﴿لَيْسَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ جُنَاحٌ فِيمَا طَعِمُوا﴾ الْآيَةَ.

وَرَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ، عَنْ بُنْدار، غُنْدَر عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، بِهِ نَحْوٌ. وَقَالَ: حَسَنٌ صَحِيحٌ. 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ الْحَافِظُ أَبُو يُعْلَى الْمَوْصِلِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا جَعْفَرُ بْنُ حُمَيْدٍ الْكُوفِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ الْقُمِّيُّ، عَنْ عِيسَى بْنِ جَارِيَةَ، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ: كَانَ رَجُلٌ يَحْمِلُ الْخَمْرَ مِنْ خَيْبَرَ إِلَى الْمَدِينَةِ فَيَبِيعُهَا مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ، فَحُمِّلَ مِنْهَا بِمَالٍ فَقَدِمَ بِهَا الْمَدِينَةَ، فَلَقِيَهُ رَجُلٌ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ فَقَالَ: يَا فُلَانُ، إِنَّ الْخَمْرَ قَدْ حَرُمَتْ فَوَضَعَهَا حَيْثُ انْتَهَى عَلَى تَلّ، وَسَجَّى عَلَيْهَا بِأَكْسِيَةٍ، ثُمَّ أَتَى النَّبِيَّ ﷺ فَقَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، بَلَغَنِي أَنَّ الْخَمْرَ قَدْ حُرِّمَتْ؟ قَالَ: "أَجَلْ" قَالَ: لِي أَنْ أَرُدَّهَا عَلَى مَنِ ابْتَعْتُهَا مِنْهُ؟ قَالَ: "لَا يَصْلُحُ رَدُّهَا". قَالَ: لِي أَنْ أُهْدِيَهَا إِلَى مَنْ يُكَافِئُنِي مِنْهَا؟ قَالَ: "لَا". قَالَ: فَإِنَّ فِيهَا مَالًا لِيَتَامَى فِي حِجْرِي؟ قال: "إذ أَتَانَا مَالُ الْبَحْرِينِ فَأْتِنَا نعوّضُ أَيْتَامَكَ مِنْ مَالِهِمْ". ثُمَّ نَادَى بِالْمَدِينَةِ، فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، الْأَوْعِيَةُ نَنْتَفِعُ بِهَا؟. قَالَ: "فَحُلُّوا أَوْكِيَتَهَا". فَانْصَبَّتْ حَتَّى اسْتَقَرَّتْ فِي بَطْنِ الْوَادِي هَذَا حَدِيثٌ غَرِيبٌ. 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيع، حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنِ السُّدِّي، عَنْ أَبِي هُبيرة -وَهُوَ يَحْيَى بْنُ عَبَّاد الْأَنْصَارِيُّ-عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ؛ أَنَّ أَبَا طَلْحَةَ سَأَلَ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ عَنِ أَيْتَامٍ فِي حِجْرِهِ وَرِثُوا خَمْرًا فَقَالَ: "أَهْرِقْهَا". قَالَ: أَفَلَا نَجْعَلُهَا خَلًّا؟ قَالَ: "لَا".

وَرَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ، وَأَبُو دَاوُدَ، وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ الثَّوْرِيِّ، بِهِ نَحْوَهُ. 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ رَجاء، حَدَّثَنَا عبد العزيز بن أَبِي سَلَمَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا هِلَالُ بْنُ أَبِي هِلَالٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءِ بْنِ يَسَارٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو قَالَ: إِنَّ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ الَّتِي فِي الْقُرْآنِ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّمَا الْخَمْرُ وَالْمَيْسِرُ وَالأنْصَابُ وَالأزْلامُ رِجْسٌ مِنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطَانِ فَاجْتَنِبُوهُ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ﴾ قَالَ: هِيَ فِي التَّوْرَاةِ: "إِنَّ اللَّهَ أَنْزَلَ الْحَقَّ لِيُذْهِبَ بِهِ الْبَاطِلَ، وَيُبْطِلَ بِهِ اللَّعِبَ، وَالْمَزَامِيرَ، والزَّفْن، والكِبَارات -يَعْنِي الْبَرَابِطَ--وَالزَّمَّارَاتِ -يَعْنِي بِهِ الدُّفَّ-وَالطَّنَابِيرَ-وَالشِّعْرَ، وَالْخَمْرَ مَرَّةً لِمَنْ طَعِمَهَا. أَقْسَمَ اللَّهُ بِيَمِينِهِ وَعِزَّةِ حَيْله مَنْ شَرِبَهَا بَعْدَ مَا حَرَّمْتُهَا لَأُعَطِّشَنَّهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ، وَمَنْ تَرَكَهَا بَعْدَ مَا حَرَّمْتُهَا لَأَسْقِيَنَّهُ إِيَّاهَا فِي حَظِيرَةِ الْقُدْسِ".

وَهَذَا إِسْنَادٌ صَحِيحٌ.

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ وَهْب: أَخْبَرَنِي عَمْرُو بْنُ الحارث؛ أَنَّ عَمْرَو بْنَ شُعَيب حَدَّثَهُمْ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ، عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "مَنْ تَرَكَ الصَّلَاةَ سُكْرًا مَرَّةً وَاحِدَةً، فَكَأَنَّمَا كَانَتْ لَهُ الدُّنْيَا وَمَا عَلَيْهَا فَسُلِبَهَا، وَمَنْ تَرَكَ الصَّلَاةَ سُكْرًا أَرْبَعَ مَرَّاتٍ، كَانَ حَقًّا عَلَى اللَّهِ أَنْ يَسْقِيَهُ مِنْ طينة الْخَبَالِ". قِيلَ: وَمَا طِينَةُ الْخَبَالِ؟ قَالَ: "عُصَارَةُ أَهْلِ جَهَنَّمَ".

وَرَوَاهُ أَحْمَدُ، مِنْ طَرِيقِ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ. 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ أَبُو دَاوُدَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ رَافِعٍ، حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ عُمَرَ الصَّنْعَانِيُّ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ النُّعْمَانَ -هُوَ ابْنُ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ الجَنَدي-يَقُولُ عَنْ طَاوُسٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قَالَ: "كُلُّ مَخمَّر خَمْر، وَكُلُّ مُسْكِر حَرَام، وَمَنْ شَرِبَ مُسْكِرًا بُخِسَتْ صَلَاتُهُ أَرْبَعِينَ صَبَاحًا، فَإِنْ تَابَ تَابَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ، فَإِنْ عَادَ الرَّابِعَةَ كَانَ حَقًّا عَلَى اللَّهِ أَنْ يُسقيه مِنْ طِينة الخَبَال". قيل: وما طينة الْخَبَالِ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ: "صَدِيدُ أَهْلِ النَّارِ، وَمَنْ سَقَاهُ صَغِيرًا لَا يَعْرِفُ حَلَالَهُ مِنْ حَرَامِهِ، كَانَ حَقًّا عَلَى اللَّهِ أَنْ يَسْقِيَهُ مِنْ طينة الخبال"

تفرد أَبُو دَاوُدَ. 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ الشَّافِعِيُّ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ: أَنْبَأَنَا مَالِكٌ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "مَنْ شَرِبَ الْخَمْرَ فِي الدُّنْيَا، ثُمَّ لَمْ يَتُبْ مِنْهَا حُرمها فِي الْآخِرَةِ".

أَخْرَجَهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ وَمُسْلِمٌ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ مَالِكٍ، بِهِ. 

وَرَوَى مُسْلِمٌ عَنْ أَبِي الرَّبِيعِ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "كُلُّ مُسكر خَمْرٌ، وَكُلُّ مُسْكِرٍ حَرَامٌ، وَمَنْ شَرِبَ الْخَمْرَ فَمَاتَ وَهُوَ يُدْمنها وَلَمْ يَتُبْ مِنْهَا لَمْ يَشْرَبْهَا فِي الْآخِرَةِ". 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ ابْنُ وَهْب: أَخْبَرَنِي عُمَرُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ يَسار؛ أنه سمع سالم بن عَبْدِ اللَّهِ يَقُولُ: قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُمَرَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "ثَلَاثَةٌ لَا يَنْظُرُ اللَّهُ إِلَيْهِمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ الْعَاقُّ لِوَالِدَيْهِ، والمُدْمِن الْخَمْرَ، والمنَّان بِمَا أَعْطَى".

وَرَوَاهُ النَّسَائِيُّ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ زُرَيْع، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ العُمَري، بِهِ.
