وَرَوَى أَحْمَدُ، عَنْ غُنْدَر، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي زِيَادٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنِ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قَالَ: "لَا يَدْخُلُ الْجَنَّةَ منَّان وَلَا عَاقٌّ، وَلَا مُدْمِن خَمْرٍ". 

وَرَوَاهُ أَحْمَدُ أَيْضًا، عَنْ عَبْدِ الصَّمَدِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي زِيَادٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، بِهِ. وَعَنْ مَرْوَانَ بْنِ شُجَاعٍ، عَنْ خَصِيف، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، بِهِ وَرَوَاهُ النَّسَائِيُّ عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ زَكَرِيَّا، عَنِ الحسين الجَعْفِي، عن زائدة، عن بن ابْنِ أَبِي زِيَادٍ، عَنْ سَالِمِ بْنِ أَبِي الْجَعْدِ وَمُجَاهِدٍ، كِلَاهُمَا عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ، بِهِ. 

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ سَالِمِ بْنِ أَبِي الْجَعْدِ، عَنْ جَابَانَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قَالَ: "لَا يَدْخُلُ الْجَنَّةَ عَاقٌّ، وَلَا مُدْمِن خَمْرٍ، وَلَا منَّان، وَلَا وَلَدُ زنْيَة". 

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ عَنْ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ هَمَّامٍ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ سَالِمٍ، عَنْ جَابَانَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، بِهِ وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ أَيْضًا عَنْ غُنْدر وَغَيْرِهِ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ سَالِمٍ، عَنْ نُبَيْط بْنِ شُرَيط، عَنْ جَابَانَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قَالَ: "لَا يَدْخُلُ الْجَنَّةَ مَنَّانٌ، وَلَا عَاقٌّ وَالِدَيْهِ، وَلَا مُدْمِنُ خَمْرٍ".

وَرَوَاهُ النَّسَائِيُّ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ شُعْبَةَ كَذَلِكَ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: وَلَا نَعْلَمُ أَحَدًا تَابَعَ شُعْبَةَ عَنْ نُبَيْطِ بْنِ شَرِيطٍ. 

وَقَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ: لَا يُعْرَفُ لِجَابَانَ سَمَاعٌ مِنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، وَلَا لِسَالِمٍ مِنْ جَابَانَ وَلَا نُبَيْطٍ.

وَقَدْ رُوِيَ هَذَا الْحَدِيثُ مِنْ طَرِيقِ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ -وَمِنْ طَرِيقِهِ أَيْضًا، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، فَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَقَالَ الزُّهْرِيُّ: حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ بْنِ هِشَامٍ، أَنَّ أَبَاهُ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ عُثْمَانَ بْنَ عَفَّانَ يَقُولُ: اجْتَنِبُوا الْخَمْرَ، فَإِنَّهَا أُمُّ الْخَبَائِثِ، إِنَّهُ كَانَ رَجُلٌ فِيمَنْ خَلَا قَبْلَكُمْ يَتَعَبَّدُ وَيَعْتَزِلُ النَّاسَ، فَعَلقته امْرَأَةٌ غَوية، فَأَرْسَلَتْ إِلَيْهِ جَارِيَتَهَا فَقَالَتْ: إِنَّا نَدْعُوكَ لِشَهَادَةٍ. فَدَخَلَ مَعَهَا، فطفقت كُلَّمَا دَخَلَ بَابًا أَغْلَقَتْهُ دُونَهُ، حَتَّى أَفْضَى إِلَى امْرَأَةٍ وَضِيئَةٍ عِنْدَهَا غُلَامٌ وَبَاطِيَةُ خَمْرٍ، فَقَالَتْ: إِنِّي وَاللَّهِ مَا دَعَوْتُكَ لِشَهَادَةٍ وَلَكِنْ دَعَوْتُكَ لِتَقَعَ عَليّ أَوْ تَقْتُلَ هَذَا الْغُلَامَ، أَوْ تَشْرَبَ هَذَا الْخَمْرَ. فَسَقَتْهُ كَأْسًا، فَقَالَ: زِيدُونِي، فَلَمْ يَرِم حَتَّى وَقَعَ عَلَيْهَا، وَقَتَلَ النَّفْسَ، فَاجْتَنِبُوا الْخَمْرَ فَإِنَّهَا لَا تَجْتَمِعُ هِيَ وَالْإِيمَانُ أَبَدًا إِلَّا أَوْشَكَ أَحَدُهُمَا أَنْ يُخْرِجَ صَاحِبَهُ.

رَوَاهُ الْبَيْهَقِيُّ وَهَذَا إِسْنَادٌ صَحِيحٌ. وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ أَبِي الدُّنْيَا فِي كِتَابِهِ "ذَمُّ الْمُسْكِرِ" عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ بَزِيع، عَنِ الْفُضَيْلِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ النُّمَيْرِيِّ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، بِهِ مَرْفُوعًا وَالْمَوْقُوفُ أَصَحُّ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَلَهُ شَاهِدٌ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ، عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: "لَا يَزْنِي الزَّانِي حِينَ يَزْنِي وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ، وَلَا يَسْرِقُ سَرِقَةً حِينَ يَسْرِقُهَا وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ، وَلَا يَشْرَبُ الْخَمْرَ حِينَ يَشْرَبُهَا وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ". 

وَقَالَ أَحْمَدُ بْنُ حَنْبَلٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَسْوَدُ بْنُ عَامِرٍ، أَخْبَرْنَا إِسْرَائِيلُ، عَنْ سِماك، عَنْ عِكْرِمَة، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: لَمَّا حُرِّمَتِ الْخَمْرُ قَالَ أُنَاسٌ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، أَصْحَابُنَا الَّذِينَ مَاتُوا وَهُمْ يَشْرَبُونَهَا؟ فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿لَيْسَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ جُنَاحٌ فِيمَا طَعِمُوا﴾ الْآيَةَ. قَالَ: وَلَمَّا حُوّلت الْقِبْلَةُ قَالَ أُنَاسٌ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، أَصْحَابُنَا الَّذِينَ مَاتُوا وَهُمْ يُصَلُّونَ إِلَى بَيْتِ الْمَقْدِسِ؟ فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿وَمَا كَانَ اللَّهُ لِيُضِيعَ إِيمَانَكُمْ ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ:١٤٣] .

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا دَاوُدُ بْنُ مِهْران الدَّبَّاغُ، حَدَّثَنَا دَاوُدُ -يَعْنِي الْعَطَّارَ-عَنِ ابْنِ خُثَيْم، عَنْ شَهْرِ بْنِ حَوْشَب، عَنْ أَسْمَاءَ بِنْتِ يَزِيدَ، أَنَّهَا سَمِعَتِ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ يَقُولُ: "من شَرِبَ الْخَمْرَ لَمْ يَرْضَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ أَرْبَعِينَ لَيْلَةً، إِنْ مَاتَ مَاتَ كَافِرًا، وَإِنْ تَابَ تَابَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ. وَإِنْ عَادَ كَانَ حَقًّا عَلَى اللَّهِ أَنْ يَسْقِيَهُ من طِينة الخَبَال". قَالَتْ: قُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، وَمَا طِينَةُ الْخَبَالِ؟ قَالَ: "صَدِيدُ أَهْلِ النَّارِ". 

وَقَالَ الْأَعْمَشُ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ عَلْقَمَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ قَالَ لَمَّا نَزَلَتْ: ﴿لَيْسَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ جُنَاحٌ فِيمَا طَعِمُوا إِذَا مَا اتَّقَوْا وَآمَنُوا﴾ فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "قِيلَ لِي: أَنْتَ مِنْهُمْ".

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ، وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ، وَالنَّسَائِيُّ، مِنْ طَرِيقِهِ. 

وَقَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ الْإِمَامِ أَحْمَدَ: قَرَأْتُ عَلَى أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَاصِمٍ، حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ الْهِجْرِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "إِيَّاكُمْ وَهَاتَانِ الْكَعْبَتَانِ الْمُوَسَّمَتَانِ اللتان تزجران زجرًا، فإنهما مَيْسِر العَجَم".

---

## 5:94-95 — verses 94-95 (shared commentary)

### Translation

Al-Walibi said, on the authority of Ibn Abbas, regarding His saying, {Allah will surely test you with something of the game that your hands and your spears can reach}: it refers to the weak among the game and its young ones. Allah tests His servants with it during their ihram, such that if they wished, they could take it with their hands. So Allah forbade them from approaching it.

Mujahid said: {that your hands can reach} means the small game and its young, {and your spears} means the large game.

Muqatil ibn Hayyan said: this verse was revealed during the Umrah of al-Hudaybiyyah. The wild game, birds, and animals surrounded them in their camps, the like of which they had never seen before. So Allah forbade them from killing it while they were in the state of ihram.

{So that Allah may know who fears Him in the unseen} means: He, exalted is He, tests them with game that surrounds them in their camps, such that they are able to take it with their hands and spears, secretly or openly, in order to make manifest the obedience of whoever among them obeys, in his secret and his open conduct, just as He, exalted is He, said: {Indeed, those who fear their Lord in the unseen will have forgiveness and a great reward} [al-Mulk: 12].

As for His saying here, {But whoever transgresses after that}, al-Suddi and others said it means: after this notification, warning, and prior instruction, {for him is a painful punishment}, that is, because of his opposition to Allah's command and His law.

Then He, exalted is He, said: {O you who believe, do not kill game while you are in the state of ihram}. This is a prohibition from Him, exalted is He, of killing game while in the state of ihram, and a forbiddance of engaging in it during that state. In meaning, this applies to what is edible and what derives from it and from other than it. As for what is not edible among land animals, according to al-Shafi'i it is permissible for the muhrim (person in ihram) to kill it. The majority hold that killing it is likewise forbidden, with the exception of what is established in the two Sahihs, through the route of al-Zuhri, from Urwah, from Aishah, Mother of the Believers, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Five are wicked creatures that may be killed both inside and outside the sacred precinct: the crow, the kite, the scorpion, the mouse, and the biting dog."

Malik said, from Nafi, from Ibn Umar, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "There are five animals for which there is no sin upon the muhrim in killing them: the crow, the kite, the scorpion, the mouse, and the biting dog." Both [al-Bukhari and Muslim] recorded it.

Ayyub also narrated it, from Nafi, from Ibn Umar, similarly. Ayyub said: I asked Nafi about the snake. He said: there is no doubt about the snake, and there is no disagreement about killing it.

Some scholars, such as Malik and Ahmad, added to the biting dog the wolf, the lion, the leopard, and the cheetah, since they are more harmful than it. And Allah knows best. Sufyan ibn Uyaynah and Zayd ibn Aslam said: the biting dog encompasses all these predatory, aggressive beasts. Those who held this view took support from what was reported, that when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ invoked against Utbah ibn Abi Lahab, he said: "O Allah, set your dog upon him in Syria," and a wild beast devoured him at al-Zarqa. They said: if he kills anything other than these five, he owes compensation for it, such as the hyena, the fox, the wildcat, and the like.

Malik said: likewise excepted from this are the young of these five explicitly mentioned animals, and the young of the predatory beasts added to them.

Al-Shafi'i, may Allah have mercy on him, said: it is permissible for the muhrim to kill everything whose flesh is not eaten, with no distinction between its young and its adults. He made the unifying reason (illah) the fact that it is not eaten.

Abu Hanifah said: the muhrim may kill the biting dog and the wolf, since it is a wild dog. If he kills anything other than these two, he owes compensation, except if some other predator attacks him and he kills it, in which case he owes no compensation. This is also the view of al-Awza'i and al-Hasan ibn Salih ibn Hayy.

Zufar ibn al-Hudhayl said: he must pay compensation for anything besides these, even if it attacked him.

Some people said: what is meant by the crow here is the pied crow, that is, the one whose belly and back have white on them, as opposed to the entirely black crow and the entirely white one. This is based on what al-Nasa'i narrated from Amr ibn Ali al-Fallas, from Yahya al-Qattan, from Shu'bah, from Qatadah, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, from Aishah, from the Prophet ﷺ, that he said: "There are five that the muhrim may kill: the snake, the mouse, the kite, the pied crow, and the biting dog."

The majority hold that what is meant is more general than that, based on what is established in the two Sahihs where the term is used unqualified.

Malik, may Allah have mercy on him, said: the muhrim should not kill the crow unless it attacks him and harms him.

Mujahid ibn Jabr and a group said: he should not kill it but should throw stones at it to drive it away. The same is reported from Ali.

Hushaym narrated: [chain] Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad, from Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Nu'm, from Abu Sa'id, from the Prophet ﷺ, that he was asked what the muhrim may kill, and he said: "The snake, the scorpion, and the little vicious mouse, and he should throw stones at the crow but not kill it, and the biting dog, and the kite, and the aggressive predator."

Abu Dawud narrated it from Ahmad ibn Hanbal, and al-Tirmidhi from Ahmad ibn Mani, both from Hushaym. Ibn Majah narrated it from Abu Kurayb, from Muhammad ibn Fudayl, both from Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad, who is weak, with this chain. Al-Tirmidhi said: this is a hasan (sound) hadith.

Regarding His saying, exalted is He, {And whoever among you kills it intentionally, then the compensation is the equivalent of what he killed, of livestock}: Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Ayyub said: I was informed, on the authority of Tawus, who said: judgment is not passed on one who strikes game by mistake; judgment is only passed on one who does so intentionally.

This is a gharib (unusual) position from Tawus, and he holds to the apparent wording of the verse.

Mujahid ibn Jubayr said: what is meant by "intentional" here is one who deliberately kills the game while having forgotten his state of ihram. As for one who deliberately kills game while remembering his ihram, his matter is more serious than to be atoned for merely by compensation, and his ihram has become void.

Ibn Jarir narrated this from him through the route of Ibn Abi Najih and Layth ibn Abi Sulaym and others, from him. This too is a gharib position. What the majority hold is that the one who does so intentionally and the one who does so forgetfully are equal in the obligation of compensation upon them. Al-Zuhri said: the Book indicates the case of the intentional one, and the Sunnah established the ruling for the forgetful one. The meaning of this is that the Qur'an indicates the obligation of compensation upon the one who acts intentionally, and indicates his sinfulness, by His saying: {that he may taste the consequence of his conduct. Allah has forgiven what has passed, but whoever repeats it, Allah will take retribution from him}. And the Sunnah came, through the rulings of the Prophet ﷺ and the rulings of his companions, establishing the obligation of compensation in the case of mistake, just as the Book indicated it in the case of intent. Moreover, killing game is destruction of property, and destruction of property entails liability whether done intentionally or through forgetfulness, except that the one who acts intentionally is sinful while the one who errs is not blamed.

Regarding His saying, {then the compensation is the equivalent of what he killed, of livestock}: Ibn Jarir related that Ibn Mas'ud read it as: "then its compensation is the equivalent of what he killed, of livestock."

In His saying, {then the compensation is the equivalent of what he killed, of livestock}, according to either reading, there is evidence for the position taken by Malik, al-Shafi'i, Ahmad, and the majority, namely the obligation of compensation equivalent to what the muhrim killed, when the killed animal has an equivalent among domestic animals. This is contrary to Abu Hanifah, may Allah have mercy on him, who required payment of the value, whether or not the game killed had an equivalent, saying that the person has a choice: if he wishes he may give its price in charity, and if he wishes he may buy a sacrificial animal with it. What the Companions ruled regarding equivalents is more worthy of being followed, for they ruled that for an ostrich a camel is due, for a wild cow a domestic cow is due, and for a gazelle a goat is due. The mention of the Companions' rulings and their chains of transmission is established in the Book of Rulings (Kitab al-Ahkam). As for when the game has no equivalent, Ibn Abbas ruled that its price should be given, to be taken to Mecca. Al-Bayhaqi narrated this.

His saying, {to be judged by two just men among you}, means that the compensation, whether in terms of an equivalent animal or the value where there is no equivalent, is to be judged by two just men from among the Muslims. The scholars differed regarding the killer: is it permissible for him to be one of the two judges? There are two views. One: no, because he might be suspected of bias in judging his own case. This is the position of Malik. The second: yes, based on the general wording of the verse. This is the position of al-Shafi'i and Ahmad.

Those who held the first view argued that the judge cannot also be the one judged in one and the same case.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: my father reported to us, Abu Nu'aym al-Fadl ibn Dukayn reported to us, Ja'far, meaning Ibn Burqan, reported to us, from Maymun ibn Mihran, that a bedouin came to Abu Bakr and said: "I killed game while I was in the state of ihram, so what do you see as the required compensation upon me?" Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, said to Ubayy ibn Ka'b, who was sitting with him: "What do you think about what he has said?" The bedouin said: "I came to you because you are the caliph of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, asking you, yet here you are asking someone other than yourself?" Abu Bakr said: "What do you find objectionable about that? Allah, exalted is He, says: {then a compensation of the like of what he killed from the cattle, to be judged by two just men among you}. So I consulted my companion, and when we agree upon a matter, we will inform you of it."

This is a good chain of transmission, but it is disconnected between Maymun and al-Siddiq, and such a thing is plausible here. Al-Siddiq clarified the ruling to him with gentleness and calm, since he saw that he was an ignorant bedouin, and the remedy for ignorance is teaching. But if the one raising the objection is someone attributed to knowledge, then consider what Ibn Jarir said:

Hannad and Abu Hisham al-Rifa'i reported to us, they said: Waki' ibn al-Jarrah reported to us, from al-Mas'udi, from Abd al-Malik ibn 'Umayr, from Qabisah ibn Jabir, who said: We set out as pilgrims, and whenever we had prayed the dawn prayer we would lead our mounts along, walking together and talking. He said: One morning, a gazelle appeared before us, or crossed our path, and a man who was with us threw a stone at it, striking it right on the base of its skull, so that it fell dead where it had been struck. He said: We made a grave matter of it before him. When we arrived in Mecca, I went with him until we came to 'Umar, may Allah be pleased with him. He said: The man related the story to him. He said: Beside 'Umar sat a man whose face was like a piece of polished silver, meaning Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf. 'Umar turned to his companion and spoke with him. Then he turned to the man and said: "Did you kill it deliberately or by mistake?" The man said: "I aimed deliberately at throwing at it, but I did not intend to kill it." 'Umar said: "I see that you have combined intent and mistake together. Go and take a sheep, slaughter it, give its meat in charity, and keep its hide for yourself." He said: We got up from before him, and I said to my companion: "O man, magnify the sacred rites of Allah! The Commander of the Faithful did not even know what ruling to give you until he asked his companion: go to your she-camel and slaughter it, and he did so."

Qabisah said: And I do not remember [precisely] the verse from Surat al-Ma'idah: {to be judged by two just men among you}. He said: My statement reached 'Umar, and he suddenly came upon us with the whip in hand. He said: He then began striking my companion severely with the whip, saying: "You kill within the sacred precinct and belittle the ruling?" He said: Then he turned to me, and I said: "O Commander of the Faithful, I will not permit you today anything from me that is forbidden to you." He said: "O Qabisah ibn Jabir, I see that you are young in age, broad in chest, clear in speech, and indeed a young man may have nine good character traits and one bad one, and the bad trait can corrupt the good traits, so beware of the missteps of youth."

Hushaym also related this story from Abd al-Malik ibn 'Umayr, from Qabisah, in similar fashion. He also related it from Husayn, from al-Sha'bi, from Qabisah, in similar fashion. Bakr ibn Abdullah al-Muzani and Muhammad ibn Sirin also mentioned it as a mursal (report with a missing link at the end) report from 'Umar.

Ibn Jarir said: Ibn Bashshar reported to us, Abd al-Rahman reported to us, Shu'bah reported to us, from Mansur, from Abu Wa'il, Abu Jarir al-Bajali informed me, saying: I struck down a gazelle while I was in the state of ihram, and I mentioned that to 'Umar, and he said: "Go to two men from among your brothers, and let them give a ruling concerning you." So I went to Abd al-Rahman and Sa'd, and they ruled that I owed a reddish-white male goat.

Ibn Jarir said: Ibn Waki' reported to us, Ibn 'Uyaynah reported to us, from Mukharriq, from Tariq, who said: Arbad's mount trampled a gazelle, killing it, while he was in the state of ihram. He came to 'Umar so that he might rule on the matter, and 'Umar said to him: "Give a ruling along with me." So the two of them ruled that a young goat, one that had reached the point of both drinking water and grazing on trees, was owed. Then 'Umar said: {to be judged by two just men among you}.

In this there is an indication of the permissibility of the one who committed the killing being one of the two arbiters, as al-Shafi'i and Ahmad, may Allah have mercy on them both, have said.

They differed as to whether the ruling must be newly rendered for every case that a person in ihram happens into, requiring two just men to judge it, even if the Companions had already given a ruling on a similar case before, or whether one may rely on the earlier rulings of the Companions. There are two positions on this. Al-Shafi'i and Ahmad said: one follows what the Companions ruled in that matter, and they made this an established legal precedent from which one does not depart, while whatever the Companions did not rule upon is to be referred to two just men. Malik and Abu Hanifah said: rather, a ruling is required for every single individual case, whether or not a similar ruling exists from the Companions, because of the statement of Allah, exalted is He: {to be judged by two just men among you}.

And His statement, exalted is He: {an offering reaching the Ka'bah}, meaning: arriving at the Ka'bah, and what is meant is its arrival within the sacred precinct, in that it be slaughtered there and its meat distributed among the poor of the sacred precinct. This is a matter agreed upon in this particular case.

And His statement: {or an expiation by feeding the needy, or the equivalent of that in fasting}, meaning: if the person in ihram cannot find the like of what he killed among the cattle, or if the game killed has no equivalent among such animals, or if we hold that there is a choice at this point among the compensation, the feeding, and the fasting, as is the position of Malik, Abu Hanifah, Abu Yusuf, Muhammad ibn al-Hasan, one of the two positions of al-Shafi'i, and the well-known position of Ahmad, may Allah have mercy on them, based on the apparent sense of the verse's word "or," for it indicates a choice. The other position is that these are to be taken in sequence.

The manner of this is that one turns to the monetary value, and the game killed is assessed at its value according to Malik, Abu Hanifah and his companions, Hammad, and Ibrahim. Al-Shafi'i said: the like of it from the cattle is assessed, as if it were available, then food is purchased with that amount and given in charity, with each poor person receiving a mudd (a measure) of it according to al-Shafi'i, Malik, and the jurists of the Hijaz, and this is what Ibn Jarir chose.

Abu Hanifah and his companions said: each poor person is to be given two mudds, and this is also the position of Mujahid.

Ahmad said: a mudd of wheat, or two mudds of anything else. If one cannot find this, or if we hold that there is a choice, one fasts a day in place of feeding each poor person.

Ibn Jarir said: others said: one fasts a day in place of each sa' (a larger measure), as in the compensation for one who indulges in comfort by shaving the head or the like, for the lawgiver commanded Ka'b ibn 'Ujrah to divide a faraq (measure) among six people, or to fast three days, and a faraq is three sa's.

They differed as to the place where this feeding should occur. Al-Shafi'i said: its place is the sacred precinct, and this is the position of 'Ata'. Malik said: the feeding takes place in the location where the game was struck down, or the nearest location to it. Abu Hanifah said: if he wishes he may feed within the sacred precinct, and if he wishes he may feed elsewhere.

Mention of the sayings of the predecessors on this matter:

Ibn Abi Hatim said: my father reported to us, Yahya ibn al-Mughirah reported to us, Jarir reported to us, from Mansur, from al-Hakam, from Miqsam, from Ibn Abbas, concerning His statement: {then a compensation of the like of what he killed from the cattle, to be judged by two just men among you, an offering reaching the Ka'bah, or an expiation by feeding the needy, or the equivalent of that in fasting}, he said: when the person in ihram strikes down game, a compensation from the cattle is judged against him. If he finds his required compensation, he slaughters it and gives it in charity. If he cannot find it, its price is examined, then its price is assessed in terms of food, and he fasts a day in place of each half sa'. He said, concerning: {or an expiation by feeding the needy, or the equivalent of that in fasting}: what is meant by the food is really the fasting, for when the food is found, the compensation for it is found.

Ibn Jarir also related it, through the route of Jarir.

Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas, concerning: {an offering reaching the Ka'bah, or an expiation by feeding the needy, or the equivalent of that in fasting}: when the person in ihram kills some game, a ruling is made against him regarding it. If he kills a gazelle or the like of it, he owes a sheep to be slaughtered in Mecca. If he cannot find one, then feeding six poor persons. If he cannot do that, then fasting three days. If he kills a camel or the like of it, he owes a cow. If he cannot find one, he feeds twenty poor persons. If he cannot do that, he fasts twenty days. And if he kills an ostrich or a wild donkey or the like of it, he owes a badanah (a large sacrificial camel) from the camels. If he cannot find one, he feeds thirty poor persons. If he cannot do that, he fasts thirty days.

Ibn Abi Hatim and Ibn Jarir related this, and added: the food is a mudd, a mudd, sufficient to satisfy them.

Jabir al-Ju'fi said, from 'Amir al-Sha'bi, 'Ata', and Mujahid, concerning: {or the equivalent of that in fasting}, they said: the feeding is only for one who cannot reach the offering. Ibn Jarir related this.

And likewise Ibn Jurayj related from Mujahid, and Asbat from al-Suddi, that these are to be taken in sequence.

'Ata, Ikrimah, and Mujahid, in the narration of al-Dahhak, and Ibrahim al-Nakha'i said: it is a matter of choice. This is also the narration of al-Layth, from Mujahid, from Ibn 'Abbas. Ibn Jarir, may Allah have mercy on him, chose this view.

Regarding His saying, {that he may taste the evil consequence of his conduct}: that is, We made expiation obligatory upon him so that he might taste the punishment of the act in which he committed the violation. {Allah has forgiven what has passed}: that is, in the time of the Jahiliyyah (pre-Islamic ignorance), for one who has done good in Islam and followed Allah's law and has not committed the transgression.

Then He said: {but whoever returns, Allah will take retribution from him}: that is, whoever does that after it has been prohibited in Islam and after the legal ruling has reached him, Allah will take retribution from him, {and Allah is Mighty, Possessor of Retribution}.

Ibn Jurayj said: I asked 'Ata: what is meant by {Allah has forgiven what has passed}? He said: what occurred during the Jahiliyyah. I said: and what is meant by {but whoever returns, Allah will take retribution from him}? He said: whoever returns to it within Islam, Allah will take retribution from him, and along with that the expiation remains due upon him. I said: is there a fixed limit to the "returning" that you know of? He said: No. I said: do you consider it a duty upon the ruler (imam) to punish him? He said: No, it is a sin he has committed between himself and Allah, Mighty and Majestic, but he must pay the ransom (expiation). Ibn Jarir related this.

It has also been said that its meaning is: Allah will take retribution from him through the expiation itself. This was said by Sa'id ibn Jubayr and 'Ata.

The majority of the early and later scholars hold that whenever a muhrim (one in the state of ihram) kills game, the compensation becomes obligatory, with no difference between the first instance and the second, however many times it is repeated, whether the killing was by mistake or intentional.

'Ali ibn Abi Talhah reported from Ibn 'Abbas that he said: whoever kills game by mistake while in ihram, judgment (regarding compensation) is passed upon him every time he kills it. But if he kills it intentionally, judgment is passed upon him only once; if he returns to it again, he is told: Allah will take retribution from you, as Allah, Mighty and Majestic, has said.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] from Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas, concerning one who kills game and judgment is passed upon him and then he returns to it: he said: no judgment is passed upon him again; Allah will take retribution from him.

Shurayh, Mujahid, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, al-Hasan al-Basri, and Ibrahim al-Nakha'i said likewise. Ibn Jarir related these views, then he chose the first opinion.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] from al-Hasan al-Basri, that a man killed game and it was overlooked for him, then he returned and killed another animal of game, so fire descended from the sky and burned him. This is the meaning of His saying: {but whoever returns, Allah will take retribution from him}.

Ibn Jarir said, regarding His statement {and Allah is Mighty, Possessor of Retribution}: He, exalted is His mention, says: Allah is unassailable in His dominion; no overpowering one can overpower Him, and nothing prevents Him from taking retribution against whomever He wills to take retribution from, nor is there anything that can prevent the punishment of whomever He intends to punish, for creation is His creation and command is His command; His is might and unassailable power.

Regarding His saying {Possessor of Retribution}: it means that He is the one who metes out punishment to whoever disobeys Him, for his disobedience toward Him.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

قَالَ الْوَالِبِيُّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿لَيَبْلُوَنَّكُمُ اللَّهُ بِشَيْءٍ مِنَ الصَّيْدِ تَنَالُهُ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَرِمَاحُكُمْ﴾ قَالَ: هُوَ الضَّعِيفُ مِنَ الصَّيْدِ وَصَغِيرِهِ، يَبْتَلِي اللَّهُ بِهِ عباده في إحرامهم، حتى لو شاؤوا يَتَنَاوَلُونَهُ بِأَيْدِيهِمْ. فَنَهَاهُمُ اللَّهُ أَنْ يَقْرَبُوهُ.

وَقَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ: ﴿تَنَالُهُ أَيْدِيكُمْ﴾ يَعْنِي: صِغَارَ الصَّيْدِ وَفِرَاخَهُ ﴿وَرِمَاحِكُمْ﴾ يَعْنِي: كِبَارَهُ.

وَقَالَ مُقَاتِل بْنُ حَيَّان: أُنْزِلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ فِي عُمْرة الحُدَيْبِيَّة، فَكَانَتِ الْوَحْشُ وَالطَّيْرُ وَالصَّيْدُ تَغْشَاهُمْ فِي رِحَالِهِمْ، لَمْ يَرَوْا مِثْلَهُ قَطُّ فِيمَا خَلَا فَنَهَاهُمُ اللَّهُ عَنْ قَتْلِهِ وَهُمْ مُحْرِمُونَ.

﴿لِيَعْلَمَ اللَّهُ مَنْ يَخَافُهُ بِالْغَيْبِ﴾ يَعْنِي: أَنَّهُ تَعَالَى يَبْتَلِيهِمْ بِالصَّيْدِ يَغْشَاهُمْ فِي رِحَالِهِمْ، يَتَمَكَّنُونَ مِنْ أَخْذِهِ بِالْأَيْدِي وَالرِّمَاحِ سِرًّا وَجَهْرًا لِيُظْهِرَ طَاعَةَ مَنْ يُطِيعُ مِنْهُمْ فِي سِرِّهِ وَجَهْرِهِ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُمْ بِالْغَيْبِ لَهُمْ مَغْفِرَةٌ وَأَجْرٌ كَبِيرٌ﴾ [الْمُلْكِ:١٢] .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ هَاهُنَا: ﴿فَمَنِ اعْتَدَى بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ﴾ قَالَ السُّدِّيُّ وَغَيْرُهُ: يَعْنِي بَعْدَ هَذَا الْإِعْلَامِ وَالْإِنْذَارِ وَالتَّقَدُّمِ ﴿فَلَهُ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ﴾ أَيْ: لِمُخَالَفَتِهِ أَمْرَ اللَّهِ وَشَرْعَهُ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَقْتُلُوا الصَّيْدَ وَأَنْتُمْ حُرُمٌ﴾ وَهَذَا تَحْرِيمٌ مِنْهُ تَعَالَى لِقَتْلِ الصَّيْدِ فِي حَالِ الْإِحْرَامِ، وَنُهِيَ عَنْ تَعَاطِيهِ فِيهِ. وَهَذَا إِنَّمَا يَتَنَاوَلُ مِنْ حَيْثُ الْمَعْنَى الْمَأْكُولَ وَمَا يَتَوَلَّدُ مِنْهُ وَمِنْ غَيْرِهِ، فَأَمَّا غَيْرُ الْمَأْكُولِ مِنْ حَيَوَانَاتِ الْبَرِّ، فَعِنْدَ الشَّافِعِيِّ يَجُوزُ لِلْمُحْرِمِ قَتْلُهَا. وَالْجُمْهُورُ عَلَى تَحْرِيمِ قَتْلِهَا أَيْضًا، وَلَا يُسْتَثْنَى مِنْ ذَلِكَ إِلَّا مَا ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ مِنْ طَرِيقِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ عُرْوَة، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ أُمِّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "خَمْسُ فَواسِق يُقْتَلْنَ فِي الحِلِّ والحَرَم الغُراب وَالْحِدَأَةُ، والعَقْرب، وَالْفَأْرَةُ، وَالْكَلْبُ العَقُور". 

وَقَالَ مَالِكٌ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "خَمْسٌ مِنَ الدَّوَابِّ لَيْسَ عَلَى الْمُحْرِمِ فِي قَتْلِهِنَّ جُنَاح: الْغُرَابُ، وَالْحِدَأَةُ، وَالْعَقْرَبُ، وَالْفَأْرَةُ، وَالْكَلْبُ الْعَقُورُ". أَخْرَجَاهُ. 

وَرَوَاهُ أَيُّوبُ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، مِثْلَهُ. قَالَ أَيُّوبُ، قُلْتُ لِنَافِعٍ: فَالْحَيَّةُ؟ قال: الحية لا شك فِيهَا، وَلَا يُخْتَلَفُ فِي قَتْلِهَا. 

وَمِنَ الْعُلَمَاءِ -كَمَالِكٍ وَأَحْمَدَ-مَنْ أَلْحَقَ بِالْكَلْبِ الْعَقُورِ الذِّئْبَ، والسَّبْعُ، والنِّمْر، والفَهْد؛ لِأَنَّهَا أَشَدُّ ضَرَرًا مِنْهُ فَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ. وَقَالَ سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ وَزَيْدُ بْنُ أَسْلَمَ: الْكَلْبُ الْعَقُورُ يَشْمَلُ هَذِهِ السِّبَاعَ الْعَادِيَةَ كُلَّهَا. وَاسْتَأْنَسَ مَنْ قَالَ بِهَذَا بِمَا رُوِيَ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ لَمَّا دَعَا عَلَى عُتْبَةَ بْنِ أَبِي لَهَبٍ قَالَ: "اللَّهُمَّ سَلِّط عَلَيْهِ كَلْبَكَ بِالشَّامِ" فَأَكَلَهُ السَّبُعُ بِالزَّرْقَاءِ، قَالُوا: فَإِنْ قَتَلَ مَا عَدَاهُنَّ فَدَاها كَالضَّبُعِ وَالثَّعْلَبِ وَهِرِّ الْبَرِّ وَنَحْوِ ذَلِكَ.

قَالَ مَالِكٌ: وَكَذَا يُسْتَثْنَى مِنْ ذَلِكَ صِغَارُ هَذِهِ الْخَمْسِ الْمَنْصُوصِ عَلَيْهَا، وَصِغَارُ الْمُلْحَقِ بِهَا مِنَ السِّبَاعِ الْعَوَادِي.

وَقَالَ الشَّافِعِيُّ [رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ] يَجُوزُ لِلْمُحْرِمِ قَتْلُ كُلِّ مَا لَا يُؤْكَلُ لَحْمُهُ، وَلَا فَرْقَ بَيْنَ صِغَارِهِ وَكِبَارِهِ. وَجَعَلَ الْعِلَّةَ الْجَامِعَةَ كَوْنَهَا لَا تُؤْكَلُ.

وَقَالَ أَبُو حَنِيفَةَ: يَقْتُلُ الْمَحْرِمُ الْكَلْبَ الْعَقُورَ وَالذِّئْبَ؛ لِأَنَّهُ كَلْبٌ بَرِّيٌّ، فَإِنْ قَتَلَ غَيْرَهُمَا فَدَاه، إِلَّا أَنْ يَصُولَ عَلَيْهِ سَبُعٌ غَيْرُهُمَا فَيَقْتُلُهُ فَلَا فِدَاءَ عَلَيْهِ. وَهَذَا قَوْلُ الْأَوْزَاعِيِّ، وَالْحَسَنِ بْنِ صَالِحِ بْنِ حُيَيٍّ.

وَقَالَ زُفَر بْنُ الْهُذَيْلِ: يَفْدِي مَا سِوَى ذَلِكَ وَإِنْ صَالَ عَلَيْهِ.

وَقَالَ بَعْضُ النَّاسِ: الْمُرَادُ بِالْغُرَابِ هَاهُنَا الْأَبْقَعُ وَهُوَ الَّذِي فِي بَطْنِهِ وَظَهْرِهِ بَيَاضٌ، دُونَ الْأَدْرَعِ وَهُوَ الْأَسْوَدُ، وَالْأَعْصَمِ وَهُوَ الْأَبْيَضُ؛ لِمَا رَوَاهُ النَّسَائِيُّ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ عَلِيٍّ الفَلاس، عَنْ يَحْيَى القَطَّان، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قَالَ: "خَمْسٌ يَقْتُلُهُنَّ الْمُحْرِمُ: الْحَيَّةُ، وَالْفَأْرَةُ، وَالْحِدَأَةُ، وَالْغُرَابُ الْأَبْقَعُ، وَالْكَلْبُ الْعَقُورُ".

وَالْجُمْهُورُ عَلَى أَنَّ الْمُرَادَ بِهِ أَعَمُّ مِنْ ذَلِكَ؛ لِمَا ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ مِنْ إِطْلَاقِ لَفْظِهِ.

وَقَالَ مَالِكٌ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ: لَا يَقْتُلُ الْمَحْرِمُ الْغُرَابَ إِلَّا إِذَا صَالَ عَلَيْهِ وَآذَاهُ.

وَقَالَ مُجَاهِدُ بْنُ جَبْر وَطَائِفَةٌ: لَا يَقْتُلُهُ بَلْ يَرْمِيهِ. وَيُرْوَى مَثَلُهُ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ.

وَقَدْ رَوَى هُشَيْم: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي زِيَادٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي نُعْم، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ؛ أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَمَّا يَقْتُلُ الْمُحْرِمُ، فَقَالَ: "الْحَيَّةَ، وَالْعَقْرَبَ، والفُوَيْسِقَة، وَيَرْمِي الْغُرَابَ وَلَا يَقْتُلُهُ، وَالْكَلْبَ الْعَقُورَ، وَالْحِدَأَةَ، وَالسَّبُعَ العادي".

رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ حَنْبَلٍ، وَالتِّرْمِذِيِّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مَنِيعٍ، كِلَاهُمَا عَنْ هشيم. وابن ماجه، عن أبي كريم عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ فُضَيْلٍ، كِلَاهُمَا عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي زِيَادٍ، وَهُوَ ضَعِيفٌ، بِهِ. وَقَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: هَذَا حَدِيثٌ حَسَنٌ. 

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَنْ قَتَلَهُ مِنْكُمْ مُتَعَمِّدًا فَجَزَاءٌ مِثْلُ مَا قَتَلَ مِنَ النَّعَمِ﴾ قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَعِيدٍ الْأَشَجُّ، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ قَالَ: نُبِّئْتُ عَنْ طَاوُسٍ قَالَ: لَا يُحْكَمُ عَلَى مَنْ أَصَابَ صَيْدًا خَطَأً، إِنَّمَا يُحْكَمُ عَلَى مَنْ أَصَابَهُ مُتَعَمِّدًا.

وَهَذَا مَذْهَبٌ غَرِيبٌ عَنْ طَاوُسٍ، وَهُوَ مُتَمَسِّكٌ بِظَاهِرِ الْآيَةِ.

وَقَالَ مُجَاهِدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ: الْمُرَادُ بِالْمُتَعَمِّدِ هُنَا الْقَاصِدُ إِلَى قَتْلِ الصَّيْدِ، النَّاسِي لِإِحْرَامِهِ. فَأَمَّا الْمُتَعَمِّدُ لِقَتْلِ الصَّيْدِ مَعَ ذِكْرِهِ لِإِحْرَامِهِ، فَذَاكَ أَمْرُهُ أَعْظَمُ مِنْ أَنْ يَكْفُرَ، وَقَدْ بَطُلَ إِحْرَامُهُ.

رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ عَنْهُ مِنْ طَرِيقِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيح وَلَيْثِ بْنِ أَبِي سُلَيْمٍ وَغَيْرِهِمَا، عَنْهُ. وَهُوَ قَوْلٌ غَرِيبٌ أَيْضًا. وَالَّذِي عَلَيْهِ الْجُمْهُورُ أَنَّ الْعَامِدَ وَالنَّاسِيَ سَوَاءٌ فِي وُجُوبِ الْجَزَاءِ عَلَيْهِ. قَالَ الزُّهْرِيُّ: دَلَّ الْكِتَابُ عَلَى الْعَامِدِ، وَجَرَتِ السُّنَّةُ عَلَى النَّاسِي، وَمَعْنَى هَذَا أَنَّ الْقُرْآنَ دَلَّ عَلَى وُجُوبِ الْجَزَاءِ عَلَى الْمُتَعَمِّدِ وَعَلَى تَأْثِيمِهِ بِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿لِيَذُوقَ وَبَالَ أَمْرِهِ عَفَا اللَّهُ عَمَّا سَلَفَ وَمَنْ عَادَ فَيَنْتَقِمُ اللَّهُ مِنْهُ﴾ وَجَاءَتِ السُّنَّةُ مِنْ أَحْكَامِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ وَأَحْكَامِ أَصْحَابِهِ بِوُجُوبِ الْجَزَاءِ فِي الْخَطَأِ، كَمَا دَلَّ الْكِتَابُ عَلَيْهِ فِي العَمْد، وَأَيْضًا فَإِنَّ قَتْلَ الصَّيْدِ إِتْلَافٌ، وَالْإِتْلَافُ مَضْمُونٌ فِي الْعَمْدِ وَفِي النِّسْيَانِ، لَكِنَّ الْمُتَعَمِّدَ مَأْثُومٌ وَالْمُخْطِئَ غَيْرُ مَلُوم.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿فَجَزَاءٌ مِثْلُ مَا قَتَلَ مِنَ النَّعَمِ﴾ وَحَكَى ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ أَنَّ ابْنَ مَسْعُودٍ قَرَأَهَا: "فَجَزَاؤُهُ مِثْلُ مَا قَتَلَ مِنَ النَّعَمِ".

وَفِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿فَجَزَاءٌ مِثْلُ مَا قَتَلَ مِنَ النَّعَمِ﴾ عَلَى كُلٍّ مِنَ الْقِرَاءَتَيْنِ دَلِيلٌ لِمَا ذَهَبَ إِلَيْهِ مَالِكٌ، وَالشَّافِعِيُّ، وَأَحْمَدُ، وَالْجُمْهُورُ مِنْ وُجُوبِ الْجَزَاءِ مِنْ مِثْلِ مَا قَتَلَهُ الْمُحْرِمُ، إِذَا كَانَ لَهُ مِثْلٌ مِنَ الْحَيَوَانِ الْإِنْسِيِّ، خِلَافًا لِأَبِي حَنِيفَةَ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ، حَيْثُ أَوْجَبَ الْقِيمَةَ سَوَاءٌ كَانَ الصَّيْدُ الْمَقْتُولُ مِثْلِيًّا أَوْ غَيْرَ مِثْلِيٍّ، قَالَ: وَهُوَ مُخَيَّرٌ إِنْ شَاءَ تَصَدَّقَ بِثَمَنِهِ، وَإِنْ شَاءَ اشْتَرَى بِهِ هَدْيًا. وَالَّذِي حَكَمَ بِهِ الصَّحَابَةُ فِي الْمِثْلِ أَوْلَى بِالِاتِّبَاعِ، فَإِنَّهُمْ حَكَمُوا فِي النَّعَامَةِ بِبَدَنَةٍ، وَفِي بَقَرَةِ الْوَحْشِ بِبَقَرَةٍ، وَفِي الْغَزَالِ بِعَنْزٍ وذكْرُ قَضَايَا الصَّحَابَةِ وَأَسَانِيدُهَا مُقَرَّرٌ فِي كِتَابِ "الْأَحْكَامِ"، وَأَمَّا إِذَا لَمْ يَكُنِ الصَّيْدُ مِثْلِيًّا فَقَدْ حَكَمَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ فِيهِ بِثَمَنِهِ، يُحْمَلُ إِلَى مَكَّةَ. رَوَاهُ الْبَيْهَقِيُّ. وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿يَحْكُمُ بِهِ ذَوَا عَدْلٍ مِنْكُمْ﴾ يَعْنِي أَنَّهُ يَحْكُمُ بِالْجَزَاءِ فِي الْمَثَلِ، أَوْ بِالْقِيمَةِ فِي غَيْرِ الْمَثَلِ، عَدْلَانِ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ، وَاخْتَلَفَ الْعُلَمَاءُ فِي الْقَاتِلِ: هَلْ يَجُوزُ أَنْ يكون أحد الحكمين؟ على قولين: أَحَدُهُمَا: " لَا؛ لِأَنَّهُ قَدْ يُتَّهم فِي حُكْمِهِ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ، وَهَذَا مَذْهَبُ مَالِكٍ.

وَالثَّانِي: نَعَمْ؛ لِعُمُومِ الْآيَةِ. وَهُوَ مَذْهَبُ الشَّافِعِيِّ، وَأَحْمَدَ.

وَاحْتَجَّ الْأَوَّلُونَ بِأَنَّ الْحَاكِمَ لَا يَكُونُ مَحْكُومًا عَلَيْهِ فِي صُورَةٍ وَاحِدَةٍ.

قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو نُعَيْم الْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْن، حَدَّثَنَا جَعْفَرٌ -هُوَ ابْنُ بُرْقَان-عَنْ مَيْمُونِ بْنِ مِهْران؛ أَنَّ أَعْرَابِيًّا أَتَى أَبَا بَكْرٍ قَالَ: قَتَلْتُ صَيْدًا وَأَنَا مُحْرِمٌ، فَمَا تَرَى عليَّ مِنَ الْجَزَاءِ؟ فَقَالَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، لِأُبَيِّ بْنِ كَعْبٍ وَهُوَ جَالِسٌ عِنْدَهُ: مَا تَرَى فِيمَا قَالَ؟ فَقَالَ الْأَعْرَابِيُّ: أَتَيْتُكَ وَأَنْتَ خَلِيفَةُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ أَسْأَلُكَ، فَإِذَا أَنْتَ تَسْأَلُ غَيْرَكَ؟ فَقَالَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ: وَمَا تُنْكِرُ؟ يَقُولُ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَجَزَاءٌ مِثْلُ مَا قَتَلَ مِنَ النَّعَمِ يَحْكُمُ بِهِ ذَوَا عَدْلٍ مِنْكُمْ﴾ فَشَاوَرْتُ صَاحِبِي حَتَّى إِذَا اتَّفَقْنَا عَلَى أَمْرٍ أَمَرْنَاكَ بِهِ.

وَهَذَا إِسْنَادٌ جَيِّدٌ، لَكِنَّهُ مُنْقَطِعٌ بَيْنَ مَيْمُونٍ وَبَيْنَ الصِّدِّيقِ، وَمِثْلُهُ يُحْتَمَلُ هَاهُنَا. فَبَيَّنَ لَهُ الصِّدِّيقُ الْحُكْمَ بِرِفْقٍ وتُؤدَة، لَمَّا رَآهُ أَعْرَابِيًّا جَاهِلًا وَإِنَّمَا دَوَاءُ الْجَهْلِ التَّعْلِيمُ، فَأَمَّا إِذَا كَانَ الْمُعْتَرِضُ مَنْسُوبًا إِلَى الْعِلْمِ، فَقَدْ قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ:

حَدَّثَنَا هَنَّاد وَأَبُو هِشَامٍ الرِّفَاعِيُّ قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيع بْنُ الْجَرَّاحِ، عَنِ الْمَسْعُودِيِّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ عُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ قَبِيصة بْنِ جَابِرٍ قَالَ: خَرَجْنَا حُجَّاجًا، فَكُنَّا إِذَا صَلَّيْنَا الْغَدَاةَ اقْتَدْنَا رَوَاحِلَنَا نَتَمَاشَى نَتَحَدَّثُ، قَالَ: فَبَيْنَمَا نَحْنُ ذَاتَ غَدَاةٍ إِذْ سَنَحَ لَنَا ظَبْيٌ -أَوْ: بَرِحَ-فَرَمَاهُ رَجُلٌ كَانَ مَعَنَا بِحَجَرٍ فَمَا أَخْطَأَ خُشَّاءه فَرَكِبَ رَدْعه مَيِّتًا، قَالَ: فَعَظَّمْنا عَلَيْهِ، فَلَمَّا قَدِمْنَا مَكَّةَ خَرَجْتُ مَعَهُ حَتَّى أَتَيْنَا عُمَرَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: فَقَصَّ عَلَيْهِ الْقِصَّةَ قَالَ: وَإِلَى جَنْبِهِ رَجُلٌ كَأَنَّ وَجْهَهُ قُلْب فِضَّةٍ -يَعْنِي عَبْدَ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنَ عَوْفٍ-فَالْتَفَتَ عُمَرُ إِلَى صَاحِبِهِ فَكَلَّمَهُ قَالَ: ثُمَّ أَقْبَلَ عَلَى الرَّجُلِ فَقَالَ: أَعَمْدًا قَتَلْتَهُ أَمْ خَطَأً؟ قَالَ الرَّجُلُ: لَقَدْ تَعَمَّدْتُ رَمْيَهُ، وَمَا أَرَدْتُ قَتْلَهُ. فَقَالَ عُمَرُ: مَا أَرَاكَ إِلَّا قَدْ أَشْرَكْتَ بَيْنَ الْعَمْدِ وَالْخَطَأِ، اعْمَدْ إِلَى شَاةٍ فَاذْبَحْهَا وَتَصَدَّقْ بِلَحْمِهَا وَاسْتَبْقِ إِهَابَهَا. قَالَ: فَقُمْنَا مِنْ عِنْدِهِ، فَقُلْتُ لِصَاحِبِي: أَيُّهَا الرَّجُلُ، عَظّم شَعَائِرَ اللَّهِ، فَمَا دَرَى أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ مَا يُفْتِيكَ حَتَّى سَأَلَ صَاحِبَهُ: اعْمَدْ إِلَى نَاقَتِكَ فَانْحَرْهَا، فَفَعَلَ ذَاكَ. قَالَ قَبِيصَةُ: وَلَا أَذْكُرُ الْآيَةَ مِنْ سُورَةِ الْمَائِدَةِ: ﴿يَحْكُمُ بِهِ ذَوَا عَدْلٍ مِنْكُمْ﴾ قَالَ: فَبَلَغَ عُمَرَ مَقَالَتِي، فَلَمْ يَفْجَأْنَا مِنْهُ إِلَّا وَمَعَهُ الدِّرَّةُ. قَالَ: فَعَلَا صَاحِبِي ضَرْبًا بِالدِّرَّةِ، وَجَعَلَ يَقُولُ: أَقَتَلْتَ فِي الْحَرَمِ وسفَّهت الْحُكْمَ؟ قَالَ: ثُمَّ أَقْبَلَ عليَّ فَقُلْتُ: يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ، لَا أُحِلُّ لَكَ الْيَوْمَ شَيْئًا يحْرُم عَلَيْكَ مِنِّي، قَالَ: يَا قَبِيصَةَ بْنَ جَابِرٍ، إِنِّي أَرَاكَ شَابَّ السِّنِّ، فَسِيحَ الصَّدْرِ، بَيِّنَ اللِّسَانِ، وَإِنَّ الشَّابَّ يَكُونُ فِيهِ تِسْعَةُ أَخْلَاقٍ حَسَنَةٍ وَخُلُقٌ سَيِّئٌ، فَيُفْسِدُ الخلقُ السَّيِّئُ الأخلاقَ الْحَسَنَةَ، فَإِيَّاكَ وَعَثَرَاتِ الشَّبَابِ.

وَقَدْ رَوَى هُشَيْم هَذِهِ الْقِصَّةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ عُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ قَبِيصَةَ، بِنَحْوِهِ. وَرَوَاهَا أَيْضًا عَنْ حُصَيْن، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ قَبِيصَةَ، بِنَحْوِهِ. وَذَكَرَهَا مرسلة عن عُمَر: بن بَكْرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْمُزَنِيُّ، وَمُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سِيرين.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ بَشَّار، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ، حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ أَبِي وَائِلٍ، أَخْبَرَنِي أَبُو جَرِيرٍ البَجَلِيّ قَالَ: أَصَبْتُ ظَبْيًا وَأَنَا مُحْرِمٌ، فَذَكَرْتُ ذَلِكَ لِعُمَرَ، فَقَالَ: ائْتِ رَجُلَيْنِ مِنْ إِخْوَانِكَ فَلْيَحْكُمَا عَلَيْكَ. فَأَتَيْتُ عَبْدَ الرَّحْمَنِ وَسَعْدًا، فَحَكَمَا عَلَيَّ بتَيْس أَعْفَرَ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ وَكِيع، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنة، عَنْ مُخارق، عَنْ طَارِقٍ قَالَ: أَوَطَأَ أَرْبَدُ ظَبْيًا فَقَتَلْتُهُ وَهُوَ مُحْرِمٌ فَأَتَى عُمَرَ؛ لِيَحْكُمَ عَلَيْهِ، فَقَالَ لَهُ عُمَرُ: احْكُمْ مَعِي، فَحَكَمَا فِيهِ جَدْيًا، قَدْ جَمَعَ الْمَاءَ وَالشَّجَرَ. ثُمَّ قَالَ عُمَرُ: ﴿يَحْكُمُ بِهِ ذَوَا عَدْلٍ مِنْكُمْ﴾

وَفِي هَذَا دَلَالَةٌ عَلَى جَوَازِ كَوْنِ الْقَاتِلِ أَحَدِ الْحَكَمَيْنِ، كَمَا قَالَهُ الشَّافِعِيُّ وَأَحْمَدُ، رَحِمَهُمَا اللَّهُ.

وَاخْتَلَفُوا: هَلْ تُسْتَأْنَفُ الْحُكُومَةُ فِي كُلِّ مَا يُصِيبُهُ الْمُحْرِمُ، فَيَجِبُ أَنْ يَحْكُمَ فِيهِ ذَوَا عَدْلٍ، وَإِنْ كَانَ قَدْ حَكَمَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِ الصَّحَابَةُ، أَوْ يَكْتَفِي بِأَحْكَامِ الصَّحَابَةِ الْمُتَقَدِّمَةِ؟ عَلَى قَوْلَيْنِ، فَقَالَ الشَّافِعِيُّ وَأَحْمَدُ: يُتْبَعُ فِي ذَلِكَ مَا حَكَمَتْ بِهِ الصَّحَابَةُ وَجَعَلَاهُ شَرْعًا مُقَرَّرًا لَا يُعْدَلُ عَنْهُ، وَمَا لَمْ يَحْكُمْ فِيهِ الصَّحَابَةُ يُرْجَعُ فِيهِ إِلَى عَدْلَيْنِ. وَقَالَ مَالِكٌ وَأَبُو حَنِيفَةَ: بَلْ يَجِبُ الْحُكْمُ فِي كُلِّ فَرْدٍ فَرَدٍ، سَوَاءٌ وُجِدَ لِلصَّحَابَةِ فِي مِثْلِهِ حُكْمٌ أَمْ لَا؛ لِقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿يَحْكُمُ بِهِ ذَوَا عَدْلٍ مِنْكُمْ﴾

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿هَدْيًا بَالِغَ الْكَعْبَةِ﴾ أَيْ: وَاصِلًا إِلَى الْكَعْبَةِ، وَالْمُرَادُ وُصُولُهُ إِلَى الْحَرَمِ، بِأَنْ يُذْبَحَ هُنَاكَ، وَيُفَرَّقَ لَحْمُهُ عَلَى مَسَاكِينِ الْحَرَمِ. وَهَذَا أَمْرٌ مُتَّفَقٌ عَلَيْهِ فِي هَذِهِ الصُّورَةِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿أَوْ كَفَّارَةٌ طَعَامُ مَسَاكِينَ أَوْ عَدْلُ ذَلِكَ صِيَامًا﴾ أَيْ: إِذَا لَمْ يَجِدِ الْمُحْرِمُ مِثْلَ مَا قَتَلَ مِنَ النعَم أَوْ لَمْ يَكُنِ الصَّيْدُ الْمَقْتُولُ مِنْ ذَوَاتِ الْأَمْثَالِ، أَوْ قُلْنَا بِالتَّخْيِيرِ فِي هَذَا الْمَقَامِ مِنَ الْجَزَاءِ وَالْإِطْعَامِ وَالصِّيَامِ، كَمَا هُوَ قَوْلُ مَالِكٍ، وَأَبِي حَنِيفَةَ، وَأَبِي يُوسُفَ، وَمُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَأَحَدُ قَوْلَيِ الشَّافِعِيِّ، وَالْمَشْهُورُ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ رَحِمَهُمُ اللَّهُ، لِظَاهِرِ الْآيَةِ "أَوْ" فَإِنَّهَا لِلتَّخْيِيرِ. وَالْقَوْلُ الْآخَرُ: أَنَّهَا عَلَى التَّرْتِيبِ.

فَصُورَةُ ذَلِكَ أَنْ يَعْدِلَ إِلَى الْقِيمَةِ، فيُقوّم الصَّيْدُ الْمَقْتُولُ عِنْدَ مَالِكٍ، وَأَبِي حَنِيفَةَ وَأَصْحَابِهِ، وَحَمَّادٍ، وَإِبْرَاهِيمَ. وَقَالَ الشَّافِعِيُّ: يُقَوَّمُ مِثْلُهُ مِنَ النَّعَمِ لَوْ كَانَ مَوْجُودًا، ثُمَّ يُشْتَرَى بِهِ طَعَامٌ وَيُتَصَدَّقُ بِهِ، فَيُصْرَفُ لِكُلِّ مِسْكِينٍ مُدٌ مِنْهُ عِنْدَ الشَّافِعِيِّ، وَمَالِكٍ، وَفُقَهَاءِ الْحِجَازِ، وَاخْتَارَهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ.

وَقَالَ أَبُو حَنِيفَةَ وَأَصْحَابُهُ: يُطعِم كُلُّ مِسْكين مُدَّيْن، وَهُوَ قَوْلُ مُجَاهِدٍ.

وَقَالَ أَحْمَدُ: مُدّ مِنْ حِنْطَةٍ، أَوْ مُدَّانِ مِنْ غَيْرِهِ. فَإِنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ، أَوْ قُلْنَا بِالتَّخْيِيرِ صَامَ عَنْ إِطْعَامِ كُلِّ مِسْكِينٍ يَوْمًا.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: وَقَالَ آخَرُونَ: يَصُومُ مَكَانَ كُلِّ صَاعٍ يَوْمًا. كَمَا فِي جَزَاءِ الْمُتَرَفِّهِ بِالْحَلْقِ وَنَحْوِهِ، فَإِنَّ الشَّارِعَ أَمَرَ كعب بن عُجْرَة أن يقسم فَرَقًا بَيْنَ سِتَّةٍ، أَوْ يَصُومَ ثَلَاثَةَ أَيَّامٍ، والفَرَقُ ثَلَاثَةُ آصُعٍ.

وَاخْتَلَفُوا فِي مَكَانِ هَذَا الْإِطْعَامِ، فَقَالَ الشَّافِعِيُّ: مَحَلُّهُ الْحَرَمُ، وَهُوَ قَوْلُ عَطَاءٍ. وَقَالَ مَالِكٌ: يُطْعَمُ فِي الْمَكَانِ الَّذِي أَصَابَ فِيهِ الصَّيْدَ، أَوْ أَقْرَبِ الْأَمَاكِنِ إِلَيْهِ. وَقَالَ أَبُو حَنِيفَةَ: إِنْ شَاءَ أَطْعَمَ فِي الحرم، وإن شاء أطعم في غيره.

ذِكْرُ أَقْوَالِ السَّلَفِ فِي هَذَا الْمَقَامِ:

قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ الْمُغِيرَةِ، حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ مقْسَم، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿فَجَزَاءٌ مِثْلُ مَا قَتَلَ مِنَ النَّعَمِ يَحْكُمُ بِهِ ذَوَا عَدْلٍ مِنْكُمْ هَدْيًا بَالِغَ الْكَعْبَةِ أَوْ كَفَّارَةٌ طَعَامُ مَسَاكِينَ أَوْ عَدْلُ ذَلِكَ صِيَامًا﴾ قَالَ: إِذَا أَصَابَ المحرمُ الصيدَ حُكِمَ عَلَيْهِ جَزَاؤُهُ مِنَ النَّعَمِ، فَإِنْ وَجَدَ جَزَاءَهُ، ذَبَحَهُ فَتَصَدَّقَ بِهِ. وَإِنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ نَظَرَ كَمْ ثَمَنُهُ، ثُمَّ قُوّم ثَمَنُهُ طَعَامًا، فَصَامَ مَكَانَ كُلِّ نِصْفِ صَاعٍ يَوْمًا، قَالَ: ﴿أَوْ كَفَّارَةٌ طَعَامُ مَسَاكِينَ أَوْ عَدْلُ ذَلِكَ صِيَامًا﴾ قَالَ: إِنَّمَا أُرِيدَ بِالطَّعَامِ الصِّيَامُ، أَنَّهُ إِذْ وُجِدَ الطَّعَامُ وُجِدَ جَزَاؤُهُ.

وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ، مِنْ طَرِيقِ جَرِيرٍ.

وَقَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: ﴿هَدْيًا بَالِغَ الْكَعْبَةِ أَوْ كَفَّارَةٌ طَعَامُ مَسَاكِينَ أَوْ عَدْلُ ذَلِكَ صِيَامًا﴾ إِذَا قَتَلَ الْمُحْرِمُ شَيْئًا مِنَ الصَّيْدِ، حُكِمَ عَلَيْهِ فِيهِ. فَإِنْ قَتَلَ ظَبْيًا أَوْ نَحْوَهُ، فَعَلَيْهِ شَاةٌ تُذْبَحُ بِمَكَّةَ. فَإِنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ فَإِطْعَامُ سِتَّةِ مَسَاكِينَ فَإِنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ فَصِيَامُ ثَلَاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ. فَإِنْ قَتَلَ إبِلا أَوْ نَحْوَهُ، فَعَلَيْهِ بَقَرَةٌ. فَإِنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ أَطْعَمَ عِشْرِينَ مِسْكِينًا. فَإِنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ صَامَ عِشْرِينَ يَوْمًا. وَإِنَّ قَتَلَ نَعَامَةً أَوْ حمارَ وَحْشٍ أَوْ نَحْوَهُ، فَعَلَيْهِ بَدَنَةٌ مِنَ الْإِبِلِ. فَإِنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ أَطْعَمَ ثَلَاثِينَ مِسْكِينًا. فَإِنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ صَامَ ثَلَاثِينَ يَوْمًا.

رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ وَابْنُ جَرِيرٍ، وَزَادَ: وَالطَّعَامُ مُدٌّ مُدّ تشبَعهم. 

وَقَالَ جَابِرٌ الجُعْفي، عَنْ عَامِرٍ الشَّعْبِيِّ وَعَطَاءٍ وَمُجَاهِدٍ: ﴿أَوْ عَدْلُ ذَلِكَ صِيَامًا﴾ قَالُوا: إِنَّمَا الطَّعَامُ لِمَنْ لَا يَبْلُغُ الْهَدْيَ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ.

وَكَذَا رَوَى ابْنُ جُرَيْج عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، وَأَسْبَاطٍ عَنِ السُّدِّي أَنَّهَا عَلَى التَّرْتِيبِ.

وَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ، وَعِكْرِمَةُ، وَمُجَاهِدٌ -فِي رِوَايَةِ الضَّحَّاكِ-وَإِبْرَاهِيمَ النَّخَعِي: هِيَ عَلَى الْخِيَارِ. وَهُوَ رِوَايَةُ اللَّيْثِ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ. وَاخْتَارَ ذَلِكَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿لِيَذُوقَ وَبَالَ أَمْرِهِ﴾ أَيْ: أَوْجَبْنَا عَلَيْهِ الْكَفَّارَةَ لِيَذُوقَ عُقُوبَةَ فِعْلِهِ الَّذِي ارْتَكَبَ فِيهِ الْمُخَالَفَةَ ﴿عَفَا اللَّهُ عَمَّا سَلَفَ﴾ أَيْ: فِي زَمَانِ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ، لِمَنْ أَحْسَنَ فِي الْإِسْلَامِ وَاتَّبَعَ شَرْعَ اللَّهِ، وَلَمْ يَرْتَكِبِ الْمَعْصِيَةَ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿َ وَمَنْ عَادَ فَيَنْتَقِمُ اللَّهُ مِنْهُ﴾ أَيْ: وَمَنْ فَعَلَ ذَلِكَ بَعْدَ تَحْرِيمِهِ فِي الْإِسْلَامِ وَبُلُوغِ الْحُكْمِ الشَّرْعِيِّ إِلَيْهِ فَيَنْتَقِمُ اللَّهُ مِنْهُ وَاللَّهُ عَزِيزٌ ذُو انْتِقَامٍ

قَالَ ابْنُ جُرَيْج، قُلْتُ لِعَطَاءٍ: مَا ﴿عَفَا اللَّهُ عَمَّا سَلَفَ﴾ قَالَ: عَمَّا كَانَ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ. قَالَ: قُلْتُ: وَمَا ﴿َ وَمَنْ عَادَ فَيَنْتَقِمُ اللَّهُ مِنْهُ﴾ ؟ قَالَ: وَمَنْ عَادَ فِي الْإِسْلَامِ، فَيَنْتَقِمُ اللَّهُ مِنْهُ، وَعَلَيْهِ مَعَ ذَلِكَ الْكَفَّارَةُ قَالَ: قُلْتُ: فَهَلْ فِي الْعَوْدِ حَدُّ تَعْلَمُهُ؟ قَالَ: لَا. قَالَ: قُلْتُ: فَتَرَى حَقًّا عَلَى الْإِمَامِ أَنْ يُعَاقِبَهُ؟ قَالَ: لَا هُوَ ذَنْبٌ أَذْنَبَهُ فِيمَا بَيْنَهُ وَبَيْنَ اللَّهِ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، وَلَكِنْ يَفْتَدِي. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ. 

وَقِيلَ مَعْنَاهُ: فَيَنْتَقِمُ اللَّهُ مِنْهُ بِالْكَفَّارَةِ. قَالَهُ سَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ، وَعَطَاءٌ.

ثُمَّ الْجُمْهُورُ مِنَ السَّلَفِ وَالْخَلَفِ، عَلَى أَنَّهُ مَتَى قَتَلَ الْمُحْرِمُ الصَّيْدَ وَجَبَ الْجَزَاءُ، وَلَا فَرْقَ بَيْنَ الْأُولَى وَالثَّانِيَةِ وَإِنْ تَكَرَّرَ مَا تَكَرَّرَ، سَوَاءٌ الْخَطَأُ فِي ذَلِكَ وَالْعَمْدُ.

وَقَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: مَنْ قَتَلَ شَيْئًا مِنَ الصَّيْدِ خَطَأً، وَهُوَ مُحْرِمٌ، يُحْكَمُ عَلَيْهِ فِيهِ كُلَّمَا قَتَلَهُ، وَإِنْ قَتْلَهُ عَمْدًا يُحْكَمُ عَلَيْهِ فِيهِ مَرَّةً وَاحِدَةً، فَإِنْ عَادَ يُقَالُ لَهُ: يَنْتَقِمُ اللَّهُ مِنْكَ كَمَا قَالَ اللَّهُ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا عَمْرُو بْنُ عَلِيٍّ، حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ وَابْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ جَمِيعًا، عَنْ هِشَامٍ -هُوَ ابْنُ حَسَّانٍ-عَنْ عِكْرِمَة، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِيمَنْ أَصَابَ صَيْدًا فحُكم عَلَيْهِ ثُمَّ عَادَ، قَالَ: لَا يُحْكَمُ عَلَيْهِ، يَنْتَقِمُ اللَّهُ مِنْهُ.

وَهَكَذَا قَالَ شُرَيْح، وَمُجَاهِدٌ، وَسَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ، وَالْحَسَنُ الْبَصْرِيُّ، وَإِبْرَاهِيمُ النَّخَعِي. رَوَاهُنَّ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ، ثُمَّ اخْتَارَ الْقَوْلَ الْأَوَّلَ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا الْعَبَّاسُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ الْعَبْدِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا المُعْتَمِر بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ زَيْدٍ أَبِي الْمُعَلَّى، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ الْبَصْرِيِّ؛ أَنَّ رَجُلًا أَصَابَ صَيْدًا، فَتَجَوَّزَ عَنْهُ، ثُمَّ عَادَ فَأَصَابَ صَيْدًا آخَرَ، فَنَزَلَتْ نَارٌ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ فَأَحْرَقَتْهُ فَهُوَ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَمَنْ عَادَ فَيَنْتَقِمُ اللَّهُ مِنْهُ﴾

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَاللَّهُ عَزِيزٌ ذُو انْتِقَامٍ﴾ يَقُولُ عَزَّ ذِكْرُهُ: وَاللَّهُ مَنِيعٌ فِي سُلْطَانِهِ لَا يَقْهَرُهُ قَاهِرٌ، وَلَا يَمْنَعُهُ مِنَ الِانْتِقَامِ مِمَّنِ انْتَقَمَ مِنْهُ، وَلَا مِنْ عُقُوبَةِ مَنْ أَرَادَ عُقُوبَتَهُ مَانِعٌ؛ لِأَنَّ الْخَلْقَ خَلْقُهُ، وَالْأَمْرَ أَمْرُهُ، لَهُ الْعِزَّةُ وَالْمَنَعَةُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿ذُو انْتِقَامٍ﴾ يَعْنِي: أَنَّهُ ذُو مُعَاقَبَةٍ لِمَنْ عَصَاهُ على معصيته إياه.

---

## 5:96-99 — verses 96-99 (shared commentary)

### Translation

Ibn Abi Talhah said, on the authority of Ibn Abbas (in one report from him), and Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, and Sa'id ibn Jubayr, and others, regarding His saying {Lawful for you is the game of the sea}: meaning what is caught from it fresh. {and its food}: what is taken as provision from it, salted and dried.

Ibn Abbas said, in the well-known report from him: its game is what is taken from it alive, and {its food} is what it casts out dead.

This is likewise reported from Abu Bakr al-Siddiq, Zayd ibn Thabit, Abdullah ibn Amr, and Abu Ayyub al-Ansari, may Allah be pleased with them, and from Ikrimah, Abu Salamah ibn Abd al-Rahman, Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, and al-Hasan al-Basri.

Sufyan ibn Uyaynah said, on the authority of Amr ibn Dinar, from Ikrimah, from Abu Bakr al-Siddiq, that he said: {and its food} is everything that is in it. Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim both narrated this.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Simak said: I was told, on the authority of Ibn Abbas, that he said: Abu Bakr delivered a sermon to the people and said: {Lawful for you is the game of the sea and its food, as provision for you}, and "its food" is what is cast up.

He said: [chain] Abu Mijlaz, from Ibn Abbas, regarding His saying {Lawful for you is the game of the sea and its food}, said: {and its food} is what is cast up.

Ikrimah said, on the authority of Ibn Abbas: {and its food} is what it casts out dead. Ibn Jarir also narrated this.

Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib said: its food is what it casts out alive, or what is exposed and then dies. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated this.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Nafi' reported that Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Hurayrah asked Ibn Umar, saying: The sea has cast up many dead fish, may we eat them? He said: Do not eat them. When Abdullah returned to his family, he took the mushaf and read Surat al-Ma'idah, and came to this verse, {and its food, as provision for you and for travelers}, and he said: Go and tell him to eat it, for it is indeed its food.

Thus Ibn Jarir chose the view that what is meant by "its food" is what dies within it. He said: A report has been narrated on this matter, though some transmit it as mawquf (halted report).

[chain] Abu Hurayrah said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, regarding {Lawful for you is the game of the sea and its food, as provision for you}: "its food is what it casts out dead."

Then he said: Some have narrated this hadith as halted upon Abu Hurayrah: [chain] Abu Hurayrah, regarding His saying {Lawful for you is the game of the sea and its food}, said: its food is what it casts out dead.

As for His saying, {as provision for you and for travelers}: that is, benefit and sustenance for you, O those addressed, {and for travelers}, which is the plural of sayyar (traveler). Ikrimah said: for whoever is present at the sea, and "the travelers" are those journeying.

Someone other than him said: the fresh of it is for whoever catches it while present at the sea, and {its food} is what dies within it, or what is caught from it and salted and dried as provision for travelers and those far from the sea.

Something similar has been reported from Ibn Abbas, Mujahid, al-Suddi, and others. The majority of scholars have used this noble verse as evidence for the lawfulness of the dead of the sea, along with what Imam Malik ibn Anas narrated, from Wahb ibn Kaysan, from Jabir ibn Abdullah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent out an expedition toward the coast, and placed Abu Ubaydah ibn al-Jarrah in command over them. They numbered three hundred. He said: And I was among them. He said: We set out, and when we were partway along the road, our provisions ran out. Abu Ubaydah ordered that the provisions of the whole army be gathered, and when it was all collected it amounted to two bags of dates. He said: He would give us a small ration each day until it too ran out, and we would receive only a single date each. I said: What good does a single date do? He said: We came to feel the loss of even that when it ran out. He said: Then we came to the sea, and there was a fish like a small hill. That army ate from it for eighteen nights. Then Abu Ubaydah ordered that two of its ribs be set up, and he ordered a mount to be saddled, and it passed beneath them without touching them.

This hadith is recorded in the two Sahihs and has several chains of transmission from Jabir.

In Sahih Muslim, from the narration of Abu al-Zubayr, from Jabir: There was on the seashore something like a huge mound of sand. We went to it, and it turned out to be a creature called al-anbar (whale). Abu Ubaydah said: It is carrion. Then he said: But are we not the messengers of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and in the path of Allah? And you have been driven by necessity, so eat. He said: We stayed by it for a month, being three hundred men, until we grew fat. I recall how we used to scoop out oil in jars from the hollow of its eye, and we cut from it pieces like a bull, or, the size of a bull. He said: Abu Ubaydah took thirteen men from among us and seated them in the hollow of its eye, and he took one of its ribs and set it upright, then had the largest camel among us saddled and it passed beneath it. We took some of its meat as dried provisions. When we arrived in Madinah we went to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and mentioned that to him, and he said: "It is provision that Allah brought forth for you; do you have any of its meat with you, that you might feed us?" He said: So we sent some of it to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and he ate it. In some of Muslim's narrations, it is stated that they were with the Prophet ﷺ when they found this fish. Some said: this is a separate incident, while others said: rather it is one and the same event, but they were first with the Prophet ﷺ, then he sent them as a detachment with Abu Ubaydah, and they found this fish during that expedition with Abu Ubaydah, and Allah knows best.

Malik said, on the authority of Safwan ibn Sulaym, from Sa'id ibn Salamah, of the family of Ibn al-Azraq, that al-Mughirah ibn Abi Burdah, who was of Banu Abd al-Dar, informed him that he heard Abu Hurayrah say: A man asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, saying: O Messenger of Allah, we sail upon the sea and carry with us only a little water; if we perform ablution with it we become thirsty, so may we perform ablution with seawater? The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "It is the purifier, its water is pure and its dead are lawful."

This hadith was narrated by the two Imams al-Shafi'i and Ahmad ibn Hanbal, and by the compilers of the four Sunan works, and it was authenticated by al-Bukhari, al-Tirmidhi, Ibn Khuzaymah, Ibn Hibban, and others. It has also been narrated, with similar wording, from a group of the Companions from the Prophet ﷺ.

Imam Ahmad, Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, and Ibn Majah narrated, through several chains, from Hammad ibn Salamah: [chain] Abu al-Muhazzim, who is Yazid ibn Sufyan, said: I heard Abu Hurayrah say: We were with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on a pilgrimage, or an umrah, and we came upon a swarm of locusts, so we began striking them with our sticks and whips and killing them. Then it fell upon our minds with worry, and we said: What are we to do, being in the state of ihram? So we asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and he said: "There is no harm in the game of the sea."

Abu al-Muhazzim is weak, and Allah knows best.

Ibn Majah said: [chain] Musa ibn Muhammad ibn Ibrahim, from his father, from Jabir and Anas ibn Malik, that the Prophet ﷺ, when he invoked against locusts, would say: "O Allah, destroy their large ones, kill their small ones, spoil their eggs, cut off their root, and hold back their mouths from our means of livelihood and our provisions, for You are the Hearer of prayer." Khalid said: O Messenger of Allah, how can you invoke against a host among the hosts of Allah by cutting off its root? He said: "Locusts are the sneeze of the fish (or: what the fish scatters) in the sea." Hashim said: Ziyad said: I was told by someone who saw the fish scattering it. Ibn Majah alone narrated this.

Al-Shafi'i narrated, from Sa'id, from Ibn Jurayj, from Ata', from Ibn Abbas, that he disapproved of one who hunted locusts within the sacred precinct (al-Haram).

Those jurists who held the view that all creatures of the sea may be eaten, without exception, used this noble verse as evidence for that. It was mentioned previously from al-Siddiq that he said: {its food} is everything that is in it.

Some excluded frogs and permitted everything else, on the basis of what Imam Ahmad, Abu Dawud, and al-Nasa'i narrated, from the report of Ibn Abi Dhi'b, from Sa'id ibn Khalid, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, from Abd al-Rahman ibn Uthman al-Taymi, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ "forbade the killing of the frog." And al-Nasa'i narrated from Abdullah ibn Amr, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade the killing of the frog, and said: "Its croaking is glorification (tasbih)."

Others said: from sea game, fish may be eaten, but the frog may not be eaten. They differed concerning what is other than these two. It was said: all the rest may be eaten. It was said: it may not be eaten. It was said: whatever has a land counterpart that is eaten, its like from the sea may be eaten, and whatever has a land counterpart that is not eaten may not be eaten. All of these are positions within the school of al-Shafi'i, may God have mercy on him.

Abu Hanifah, may God have mercy on him, said: what dies in the sea may not be eaten, just as what dies on land may not be eaten, because of the general meaning of His saying: {Forbidden to you is the dead animal (maytah)} [al-Ma'idah: 3].

A hadith to this effect has been transmitted. Ibn Mardawayh said:

[chain] Abd al-Baqi, that is Ibn Qani', told us, al-Husayn ibn Ishaq al-Tustari and Abd Allah ibn Musa ibn Abi Uthman told us, they said al-Husayn ibn Zayd al-Tahhan told us, Hafs ibn Ghiyath told us, from Ibn Abi Dhi'b, from Abu al-Zubayr, from Jabir, who said: the Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Whatever you catch alive and it then dies, eat it, but whatever the sea casts up dead, floating, do not eat it."

Then he narrated it also through the route of Isma'il ibn Umayyah and Yahya ibn Abi Unaysah, from Abu al-Zubayr, from Jabir, with the same wording. It is munkar (a rejected/unreliable report).

The majority among the companions of Malik, al-Shafi'i, and Ahmad ibn Hanbal have argued on the basis of the hadith of "the ambergris" mentioned earlier, and on the basis of the hadith: "It is that whose water is purifying and whose dead is lawful," which has likewise been mentioned earlier.

The imam Abu Abd Allah al-Shafi'i narrated, from Abd al-Rahman ibn Zayd ibn Aslam, from his father, from Ibn Umar, who said: the Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Two dead things and two bloods have been made lawful for us. As for the two dead things, they are the fish and the locust, and as for the two bloods, they are the liver and the spleen."

Ahmad, Ibn Majah, al-Daraqutni, and al-Bayhaqi narrated it as well. It has corroborating reports, and it has also been narrated as mawquf (a halted report, stopping at a companion rather than being attributed to the Prophet ﷺ). And God knows best.

His saying: {and forbidden to you is the hunting of land game while you are in the state of ihram} that is, while you are in your state of ihram, hunting is forbidden to you. In this there is indication of the prohibition of that. So if the one in ihram hunts game deliberately, he sins and owes compensation, or if by mistake, he owes compensation and it becomes forbidden for him to eat it, because in his case it is like the dead animal (maytah), and likewise in the case of others, whether they are in ihram or not, according to Malik and al-Shafi'i in one of his two positions. This is also the view of Ata', al-Qasim, Salim, Abu Yusuf, Muhammad ibn al-Hasan, and others. If he eats it or eats something of it, is there an obligation of compensation upon him for that as well? There are two positions among the scholars on this:

One of them: yes. Abd al-Razzaq said, from Ibn Jurayj, from Ata', who said: if he slaughters it and then eats it, then two expiations are owed. A group held to this.

The second: there is no compensation owed for eating it. Malik ibn Anas stated this explicitly.

Abu Umar ibn Abd al-Barr said: the schools of the jurists of the various regions and the majority of the scholars hold to this. Abu Umar then explained the reasoning by analogy to someone who has intercourse repeatedly before the legal punishment is carried out, for he owes only a single punishment.

Abu Hanifah said: he owes the value of what he ate.

Abu Thawr said: if the one in ihram kills game, he owes its compensation, but it is lawful to eat that game, except that I dislike it for the one who killed it, because of the report from the Messenger of God ﷺ: "Land game is lawful for you, so long as you have not hunted it yourselves or had it hunted for you."

This hadith will be explained later. His statement permitting it for the killer himself is a strange (gharib) position. As for permitting it for someone other than the killer, there is disagreement on that. We have already mentioned the prohibition held by those mentioned earlier. Others said it is permissible for someone other than the killer, whether they are in ihram or not, on the basis of this hadith. And God knows best.

As for when someone not in ihram hunts game and then gives it as a gift to someone in ihram, some have gone to the view that it is permissible unconditionally, without distinguishing whether he hunted it specifically for that person or not. Abu Umar ibn Abd al-Barr related this position from Umar ibn al-Khattab, Abu Hurayrah, al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam, Ka'b al-Ahbar, Mujahid, Ata' (in one report), and Sa'id ibn Jubayr. He said: the scholars of Kufa held this view as well.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Muhammad ibn Abd Allah ibn Bazi' told us, Bishr ibn al-Mufaddal told us, Sa'id told us, from Qatadah, that Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab told him, from Abu Hurayrah, that he was asked about the meat of game hunted by someone not in ihram: may the one in ihram eat it? He said: he gave the ruling that it may be eaten. Then he met Umar ibn al-Khattab and told him what had happened, and Umar said: had you given them a ruling other than this, I would have made your head ache severely.

Others said: it is not permissible at all for the one in ihram to eat game, and they prohibited this unconditionally, on the basis of the general meaning of this noble verse.

Abd al-Razzaq said, from Ma'mar, from Ibn Tawus, and Abd al-Karim ibn Abi Umayyah, from Tawus, from Ibn Abbas, that he disliked eating the meat of game for the one in ihram, and said: it is unqualified (mubhamah, unrestricted in scope). That is, referring to His saying: {and forbidden to you is the hunting of land game while you are in the state of ihram}.

He said: Ma'mar told me, from al-Zuhri, from Ibn Umar, that he used to dislike for the one in ihram to eat from the meat of game under all circumstances.

Ma'mar said: Ayyub told me, from Nafi', from Ibn Umar, the same.

Ibn Abd al-Barr said: Tawus and Jabir ibn Zayd held this view as well, and al-Thawri and Ishaq ibn Rahwayh (in one report) went to it. Something similar has been narrated from Ali ibn Abi Talib. Ibn Jarir narrated it through the route of Sa'id ibn Abi Arubah, from Qatadah, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab: that Ali disliked the meat of game for the one in ihram under all circumstances.

Malik, al-Shafi'i, Ahmad ibn Hanbal, Ishaq ibn Rahwayh (in one report), and the majority said: if the one not in ihram had specifically intended the one in ihram with that game, it is not permissible for the one in ihram to eat it, because of the hadith of al-Sa'b ibn Jaththamah: that he gave a gift to the Prophet ﷺ of a wild donkey, while the Prophet was at al-Abwa', or at Waddan, and he returned it to him. When he saw what was on his face, he said: "We have only returned it to you because we are in the state of ihram."

This hadith is recorded in the two Sahihs, and it has many wordings. They said: the reasoning is that the Prophet ﷺ supposed that this had been hunted specifically for him, and so he returned it for that reason. But if the one not in ihram did not specifically intend him by the hunting, then it is permissible for him to eat from it, because of the hadith of Abu Qatadah when he hunted a wild donkey. He was not in ihram, while his companions were in ihram, so they hesitated to eat it. Then they asked the Messenger of God ﷺ, and he said: "Did any of you point to it, or help in killing it?" They said: no. He said: "Then eat." And the Messenger of God ﷺ ate from it.

This story is also established in the two Sahihs with many wordings.

The imam Ahmad said: [chain] Sa'id ibn Mansur and Qutaybah ibn Sa'id told us, they said Ya'qub ibn Abd al-Rahman told us, from Amr ibn Abi Amr, from al-Muttalib ibn Abd Allah ibn Hantab, from Jabir ibn Abd Allah, who said: the Messenger of God ﷺ said, and Qutaybah said in his version: I heard the Messenger of God ﷺ say: "Land game is lawful for you," Sa'id said: "while you are in the state of ihram," "so long as you have not hunted it yourselves or had it hunted for you."

Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, and al-Nasa'i all likewise narrated it from Qutaybah. Al-Tirmidhi said: we do not know that al-Muttalib heard directly from Jabir.

The imam Muhammad ibn Idris al-Shafi'i narrated it through the route of Amr ibn Abi Amr, from his patron al-Muttalib, from Jabir, then said: this is the best hadith narrated on this subject and the soundest by analogy.

Malik said, from Abd Allah ibn Abi Bakr, from Abd Allah ibn Amir ibn Rabi'ah, who said: I saw Uthman ibn Affan at al-Arj, while he was in ihram, on a hot day, having covered his face with a cloak of crimson wool. Then meat of game was brought to him, and he said to his companions: eat. They said: will you not eat yourself? He said: I am not in the same situation as you, for it was hunted specifically for my sake.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ -فِي رِوَايَةٍ عَنْهُ-وَسَعِيدُ بْنُ الْمُسَيَّبِ، وَسَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ، وَغَيْرُهُمْ في قوله: ﴿أُحِلَّ لَكُمْ صَيْدُ الْبَحْرِ﴾ يَعْنِي: مَا يَصْطَادُ مِنْهُ طَرِيًّا ﴿وَطَعَامُهُ﴾ مَا يَتَزَوَّدُ مِنْهُ مَلِيحًا يَابِسًا.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ فِي الرِّوَايَةِ الْمَشْهُورَةِ عَنْهُ: صَيْدُهُ مَا أُخِذَ مِنْهُ حَيًّا ﴿وَطَعَامُهُ﴾ مَا لَفَظَهُ مَيِّتًا.

وَهَكَذَا رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرٍ الصِّدِّيقِ وَزَيْدِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ، وَعَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، وَأَبِي أَيُّوبَ الْأَنْصَارِيِّ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ. وَعِكْرِمَةَ، وَأَبِي سَلَمَةَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، وَإِبْرَاهِيمَ النخَعي، وَالْحَسَنِ الْبَصْرِيِّ.

قَالَ سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ دِينَارٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرٍ الصِّدِّيقِ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: ﴿وَطَعَامُهُ﴾ كُلُّ مَا فِيهِ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ وَابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ حُمَيْدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ سِمَاك قَالَ: حُدِّثتُ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: خَطَبَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ النَّاسَ فَقَالَ: ﴿أُحِلَّ لَكُمْ صَيْدُ الْبَحْرِ وَطَعَامُهُ مَتَاعًا لَكُمْ﴾ وَطَعَامُهُ مَا قُذِفَ.

قَالَ: وَحَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّة، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ التَّيْمِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي مِجْلَز، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿أُحِلَّ لَكُمْ صَيْدُ الْبَحْرِ وَطَعَامُهُ﴾ قَالَ ﴿وَطَعَامُهُ﴾ مَا قُذِفَ.

وَقَالَ عِكْرِمَةُ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: ﴿وَطَعَامُهُ﴾ مَا لَفَظَ مِنْ مَيْتَةٍ. وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ أَيْضًا.

وَقَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ الْمُسَيَّبِ: طَعَامُهُ مَا لَفَظَهُ حَيًّا، أَوْ حَسِرَ عَنْهُ فَمَاتَ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ بَشَّار، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ، حَدَّثَنَا أَيُّوبُ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ؛ أَنَّ عَبْدَ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنَ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ سَأَلَ ابْنَ عُمَرَ فَقَالَ: إِنَّ الْبَحْرَ قَدْ قَذَفَ حِيتَانًا كَثِيرًا مَيْتًا أَفَنَأْكُلُهُ؟ فَقَالَ: لَا تَأْكُلُوهُ. فَلَمَّا رَجَعَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ إِلَى أَهْلِهِ أَخَذَ الْمُصْحَفَ فَقَرَأَ سُورَةَ الْمَائِدَةِ، فَأَتَى هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ ﴿وَطَعَامُهُ مَتَاعًا لَكُمْ وَلِلسَّيَّارَةِ﴾ فَقَالَ: اذْهَبْ فَقُلْ لَهُ فَلْيَأْكُلْهُ، فَإِنَّهُ طَعَامُهُ.

وَهَكَذَا اخْتَارَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ أَنَّ الْمُرَادَ بِطَعَامِهِ مَا مَاتَ فِيهِ، قَالَ: وَقَدْ رُوِيَ فِي ذَلِكَ خَبَرٌ، وَإِنَّ بَعْضَهُمْ يَرْوِيهِ مَوْقُوفًا. 

حَدَّثَنَا هَنَّاد بْنُ السُّرِّي قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: ﴿أُحِلَّ لَكُمْ صَيْدُ الْبَحْرِ وَطَعَامُهُ مَتَاعًا لَكُمْ﴾ قَالَ: طَعَامُهُ مَا لَفَظَهُ مَيِّتًا".

ثُمَّ قَالَ: وَقَدْ وَقَفَ بعضهم هذا الحديث على أبي هريرة: حَدَّثَنَا هَنَّادٌ، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿أُحِلَّ لَكُمْ صَيْدُ الْبَحْرِ وَطَعَامُهُ﴾ قَالَ: طَعَامُهُ: مَا لَفَظَهُ مَيِّتًا.

وقوله: ﴿ُ مَتَاعًا لَكُمْ وَلِلسَّيَّارَةِ﴾ أَيْ: مَنْفَعَةً وقُوتًا لَكُمْ أَيُّهَا الْمُخَاطَبُونَ ﴿وَلِلسَّيَّارَةِ﴾ وَهُوَ جَمْعُ سيَّار. قَالَ عِكْرِمَةُ: لِمَنْ كَانَ بِحَضْرَةِ الْبَحْرِ وَلِلسَّيَّارَةِ: السَفْر. 

وَقَالَ غَيْرُهُ: الطَّرِيُّ مِنْهُ لِمَنْ يَصْطَادُهُ مِنْ حَاضِرَةِ الْبَحْرِ، وَ ﴿طَعَامُهُ﴾ مَا مَاتَ فِيهِ أَوِ اصْطِيدَ مِنْهُ ومُلِّح وَقُدِّدَ زَادًا لِلْمُسَافِرِينَ وَالنَّائِينَ عَنِ الْبَحْرِ.

وَقَدْ رُوِيَ نَحْوُهُ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، والسُّدِّي وَغَيْرِهِمْ. وَقَدِ اسْتَدَلَّ جُمْهُورُ الْعُلَمَاءِ عَلَى حِلِّ مَيْتَةِ الْبَحْرِ بِهَذِهِ الْآيَةِ الْكَرِيمَةِ، وَبِمَا رَوَاهُ الْإِمَامُ مَالِكُ بْنُ أَنَسٍ، عَنْ وَهْبِ بْنِ كَيْسَان، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ: بَعَثَ رسولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ بَعْثًا قِبَل السَّاحِلِ، فأمَّر عَلَيْهِمْ أَبَا عُبَيْدَةَ بْنَ الْجَرَّاحِ، وَهُمْ ثَلَاثُمِائَةٍ، قَالَ: وَأَنَا فِيهِمْ. قَالَ: فَخَرَجْنَا، حَتَّى إِذَا كُنَّا بِبَعْضِ الطَّرِيقِ فَنِيَ الزَّادُ، فَأَمَرَ أَبُو عُبَيْدَةَ بِأَزْوَادِ ذَلِكَ الْجَيْشِ، فجُمع ذَلِكَ كُلُّهُ، فَكَانَ مَزْوَدَيْ تَمْرٍ، قَالَ: فَكَانَ يُقَوِّتُنَا كُلَّ يَوْمٍ قَلِيلًا قَلِيلًا حَتَّى فَنِيَ، فَلَمْ يَكُنْ يُصِيبُنَا إِلَّا تَمْرَةً تَمْرَةً. فَقُلْتُ: وَمَا تُغْنِي تَمْرَةٌ؟ فَقَالَ: فَقَدْ وَجَدْنَا فَقْدَهَا حِينَ فَنِيَتْ، قَالَ: ثُمَّ انْتَهَيْنَا إِلَى الْبَحْرِ، فَإِذَا حُوتٌ مِثْلُ الظَّرِب، فَأَكَلَ مِنْهُ ذَلِكَ الْجَيْشُ ثَمَانِيَ عَشْرَةَ لَيْلَةً. ثُمَّ أَمَرَ أَبُو عُبَيْدَةَ بِضِلْعَيْنِ مِنْ أَضْلَاعِهِ فَنُصِبَا، ثُمَّ أَمَرَ بِرَاحِلَةٍ فَرُحِلَتْ، وَمَرَّتْ تَحْتَهُمَا فَلَمْ تُصِبْهُمَا. 

وَهَذَا الْحَدِيثُ مُخَرَّجٌ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ وَلَهُ طُرُقٌ عَنْ جَابِرٍ.

وَفِي صَحِيحِ مُسْلِمٍ مِنْ رِوَايَةِ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ: فَإِذَا عَلَى سَاحِلِ الْبَحْرِ مِثْلُ الْكَثِيبِ الضَّخْمِ، فَأَتَيْنَاهُ فَإِذَا بِدَابَّةٍ يُقَالُ لَهَا: الْعَنْبَرُ قَالَ: قَالَ أَبُو عُبَيْدَةَ: مَيْتة، ثُمَّ قَالَ: لَا نَحْنُ رُسُلُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ وَفِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ، وَقَدِ اضْطُرِرْتُمْ فَكُلُوا قَالَ: فَأَقَمْنَا عَلَيْهِ شَهْرًا وَنَحْنُ ثَلَاثُمِائَةٍ حَتَّى سَمِنَّا. وَلَقَدْ رأيتُنا نَغْتَرِفُ مِنْ وَقْب عَيْنِهِ بِالْقِلَالِ الدُّهْنَ، وَنَقْتَطِعُ مِنْهُ الفِدْر كَالثَّوْرِ، أَوْ: كقَدْر الثَّوْرِ، قَالَ: وَلَقَدْ أَخَذَ مِنَّا أَبُو عُبَيْدَةَ ثَلَاثَةَ عَشَرَ رَجُلًا فَأَقْعَدَهُمْ فِي وَقْب عَيْنِهِ، وَأَخَذَ ضِلْعًا مِنْ أَضْلَاعِهِ فَأَقَامَهَا، ثُمَّ رَحَلَ أَعْظَمَ بِعِيرٍ مَعَنَا فَمَرَّ مِنْ تَحْتِهَا، وَتَزَوَّدْنَا مِنْ لَحْمِهِ وَشَائِقَ. فَلَمَّا قَدِمْنَا الْمَدِينَةَ أَتَيْنَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ. فَذَكَرْنَا ذَلِكَ لَهُ، فَقَالَ: "هُوَ رِزْقٌ أَخْرَجَهُ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ، هَلْ مَعَكُمْ مِنْ لَحْمِهِ شَيْءٌ فَتُطْعِمُونَا؟ " قَالَ: فَأَرْسَلْنَا إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ مِنْهُ فَأَكَلَهُ. وَفِي بَعْضِ رِوَايَاتِ مُسْلِمٍ: أَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا مَعَ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ حِينَ وَجَدُوا هَذِهِ السَّمَكَةَ. فَقَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ: هِيَ وَاقِعَةٌ أُخْرَى، وَقَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ: بَلْ هِيَ قَضِيَّةٌ وَاحِدَةٌ، وَلَكِنْ كَانُوا أَوَّلًا مَعَ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، ثُمَّ بَعَثَهُمْ سَرِيَّةً مَعَ أَبِي عُبَيْدَةَ، فَوَجَدُوا هَذِهِ فِي سَرِيَّتِهِمْ تِلْكَ مَعَ أَبِي عبيدة، والله أعلم. وَقَالَ مَالِكٌ، عَنْ صَفْوَانَ بْنِ سُلَيم، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ سَلَمة -مِنْ آلِ ابْنِ الْأَزْرَقِ: أَنَّ الْمُغِيرَةَ بْنَ أَبِي بُرْدَةَ-وَهُوَ مِنْ بَنِي عَبْدِ الدَّارِ-أَخْبَرَهُ، أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ يَقُولُ: سَأَلَ رَجُلٌ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَقَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنَّا نَرْكَبُ الْبَحْرَ، وَنَحْمِلُ مَعَنَا الْقَلِيلَ مِنَ الْمَاءِ، فَإِنْ تَوَضَّأْنَا بِهِ عَطِشْنَا، أَفَنَتَوَضَّأُ بِمَاءِ الْبَحْرِ؟ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "هُوَ الطَّهُور مَاؤُهُ الحِلّ مَيْتَتُهُ".

وَقَدْ رَوَى هَذَا الْحَدِيثَ الْإِمَامَانِ الشَّافِعِيُّ، وَأَحْمَدُ بْنُ حَنْبَلٍ، وَأَهْلُ السُّنَنِ الْأَرْبَعَةِ، وَصَحَّحَهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ، وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ، وَابْنُ خُزَيْمَةَ، وَابْنُ حِبَّان، وَغَيْرُهُمْ. وَقَدْ رُوِيَ عَنْ جَمَاعَةٍ مِنَ الصَّحَابَةِ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ بِنَحْوِهِ.

وَقَدْ رَوَى الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ، وَأَبُو دَاوُدَ، وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ، وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ، مِنْ طُرُقٍ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو المُهَزّم -هُوَ يَزِيدُ بْنُ سُفْيَانَ-سَمِعْتُ أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ يَقُولُ: كُنَّا مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ فِي حَجٍّ -أَوْ عُمْرَةٍ-فَاسْتَقْبَلْنَا رِجْل جَراد، فَجَعَلْنَا نَضْرِبُهُنَّ بِعِصِيِّنَا وَسِيَاطِنَا فَنَقْتُلُهُنَّ، فَأُسْقِطَ فِي أَيْدِينَا، فَقُلْنَا: مَا نَصْنَعُ وَنَحْنُ مُحْرِمُونَ؟ فَسَأَلْنَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَقَالَ: "لَا بَأْسَ بِصَيْدِ الْبَحْرِ" 

أَبُو المُهَزّم ضَعِيفٌ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ مَاجَهْ: حَدَّثَنَا هَارُونُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الحَمَّال، حَدَّثَنَا هَاشِمُ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ، حَدَّثَنَا زِيَادُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ عُلاثة، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ وَأَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ كَانَ إِذَا دَعَا عَلَى الْجَرَادِ قَالَ: "اللَّهُمَّ أهْلك كِبَارَهُ، وَاقْتُلْ صِغَارَهُ، وأفسدْ بَيْضَهُ، وَاقْطَعْ دَابِرَهُ، وَخُذْ بِأَفْوَاهِهِ عَنْ مَعَايِشِنَا وَأَرْزَاقِنَا، إِنَّكَ سَمِيعُ الدُّعَاءِ". فَقَالَ خَالِدٌ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، كَيْفَ تَدْعُو عَلَى جُنْدٍ مِنْ أَجْنَادِ اللَّهِ بِقَطْعِ دَابِرِهِ؟ فَقَالَ: "إِنَّ الْجَرَادَ نَثْرَة الْحُوتِ فِي الْبَحْرِ". قَالَ هَاشِمٌ: قَالَ زِيَادٌ: فَحَدَّثَنِي مَنْ رَأَى الْحُوتَ يَنْثُرُهُ. تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ ابْنُ مَاجَهْ. 

وَقَدْ رَوَى الشَّافِعِيُّ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْج، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: أَنَّهُ أَنْكَرَ عَلَى مَنْ يَصِيدُ الْجَرَادَ فِي الْحَرَمِ.

وَقَدِ احْتَجَّ بِهَذِهِ الْآيَةِ الْكَرِيمَةِ مَنْ ذَهَبَ مِنَ الْفُقَهَاءِ إِلَى أَنَّهُ تُؤْكَلُ دَوَابُّ الْبَحْرِ، وَلَمْ يَسْتَثْنِ مِنْ ذَلِكَ شَيْئًا. وَقَدْ تَقَدَّمَ عَنِ الصِّدِّيقِ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: ﴿طَعَامُهُ﴾ كُلُّ مَا فِيهِ.

وَقَدِ اسْتَثْنَى بَعْضُهُمُ الضَّفَادِعَ وَأَبَاحَ مَا سِوَاهَا؛ لِمَا رَوَاهُ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ، وَأَبُو دَاوُدَ وَالنَّسَائِيُّ مِنْ رِوَايَةِ ابْنِ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ التَّيْمِيِّ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عليه وسلم "نهى عن قتل الضفدع". وَلِلنَّسَائِيِّ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو قَالَ: نَهَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ عَنْ قَتْلِ الضِّفْدِعِ، وَقَالَ: نَقِيقُها تَسْبِيحٌ. 

وَقَالَ آخَرُونَ: يُؤْكَلُ مِنْ صَيْدِ الْبَحْرِ السَّمَكُ، وَلَا يُؤْكَلُ الضِّفْدِعُ. وَاخْتَلَفُوا فِيمَا سِوَاهُمَا، فَقِيلَ: يُؤْكَلُ سَائِرُ ذَلِكَ، وَقِيلَ: لَا يُؤْكَلُ. وَقِيلَ: مَا أُكِلَ شَبَهَهُ مِنَ الْبَرِّ أُكِلَ مِثْلُهُ فِي الْبَحْرِ، وَمَا لَا يُؤْكَلُ شَبَهُهُ لَا يُؤْكَلُ. وَهَذِهِ كُلُّهَا وُجُوهٌ فِي مَذْهَبِ الشَّافِعِيِّ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ.

قَالَ أَبُو حَنِيفَةَ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ: لَا يُؤْكَلُ مَا مَاتَ فِي الْبَحْرِ، كَمَا لَا يُؤْكَلُ مَا مَاتَ فِي الْبَرِّ؛ لِعُمُومِ قَوْلِهِ: ﴿حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَيْتَةُ﴾ [الْمَائِدَةِ: ٣] .

وَقَدْ وَرَدَ حَدِيثٌ بِنَحْوِ ذَلِكَ، فَقَالَ ابْنُ مَرْدَوَيْهِ:

حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْبَاقِي -هُوَ ابْنُ قَانِعٍ-حَدَّثَنَا الْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ التُّسْتَرِيّ وَعَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُوسَى بْنِ أَبِي عُثْمَانَ قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا الْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ الطَّحَّانُ، حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِياث، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَا صِدْتُموه وَهُوَ حَيٌّ فَمَاتَ فَكُلُوهُ، وَمَا أَلْقَى الْبَحْرُ مَيِّتًا طَافِيًا فَلَا تَأْكُلُوهُ".

ثُمَّ رَوَاهُ مِنْ طَرِيقِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أُمَيَّةَ، وَيَحْيَى بْنِ أَبِي أُنَيْسَة، عَنْ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ عَنْ جَابِرٍ بِهِ. وَهُوَ مُنْكَرٌ. 

وَقَدِ احْتَجَّ الْجُمْهُورُ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ مَالِكٍ، وَالشَّافِعِيِّ، وَأَحْمَدَ بْنِ حَنْبَلٍ، بِحَدِيثِ "العَنْبَر" الْمُتَقَدِّمِ ذِكْرُهُ، وَبِحَدِيثِ: "هُوَ الطَّهُورُ مَاؤُهُ الْحِلُّ مَيْتَتُهُ"، وَقَدْ تَقَدَّمَ أَيْضًا.

وَرَوَى الْإِمَامُ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الشَّافِعِيُّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "أحِلَّت لَنَا ميتتان ودَمَان، فأما الميتتان فالحوت والجراد، وأما الدَّمَانِ فَالْكَبِدُ وَالطُّحَالُ".

وَرَوَاهُ أَحْمَدُ وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ، وَالدَّارَقُطْنِيُّ وَالْبَيْهَقِيُّ. وَلَهُ شَوَاهِدُ، وَرُوِيَ مَوْقُوفًا، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَحُرِّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ صَيْدُ الْبَرِّ مَا دُمْتُمْ حُرُمًا﴾ أَيْ: فِي حَالِ إِحْرَامِكُمْ يَحْرُمُ عَلَيْكُمُ الِاصْطِيَادُ. فَفِيهِ دَلَالَةٌ عَلَى تَحْرِيمٍ ذَلِكَ فَإِذَا اصْطَادَ الْمُحْرِمُ الصَّيْدَ مُتَعَمِّدًا أثمَ وغَرم، أَوْ مُخْطِئًا غُرِّمَ وَحَرُمَ عَلَيْهِ أَكْلُهُ؛ لِأَنَّهُ فِي حَقِّهِ كَالْمَيْتَةِ، وَكَذَا فِي حَقِّ غَيْرِهِ مِنَ الْمُحْرِمِينَ وَالْمُحِلِّينَ عِنْدَ مَالِكٍ وَالشَّافِعِيِّ -فِي أَحَدِ قَوْلَيْهِ-وَبِهِ يَقُولُ عَطَاءٌ، وَالْقَاسِمُ، وَسَالِمٌ، وَأَبُو يُوسُفَ، وَمُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ، وَغَيْرُهُمْ. فَإِنْ أَكَلَهُ أَو شَيْئًا مِنْهُ، فَهَلْ يَلْزَمُهُ جَزَاءٌ؟ فِيهِ قَوْلَانِ لِلْعُلَمَاءِ:

أَحَدُهُمَا: نَعَمْ، قَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْج، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: إِنْ ذَبَحَهُ ثُمَّ أَكَلَهُ فَكَفَّارَتَانِ، وَإِلَيْهِ ذَهَبَ طَائِفَةٌ.
