وَالثَّانِي: لَا جَزَاءَ عَلَيْهِ بِأَكْلِهِ. نَصَّ عَلَيْهِ مَالِكُ بْنُ أَنَسٍ.

قَالَ أَبُو عُمَرَ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْبَرِّ: وَعَلَى هَذَا مَذَاهِبُ فُقَهَاءِ الْأَمْصَارِ، وَجُمْهُورُ الْعُلَمَاءِ. ثُمَّ وَجَّهَهُ أَبُو عُمَرَ بِمَا لَوْ وَطِئَ ثُمَّ وَطِئَ ثُمَّ وَطِئَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَحُدَّ، فَإِنَّمَا عَلَيْهِ حَدٌّ وَاحِدٌ. 

وَقَالَ أَبُو حَنِيفَةَ: عَلَيْهِ قِيمَةُ مَا أَكَلَ.

وَقَالَ أَبُو ثَوْرٍ: إِذَا قَتَلَ الْمُحْرِمُ الصَّيْدَ فَعَلَيْهِ جَزَاؤُهُ، وَحَلَالٌ أَكْلُ ذَلِكَ الصَّيْدِ، إِلَّا أَنَّنِي أَكْرَهُهُ لِلَّذِي قَتَلَهُ، لِلْخَبَرِ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "صَيْد البَرِّ لَكُمْ حَلَالٌ، مَا لَمْ تُصِيدوه

أَوْ يُصَدْ لَكُمْ".

وَهَذَا الْحَدِيثُ سَيَأْتِي بَيَانُهُ. وَقَوْلُهُ بِإِبَاحَتِهِ لِلْقَاتِلِ غَرِيبٌ، وَأَمَّا لِغَيْرِهِ فَفِيهِ خِلَافٌ. قَدْ ذَكَرْنَا الْمَنْعَ عَمَّنْ تَقَدَّمَ. وَقَالَ آخَرُونَ. بِإِبَاحَتِهِ لِغَيْرِ الْقَاتِلِ، سَوَاءٌ الْمُحْرِمُونَ وَالْمُحِلُّونَ؛ لِهَذَا الْحَدِيثِ. وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَأَمَّا إِذَا صَادَ حَلال صَيْدًا فَأَهْدَاهُ إِلَى مُحْرِمٍ، فَقَدْ ذَهَبَ ذَاهِبُونَ إِلَى إِبَاحَتِهِ مُطْلَقًا، وَلَمْ يَسْتَفْصِلُوا بَيْنَ أَنْ يَكُونَ قَدْ صَادَهُ لِأَجْلِهِ أَمْ لَا. حَكَى هَذَا الْقَوْلَ أَبُو عُمَرَ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْبَرِّ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ، وَأَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، وَالزُّبَيْرِ بْنِ الْعَوَّامِ، وَكَعْبِ الْأَحْبَارِ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ وَعَطَاءٍ -فِي رِوَايَةٍ-وَسَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ. قَالَ: وَبِهِ قَالَ الْكُوفِيُّونَ.

قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ بَزِيع، حَدَّثَنَا بِشْر بْنُ الْمُفَضَّلِ، حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدٌ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، أَنَّ سَعِيدَ بْنَ الْمُسَيَّبِ حَدَّثَهُ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ؛ أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنْ لَحْمِ صَيْدٍ صَادَهُ حَلال، أَيَأْكُلُهُ الْمُحْرِمُ؟ قَالَ: فَأَفْتَاهُمْ بِأَكْلِهِ. ثُمَّ لَقِيَ عُمَرَ بْنَ الْخَطَّابِ فَأَخْبَرَهُ بِمَا كَانَ مِنْ أَمْرِهِ، فَقَالَ: لَوْ أَفْتَيْتَهُمْ بِغَيْرِ هَذَا لأوجعتُ لَكَ رَأْسَكَ.

وَقَالَ آخَرُونَ: لَا يَجُوزُ أَكْلُ الصَّيْدِ لِلْمُحْرمِ بِالْكُلِّيَّةِ، وَمَنَعُوا مِنْ ذَلِكَ مُطْلَقًا؛ لِعُمُومِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ الْكَرِيمَةِ.

وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، عَنْ مَعْمَر، عَنِ ابْنِ طَاوُسٍ وَعَبْدُ الْكَرِيمِ بْنُ أَبِي أميَّة، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ؛ أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ أَكْلَ لَحْمِ الصَّيْدِ لِلْمُحْرِمِ. وَقَالَ: هِيَ مُبْهَمَةٌ. يَعْنِي قَوْلَهُ: ﴿وَحُرِّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ صَيْدُ الْبَرِّ مَا دُمْتُمْ حُرُمًا﴾ .

قَالَ: وَأَخْبَرَنِي مَعْمَرٌ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ؛ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَكْرَهُ لِلْمُحْرِمِ أَنْ يَأْكُلَ مِنْ لَحْمِ الصَّيْدِ عَلَى كل حال.

قَالَ مَعْمَرٌ: وَأَخْبَرَنِي أَيُّوبُ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، مِثْلَهُ.

قَالَ ابْنُ عَبْدِ الْبَرِّ: وَبِهِ قَالَ طَاوُسٌ، وَجَابِرُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ، وَإِلَيْهِ ذهب الثوري، وإسحاق بن رَاهْوَيْهِ -فِي رِوَايَةٍ-وَقَدْ رُوِيَ نَحْوُهُ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ، رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ مِنْ طَرِيقِ سَعِيدِ بْنِ أَبِي عَرُوبة، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ: أَنَّ عَلِيًّا كَرِهَ لَحْمَ الصَّيْدِ لِلْمُحْرِمِ عَلَى كُلِّ حَالٍ.

وَقَالَ مَالِكٌ، وَالشَّافِعِيُّ، وَأَحْمَدُ بْنُ حَنْبَلٍ، وَإِسْحَاقُ بن رَاهْوَيْهِ -فِي رِوَايَةٍ-وَالْجُمْهُورُ: إِنْ كَانَ الْحَلَالُ قَدْ قَصَدَ الْمُحْرِمَ بِذَلِكَ الصَّيْدِ، لَمْ يَجُزْ لِلْمُحْرِمِ أَكْلُهُ؛ لِحَدِيثِ الصَّعْبِ بْنِ جَثَّامَةَ: أَنَّهُ أَهْدَى لِلنَّبِيِّ ﷺ حِمَارًا وَحَشِيًا، وَهُوَ بِالْأَبْوَاءِ -أَوْ: بوَدّان-فَرَدَّهُ عَلَيْهِ، فَلَمَّا رَأَى مَا فِي وَجْهِهِ قَالَ: "إِنَّا لَمْ نرُدَّه عَلَيْكَ إِلَّا أَنَّا حُرُم".

وَهَذَا الْحَدِيثُ مُخَرَّجٌ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ، وَلَهُ أَلْفَاظٌ كَثِيرَةٌ قَالُوا: فَوَجْهُهُ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ ظَنَّ أَنَّ هَذَا إِنَّمَا صَادَهُ مِنْ أَجْلِهِ، فَرَدَّهُ لِذَلِكَ. فَأَمَّا إِذَا لَمْ يَقْصِدْهُ بِالِاصْطِيَادِ فَإِنَّهُ يَجُوزُ لَهُ الْأَكْلُ مِنْهُ؛ لِحَدِيثِ أَبِي قَتَادَةَ حِينَ صَادَ حِمَارَ وَحْش، كَانَ حَلَالًا لَمْ يُحْرِمْ، وَكَانَ أَصْحَابُهُ مُحْرِمِينَ، فَتَوَقَّفُوا فِي أَكْلِهِ. ثُمَّ سَأَلُوا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَقَالَ: "هَلْ كَانَ مِنْكُمْ أَحَدٌ أَشَارَ إِلَيْهَا، أَوْ أَعَانَ فِي قَتْلِهَا؟ " قَالُوا: لَا. قَالَ: "فَكُلُوا". وَأَكَلَ مِنْهَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ.

وَهَذِهِ الْقِصَّةُ ثَابِتَةٌ أَيْضًا فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ بِأَلْفَاظٍ كَثِيرَةٍ. 

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ وَقُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ أَبِي عَمْرٍو، عَنِ الْمُطَّلِبِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ حَنْطَب، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ -وَقَالَ قُتَيْبَةُ فِي حَدِيثِهِ: سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ-يَقُولُ: "صَيْدُ الْبَرِّ لَكُمْ حَلَالٌ -قَالَ سَعِيدٌ: وَأَنْتُمْ حُرُمٌ-مَا لَمْ تُصِيدوه أَوْ يُصَدْ لَكُمْ".

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ وَالنَّسَائِيُّ جَمِيعًا، عَنْ قُتَيْبَةَ. وَقَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: لَا نَعْرِفُ لِلْمُطَّلِبِ سَمَاعًا مِنْ جَابِرٍ. 

وَرَوَاهُ الْإِمَامُ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِدْرِيسَ الشَّافِعِيُّ، مِنْ طَرِيقِ عَمْرِو بْنِ أَبِي عَمْرٍو، عَنْ مَوْلَاهُ الْمُطَّلِبِ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ ثُمَّ قَالَ: وَهَذَا أَحْسَنُ حَدِيثٍ رُوِيَ فِي هَذَا الْبَابِ وَأَقْيَسُ.

وَقَالَ مَالِكٌ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَامِرِ بْنِ رَبِيعَةَ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عُثْمَانَ بْنَ عَفَّانَ بالعَرْج، وَهُوَ مُحْرِمٌ فِي يَوْمٍ صَائِفٍ، قَدْ غَطَّى وَجْهَهُ بِقَطِيفَةِ أرجوان، ثم أتي بلحم صيد فقال لِأَصْحَابِهِ: كُلُوا، فَقَالُوا: أوَلا تَأْكُلُ أَنْتَ؟ فَقَالَ: إِنِّي لَسْتُ كَهَيْئَتِكُمْ، إِنَّمَا صِيدَ مِنْ أَجْلِي.

---

## 5:100-102 — verses 100-102 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Almighty says to His Messenger ﷺ: {Say}, O Muhammad: {The corrupt and the good are not equal, even though the abundance of corruption may impress you}, meaning: O mankind, the abundance of what is corrupt. This means that a small amount of what is lawful and beneficial is better than a large amount of what is unlawful and harmful, as it has come in the hadith: "What is little but sufficient is better than what is abundant but distracting."

Abu al-Qasim al-Baghawi said in his Mu'jam: [chain] from Tha'labah ibn Hatib al-Ansari, that he said: O Messenger of Allah, ask Allah to grant me wealth. The Prophet ﷺ said: "A little for which you give thanks is better than much which you cannot bear."

{So fear Allah, O people of understanding}, meaning: O possessors of sound and upright intellects, avoid the unlawful and leave it, be content with the lawful and let it suffice you, {so that you may succeed}, meaning: in this world and the next.

Then the Almighty says: {O you who believe, do not ask about things which, if made clear to you, would trouble you}. This is a discipline from Allah [the Almighty] to His believing servants, and a prohibition against their asking about things from which there is no benefit for them in asking and probing, because if those matters were disclosed to them, it might trouble them and be hard for them to hear, as it has come in the hadith that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Let no one convey to me anything about anyone else, for I love to come out to you with a sound heart."

Al-Bukhari said: [chain] from Anas ibn Malik, who said: The Prophet ﷺ gave a sermon the like of which I had never heard. He said: "If you knew what I know, you would laugh little and weep much." He said: So the companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ covered their faces, weeping with a stifled sound. Then a man said: Who is my father? He said: "So-and-so." Then this verse was revealed: {Do not ask about things}.

It was narrated by al-Nadr and Rawh ibn 'Ubadah, from Shu'bah. Al-Bukhari narrated it in another place, as did Muslim, Ahmad, al-Tirmidhi, and al-Nasa'i, through various chains from Shu'bah ibn al-Hajjaj, with the same content.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] from Qatadah, concerning His saying: {O you who believe, do not ask about things which, if made clear to you, would trouble you}, the verse: he said: We were told that Anas ibn Malik told him that the people used to ask the Messenger of Allah ﷺ questions until they wore him out with questioning. So one day he came out to them and ascended the pulpit, and said: "Do not ask me about anything today except that I will explain it to you." The companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ were apprehensive that something imminent was about to happen. So I began not to turn right or left except that I found everyone wrapping his head in his garment, weeping. Then a man stood up, one who used to be disputed over and called by a name other than his father's, and said: O Prophet of Allah, who is my father? He said: "Your father is Hudhafah." Then 'Umar stood, or it was said: 'Umar spoke up, and said: We are content with Allah as Lord, with Islam as religion, and with Muhammad as Messenger, seeking refuge with Allah, or he said: I seek refuge with Allah, from the evil of trials. He said: And the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "I have never seen good and evil as I have seen today. Paradise and the Fire were pictured for me until I saw them just beyond the wall." Both al-Bukhari and Muslim reported it through the route of Sa'id.

Ma'mar narrated it from al-Zuhri, from Anas, in similar or nearly similar wording. Al-Zuhri said: The mother of 'Abdullah ibn Hudhafah said: I have never seen a more undutiful son than you. Were you not afraid that your mother might have committed what the people of the Jahiliyyah committed, and so you exposed her before all the people? He said: By Allah, even if he had attributed me to a black slave, I would have accepted it.

Ibn Jarir also said: [chain] from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ came out angry, his face flushed red, until he sat on the pulpit. A man stood up to him and said: Where is my father? He said: "In the Fire." Then another stood and said: Who is my father? He said: "Your father is Hudhafah." Then 'Umar ibn al-Khattab stood and said: We are content with Allah as Lord, with Islam as religion, with Muhammad as Prophet, and with the Qur'an as guide. We, O Messenger of Allah, are recently come from Jahiliyyah and idolatry, and Allah knows best who our fathers are. He said: So his anger subsided, and this verse was revealed: {O you who believe, do not ask about things which, if made clear to you, would trouble you}. Its chain is good.

This story has been mentioned in mursal form by more than one of the Salaf, among them Asbat, from al-Suddi, who said concerning His saying: {O you who believe, do not ask about things which, if made clear to you, would trouble you}: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ became angry one day, and stood up to give a sermon, saying: "Ask me, for you will not ask me about anything except that I will inform you of it." Then a man from Quraysh, from Banu Sahm, called 'Abdullah ibn Hudhafah, about whom there had been slander, stood up and said: O Messenger of Allah, who is my father? He said: "Your father is so-and-so," attributing him to his father. Then 'Umar ibn al-Khattab stood up to him and kissed his foot, and said: O Messenger of Allah, we are content with Allah as Lord, with you as Prophet, with Islam as religion, and with the Qur'an as guide. So pardon us, may Allah pardon you. He kept on until he was satisfied. And on that day he said: "The child belongs to the marriage bed, and for the adulterer is stoning."

Then al-Bukhari said: [chain] from Ibn 'Abbas, who said: There were people who used to ask the Messenger of Allah ﷺ questions out of mockery. A man would say: Who is my father? And a man whose she-camel had strayed would say: Where is my she-camel? So Allah revealed concerning them this verse: {O you who believe, do not ask about things which, if made clear to you, would trouble you}, until the end of the whole verse. Al-Bukhari alone transmitted this.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] from 'Ali, who said: When this verse was revealed: {And pilgrimage to the House is a duty owed to Allah by mankind, for whoever is able to find a way there} [Al 'Imran: 97], they said: O Messenger of Allah, every year? He remained silent. They said: Is it every year? He remained silent. He said: Then they said: Is it every year? He said: "No, and had I said yes, it would have become obligatory." So Allah revealed: {O you who believe, do not ask about things which, if made clear to you, would trouble you}, to the end of the verse.

Al-Tirmidhi and Ibn Majah likewise narrated it through the route of Mansur ibn Wardan, with the same content. Al-Tirmidhi said: gharib (singular) from this route, and I heard al-Bukhari say: Abu al-Bakhtari did not meet 'Ali.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Allah has prescribed the pilgrimage for you." A man said: Every year, O Messenger of Allah? He turned away from him, until the man repeated it two or three times. Then he said: "Who is the questioner?" He said: So-and-so. He said: "By the One in Whose hand is my soul, had I said yes, it would have become obligatory, and had it become obligatory upon you, you would not have been able to bear it, and had you abandoned it, you would have disbelieved." So Allah, Mighty and Sublime, revealed: {O you who believe, do not ask about things which, if made clear to you, would trouble you}, until He completed the verse.

Then Ibn Jarir narrated it through the route of al-Husayn ibn Waqid, from Muhammad ibn Ziyad, from Abu Hurayrah, and he said: Then Mihsan al-Asadi stood up, and in another narration through this route: 'Ukkashah ibn Mihsan, which is more likely correct. Ibrahim ibn Muslim al-Hajari is weak (in hadith transmission).

Ibn Jarir also said: [chain] from Abu Umamah al-Bahili, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ stood up among the people and said: "The pilgrimage has been prescribed for you."

A man from the Bedouins stood up and said, "Every year?" The Messenger of Allah's ﷺ speech stopped, he fell silent and became angered, and he remained so for a long while. Then he spoke and said, "Who is the questioner?" The Bedouin said, "It is I." He said, "Woe to you, what would guarantee your safety if I said yes? By Allah, if I said yes it would become obligatory, and if it became obligatory you would not be able to bear it, and you would then disbelieve. Indeed, those before you were destroyed only by leaders who multiplied difficulties. By Allah, if I permitted for you everything on earth and forbade you only the space of a footprint from it, you would fall into it." He said, at that Allah revealed: {O you who believe, do not ask about things which, if made clear to you, would trouble you} to the end of the verse. Its chain of transmission (isnad) contains weakness.

The apparent meaning of the verse is a prohibition against asking about things which, if a person came to know them, would trouble him. So it is preferable to turn away from such things and leave them alone. How excellent is the hadith narrated by Imam Ahmad, where he said:

[chain] Hajjaj told us, I heard Isra'il ibn Yunus, from al-Walid ibn Abi Hisham, client of al-Hamdani, from Zayd ibn Za'idah, from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to his companions, "Let no one convey to me anything about anyone, for I love to come out to you with a sound heart." [the rest of the hadith].

Abu Dawud and al-Tirmidhi narrated it from the hadith of Isra'il, Abu Dawud said "from al-Walid," while al-Tirmidhi said "from Isra'il, from al-Suddi, from al-Walid ibn Abi Hashim," with the same content. Then al-Tirmidhi said: gharib (singular) from this route.

His saying, {And if you ask about them when the Qur'an is being revealed, they will be made clear to you}, means: if you ask about these things which you have been forbidden to ask about, at the time when the revelation is coming down upon the Messenger, they will be explained to you, and that is easy [for Allah].

Then He said, {Allah has pardoned that}, meaning: what happened from you before that, {and Allah is Forgiving, Forbearing}.

It has also been said that what is meant by His saying, {And if you ask about them when the Qur'an is being revealed, they will be made clear to you} is: do not ask about matters that you initiate questions about, for perhaps, because of your questioning, a stringency or restriction might be revealed. It has come in the hadith: "The gravest in sin among the Muslims is the one who asked about something that was not forbidden, and it became forbidden because of his questioning." However, when the Qur'an comes down about a matter in general terms, and you then ask about clarifying it, it will be made clear to you because of your need for it. {Allah has pardoned that}, meaning: whatever He did not mention in His Book is among that which He has pardoned, so remain silent about it as He remained silent about it. In the Sahih, from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, he said: "Leave me as I have left you, for indeed those before you were destroyed only by their excessive questioning and their disputing with their prophets."

Also in a sound hadith: "Indeed Allah has laid down obligations, so do not neglect them; He has set limits, so do not transgress them; He has forbidden things, so do not violate them; and He has remained silent about matters, out of mercy for you, not out of forgetfulness, so do not ask about them."

Then He said, {A people before you asked about them, then came to disbelieve because of them}, meaning: a people before you asked these forbidden questions, and were given answers about them, but then they did not believe in them, and so they came to disbelieve, that is, because of them. That is, the matters were explained to them but they did not benefit from them, because they did not ask seeking guidance, but rather asked out of obstinacy and stubbornness.

Al-'Awfi said, from Ibn Abbas, regarding His saying {O you who believe, do not ask about things which, if made clear to you, would trouble you}: this was because the Messenger of Allah ﷺ called out to the people and said, "O people, Hajj has been prescribed for you." A man from Banu Asad stood up and said, "O Messenger of Allah, every year?" The Messenger of Allah ﷺ became severely angered and said, "By the One in whose hand is my soul, if I said yes it would become obligatory, and if it became obligatory you would not be able to do it, and then you would disbelieve. So leave me as I have left you, and when I command you with something, do it, and when I forbid you something, refrain from it." Then Allah revealed: {O you who believe, do not ask about things which, if made clear to you, would trouble you}. He forbade them to ask questions like those the Christians asked about the Table, and thus they came to disbelieve because of it. So Allah forbade that and said: do not ask about matters concerning which, if the Qur'an were to come down with severity, it would trouble you, but wait, for when the Qur'an comes down, you will not ask about anything except that you will find its clarification. Narrated by Ibn Jarir.

Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas, regarding {O you who believe, do not ask about things which, if made clear to you, would trouble you, and if you ask about them when the Qur'an is being revealed, they will be made clear to you}: he said, when the verse concerning Hajj was revealed, the Prophet ﷺ called out among the people and said, "O people, Allah has prescribed Hajj for you, so perform Hajj." They said, "O Messenger of Allah, is it for one year, or every year?" He said, "No, rather one year. If I had said every year, it would have become obligatory, and if it became obligatory you would have disbelieved." Then Allah, exalted is He, said, {O you who believe, do not ask about things} up to His saying, {then came to disbelieve because of them}. Narrated by Ibn Jarir.

Khusayf said, from Mujahid, from Ibn Abbas, regarding {do not ask about things}: he said, this refers to al-bahirah, al-wasilah, al-sa'ibah, and al-hami (categories of camels dedicated by pagan custom). Do you not see that He says afterward, {Allah has not appointed any bahirah, nor such and such}? He said, as for Ikrimah, he said: they used to ask him about signs (miracles), and they were forbidden from that. Then He said, {A people before you asked about them, then came to disbelieve because of them}. Narrated by Ibn Jarir.

Ikrimah, may Allah have mercy on him, meant that what is intended by this prohibition is asking for signs to occur, just as Quraysh asked that rivers be made to flow for them, and that al-Safa be turned into gold for them, and other such things, and just as the Jews asked that a book be sent down to them from the sky. Allah, exalted is He, has said: {And nothing prevented Us from sending signs except that the former peoples denied them. And We gave Thamud the she-camel as a visible sign, but they wronged her. And We do not send signs except as a warning} [al-Isra: 59]. And He, exalted is He, said: {And they swore by Allah their strongest oaths that if a sign came to them, they would surely believe in it. Say, "The signs are only with Allah." And what will make you perceive that even if it came, they would not believe. And We will turn away their hearts and their eyes just as they did not believe in it the first time, and We will leave them in their transgression, wandering blindly. And even if We had sent down to them the angels, and the dead had spoken to them, and We had gathered together every thing before them, they would not have believed unless Allah willed. But most of them are ignorant} [al-An'am: 109-111].

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى لِرَسُولِهِ ﷺ: ﴿قُلْ﴾ يَا مُحَمَّدُ: ﴿لَا يَسْتَوِي الْخَبِيثُ وَالطَّيِّبُ وَلَوْ أَعْجَبَكَ﴾ أَيْ: يَا أَيُّهَا الْإِنْسَانُ ﴿كَثْرَةُ الْخَبِيثِ﴾ يَعْنِي: أَنَّ الْقَلِيلَ الْحَلَالَ النَّافِعَ خَيْرٌ مِنَ الْكَثِيرِ الْحَرَامِ الضَّارِّ، كَمَا جَاءَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ: "مَا قَلَّ وكَفَى، خَيْرٌ مِمَّا كَثُر وألْهَى".

وَقَالَ أَبُو الْقَاسِمِ البَغَوِيُّ فِي مُعْجَمِهِ: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ زُهَيْر، حَدَّثَنَا الحَوْطِي، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ شُعَيْبٍ، حَدَّثَنَا مُعان بْنُ رِفاعة، عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ يَزِيدَ، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ، عَنْ أَبِي أمامة أَنَّهُ أَخْبَرَهُ عَنْ ثَعْلَبَةَ بْنِ حَاطِبٍ الْأَنْصَارِيِّ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، ادْعُ اللَّهَ أَنْ يَرْزُقَنِي مَالًا. فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "قَلِيلٌ تُؤَدِّي شُكْرَهُ خَيْرٌ مِنْ كَثِيرٍ لَا تُطِيقُهُ". 

﴿فَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ يَا أُولِي الألْبَابِ﴾ أَيْ: يَا ذَوِي الْعُقُولِ الصَّحِيحَةِ الْمُسْتَقِيمَةِ، وَتَجَنَّبُوا الْحَرَامَ وَدَعُوهُ، وَاقْنَعُوا بِالْحَلَالِ وَاكْتَفُوا بِهِ ﴿لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ﴾ أَيْ: فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ إِنْ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ تَسُؤْكُمْ﴾ هَذَا تَأْدِيبٌ مِنَ اللَّهِ [تَعَالَى] لِعِبَادِهِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ، وَنَهْيٌ لَهُمْ عَنْ أَنْ يَسْأَلُوا ﴿عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ﴾ مِمَّا لَا فَائِدَةَ لَهُمْ فِي السُّؤَالِ وَالتَّنْقِيبِ عَنْهَا؛ لِأَنَّهَا إِنْ أُظْهِرَتْ لَهُمْ تِلْكَ الْأُمُورُ رُبَّمَا سَاءَتْهُمْ وَشَقَّ عَلَيْهِمْ سَمَاعُهَا، كَمَا جَاءَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "لَا يُبْلغني أَحَدٌ عَنْ أَحَدٍ شَيْئًا، إِنِّي أُحِبُّ أَنْ أَخْرُجَ إِلَيْكُمْ وَأَنَا سَلِيمُ الصَّدْرِ". 

وَقَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا مُنْذِر بْنُ الْوَلِيدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْجَارُودِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا أبي، حدثنا شعبة، عن مُوسَى بْنِ أَنَسٍ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ قَالَ: خَطَبَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ خُطبة مَا سَمِعْتُ مِثْلَهَا قَطُّ، قَالَ "لَوْ تَعْلَمُونَ مَا أَعْلَمُ لَضَحِكْتُمْ قَلِيلًا وَلَبَكَيْتُمْ كَثِيرًا" قَالَ: فَغَطَّى أَصْحَابُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ وُجُوهَهُمْ لَهُمْ حَنِينٌ. فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ: مَنْ أَبِي؟ قَالَ: "فَلَانٌ"، فَنَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ: ﴿لَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ﴾

رَوَاهُ النَّضْر وَرَوْحُ بْنُ عُبَادَةَ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ فِي غَيْرِ هَذَا الْمَوْضِعِ، وَمُسْلِمٌ، وَأَحْمَدُ، وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ، وَالنَّسَائِيُّ مِنْ طُرُقٍ عَنْ شُعْبَةَ بْنِ الْحَجَّاجِ، بِهِ. 

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا بِشْر، حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ، حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدٌ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ إِنْ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ تَسُؤْكُمْ﴾ الْآيَةَ، قَالَ: فَحَدَّثَنَا أَنَّ أَنَسَ بْنَ مَالِكٍ حَدَّثَهُ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم سألوه حَتَّى أَحْفَوْهُ بِالْمَسْأَلَةِ، فَخَرَجَ عَلَيْهِمْ ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ فَصَعِدَ الْمِنْبَرَ، فَقَالَ: "لَا تَسْأَلُوا الْيَوْمَ عَنْ شَيْءٍ إِلَّا بَيَّنْتُهُ لَكُمْ". فَأَشْفَقَ أَصْحَابُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ أَنْ يَكُونَ بَيْنَ يَدَيْ أَمْرٍ قَدْ حَضَر، فَجَعَلْتُ لَا أَلْتَفِتُ يَمِينًا وَلَا شِمَالًا إِلَّا وَجَدْتُ كُلًّا لَافًّا رَأْسَهُ فِي ثَوْبِهِ يَبْكِي، فَأَنْشَأَ رَجُلٌ كَانَ يُلاحي فَيُدْعَى إِلَى غَيْرِ أَبِيهِ، فَقَالَ: يَا نَبِيَّ اللَّهِ، مَنْ أَبِي؟ قَالَ: "أَبُوكَ حُذَافَةُ". قَالَ: ثُمَّ قَامَ عُمَرُ -أَوْ قَالَ: فَأَنْشَأَ عُمَرُ-فَقَالَ: رَضِينَا بِاللَّهِ رِبَّا، وَبِالْإِسْلَامِ دِينًا، وَبِمُحَمَّدٍ رَسُولًا عَائِذًا بِاللَّهِ -أَوْ قَالَ: أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ-مِنْ شَرِّ الْفِتَنِ قَالَ: وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "لم أَرَ فِي الْخَيْرِ وَالشَّرِّ كَالْيَوْمِ قَطُّ، صُوِّرَتْ لِيَ الْجَنَّةُ وَالنَّارُ حَتَّى رَأَيْتُهُمَا دُونَ الْحَائِطِ". أَخْرَجَاهُ مِنْ طَرِيقِ سَعِيدٍ. 

وَرَوَاهُ مَعْمَر، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ بِنَحْوِ ذَلِكَ -أَوْ قَرِيبًا مِنْهُ-قَالَ الزُّهْرِيُّ: فَقَالَتْ أُمُّ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ حُذَافَةَ: مَا رَأَيْتُ وَلَدًا أَعَقَّ مِنْكَ قَطُّ، أَكُنْتَ تَأْمَنُ أَنْ تَكُونَ أُمُّكَ قَدْ قارفَتْ مَا قارفَ أهلُ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ فَتَفْضَحُهَا عَلَى رُؤُوسِ النَّاسِ، فَقَالَ: وَاللَّهِ لَوْ أَلْحَقَنِي بِعَبْدٍ أَسْوَدَ للحقتُه. 

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ أَيْضًا: حَدَّثَنَا الْحَارِثُ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْعَزِيزِ، حَدَّثَنَا قَيْس، عَنْ أَبِي حَصِين، عَنْ أَبِي صَالِحٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ: خَرَجَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ وَهُوَ غَضْبَانُ مُحْمَارٌّ وَجْهُهُ حَتَّى جَلَسَ عَلَى الْمِنْبَرِ، فَقَامَ إِلَيْهِ رَجُلٌ فَقَالَ: أَيْنَ أَبِي ؟ فَقَالَ: "فِي النَّارِ" فَقَامَ آخَرُ فَقَالَ: مَنْ أَبِي؟ فَقَالَ: "أَبُوكَ حُذَافَةُ"، فَقَامَ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ فَقَالَ: رَضِينَا بِاللَّهِ رَبًّا، وَبِالْإِسْلَامِ دِينًا، وَبِمُحَمَّدٍ نَبِيًّا، وَبِالْقُرْآنِ إِمَامًا، إِنَّا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ حَدِيثو عَهْدٍ بِجَاهِلِيَّةٍ وشرْك، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ مَنْ آبَاؤُنَا. قَالَ: فَسَكَنَ غَضَبُهُ، وَنَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ إِنْ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ تَسُؤْكُمْ﴾ إِسْنَادُهُ جَيِّدٌ. 

وَقَدْ ذَكَرَ هَذِهِ الْقِصَّةَ مُرْسَلَةً غَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ مِنَ السَّلَفِ، مِنْهُمْ أَسْبَاطٌ عن السُّدِّي أنه قال في قوله: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ إِنْ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ تَسُؤْكُمْ﴾ قَالَ: غَضِبَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَوْمًا مِنَ الْأَيَّامِ، فَقَامَ خَطِيبًا فَقَالَ: "سَلُونِي، فَإِنَّكُمْ لَا تَسْأَلُونِي عَنْ شَيْءٍ إِلَّا أَنْبَأَتُكُمْ بِهِ". فَقَامَ إِلَيْهِ رَجُلٌ مِنْ قُرَيْشٍ، مِنْ بَنِي سَهْمٍ، يُقَالُ لَهُ: عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ حُذَافة، وَكَانَ يُطْعَن فِيهِ، فَقَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، مَنْ أَبِي؟ فَقَالَ: "أَبُوكَ فَلَانٌ"، فَدَعَاهُ لِأَبِيهِ، فَقَامَ إِلَيْهِ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ فَقَبَّلَ رِجْلَهُ، وَقَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، رَضِينَا بِاللَّهِ رَبًّا، وَبِكَ نَبِيًّا، وَبِالْإِسْلَامِ دِينًا، وَبِالْقُرْآنِ إِمَامًا، فَاعْفُ عَنَّا عَفَا اللَّهُ عَنْكَ، فَلَمْ يَزَلْ بِهِ حَتَّى رَضِيَ، فَيَوْمَئِذٍ قَالَ: "الْوَلَدُ للفِرَاش وللعاهرِ الحَجَر".

ثُمَّ قَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا الفَضْل بْنُ سَهْل، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو النَّضْر، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَيْثَمَة، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الجُويرية، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: كَانَ قَوْمٌ يَسْأَلُونَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ اسْتِهْزَاءً، فَيَقُولُ الرَّجُلُ: مَنْ أَبِي؟ وَيَقُولُ الرَّجُلُ تَضل ناقتُه: أَيْنَ نَاقَتِي؟ فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ فِيهِمْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ إِنْ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ تَسُؤْكُمْ﴾ حَتَّى فَرَغَ مِنَ الْآيَةِ كُلِّهَا. تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ الْبُخَارِيُّ. 

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا مَنْصُورُ بْنُ وَرْدَان الْأَسَدِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيِّ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ أَبِي البَخْتَريّ -وَهُوَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ فَيْرُوزَ-عَنْ عَلِيٍّ قَالَ: لَمَّا نَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ: ﴿وَلِلَّهِ عَلَى النَّاسِ حِجُّ الْبَيْتِ مَنِ اسْتَطَاعَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلا﴾ [آلِ عِمْرَانَ: ٩٧] قَالُوا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، كُلَّ عَامٍ؟ فَسَكَتَ. فَقَالُوا: أَفِي كُلِّ عَامٍ؟ فَسَكَتَ، قَالَ: ثُمَّ قَالُوا: أَفِي كُلِّ عَامٍ؟ فَقَالَ: "لَا وَلَوْ قُلْتُ: نَعَمْ لَوَجَبَتْ"، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ إِنْ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ تَسُؤْكُمْ﴾ إِلَى آخَرِ الْآيَةِ.

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ، مِنْ طَرِيقِ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ وَرْدَانَ، بِهِ وَقَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: غَرِيبٌ مِنْ هَذَا الْوَجْهِ، وَسَمِعْتُ الْبُخَارِيَّ يَقُولُ: أَبُو الْبَخْتَرِيِّ لَمْ يُدْرِكْ عَلِيًّا.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو كُرَيْب، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ الهَجَرِيّ، عَنْ أَبِي عِيَاضٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَتَبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْحَجَّ" فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ: أَفِي كُلِّ عَامٍ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ؟ فَأَعْرَضَ عَنْهُ، حَتَّى عَادَ مَرَّتَيْنِ أَوْ ثَلَاثًا، فَقَالَ: "مَنِ السَّائِلُ؟ " فَقَالَ: فُلَانٌ. فَقَالَ: "وَالَّذِي نَفْسِي بِيَدِهِ، لَوْ قُلْتُ: نَعَمْ لوَجَبَتْ، وَلَوْ وَجَبَتْ عَلَيْكُمْ مَا أَطَقْتُمُوهُ، وَلَوْ تَرَكْتُمُوهُ لَكَفَرْتُمْ"، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ إِنْ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ تَسُؤْكُمْ﴾ حَتَّى خَتَمَ الْآيَةَ.

ثُمَّ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ مِنْ طَرِيقِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ وَاقِدٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ -وَقَالَ: فَقَامَ مِحْصَن الْأَسَدِيُّ-وَفِي رِوَايَةٍ مِنْ هَذِهِ الطَّرِيقِ: عُكَاشة بن محْصن-وهو أشبه. وَإِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ الْهَجَرِيُّ ضَعِيفٌ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ أَيْضًا: حَدَّثَنِي زَكَرِيَّا بْنُ يَحْيَى بْنِ أَبَانٍ الْمِصْرِيُّ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو زَيْدٍ عَبْدُ الرحمن ابن أَبِي الْغَمْرِ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُطِيعٍ مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ يَحْيَى، عَنْ صَفْوَانَ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، حَدَّثَنِي سُلَيْمُ بْنُ عَامِرٍ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا أُمَامَةَ الْبَاهِلِيَّ يَقُولُ: قَامَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ فِي النَّاسِ فَقَالَ: "كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْحَجُّ". فَقَامَ رَجُلٌ مِنَ الْأَعْرَابِ فَقَالَ: أَفِي كُلِّ عَامٍ؟ قَالَ: فَغَلقَ كَلَامُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ، وَأَسْكَتَ وَاسْتَغْضَبَ، وَمَكَثَ طَوِيلًا ثُمَّ تَكَلَّمَ فَقَالَ: "مَنِ السَّائِلُ؟ " فَقَالَ الْأَعْرَابِيُّ: أَنَا ذَا، فَقَالَ: "وَيْحَكَ، مَاذَا يُؤَمِّنُكَ أَنْ أَقُولَ: نَعَمْ، وَاللَّهِ لَوْ قَلَتُ: نَعَمْ لَوَجَبَتْ، وَلَوْ وَجَبَتْ لَكَفَرْتُمْ، أَلَا إِنَّهُ إِنَّمَا أَهْلَكَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ أَئِمَّةُ الحَرَج، وَاللَّهِ لَوْ أَنِّي أَحْلَلْتُ لَكُمْ جَمِيعَ مَا فِي الْأَرْضِ، وَحَرَّمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ مِنْهَا مَوْضِعَ خُفٍّ، لَوَقَعْتُمْ فِيهِ" قَالَ: فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ عِنْدَ ذَلِكَ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ إِنْ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ تَسُؤْكُمْ﴾ إِلَى آخَرِ الْآيَةِ. فِي إِسْنَادِهِ ضَعُفٌ.

وَظَاهِرُ الْآيَةِ النَّهْيُ عَنِ السُّؤَالِ عَنِ الْأَشْيَاءِ الَّتِي إِذَا عَلِمَ بِهَا الشَّخْصُ سَاءَتْهُ، فَالْأَوْلَى الْإِعْرَاضُ عَنْهَا وَتَرَكُهَا. وَمَا أَحْسَنَ الْحَدِيثُ الذِي رَوَاهُ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ حَيْثُ قَالَ:

حَدَّثَنَا حَجَّاج قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ إِسْرَائِيلَ بْنَ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْوَلِيدِ بْنِ أَبِي هِشَامٍ مَوْلَى الْهَمْدَانِيِّ، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ زَائِدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ لِأَصْحَابِهِ: "لَا يُبَلِّغْنِي أَحَدٌ عَنْ أَحَدٍ شَيْئًا؛ فَإِنِّي أُحِبُّ أَنْ أَخْرُجَ إِلَيْكُمْ وَأَنَا سَلِيمُ الصَّدْرِ" الْحَدِيثَ.

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ إِسْرَائِيلَ -قَالَ أَبُو دَاوُدَ: عَنِ الْوَلِيدِ-وَقَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ-عَنِ السُّدِّيِّ، عَنِ الْوَلِيدِ بْنِ أَبِي هَاشِمٍ، بِهِ. ثُمَّ قَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: غَرِيبٌ مِنْ هَذَا الْوَجْهِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَإِنْ تَسْأَلُوا عَنْهَا حِينَ يُنزلُ الْقُرْآنُ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ﴾ أَيْ: وَإِنْ تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ هَذِهِ الْأَشْيَاءِ الَّتِي نُهِيتُمْ عَنِ السُّؤَالِ عَنْهَا حِينَ يَنْزِلُ الْوَحْيُ عَلَى الرَّسُولِ تُبَيَّن لَكُمْ، وَذَلِكَ [عَلَى اللَّهِ] يَسِيرٌ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ ﴿عَفَا اللَّهُ عَنْهَا﴾ أَيْ: عَمَّا كَانَ مِنْكُمْ قبل ذلك، ﴿وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ حَلِيمٌ﴾

وقيل: المراد بقوله: ﴿وَإِنْ تَسْأَلُوا عَنْهَا حِينَ يُنزلُ الْقُرْآنُ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ﴾ أَيْ: لَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ تَسْتَأْنِفُونَ السُّؤَالَ عَنْهَا، فلعلَّه قَدْ يَنْزِلُ بِسَبَبِ سُؤَالِكُمْ تَشْدِيدٌ أَوْ تَضْيِيقٌ وَقَدْ وَرَدَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ: "أَعْظَمَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ جُرْمًا مَنْ سَأَلَ عَنْ شَيْءٍ لَمْ يُحَرّم فَحُرِّمَ مِنْ أَجْلِ مَسْأَلَتِهِ" وَلَكِنْ إِذَا نَزَلَ الْقُرْآنُ بِهَا مُجْمَلَةً فَسَأَلْتُمْ عَنْ بيانها حينئذ، تبينت لكم لاحتياجكم إليها. ﴿عَفَا اللَّهُ عَنْهَا﴾ أَيْ: مَا لَمْ يَذْكُرْهُ فِي كِتَابِهِ فَهُوَ مِمَّا عَفَا عَنْهُ، فَاسْكُتُوا أَنْتُمْ عَنْهَا كَمَا سَكَتَ عَنْهَا. وَفِي الصَّحِيحِ، عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: "ذَرُونِي مَا تُرِكْتُم؛ فَإِنَّمَا أَهْلَكَ مَنْ كَانَ قَبْلَكُمْ كَثْرَةُ سُؤَالِهِمْ وَاخْتِلَافِهِمْ عَلَى أَنْبِيَائِهِمْ". 

وَفِي الْحَدِيثِ الصَّحِيحِ أَيْضًا: " إِنَّ اللَّهَ فَرَضَ فَرَائِضَ فَلَا تُضيِّعُوها، وحَدَّ حُدُودًا فَلَا تَعْتَدُوهَا، وحَرَّم أَشْيَاءَ فَلَا تَنْتَهِكُوهَا، وَسَكَتَ عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ رَحْمَةً بِكُمْ غَيْرَ نِسْيان فَلَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْهَا". 

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿قَدْ سَأَلَهَا قَوْمٌ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ ثُمَّ أَصْبَحُوا بِهَا كَافِرِينَ﴾ أَيْ: قَدْ سَأَلَ هَذِهِ الْمَسَائِلَ الْمَنْهِيَّ عَنْهَا قومٌ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ، فَأُجِيبُوا عَنْهَا ثُمَّ لَمْ يُؤْمِنُوا بِهَا، فَأَصْبَحُوا بِهَا كَافِرِينَ، أَيْ: بِسَبَبِهَا، أَيْ: بُيِّنَتْ لَهُمْ وَلَمْ يَنْتَفِعُوا بِهَا لِأَنَّهُمْ لَمْ يَسْأَلُوا عَلَى وَجْهِ الِاسْتِرْشَادِ، وَإِنَّمَا سَأَلُوا عَلَى وَجْهِ التَّعَنُّتِ وَالْعِنَادِ.

قَالَ، العَوْفِي، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ إِنْ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ تَسُؤْكُمْ﴾ وَذَلِكَ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم أَذَّنَ فِي النَّاسِ فَقَالَ: "يَا قَوْمِ كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْحَجُّ". فَقَامَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ بَنِي أَسَدٍ فَقَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، أَفِي كُلِّ عَامٍ؟ فأغْضبَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ غَضَبًا شَدِيدًا فَقَالَ: "وَالَّذِي نَفْسِي بِيَدِهِ لَوْ قُلْتُ: نَعَمْ لَوَجَبَتْ، وَلَوْ وَجَبَتْ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُمْ، وإذًا لَكَفَرْتُمْ، فَاتْرُكُونِي مَا تَرَكْتُكُمْ، وَإِذَا أَمَرْتُكُمْ بِشَيْءٍ فَافْعَلُوا، وَإِذَا نَهَيْتُكُمْ عَنْ شَيْءٍ فَانْتَهُوا عَنْهُ". فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ إِنْ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ تَسُؤْكُمْ﴾ نَهَاهُمْ أَنْ يَسْأَلُوا عَنْ مِثْلِ الَّذِي سَأَلَتِ النَّصَارَى مِنَ الْمَائِدَةِ، فَأَصْبَحُوا بِهَا كَافِرِينَ. فَنَهَى اللَّهُ عَنْ ذَلِكَ وَقَالَ: لَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ إِنْ نَزَلَ الْقُرْآنُ فِيهَا بِتَغْلِيظٍ سَاءَكُمْ ذَلِكَ، وَلَكِنِ انْتَظَرُوا، فَإِذَا نَزَلَ الْقُرْآنُ فَإِنَّكُمْ لَا تَسْأَلُونَ عَنْ شَيْءٍ إِلَّا وَجَدْتُمْ تِبْيَانَهُ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ.

وَقَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ إِنْ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ تَسُؤْكُمْ وَإِنْ تَسْأَلُوا عَنْهَا حِينَ يُنزلُ الْقُرْآنُ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ﴾ قَالَ: لَمَّا نَزَلَتْ آيَةُ الْحَجِّ، نَادَى النَّبِيُّ ﷺ فِي النَّاسِ فَقَالَ: "يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ، إِنَّ اللَّهَ قَدْ كَتَبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْحَجَّ فَحُجُّوا". فَقَالُوا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، أَعَامًا وَاحِدًا أَمْ كُلَّ عَامٍ؟ فَقَالَ: "لَا بَلْ عَامًا وَاحِدًا، وَلَوْ قُلْتُ: كُلَّ عَامٍ لَوَجَبَتْ، وَلَوْ وَجَبَتْ لَكَفَرْتُمْ". ثُمَّ قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ﴾ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿ثُمَّ أَصْبَحُوا بِهَا كَافِرِينَ﴾ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ.

وَقَالَ خَصِيف، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: ﴿لَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ﴾ قَالَ: هِيَ الْبَحِيرَةُ وَالْوَصِيلَةُ وَالسَّائِبَةُ وَالِحَامِ، أَلَّا تَرَى أَنَّهُ يَقُولُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ "مَا جَعَلَ اللَّهُ مِنْ بَحَيْرَةٍ وَلَا كَذَا وَلَا كَذَا"، قَالَ: وَأَمَّا عِكْرِمَةُ فَقَالَ: إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا يَسْأَلُونَهُ عَنِ الْآيَاتِ، فَنُهُوا عَنْ ذَلِكَ. ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿قَدْ سَأَلَهَا قَوْمٌ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ ثُمَّ أَصْبَحُوا بِهَا كَافِرِينَ﴾ رواه ابن جرير.

يَعْنِي عِكْرِمَةُ رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ: أَنَّ الْمُرَادَ بِهَذَا النَّهْيِ عَنْ سُؤَالِ وُقُوعِ الْآيَاتِ، كَمَا سَأَلَتْ قُرَيْشٌ أَنْ يُجْرِيَ لَهُمْ أَنْهَارًا، وَأَنْ يَجْعَلَ لَهُمُ الصَّفَا ذَهَبًا وَغَيْرَ ذَلِكَ، وَكَمَا سَأَلَتِ الْيَهُودُ أَنْ يَنْزِلَ عَلَيْهِمْ كِتَابًا مِنَ السَّمَاءِ، وَقَدْ قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَا مَنَعَنَا أَنْ نُرْسِلَ بِالآيَاتِ إِلا أَنْ كَذَّبَ بِهَا الأوَّلُونَ وَآتَيْنَا ثَمُودَ النَّاقَةَ مُبْصِرَةً فَظَلَمُوا بِهَا وَمَا نُرْسِلُ بِالآيَاتِ إِلا تَخْوِيفًا﴾ [الْإِسْرَاءِ: ٥٩] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَأَقْسَمُوا بِاللَّهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمَانِهِمْ لَئِنْ جَاءَتْهُمْ آيَةٌ لَيُؤْمِنُنَّ بِهَا قُلْ إِنَّمَا الآيَاتُ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ وَمَا يُشْعِرُكُمْ أَنَّهَا إِذَا جَاءَتْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ. وَنُقَلِّبُ أَفْئِدَتَهُمْ وَأَبْصَارَهُمْ كَمَا لَمْ يُؤْمِنُوا بِهِ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ وَنَذَرُهُمْ فِي طُغْيَانِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ. وَلَوْ أَنَّنَا نزلْنَا إِلَيْهِمُ الْمَلائِكَةَ وَكَلَّمَهُمُ الْمَوْتَى وَحَشَرْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ قُبُلا مَا كَانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا إِلا أَنْ يَشَاءَ اللَّهُ وَلَكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ يَجْهَلُونَ﴾ [الْأَنْعَامِ: ١٠٩-١١١] .

---

## 5:103-104 — verses 103-104 (shared commentary)

### Translation

Al-Bukhari said: Musa ibn Isma'il narrated to us, Ibrahim ibn Sa'd narrated to us, from Salih ibn Kaysan, from Ibn Shihab, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, who said: "al-Bahirah" is the animal whose milk is withheld from the tawaghit (false deities/idols), so that no one among the people may milk it. And "al-Sa'ibah": they used to set it loose for their gods, so that nothing was to be loaded upon it. He said: and Abu Hurayrah said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "I saw Amr ibn Amir al-Khuza'i dragging his intestines in the Fire; he was the first to set loose the sa'ibah animals." And "al-Wasilah": the young she-camel that bears her firstborn, then bears a second female afterward; they used to set it loose for their tawaghit if one female was joined to another with no male between them. And "al-Ham": the stud camel that has completed a set number of matings; when it finished its breeding they left it for the tawaghit and exempted it from being loaded, so that nothing was carried upon it, and they called it al-Hami.

Muslim and al-Nasa'i likewise narrated it, from the hadith of Ibrahim ibn Sa'd, with the same wording.

Then al-Bukhari said: Abu al-Yaman said to me: Shu'ayb informed us, from al-Zuhri, who said: I heard Sa'id relating this. And Abu Hurayrah said something similar from the Prophet ﷺ. Ibn al-Had also narrated it, from Ibn Shihab, from Sa'id, from Abu Hurayrah, from the Prophet ﷺ.

Al-Hakim said: al-Bukhari meant that Yazid ibn Abd Allah ibn al-Had narrated it from Abd al-Wahhab ibn Bukht, from al-Zuhri. This is how our teacher Abu al-Hajjaj al-Muzani related it in "al-Atraf," and he was silent about it and did not draw attention to it. There is something to consider in what al-Hakim said, for Imam Ahmad and Abu Ja'far ibn Jarir both narrated it from the hadith of al-Layth ibn Sa'd, from Ibn al-Had, from al-Zuhri himself. And Allah knows best.

Then al-Bukhari said: Muhammad ibn Abi Ya'qub Abu Abd Allah al-Kirmani narrated to us, Hassan ibn Ibrahim narrated to us, Yunus narrated to us, from al-Zuhri, from Urwah, that A'ishah said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "I saw Hell consuming itself, part of it crushing another, and I saw Amr dragging his intestines; he was the first to set loose the sa'ibah animals." Al-Bukhari alone transmitted this.

Ibn Jarir said: Hannad narrated to us, Yunus ibn Bukayr narrated to us, Muhammad ibn Ishaq narrated to us, Muhammad ibn Ibrahim ibn al-Harith narrated to me, from Abu Salih, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say to Aktham ibn al-Jawn, "O Aktham, I saw Amr ibn Luhayy ibn Qam'ah ibn Khindif dragging his intestines in the Fire, and I have not seen any man more resembling another man than you resemble him, nor him you." Aktham said, "Do you fear that his resemblance will harm me, O Messenger of Allah?" The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "No, for you are a believer and he is a disbeliever. He was the first to change the religion of Ibrahim (peace be upon him), and the first to designate the bahirah, set loose the sa'ibah, and protect the hami."

He then narrated it from Hannad, from Abdah, from Muhammad ibn Amr, from Abu Salamah, from Abu Hurayrah, from the Prophet ﷺ, with similar or matching wording.

Neither of these two chains is found in the standard collections (al-kutub).

Imam Ahmad said: Amr ibn Mujammi' narrated to us, Ibrahim al-Hajari narrated to us, from Abu al-Ahwas, from Abd Allah ibn Mas'ud, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "The first to set loose the sa'ibah animals and to worship idols was Abu Khuza'ah, Amr ibn Amir, and indeed I saw him dragging his intestines in the Fire." Ahmad alone transmitted it by this route.

Abd al-Razzaq said: Ma'mar informed us, from Zayd ibn Aslam, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "I indeed know the first to set loose the sa'ibah animals, and the first to change the religion of Ibrahim (peace be upon him)." They said, "Who was he, O Messenger of Allah?" He said, "Amr ibn Luhayy, brother of the Banu Ka'b. I saw him dragging his intestines in the Fire, his stench harming the people of the Fire. And I indeed know the first who designated the bahirah animals." They said, "Who was he, O Messenger of Allah?" He said, "A man of the Banu Mudlij, who had two she-camels; he slit their ears and forbade their milk, then afterward drank their milk himself. I saw him in the Fire, with the two of them biting him with their mouths and striking him with their hooves."

This Amr is Ibn Luhayy ibn Qam'ah, one of the chiefs of Khuza'ah, who had authority over the House (the Ka'bah) after Jurhum. He was the first to change the religion of Ibrahim the Friend of Allah (peace be upon him), bringing idols into the Hijaz and calling the common people to worship them and seek nearness to Allah through them. He instituted for them these pre-Islamic legal practices (shara'i' al-jahiliyyah) concerning livestock and other things, as Allah, exalted is He, mentions in Surat al-An'am, at His saying: {And they assign to Allah, out of what He has created of crops and cattle, a portion} [al-An'am: 136], to the end of the verses concerning this.

As for al-Bahirah, Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas: it is the she-camel that, when she has borne five litters, they examine the fifth; if it is male, they slaughter it and only the men eat it, not the women. If it is female, they slit her ears and say, "This is a bahirah."

Al-Suddi and others mentioned something similar to this.

As for al-Sa'ibah, Mujahid said: it is from the sheep, in a manner similar to what was explained regarding the bahirah, except that it is the one that has given birth to offspring such that between her and six offspring she had borne young resembling her; when she bears a seventh, whether male or two males, they slaughter it, and only their men eat it, not their women.

Muhammad ibn Ishaq said: al-Sa'ibah is the she-camel that, when she has borne ten females in succession with no male among them, is set loose; she is not ridden, her hair is not shorn, and her milk is not milked by anyone except a guest.

Abu Rawq said: al-Sa'ibah was such that when a man went out and his need was fulfilled, he would set loose from his wealth a she-camel or something else, and dedicate it to the tawaghit. Whatever it then bore would belong to it (i.e., would likewise be dedicated).

Al-Suddi said: a man among them, when his need was fulfilled, or he recovered from an illness, or his wealth increased, would set loose some portion of his wealth for the idols; and whoever among the people interfered with it was punished with worldly punishment.

As for al-Wasilah, Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas: it is the ewe that, when she has borne seven litters, they examine the seventh; if it is male or female and stillborn, the men alone share in it, not the women; if it is female, they let her live; and if it is a male and a female together in one womb, they let both live and say, "Her sister has joined with him, so she has made him forbidden to us." Ibn Abi Hatim narrated this.

Abd al-Razzaq said: Ma'mar informed us, from al-Zuhri, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, concerning {nor Wasilah}: he said, al-Wasilah is from camels; the she-camel would first bear a female, then bear a second female, so they called her al-Wasilah, saying that she has joined two females with no male between them, so they used to slit her ears for their tawaghit.

The same has been reported from Imam Malik ibn Anas, may Allah have mercy on him.

Muhammad ibn Ishaq said: al-Wasilah from sheep is when a ewe bears ten females in five litters, twin pairs in each litter; she is then named al-Wasilah and left alone. Whatever she bears after that, male or female, is given to the males and not the females. If it is stillborn, they share it among themselves.

As for al-Ham, al-Awfi said, from Ibn Abbas: when a man's stud camel had impregnated ten times, it was said, "He has protected (hama)," so leave him be.

Abu Rawq and Qatadah said likewise. Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas: as for al-Ham, it is the stud camel; when its offspring's offspring is born, they say, "This one has protected his own back," so they do not load anything upon him, nor shear his hair, nor prevent him from any protected grazing land or from any watering trough he wishes to drink from, even if the trough belongs to someone other than his owner.

Ibn Wahb said: I heard Malik say: as for al-Ham, it is from camels; it would mate among the camels, and when its mating period was over, they would place peacock feathers upon it and set it loose.

Other explanations besides this have also been given regarding the interpretation of this verse. A hadith has come down concerning this, narrated by Ibn Abi Hatim, by way of Abu Ishaq al-Sabi'i, from Abu al-Ahwas al-Jushami, from his father Malik ibn Nadlah, who said: I came to the Prophet ﷺ wearing worn-out clothes, and he said to me, "Do you have any wealth?" I said, "Yes." He said, "What kind of wealth?" I said, "Every kind of wealth, camels, sheep, horses, and slaves." He said, "When Allah gives you wealth, let it be seen upon you." Then he said, "Do your camels give birth with complete ears?" I said, "Yes." He said, "Do camels ever give birth otherwise?" He said, "Then perhaps you take a razor and cut the ears of some of them, saying, 'This is a bahir,' and slit the ears of others, saying, 'This is sacred (haram)'?" I said, "Yes." He said, "Then do not do so, for everything Allah has given you is lawful for you." Then he said: {Allah has not decreed anything as bahirah, sa'ibah, wasilah, or ham}. As for al-Bahirah: it is the one whose ears they slit, so that neither his wife, nor his daughters, nor anyone of his household benefit from her wool, her fur, her hair, or her milk; when she dies, they all share in her. As for al-Sa'ibah: it is the one they set loose for their gods, going to their gods and releasing it there. As for al-Wasilah: it is the ewe that bears six litters; when she bears a seventh, her ear is slit and her horn is cut, and they say, "She has joined (wasalat)," so they do not slaughter her, nor is she struck, nor is she prevented, wherever she comes, from any watering trough. This is how the explanation of this matter is given, inserted (mudraj) within the hadith itself. It has also been narrated by another route, from Abu Ishaq, from Abu al-Ahwas Awf ibn Malik, as his own statement, and this is more likely correct.

Imam Ahmad narrated this hadith from Sufyan ibn Uyaynah, from Abu al-Za'ra' Amr ibn Amr, from his uncle Abu al-Ahwas Awf ibn Malik ibn Nadlah, from his father, with the same content; but it does not contain this explanation. And Allah knows best.

As for His saying, {But those who disbelieve invent lies against Allah, and most of them do not understand}: that is, Allah did not decree these things, nor are they, in His sight, an act of nearness to Him; rather, the polytheists fabricated this and made it into a law for themselves and a means of drawing near to Him. Yet that is not what actually results for them; rather it is a calamity upon them.

{And when it is said to them, "Come to what Allah has sent down and to the Messenger," they say, "Sufficient for us is what we found our fathers doing"}: that is, when they are called to the religion of Allah and His law, to what He has made obligatory and to abandoning what He has forbidden, they say, "What we found our fathers and forefathers upon, of ways and paths, suffices us." Allah, exalted is He, says: {Even if their fathers knew nothing at all}, that is, they understood no truth, nor recognized it, nor were guided to it. How then could they follow them, given this situation? None follows them except one who is more ignorant than they, and further astray in his path.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

قَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا مُوسَى بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ صَالِحِ بْنِ كَيْسان، عَنِ ابْنِ شِهَابٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ المسيَّب قَالَ: "الْبَحِيرَةُ": الَّتِي يُمْنَعُ دَرُّهَا لِلطَّوَاغِيتِ، فَلَا يَحْلبها أَحَدٌ مِنَ النَّاسِ. وَ"السَّائِبَةُ": كَانُوا يسيبونَها لِآلِهَتِهِمْ، لَا يُحْمَلُ عَلَيْهَا شَيْءٌ -قَالَ: وَقَالَ أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "رَأَيْتُ عمْرَو بْنَ عَامِرٍ الْخُزَاعِيَّ يجُرّ قُصْبَه فِي النَّارِ، كَانَ أَوَّلَ مَنْ سَيَّبَ السَّوَائِبَ"-و"الْوَصِيلَةُ": النَّاقَةُ الْبِكْرُ، تُبَكّر فِي أَوَّلِ نِتَاجِ الْإِبِلِ، ثُمَّ تُثَنّي بَعْدُ بِأُنْثَى، وَكَانُوا يُسَيِّبُونَهَا لِطَوَاغِيتِهِمْ، إِنْ وَصَلَتْ إحْدَاهما بِالْأُخْرَى لَيْسَ بَيْنَهُمَا ذكَر. و"الْحَامُ": فَحْلُ الْإِبِلِ يَضربُ الضرّابَ الْمَعْدُودَ، فَإِذَا قَضَى ضِرَابَهُ وَدَعُوه لِلطَّوَاغِيتِ، وَأَعْفَوْهُ عَنِ الحَمْل، فَلَمْ يُحْمَل عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ، وسَمّوه الْحَامِيَ.

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ وَالنَّسَائِيُّ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ سَعْدٍ، بِهِ. 

ثُمَّ قَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ: وَقَالَ لِي أَبُو الْيَمَانِ: أَخْبَرَنَا شُعَيْبٌ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ سَعِيدًا يُخْبِرُ بِهَذَا. وَقَالَ أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ نَحْوَهُ. وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ الْهَادِ، عَنِ ابْنِ شِهَابٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ. 

قَالَ الْحَاكِمُ: أَرَادَ الْبُخَارِيُّ أَنَّ يَزِيدَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْهَادِ رَوَاهُ عَنْ عَبْدِ الْوَهَّابِ بْنِ بُخْت، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ. كَذَا حَكَاهُ شَيْخُنَا أَبُو الْحَجَّاجِ الْمُزَنِيُّ فِي "الْأَطْرَافِ" وَسَكَتَ وَلَمْ يُنَبِّهْ عَلَيْهِ. وَفِيمَا قَالَهُ الْحَاكِمُ نَظَرٌ، فَإِنَّ الْإِمَامَ أَحْمَدَ وَأَبَا جَعْفَرِ بْنَ جَرِيرٍ رَوَيَاهُ مِنْ حَدِيثِ اللَّيْثِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ الْهَادِ، عن الزُّهْرِيِّ نَفْسِهِ. وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي يَعْقُوبَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الكِرْماني، حَدَّثَنَا حَسَّانُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، حَدَّثَنَا يُونُسُ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ عُرْوَة؛ أَنَّ عَائِشَةَ قَالَتْ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "رَأَيْتُ جَهَنَّم يَحْطِمُ بَعْضُهَا بَعْضًا، وَرَأَيْتُ عَمْرًا يَجُرُّ قُصْبه، وَهُوَ أَوَّلُ مَنْ سَيَّبَ السَّوَائِبَ". تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ الْبُخَارِيُّ. 

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حدثنا هَنَّاد، حَدَّثَنَا يُونُسُ بْنُ بُكَير، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، حَدَّثَنِي مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ، عَنْ أَبِي صَالِحٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَقُولُ لِأَكْثَمَ بْنِ الجَوْن: "يَا أَكْثَمُ، رَأَيْتُ عَمْرو بْنَ لُحَيّ بْنِ قَمعَةَ بْنِ خِنْدف يَجُرُّ قُصْبه فِي النَّارِ، فَمَا رَأَيْتُ رَجُلًا أَشْبَهَ بِرَجُلٍ مِنْكَ بِهِ، وَلَا بِهِ مِنْكَ". فَقَالَ أَكْثَمُ: تَخْشَى أَنْ يَضُرَّنِي شَبَهُهُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ؟ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "لَا إِنَّكَ مُؤْمِنٌ وَهُوَ كَافِرٌ، إِنَّهُ أَوَّلُ مَنْ غَيّر دِينَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَبَحَّرَ الْبَحِيرَةَ، وَسَيَّبَ السَّائِبَةَ، وَحَمَى الْحَامِيَ". ثُمَّ رَوَاهُ عَنْ هَنَّادٍ، عَنْ عَبْدَةَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، بِنَحْوِهِ أَوْ مِثْلِهِ. 

لَيْسَ هَذَانَ الطَّرِيقَانِ فِي الْكُتُبِ.

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا عَمْرُو بْنُ مُجَمِّع، حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ الهَجَري، عَنْ أَبِي الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قال: "إن أَوَّلَ مَنْ سَيَّب السَّوَائِبَ، وَعَبَدَ الْأَصْنَامَ، أَبُو خُزَاعَةَ عَمْرُو بْنُ عَامِرٍ، وَإِنِّي رَأَيْتُهُ يَجُرُّ أَمْعَاءَهُ فِي النَّارِ". تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ أَحْمَدُ مِنْ هَذَا الْوَجْهِ. 

وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ: أَنْبَأَنَا مَعْمَر، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "إِنِّي لَأَعْرِفُ أَوَّلَ مَنْ سَيَّبَ السَّوَائِبَ، وَأَوَّلَ مَنْ غَيَّرَ دِينَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ". قَالُوا: مَنْ هُوَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ؟ قَالَ: "عَمْرُو بْنُ لُحَيّ أَخُو بَنِي كَعْبٍ، لَقَدْ رَأَيْتُهُ يَجُرُّ قُصْبه فِي النَّارِ، يُؤذي رِيحُهُ أَهْلَ النَّارِ. وَإِنِّي لَأَعْرِفُ أَوَّلَ مَنْ بَحَّرَ الْبَحَائِرَ". قَالُوا: مَنْ هُوَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ؟ قَالَ: "رَجُلٌ مِنْ بَنِي مُدْلج، كَانَتْ لَهُ نَاقَتَانِ، فَجَدَعَ آذَانَهُمَا، وَحَرَّمَ أَلْبَانَهُمَا، ثُمَّ شَرِبَ أَلْبَانَهُمَا بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ، فَلَقَدْ رَأَيْتُهُ فِي النَّارِ وَهُمَا يَعَضَّانِهِ بِأَفْوَاهِهِمَا وَيَخْبِطَانِهِ بِأَخْفَافِهِمَا". 

فَعَمْرُو هَذَا هُوَ ابْنُ لُحَيِّ بْنِ قَمَعَة، أَحَدُ رُؤَسَاءِ خُزَاعَةَ، الَّذِينَ ولَوا الْبَيْتَ بَعْدَ جَرْهم. وَكَانَ أَوَّلَ مَنْ غَيَّرَ دِينَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ الْخَلِيلِ، فَأَدْخَلَ الْأَصْنَامَ إِلَى الْحِجَازِ، وَدَعَا الرَّعَاعَ مِنَ النَّاسِ إِلَى عِبَادَتِهَا وَالتَّقَرُّبِ بِهَا، وَشَرَعَ لَهُمْ هَذِهِ الشَّرَائِعَ الْجَاهِلِيَّةَ فِي الْأَنْعَامِ وَغَيْرِهَا، كَمَا ذَكَرَهُ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى فِي سُورَةِ الْأَنْعَامِ، عِنْدَ قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَجَعَلُوا لِلَّهِ مِمَّا ذَرَأَ مِنَ الْحَرْثِ وَالأنْعَامِ نَصِيبًا﴾ [الْأَنْعَامِ: ١٣٦] إِلَى آخَرِ الْآيَاتِ فِي ذَلِكَ.

فَأَمَّا الْبَحِيرَةُ، فَقَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: هِيَ النَّاقَةُ إِذَا نَتَجَتْ خَمْسَةَ أبْطُن نَظَرُوا إِلَى الْخَامِسِ، فَإِنْ كَانَ ذَكَرًا ذَبَحُوهُ، فَأَكَلَهُ الرِّجَالُ دُونَ النِّسَاءِ. وَإِنْ كَانَ أُنْثَى جَدَعُوا آذَانَهَا، فَقَالُوا: هَذِهِ بَحِيرَةٌ.

وَذَكَرَ السُّدِّي وَغَيْرُهُ قَرِيبًا مِنْ هَذَا.

وَأَمَّا السَّائِبَةُ، فَقَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ: هِيَ مِنَ الْغَنَمِ نَحْوَ مَا فُسِّرَ مِنَ الْبَحِيرَةِ، إِلَّا أَنَّهَا مَا وُلِدَتْ مِنْ وَلَدٍ كَانَ بَيْنَهَا وَبَيْنَ سِتَّةِ أَوْلَادٍ كَانَ عَلَى هَيْئَتِهَا، فَإِذَا وَلَدَتِ السَّابِعَ ذَكَرًا أَوْ ذَكَرَيْنِ، ذَبَحُوهُ، فَأَكَلَهُ رِجَالُهُمْ دُونَ نِسَائِهِمْ.

وَقَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ: السَّائِبَةُ: هِيَ النَّاقَةُ إِذَا وَلَدَتْ عَشْرَ إِنَاثٍ مِنَ الْوَلَدِ لَيْسَ بَيْنَهُنَّ ذَكَرٌ، سُيّبت فَلَمْ تُرْكَبْ، وَلَمْ يُجَزّ وَبَرُهَا، وَلَمْ يَحْلِبْ لَبَنَهَا إِلَّا الضَّيْفُ.

وَقَالَ أَبُو رَوْقٍ: السَّائِبَةُ: كَانَ الرَّجُلُ إِذَا خَرَجَ فَقُضيت حَاجَتُهُ، سَيَّب مِنْ مَالِهِ نَاقَةً أَوْ غَيْرَهَا، فَجَعَلَهَا لِلطَّوَاغِيتِ. فَمَا وَلَدَتْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ كَانَ لَهَا.

وَقَالَ السُّدِّي: كَانَ الرَّجُلُ مِنْهُمْ إِذَا قُضيت حَاجَتُهُ أَوْ عُوفي مِنْ مَرَضٍ أَوْ كَثُرَ مَالُهُ سَيَّب شَيْئًا مِنْ مَالِهِ لِلْأَوْثَانِ، فَمَنْ عَرَضَ لَهُ مِنَ النَّاسِ عُوقب بِعُقُوبَةٍ فِي الدُّنْيَا.

وَأَمَّا الْوَصِيلَةُ، فَقَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: هِيَ الشَّاةُ إِذَا نَتَجَتْ سَبْعَةَ أُبْطُنٍ نَظَرُوا إِلَى السَّابِعِ، فَإِنْ كَانَ ذَكَرًا أَوْ أُنْثَى وَهُوَ مَيِّتٌ اشْتَرَكَ فِيهِ الرِّجَالُ دُونَ النِّسَاءِ، وَإِنْ كَانَ أُنْثَى اسْتَحْيَوْهَا، وَإِنْ كَانَ ذَكَرًا وَأُنْثَى فِي بَطْنٍ اسْتَحْيَوْهُمَا وَقَالُوا: وَصَلَتْهُ أُخْتُهُ فَحَرَّمَتْهُ عَلَيْنَا. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ.

وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ: أَنْبَأَنَا مَعْمَر، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ: ﴿وَلا وَصِيلَةٍ﴾ قَالَ: فَالْوَصِيلَةُ مِنَ الْإِبِلِ، كَانَتِ النَّاقَةُ تَبْتَكِرُ بِأُنْثَى، ثُمَّ تُثَنَّى بِأُنْثَى، فَسَمَّوْهَا الْوَصِيلَةَ، وَيَقُولُونَ: وَصَلَتْ أُنْثَيَيْنِ لَيْسَ بَيْنَهُمَا ذَكَرٌ، فَكَانُوا يَجْدَعُونَهَا لِطَوَاغِيتِهِمْ.

وَكَذَا رُوِيَ عَنِ الْإِمَامِ مَالِكِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ.

وَقَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ: الْوَصِيلَةُ مِنَ الْغَنَمِ: إِذَا وَلَدَتْ عَشْرَ إِنَاثٍ فِي خَمْسَةِ أَبْطُنٍ، تَوْأَمَيْنِ تَوْأَمَيْنِ فِي كُلِّ بَطْنٍ، سُمِّيَتِ الْوَصِيلَةَ وَتُرِكَتْ، فَمَا وَلَدَتْ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ مِنْ ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثَى، جُعِلَتْ لِلذُّكُورِ دُونَ الْإِنَاثِ. وَإِنْ كَانَتْ مَيْتَةً اشْتَرَكُوا فِيهَا.

وَأَمَّا الْحَامُ، فَقَالَ العَوْفي، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: كَانَ الرَّجُلُ إِذَا لقح فحله عشرًا، قيل حام، فاتركوه.

وَكَذَا قَالَ أَبُو رَوْقٍ، وَقَتَادَةُ. وَقَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: وَأَمَّا الْحَامِ فَالْفَحْلُ مِنَ الْإِبِلِ، إِذَا وُلد لِوَلَدِهِ قَالُوا: حَمى هَذَا ظَهْرَهُ، فَلَا يَحْمِلُونَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْئًا، وَلَا يَجُزُّونَ لَهُ وَبَرًا، وَلَا يَمْنَعُونَهُ مِنْ حِمَى رَعْيٍ، وَمِنْ حَوْضٍ يَشْرَبُ مِنْهُ، وَإِنْ كَانَ الْحَوْضُ لِغَيْرِ صَاحِبِهِ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ وَهْب: سَمِعْتُ مَالِكًا يَقُولُ: أَمَّا الْحَامِ فَمِنَ الْإِبِلِ كَانَ يَضْرِبُ فِي الْإِبِلِ، فَإِذَا انْقَضَى ضِرَابُهُ جَعَلُوا عَلَيْهِ رِيشَ الطَّوَاوِيسِ وَسَيَّبُوهُ.

وَقَدْ قِيلَ غَيْرُ ذَلِكَ فِي تَفْسِيرِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ. وَقَدْ وَرَدَ فِي ذَلِكَ حَدِيثٌ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ، مِنْ طَرِيقِ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ السَّبِيعي، عَنْ أَبِي الْأَحْوَصِ الجُشَمي، عَنْ أَبِيهِ مَالِكِ بْنِ نَضْلَة قَالَ: أَتَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ فِي خَلْقان مِنَ الثِّيَابِ، فَقَالَ لِي: "هَلْ لَكَ مِنْ مَالٍ؟ " قُلْتُ نَعَمْ. قَالَ: "مِنْ أَيِّ الْمَالِ؟ " قَالَ: فَقُلْتُ: مِنْ كُلِّ الْمَالِ، مِنَ الْإِبِلِ وَالْغَنَمِ وَالْخَيْلِ وَالرَّقِيقِ. قَالَ: "فَإِذَا آتَاكَ اللَّهُ مَالًا فلْيُرَ عَلَيْكَ". ثُمَّ قَالَ: "تُنْتِجُ إِبِلُكَ وَافِيَةً آذَانُهَا؟ " قَالَ: قُلْتُ: نَعَمْ. قَالَ: "وَهَلْ تُنْتِجُ الْإِبِلُ إِلَّا كَذَلِكَ؟ " قَالَ: "فَلَعَلَّكَ تَأْخُذُ الْمُوسَى فَتَقْطَعُ آذَانَ طَائِفَةٍ مِنْهَا وَتَقُولُ: هَذِهِ بَحِيرٌ، وَتَشُقُّ آذَانَ طَائِفَةٍ مِنْهَا، وَتَقُولُ: هَذِهِ حَرَمٌ؟ " قُلْتُ: نَعَمْ. قَالَ: "فَلَا تَفْعَلْ، إِنَّ كُلَّ مَا آتَاكَ اللَّهُ لَكَ حِلٌّ"، ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿مَا جَعَلَ اللَّهُ مِنْ بَحِيرَةٍ وَلا سَائِبَةٍ وَلا وَصِيلَةٍ وَلا حَامٍ﴾ أَمَّا الْبَحِيرَةُ: فَهِيَ الَّتِي يَجْدَعُونَ آذَانَهَا، فَلَا تَنْتَفِعُ امْرَأَتُهُ وَلَا بَنَاتُهُ وَلَا أَحَدٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ بِصُوفِهَا وَلَا أَوْبَارِهَا وَلَا أَشْعَارِهَا وَلَا أَلْبَانِهَا، فَإِذَا مَاتَتِ اشْتَرَكُوا فِيهَا. وَأَمَّا السَّائِبَةُ: فَهِيَ الَّتِي يُسَيِّبُونَ لِآلِهَتِهِمْ، وَيَذْهَبُونَ إِلَى آلِهَتِهِمْ فَيُسَيِّبُونَهَا، وَأَمَّا الْوَصِيلَةُ: فَالشَّاةُ تَلِدُ سِتَّةَ أَبْطُنٍ، فَإِذَا وَلَدَتِ السَّابِعَ جُدِعَتْ وَقُطِعَ قَرْنُهَا، فَيَقُولُونَ: قَدْ وَصَلَتْ، فَلَا يَذْبَحُونَهَا وَلَا تُضْرَبُ وَلَا تُمْنَعُ مَهْمَا وَرَدَتْ عَلَى حَوْضٍ. هَكَذَا يَذْكُرُ تَفْسِيرَ ذَلِكَ مُدْرَجًا فِي الْحَدِيثِ. وَقَدْ رُوِيَ مِنْ وَجْهٍ آخَرَ عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْأَحْوَصِ عَوْفِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، مِنْ قَوْلِهِ، وَهُوَ أَشْبَهُ. 

وَقَدْ رَوَى هَذَا الْحَدِيثَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ بْنِ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي الزَّعْرَاءِ عَمْرِو بْنِ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ عَمِّهِ أَبِي الْأَحْوَصِ عَوْفِ بْنِ مَالِكِ بْنِ نَضْلَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، بِهِ. وَلَيْسَ فِيهِ تفسير هذه والله أعلم.

وقوله: ﴿وَلَكِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى اللَّهِ الْكَذِبَ وَأَكْثَرُهُمْ لا يَعْقِلُونَ﴾ أَيْ: مَا شَرَعَ اللَّهُ هَذِهِ الْأَشْيَاءَ وَلَا هِيَ عِنْدَهُ قُرْبَةٌ، وَلَكِنَّ الْمُشْرِكِينَ افْتَرَوْا ذَلِكَ وَجَعَلُوهُ شَرْعًا لَهُمْ وَقُرْبَةً يَتَقَرَّبُونَ بِهَا إِلَيْهِ. وَلَيْسَ ذَلِكَ بِحَاصِلٍ لَهُمْ، بَلْ هُوَ وَبَالٌ عَلَيْهِمْ.

﴿وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَعَالَوْا إِلَى مَا أَنزلَ اللَّهُ وَإِلَى الرَّسُولِ قَالُوا حَسْبُنَا مَا وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهِ آبَاءَنَا﴾ أَيْ: إِذَا دُعُوا إِلَى دِينِ اللَّهِ وَشَرْعِهِ وَمَا أَوجَبَهُ وتَرْك مَا حَرَّمَهُ، قَالُوا: يَكْفِينَا مَا وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهِ الآباءَ وَالْأَجْدَادَ مِنَ الطَّرَائِقِ وَالْمَسَالِكِ، قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى ﴿أَوَلَوْ كَانَ آبَاؤُهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ شَيْئًا﴾ أَيْ: لا يفهمون حقًا، ولا يَعْرِفُونَهُ، وَلَا يَهْتَدُونَ إِلَيْهِ، فَكَيْفَ يَتْبَعُونَهُمْ وَالْحَالَةُ هَذِهِ؟ لَا يَتْبَعُهُمْ إِلَّا مَنْ هُوَ أَجْهَلُ مِنْهُمْ، وَأَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا.

---

## 5:105 — verse 105

### Translation

The Almighty says, commanding His believing servants to set themselves right and to do good with their effort and their capacity, and informing them that whoever sets his own affair right will not be harmed by the corruption of those who become corrupt among people, whether that person is near to him or far from him.

Al-Awfi said, on the authority of Ibn Abbas, regarding the interpretation of this verse: The Almighty says, "When the servant obeys Me in what I have commanded him of the lawful and the unlawful, then he will not be harmed by whoever goes astray after him, if he acts according to what I commanded him."

Al-Walibi related the same from him, and Muqatil ibn Hayyan said likewise. As for His saying {O you who believe, take care of yourselves}, "yourselves" is put in the accusative as a term of urging (ighra'). {No one who goes astray will harm you if you are guided. To Allah is your return altogether, and He will inform you of what you used to do} meaning: He will requite every doer according to his deed, if it was good then good, and if it was evil then evil.

There is nothing in the verse that can be used as evidence for abandoning the commanding of good and forbidding of evil, when doing so is possible. Imam Ahmad, may Allah have mercy on him, said:

[chain] Hashim ibn al-Qasim told us, Zuhayr, meaning ibn Mu'awiyah, told us, Isma'il ibn Abi Khalid told us, Qays told us: Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, stood up, praised Allah and extolled Him, and said: O people, you recite this verse, {O you who believe, take care of yourselves. No one who goes astray will harm you if you are guided} to the end of the verse, and you place it in a position other than its proper place. I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "Indeed, when people see wrongdoing and do not change it, Allah, Mighty and Majestic, is about to encompass them all with His punishment." He said: And I heard Abu Bakr say: O people, beware of lying, for lying is far removed from faith.

This hadith was related by the compilers of the four Sunan works, and Ibn Hibban in his Sahih, and others, through many chains from many groups, from Isma'il ibn Abi Khalid, connected and attributed to the Prophet ﷺ. Some of them related it from him as a mawquf (halted report) attributed only to al-Siddiq. Al-Daraqutni and others held that the version attributed to the Prophet ﷺ (marfu') is the stronger one, and we have mentioned its chains and discussed it at length in the Musnad of al-Siddiq, may Allah be pleased with him.

Abu Isa al-Tirmidhi said: [chain] Sa'id ibn Ya'qub al-Talaqani told us, and Abdullah ibn al-Mubarak told us, Utbah ibn Abi Hakim told us, Amr ibn Jariyah al-Lakhmi told us, from Abu Umayyah al-Sha'bani, who said: I came to Abu Tha'labah al-Khushani and said to him: What do you make of this verse? He said: Which verse? I said: His saying, {O you who believe, take care of yourselves. No one who goes astray will harm you if you are guided.} He said: By Allah, you have asked one who is well informed about it. I asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ about it, and he said: "Rather, enjoin one another to good and forbid one another from evil, until, when you see greed obeyed, desire followed, this worldly life preferred, and every man of opinion pleased with his own opinion, then attend to your own particular self and leave the common people, for behind you are days in which patience will be like grasping a burning coal. For the one who acts rightly in them there is the reward of fifty men doing as you do." Abdullah ibn al-Mubarak said: Someone other than Utbah added: It was said, O Messenger of Allah, the reward of fifty men from among them or from us? He said: "Rather the reward of fifty from among you."

Al-Tirmidhi then said: This is a hasan gharib (good, singular) hadith, sound. Abu Dawud related it likewise through the route of Ibn al-Mubarak, and Ibn Majah, Ibn Jarir, and Ibn Abi Hatim related it from Utbah ibn Abi Hakim.

Abdul-Razzaq said: [chain] Ma'mar informed us, from al-Hasan, that a man asked Ibn Mas'ud about His saying, {O you who believe, take care of yourselves. No one who goes astray will harm you if you are guided.} He said: This is not its time. Today it is accepted. But the time for it is about to come: you will command, and such and such will be done to you, or he said, it will not be accepted from you. At that point, {take care of yourselves; no one who goes astray will harm you}.

Abu Ja'far al-Razi related it from al-Rabi', from Abu al-Aliyah, from Ibn Mas'ud, regarding His saying, {O you who believe, take care of yourselves. No one who goes astray will harm you if you are guided} to the end of the verse. He said: They were sitting with Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, and something occurred between two men such as occurs between people, until each of them rose against his companion. One of the men sitting with Abdullah said: Shall I not rise and command them to do good and forbid them from evil? Another man beside him said: Attend to yourself, for Allah says, {[O you who believe] take care of yourselves} to the end of the verse. He said: Ibn Mas'ud heard this and said: Enough. The interpretation of this has not yet come. Indeed the Qur'an was revealed as it was revealed, and among it are verses whose interpretation occurred before they were even revealed, and among it are verses whose interpretation occurred during the time of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and among it are verses whose interpretation occurred shortly after the Prophet ﷺ, and among it are verses whose interpretation will occur after today, and among it are verses whose interpretation will occur at the Hour, as mentioned regarding the Hour, and among it are verses whose interpretation will occur on the Day of Reckoning, as mentioned regarding the Reckoning, the Garden, and the Fire. So as long as your hearts are one, and your desires are one, and you have not become divided into factions, and none of you has tasted the harshness of another, then command and forbid. But when hearts and desires become divided, and you become clothed in factions, and some of you taste the harshness of others, then let each man attend to himself; at that point the interpretation of this verse has come to us. Related by Ibn Jarir.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Al-Hasan ibn Arafah told us, Shababah ibn Sawwar told us, al-Rabi' ibn Subayh told us, from Sufyan ibn Iqal, who said: It was said to Ibn Umar: What if you were to sit still in these days and neither command nor forbid, for Allah says, {take care of yourselves; no one who goes astray will harm you if you are guided}? Ibn Umar said: This is not for me nor for my companions. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Let the one who is present convey to the one who is absent." So we were the ones present and you are the ones absent. But this verse is for peoples who will come after us; if they speak, it will not be accepted from them.

He also said: [chain] Muhammad ibn Bashshar told us, Muhammad ibn Ja'far and Abu Asim told us, they said, Awf told us, from Sawwar ibn Shabib, who said: I was with Ibn Umar when a sharp eyed, sharp tongued man came to him and said: O Abu Abdul-Rahman, there are six men, all of whom have recited the Qur'an quickly, all of them striving hard and sparing no effort, and all of them abhor the idea of committing any base act. Yet in doing so, they bear witness against each other of shirk (associating partners with Allah). A man among the people said: What baseness could you want more than for them to bear witness against each other of shirk?

The man said: I am not asking you, I am asking the elder. He repeated the account to Abdullah, and Abdullah said: Perhaps you think, may your father have no comfort, that I would command you to go and kill them! Admonish them and forbid them; if they disobey you, then it is upon you to attend to yourself, for Allah, Mighty and Majestic, says, {O you who believe, take care of yourselves} to the end of the verse.

He also said: [chain] Ahmad ibn al-Miqdam told me, al-Mu'tamir ibn Sulayman told us, I heard my father, Qatadah told us, from Abu Mazin, who said: I went during the time of Uthman to Medina, and there were some Muslims sitting, and one of them recited this verse: {O you who believe, take care of yourselves. No one who goes astray will harm you} and the eldest of them said: The interpretation of this verse has not come today.

He said: [chain] Al-Qasim told us, al-Husayn told us, Ibn Fadalah told us, from Mu'awiyah ibn Salih, from Jubayr ibn Nufayr, who said: I was in a gathering that included companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and I was the youngest of the group. They discussed commanding good and forbidding evil, and I said: Does not Allah say in His Book, {O you who believe, take care of yourselves. No one who goes astray will harm you if you are guided}? They all turned on me with one voice and said: You extract a verse from the Qur'an without knowing it and without knowing its interpretation! Until I wished I had not spoken. They continued their conversation, and when it was time for them to rise, they said: You are a young boy, and you extracted a verse without knowing what it means. Perhaps you will live to see that time, when you see greed obeyed, desire followed, and every man of opinion pleased with his own opinion. Then attend to yourself; no one who goes astray will harm you if you are guided.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Ali ibn Sahl told us, Damrah ibn Rabi'ah told us: al-Hasan recited this verse, {O you who believe, take care of yourselves. No one who goes astray will harm you if you are guided}, and al-Hasan said: Praise be to Allah for it and praise be to Allah upon it. There was never a believer in the past, nor will there be a believer in what remains, except that beside him is a hypocrite who detests his deed.

Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab said: When you command good and forbid evil, then no one who goes astray will harm you if you are guided.

Related by Ibn Jarir, and likewise related through the route of Sufyan al-Thawri, from Abu al-Umays, from Abu al-Bakhtari, from Hudhayfah, the same. Others among the predecessors said the same.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] My father told us, Hisham ibn Khalid al-Dimashqi told us, al-Walid told us, Ibn Lahi'ah told us, from Yazid ibn Abi Habib, from Ka'b, regarding His saying, {take care of yourselves; no one who goes astray will harm you if you are guided}, he said: When the church of Damascus is torn down and made into a mosque, and the wearing of asb (a striped fabric) appears, then that is when the interpretation of this verse comes.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى آمِرًا عِبَادَهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَنْ يُصْلِحُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ وَيَفْعَلُوا الْخَيْرَ بِجُهْدِهِمْ وَطَاقَتِهِمْ، وَمُخْبِرًا لَهُمْ أَنَّهُ مَنْ أَصْلَحَ أَمْرَهُ لَا يَضُرُّهُ فَسَادُ مَنْ فَسَدَ مِنَ النَّاسِ، سَوَاءٌ كَانَ قَرِيبًا مِنْهُ أَوْ بَعِيدًا.

قَالَ العَوْفي عن ابن عباس عند تفسر هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: يَقُولُ تَعَالَى: إِذَا مَا الْعَبْدُ أَطَاعَنِي فِيمَا أَمَرْتُهُ بِهِ مِنَ الْحَلَالِ وَالْحَرَامِ فَلَا يَضُرُّهُ مَنْ ضَلَّ بَعْدَهُ، إِذَا عَمِلَ بِمَا أَمَرْتُهُ بِهِ.

وَكَذَا رَوَى الْوَالِبِيُّ عَنْهُ. وَهَكَذَا قَالَ مُقَاتِل بْنُ حَيان. فَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ﴾ نُصِبَ عَلَى الْإِغْرَاءِ ﴿لَا يَضُرُّكُمْ مَنْ ضَلَّ إِذَا اهْتَدَيْتُمْ إِلَى اللَّهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ جَمِيعًا فَيُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِمَا كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ﴾ أَيْ: فَيُجَازِي كُلَّ عَامِلٍ بِعَمَلِهِ، إِنْ خَيْرًا فَخَيْرٌ، وَإِنْ شرًّا فَشَرٌّ.

وَلَيْسَ فِي الْآيَةِ مسْتَدلٌّ عَلَى تَرْكِ الْأَمْرِ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَالنَّهْيِ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ، إِذَا كَانَ فِعْلُ ذَلِكَ مُمْكِنًا، وَقَدْ قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ:

حَدَّثَنَا هَاشِمُ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ، حَدَّثَنَا زُهَيْر -يَعْنِي ابْنَ مُعَاوِيَةَ-حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ أَبِي خَالِدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا قَيْس قَالَ: قَامَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، فَحَمِدَ اللَّهَ وَأَثْنَى عَلَيْهِ، وَقَالَ: أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ، إِنَّكُمْ تَقْرَؤُونَ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ لَا يَضُرُّكُمْ مَنْ ضَلَّ إِذَا اهْتَدَيْتُمْ﴾ إِلَى آخَرِ الْآيَةِ، وَإِنَّكُمْ تَضَعُونَهَا عَلَى غَيْرِ مَوْضِعِهَا، وَإِنِّي سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "إِنَّ النَّاسَ إِذَا رَأَوُا الْمُنْكَرَ وَلَا يُغَيِّرُونَهُ أَوْشَكَ اللَّهُ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، أَنْ يَعُمَّهُمْ بعِقَابه". قَالَ: وَسَمِعْتُ أَبَا بَكْرٍ يَقُولُ: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ، إِيَّاكُمْ والكَذِب، فَإِنَّ الْكَذِبَ مُجَانِبُ الْإِيمَانَ.

وَقَدْ رَوَى هَذَا الْحَدِيثَ أَصْحَابُ السُّنَنِ الْأَرْبَعَةِ، وَابْنُ حِبَّان فِي صَحِيحِهِ، وَغَيْرُهُمْ مِنْ طُرُقٍ كَثِيرَةٍ عَنْ جَمَاعَةٍ كَثِيرَةٍ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ، بِهِ مُتَّصِلًا مَرْفُوعًا، وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ رَوَاهُ عَنْهُ بِهِ مَوْقُوفًا عَلَى الصِّدِّيقِ وَقَدْ رَجَّحَ رَفْعَهُ الدَّارَقُطْنِيُّ وَغَيْرُهُ وَذَكَرْنَا طُرُقَهُ وَالْكَلَامُ عَلَيْهِ مطولا في مُسْنَدِ الصِّدِّيقِ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ.

وَقَالَ أَبُو عِيسَى التِّرْمِذِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ يَعْقُوبَ الطَالَقَاني، وَحَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ الْمُبَارَكِ، حَدَّثَنَا عُتْبَةُ بْنُ أَبِي حَكِيمٍ، حَدَّثَنَا عَمْرُو بْنُ جَارِيَةَ اللَّخْمِيُّ، عَنْ أَبِي أُمَيَّةَ الشَّعْباني قَالَ: أَتَيْتُ أَبَا ثَعْلَبَةَ الخُشَنِي فَقُلْتُ لَهُ: كَيْفَ تَصْنَعُ فِي هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ؟ فَقَالَ: أيَّة آيَةٍ؟ قُلْتُ: قَوْلُهُ [تَعَالَى] ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ لَا يَضُرُّكُمْ مَنْ ضَلَّ إِذَا اهْتَدَيْتُمْ﴾ فَقَالَ: أَمَا وَاللَّهِ لَقَدْ سَأَلْتَ عَنْهَا خَبِيرًا، سألتُ عَنْهَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَقَالَ: "بَلِ ائْتَمَرُوا بِالْمَعْرُوفِ، وَتَنَاهَوْا عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ، حَتَّى إِذَا رَأَيْتَ شُحّا مُطاعًا، وهَوًى مُتَّبعًا، وَدُنْيَا مُؤْثَرة، وإعجابَ كُلِّ ذِي رَأْيٍ بِرَأْيهِ، فَعَلَيْكَ بِخَاصَّةِ نَفْسِكَ، وَدَعِ الْعَوَامَّ، فَإِنَّ مِنْ وَرَائِكُمْ أَيَّامًا الصَّبْرُ فِيهِنَّ مِثْلُ القَبْضِ عَلَى الجَمْرِ، لِلْعَامِلِ فِيهِنَّ مثلُ أَجْرِ خَمْسِينَ رَجُلًا يَعْمَلُونَ كَعَمَلِكُمْ" -قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ الْمُبَارَكِ: وَزَادَ غَيْرُ عُتْبَةَ: قِيلَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، أَجْرُ خَمْسِينَ رَجُلًا مِنْهُمْ أَوْ مِنَّا؟ قَالَ: "بَلْ أَجْرُ خَمْسِينَ مِنْكُمْ".

ثُمَّ قَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: هَذَا حَدِيثٌ حَسَنٌ غَرِيبٌ صَحِيحٌ. وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ مِنْ طَرِيقِ ابْنِ الْمُبَارَكِ وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَاجَهْ، وَابْنُ جَرِيرٍ، وَابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ، عَنْ عُتْبَةَ بْنِ أَبِي حَكِيمٍ. 

وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ: أَنْبَأَنَا مَعْمَر، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ أَنَّ ابْنَ مَسْعُودٍ سَأَلَهُ رَجُلٌ عَنْ قَوْلِهِ ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ لَا يَضُرُّكُمْ مَنْ ضَلَّ إِذَا اهْتَدَيْتُمْ﴾ فَقَالَ: إِنَّ هَذَا لَيْسَ بِزَمَانِهَا، إِنَّهَا الْيَوْمَ مَقْبُولَةٌ. وَلَكِنَّهُ قَدْ أَوْشَكَ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ زَمَانُهَا، تَأْمُرُونَ فَيُصْنَعُ بِكُمْ كَذَا وَكَذَا -أَوْ قَالَ: فَلَا يُقْبَلُ مِنْكُمْ-فَحِينَئِذٍ ﴿عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ لَا يَضُرُّكُمْ مَنْ ضَلَّ﴾

وَرَوَاهُ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ الرَّازِيُّ، عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ عَنْ أَبِي الْعَالِيَةِ، عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ لَا يَضُرُّكُمْ مَنْ ضَلَّ إِذَا اهْتَدَيْتُمْ﴾ الْآيَةَ، قَالَ: كَانُوا عِنْدَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ جُلُوسًا، فَكَانَ بَيْنَ رَجُلَيْنِ بَعْضُ مَا يَكُونُ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ، حَتَّى قَامَ كُلُّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا إِلَى صَاحِبِهِ، فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ جُلَسَاءِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ: أَلَا أَقُومُ فَآمُرَهُمَا بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَأَنْهَاهُمَا عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ؟ فَقَالَ آخَرُ إِلَى جَنْبِهِ: عَلَيْكَ بِنَفْسِكَ، فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ يَقُولُ: ﴿ [يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا] عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ﴾ الْآيَةَ. قَالَ: فَسَمِعَهَا ابْنُ مَسْعُودٍ فَقَالَ: مَهْ، لَمْ يَجِئْ تَأْوِيلُ هَذِهِ بَعْدُ إِنَّ الْقُرْآنَ أُنْزِلَ حَيْثُ أُنْزِلَ وَمِنْهُ آيٌ قَدْ مَضَى تَأْوِيلُهُنَّ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَنْزِلْنَ، وَمِنْهُ آيٌ قَدْ وَقَعَ تَأْوِيلُهُنَّ عَلَى عَهْدِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ، وَمِنْهُ آيٌ قَدْ وَقَعَ تَأْوِيلُهُنَّ بَعْدَ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ بِيَسِيرٍ، وَمِنْهُ آيٌ يَقَعُ تَأْوِيلُهُنَّ بَعْدَ الْيَوْمِ، وَمِنْهُ آيٌ تَأْوِيلُهُنَّ عِنْدَ السَّاعَةِ عَلَى ما ذكر من الساعة، ومنه آي يَقَعُ تَأْوِيلُهُنَّ يَوْمَ الْحِسَابِ عَلَى مَا ذُكِرَ مِنَ الْحِسَابِ وَالْجَنَّةِ وَالنَّارِ. فَمَا دَامَتْ قُلُوبُكُمْ وَاحِدَةً، وَأَهْوَاؤُكُمْ وَاحِدَةً وَلَمْ تلْبَسوا شِيعًا، وَلَمْ يَذُق بَعْضُكُمْ بَأْسَ بَعْضٍ فَأْمُرُوا وَانْهُوا. فَإِذَا اخْتَلَفَتِ الْقُلُوبُ وَالْأَهْوَاءُ، وألبسْتُم شِيَعًا، وَذَاقَ بَعْضُكُمْ بَأْسَ بَعْضٍ فَامْرُؤٌ وَنَفْسُهُ، عِنْدَ ذَلِكَ جَاءَنَا تَأْوِيلُ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ. 

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَرفة، حَدَّثَنَا شَبَابَةُ بْنُ سَوّار، حَدَّثَنَا الرَّبِيعُ بْنُ صُبَيْح، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ بْنِ عِقَالٍ قَالَ: قِيلَ لِابْنِ عُمَرَ: لَوْ جَلَسْتَ فِي هَذِهِ الْأَيَّامِ فَلَمْ تَأْمُرْ وَلَمْ تَنْهَ، فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ قَالَ: ﴿عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ لَا يَضُرُّكُمْ مَنْ ضَلَّ إِذَا اهْتَدَيْتُمْ﴾ ؟ فَقَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ: إِنَّهَا لَيْسَتْ لِي وَلَا لِأَصْحَابِي إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "أَلَا فَلْيُبَلِّغِ الشَّاهِدُ الْغَائِبَ". فَكُنَّا نَحْنُ الشُّهُودُ وَأَنْتُمُ الغُيّب، وَلَكِنَّ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ لِأَقْوَامٍ يَجِيئُونَ مِنْ بَعْدِنَا، إِنْ قَالُوا لَمْ يُقْبَلْ مِنْهُمْ. 

وَقَالَ أَيْضًا: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَشَّار، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ وَأَبُو عَاصِمٍ قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا عَوْف، عَنْ سوَّار بْنِ شَبِيب قَالَ: كُنْتُ عِنْدَ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، إِذْ أَتَاهُ رَجُلٌ جَليد فِي الْعَيْنِ، شَدِيدُ اللِّسَانِ، فَقَالَ: يَا أَبَا عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، نَفَرٌ سِتَّةٌ كُلُّهُمْ قَدْ قَرَأَ الْقُرْآنَ فَأَسْرَعَ فِيهِ، وَكُلُّهُمْ مُجْتَهِدٌ لَا يَأْلُو وَكُلُّهُمْ بَغِيضٌ إِلَيْهِ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ دَناءة، وَهُمْ فِي ذَلِكَ يَشْهَدُ بَعْضُهُمْ عَلَى بَعْضٍ بِالشِّرْكِ. فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ: وَأَيُّ دَنَاءَةٍ تُرِيدُ أكثرَ مِنْ أَنْ يَشْهَدَ بَعْضُهُمْ عَلَى بَعْضٍ بِالشِّرْكِ؟

فَقَالَ الرَّجُلُ: إِنِّي لَسْتُ إِيَّاكَ أَسْأَلُ، إِنَّمَا أَسْأَلُ الشَّيْخَ. فَأَعَادَ عَلَى عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْحَدِيثَ، فَقَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ: لَعَلَّكَ ترى، لا أبالك، أَنِّي سَآمُرُكَ أَنْ تَذْهَبَ فَتَقْتُلُهُمْ! عظْهم وَانْهَهُمْ، فَإِنْ عَصَوْكَ فَعَلَيْكَ نَفْسك فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ يَقُولُ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ﴾ الْآيَةَ.

وَقَالَ أَيْضًا: حَدَّثَنِي أَحْمَدُ بْنُ الْمِقْدَامِ، حَدَّثَنَا المعتَمِر بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، سَمِعْتُ أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا قَتَادَةُ، عَنْ أَبِي مَازِنٍ قَالَ: انْطَلَقْتُ عَلَى عَهْدِ عُثْمَانَ إِلَى الْمَدِينَةِ، فَإِذَا قَوْمٌ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ جُلُوسٌ، فَقَرَأَ أَحَدُهُمْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ لَا يَضُرُّكُمْ مَنْ ضَلَّ﴾ فَقَالَ أكْبَرهم لَمْ يَجِئْ تَأْوِيلُ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ الْيَوْمَ.

وَقَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الْقَاسِمُ، حَدَّثَنَا الحُسَين، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فَضَالَةَ، عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ جُبَير بْنِ نُفير قَالَ: كُنْتُ فِي حَلْقَةٍ فِيهَا أَصْحَابُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ، وَإِنِّي لأصغرُ الْقَوْمِ، فَتَذَاكَرُوا بِالْأَمْرِ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَالنَّهْيِ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ، فَقُلْتُ أَنَا: أَلَيْسَ اللَّهُ يَقُولُ فِي كِتَابِهِ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ لَا يَضُرُّكُمْ مَنْ ضَلَّ إِذَا اهْتَدَيْتُمْ﴾ ؟ فَأَقْبَلُوا عَلَيَّ بِلِسَانٍ وَاحِدٍ وَقَالُوا: تَنْزِعُ آيَةً مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ وَلَا تُعَرِفُهَا، وَلَا تَدْرِي مَا تَأْوِيلُهَا!! حَتَّى تَمَنَّيْتُ أَنِّي لَمْ أَكُنْ تكلمتُ، وَأَقْبَلُوا يَتَحَدَّثُونَ، فَلَمَّا حضر قيامهم قالوا: إنك غلام حَدَثُ السِّنِّ، وَإِنَّكَ نَزَعْتَ بِآيَةٍ وَلَا تَدْرِي مَا هِيَ؟ وَعَسَى أَنْ تُدْرِكَ ذَلِكَ الزَّمَانَ، إِذَا رَأَيْتَ شُحًّا مُطَاعًا، وهَوًى مُتَّبَعًا، وَإِعْجَابَ كُلِّ ذِي رَأْيٍ بِرَأْيهِ، فَعَلَيْكَ بِنَفْسِكَ، لَا يَضُرُّكَ مَنْ ضَلَّ إِذَا اهْتَدَيْتَ. 

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ سَهْل، حَدَّثَنَا ضَمْرَة بْنُ رَبِيعَةَ قَالَ: تَلَا الْحَسَنُ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ لَا يَضُرُّكُمْ مَنْ ضَلَّ إِذَا اهْتَدَيْتُمْ﴾ فَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ بِهَا، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ عَلَيْهَا، مَا كَانَ مُؤْمِنٌ فِيمَا مَضَى، وَلَا مُؤْمِنٌ فِيمَا بَقِيَ، إِلَّا وَإِلَى جَانِبِهِ مُنَافِقٌ يَكْرَهُ عَمَلَهُ.

وَقَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ الْمُسَيَّبِ: إِذَا أَمَرْتَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَنَهَيْتَ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ، فَلَا يَضُرُّكَ مَنْ ضَلَّ إِذَا اهْتَدَيْتَ.

رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ، وَكَذَا رُوِيَ مِنْ طَرِيقِ سُفْيَانَ الثَّوْرِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي العُمَيْس، عَنْ أَبِي البَخْتَري، عَنْ حُذَيْفَةَ مِثْلُهُ، وَكَذَا قَالَ غَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ مِنَ السَّلَفِ.
