وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ الدِّمَشْقِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا الْوَلِيدُ، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ لَهِيعة، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي حَبِيبٍ، عَنْ كَعْبٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ لَا يَضُرُّكُمْ مَنْ ضَلَّ إِذَا اهْتَدَيْتُمْ﴾ قَالَ: إِذَا هُدِمَتْ كَنِيسَةُ دِمَشْقَ، فَجُعِلتْ مَسْجِدًا، وَظَهَرَ لُبْسُ العَصْب، فَحِينَئِذٍ تَأْوِيلُ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ.

---

## 5:106-108 — verses 106-108 (shared commentary)

### Translation

This noble verse contains a weighty ruling. It has been said that it is abrogated, as reported by al-Awfi from Ibn Abbas. Hammad ibn Abi Sulayman said, on the authority of Ibrahim, that it is abrogated. Others, who are the majority, according to what Ibn Jarir said, held that it is instead a firmly established (muhkam) ruling, and whoever claims abrogation must provide proof.

His saying, the Exalted: {O you who believe, let there be witnesses between you when death approaches one of you, at the time of bequest, two} is the report itself, because of His saying {witnesses between you}. It has been said that its implied form is "the witness of two," with the annexed noun omitted and the annexed-to noun put in its place. It has also been said that the wording indicates the sense of "let two witness."

His saying {of just character} describes the two, that they should both be of just character.

His saying {from among you} means from among the Muslims, according to the majority. Ali ibn Abi Talha related from Ibn Abbas concerning His saying {of just character from among you} that he said: from among the Muslims. This was reported by Ibn Abi Hatim, who then said: similar statements have been reported from Ubaydah, Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, al-Hasan, Mujahid, Yahya ibn Ya'mur, al-Suddi, Qatadah, Muqatil ibn Hayyan, and Abd al-Rahman ibn Zayd ibn Aslam.

Ibn Jarir said: Others said that what is meant by {of just character from among you} is from the tribe of the one making the bequest. This is a view reported from Ikrimah and Ubaydah and several others besides them.

Regarding His saying {or two others from outside you}, Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Sa'id ibn Jubayr said: Ibn Abbas said concerning His saying {or two others from outside you}: from other than the Muslims, meaning the People of the Book.

He then said: a similar statement has been reported from Ubaydah, Shurayh, Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, Muhammad ibn Sirin, Yahya ibn Ya'mur, Ikrimah, Mujahid, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, al-Sha'bi, Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, Qatadah, Abu Mijlaz, al-Suddi, Muqatil ibn Hayyan, and Abd al-Rahman ibn Zayd ibn Aslam.

According to what Ibn Jarir related from Ikrimah and Ubaydah regarding His saying {from among you}, namely that what is meant is from the tribe of the one making the bequest, then what would be meant here by {or two others from outside you} is from outside the tribe of the one making the bequest. A similar report has also come from Ibn Abi Hatim on the authority of al-Hasan al-Basri and al-Zuhri, may God have mercy on them both.

His saying {if you are travelling in the land}, meaning: if you are journeying, {and the affliction of death befalls you}: these are two conditions for the permissibility of taking the testimony of non-Muslim protected subjects (dhimmis) when Muslims are not to be found, namely that this should occur during travel, and that it should be regarding a bequest, as Shurayh the judge explicitly stated.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Shurayh said: the testimony of a Jew or a Christian is not admissible except during travel, and it is not admissible even during travel except in the matter of a bequest.

He then related it also through [chain] Abu Ishaq al-Sabi'i, who said: Shurayh said the same.

A similar view has been reported from the Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal, may God Almighty have mercy on him. This matter is one in which he stood alone, and the other three (schools) opposed him, saying: the testimony of dhimmis is not admissible against Muslims. Abu Hanifah permitted it among themselves, that is, dhimmis testifying against one another.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] al-Zuhri said: it has been the settled practice (sunnah) that the testimony of a disbeliever is not admissible, whether in residence or in travel; it applies only among Muslims. Ibn Zayd said: this verse was revealed concerning a man who died while none of the people of Islam were present with him, and that was in the early period of Islam, when the land was a land of war and the people were disbelievers, and people used to inherit from one another by bequest. Then the bequest was abrogated and the fixed shares (fara'id) were ordained, and people acted according to them.

This was related by Ibn Jarir, though there is something questionable in this, and God knows best.

Ibn Jarir said: there is disagreement concerning His saying {witnesses between you when death approaches one of you, at the time of bequest, two of just character from among you, or two others from outside you}, as to whether what is meant is that the bequest be entrusted to the two of them, or that they merely witness it. There are two views:

The first: that the bequest is entrusted to them, as Muhammad ibn Ishaq said, on the authority of Yazid ibn Abd Allah ibn Qusayt: Ibn Mas'ud, may God be pleased with him, was asked about this verse, and he said: this concerns a man who travels carrying wealth, and his appointed fate overtakes him; if he finds two Muslim men, he hands over his estate to them, and has two just Muslims witness over them.

This was related by Ibn Abi Hatim, though there is a break in its chain.

The second view: that the two of them are witnesses. This is the apparent sense of the wording of the noble verse. If there is no third person acting as executor along with them, then both descriptions combine in them, namely being executors and being witnesses, as in the story of Tamim al-Dari and Adi ibn Badda', which will be mentioned shortly, if God wills, and success comes from Him.

Ibn Jarir found it problematic that they should be witnesses, saying: because we do not know of any ruling in which a witness takes an oath. But this does not preclude the ruling contained in this noble verse, for it is a ruling independent in itself, and it need not conform to the analogy applied to all other rulings. Moreover, this is a specific ruling concerning a specific kind of testimony in a specific situation, in which things have been overlooked that would not be overlooked elsewhere. So when indications of suspicion arise, this witness takes an oath in accordance with what this noble verse indicates.

Concerning His saying, the Exalted: {You detain the two of them after the prayer}, [al-Awfi, from] Ibn Abbas said: meaning the afternoon prayer (salat al-'asr). Sa'id ibn Jubayr, Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, Qatadah, Ikrimah, and Muhammad ibn Sirin said likewise. Al-Zuhri said: meaning the prayer of the Muslims. Al-Suddi said, on the authority of Ibn Abbas: meaning the prayer of the religion of the two witnesses.

The purport is that these two witnesses should be made to stand after a prayer at which people have gathered in their presence, {and they both swear by God} meaning: they both take an oath by God, {if you have doubt} meaning: if some suspicion appears to you concerning the two of them, that they have betrayed the trust or misappropriated something, then they swear at that point by God, {we will not sell it}, meaning: by our oaths, as Muqatil ibn Hayyan said, {for a price}, meaning: we will not exchange it for a small compensation from this fleeting, perishing world, {even if he were a close relative}, meaning: even if the one against whom testimony is given were closely related to us, we would not show him favoritism, {and we will not conceal the testimony of God}. He attributed it to God as an honor to it and out of reverence for its importance.

Some read it as: "wa la naktumu shahadatan Allahi," in the genitive case, taken as an oath formula. This was related by Ibn Jarir from Amir al-Sha'bi.

It has also been related from some that they read it: "wa la naktumu shahadatan Allahu," but the first reading is the well-known one.

{Truly then we would be among the sinners}, meaning: if we were to do any of that, whether distorting the testimony, altering it, changing it, or concealing it entirely.

Then He, the Exalted, said: {But if it is found out that the two of them were guilty of sin}, meaning: if it becomes widely known, apparent, and confirmed regarding the two witness-executors that they betrayed the trust or misappropriated some of the property bequeathed to them, and this becomes evident against them, {then two others shall take their place from among those who have a rightful claim, the nearer relatives}. This is the reading of the majority: "istuhiqqa 'alayhim al-awlayan." It has been reported from Ali, Ubayy, and al-Hasan al-Basri that they read it: {istahaqqa 'alayhim al-awlayan}.

Al-Hakim related in al-Mustadrak, through the chain of Ishaq ibn Muhammad al-Farwi, from Sulayman ibn Bilal, from Ja'far ibn Muhammad, from his father, from Ubayd Allah ibn Abi Rafi', from Ali ibn Abi Talib: that the Prophet ﷺ recited it as {min alladhina istahaqqa 'alayhim al-awlayan}. He then said: it is sound according to the criterion of Muslim, though the two of them (al-Bukhari and Muslim) did not include it.

Some, among them Ibn Abbas, read it: "min alladhina istuhiqqa 'alayhim al-awwalin." Al-Hasan read it: "min alladhina istuhiqqa 'alayhim al-awwalan." This was related by Ibn Jarir.

According to the reading of the majority, the meaning is: whenever this is confirmed by sound report regarding the betrayal of the two witness-executors, then let two from among the heirs entitled to the estate, being among those with the prior right to inherit that wealth, stand up, {and they both swear by God, our testimony is truer than the testimony of the two of them}, meaning: our statement that the two of them betrayed the trust is truer, more correct, and more firmly established than their earlier testimony, {and we have not transgressed}, meaning: in what we have said regarding the betrayal, {truly then we would be among the wrongdoers}, meaning: if we had lied against the two of them.

This oath taken from the heirs, and the return to the statement of the two witnesses in this situation, is like the oath taken by the guardians of a murdered person when there appears a lawth (indication of guilt) against the alleged killer: the rightful claimants swear against the killer, and he is handed over to them entirely, as is established in the chapter on "al-Qasama" (the oath-based procedure) within the rulings of jurisprudence.

The Sunna has come with something similar to what this noble verse indicates. Ibn Abi Hatim said: My father narrated to us, al-Husayn ibn Ziyad narrated to us, Muhammad ibn Salama narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn Ishaq, from Abu al-Nadr, from Badhan, that is, Abu Salih the freed servant of Umm Hani' bint Abi Talib, from Ibn Abbas, from Tamim al-Dari concerning this verse: {O you who believe, testimony among you when death approaches one of you} he said: everyone was cleared of it except me and Adi ibn Badda'. The two of them were Christians who used to travel back and forth to Syria before Islam. They went to Syria for their trade, and a freed servant of Banu Sahm came to them, called Budayl ibn Abi Maryam, with merchandise, having with him a silver vessel he intended for the king, and it was the greatest part of his goods. He fell ill and made his will to the two of them, ordering them to deliver to his family what he left behind. Tamim said: when he died, we took that vessel and sold it for a thousand dirhams, then divided it between myself and Adi ibn Badda'. When we arrived at his family, we handed over to them what we had with us. They found the vessel missing and asked us about it, so we said: he left nothing but this, and he gave us nothing else. Tamim said: when I became Muslim after the arrival of the Prophet ﷺ in Medina, I felt guilt over that matter, so I went to his family and told them the story, and I handed over to them five hundred dirhams, and told them that my companion had the like of it with him. They rushed to him to make him swear by whatever was held most solemn among the people of his religion, and he swore. Then Allah revealed {O you who believe, testimony among you} up to His saying: {then let the two of them swear by Allah: our testimony is truer than their testimony}. So Amr ibn al-As and another man among them stood and swore, and the five hundred was taken away from Adi ibn Badda'.

This is likewise narrated by Abu Isa al-Tirmidhi and Ibn Jarir, both of them from al-Hasan ibn Ahmad ibn Abi Shu'ayb al-Harrani, from Muhammad ibn Salama, from Muhammad ibn Ishaq, with the same account, mentioning it, and in his version: they brought him to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, who asked them for proof, but they found none, so he ordered them to make him swear by whatever was held most solemn among the people of his religion. He swore, and Allah revealed: {O you who believe, testimony among you when death approaches one of you} up to His saying: {or fear that oaths will be turned back after their oaths}. So Amr ibn al-As and another man stood and swore, and the five hundred was taken away from Adi ibn Badda'.

Then he said: this is a gharib (singular) hadith, and its chain is not sound. Abu al-Nadr, from whom Muhammad ibn Ishaq narrated this hadith, is in my view Muhammad ibn al-Sa'ib al-Kalbi, who was known by the kunya Abu al-Nadr, and the people of knowledge of hadith have abandoned him as a source; he is the author of a tafsir. I heard Muhammad ibn Isma'il say: Muhammad ibn al-Sa'ib al-Kalbi is known by the kunya Abu al-Nadr. Then he said: we do not know of any narration by Salim Abu al-Nadr from Abu Salih the freed servant of Umm Hani'. Something of this story has been narrated from Ibn Abbas in summarized form through a different route than this one.

[chain] Sufyan ibn Wakee, Yahya ibn Adam, Ibn Abi Za'ida, Muhammad ibn Abi al-Qasim, Abd al-Malik ibn Saeed ibn Jubayr, from his father, from Ibn Abbas, who said: a man of Banu Sahm went out with Tamim al-Dari and Adi ibn Badda'. The man of Sahm died in a land where there was no Muslim. When the two of them arrived with his estate, they found missing a silver vessel inlaid with gold. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ made them swear an oath, and then the vessel was found in Mecca, and it was said: we bought it from Tamim and Adi. So two men from among the guardians of the man of Sahm stood and swore by Allah: our testimony is truer than their testimony, and that the vessel belonged to their companion. And concerning them was revealed: {O you who believe, testimony among you}.

Abu Dawud likewise narrated it from al-Hasan ibn Ali, from Yahya ibn Adam, with the same chain. Then al-Tirmidhi said: this is a hasan gharib (good, singular) hadith, and it is the hadith of Ibn Abi Za'ida.

Muhammad ibn Abi al-Qasim was from Kufa, and it was said that his hadith was acceptable (salih al-hadith). This story has also been mentioned in mursal (chain missing the Companion) form by more than one of the Tabi'in, among them Ikrima, Muhammad ibn Sirin, and Qatada. They mentioned that the oath-taking occurred after the Asr prayer, as narrated by Ibn Jarir. It was likewise mentioned in mursal form by Mujahid, al-Hasan, and al-Dahhak. This indicates that the story was well known among the predecessors and that it is sound.

Among the supporting evidence for the soundness of this story is also what Abu Ja'far ibn Jarir narrated:

Ya'qub narrated to me, Hushaym narrated to us, Zakariyya informed us, from al-Sha'bi: that a man among the Muslims was on his deathbed in Daquqa. He said: death approached him and he could not find anyone among the Muslims to witness his will, so he had two men of the People of the Book witness it. He said: the two of them then came to Kufa, and went to al-Ash'ari, meaning Abu Musa al-Ash'ari, may Allah be pleased with him, and told him, presenting his estate and his will. Al-Ash'ari said: this is a matter that has not occurred since what occurred in the time of the Prophet ﷺ. He said: so he made them swear after Asr: by Allah, we have not betrayed nor lied nor substituted nor concealed nor altered, and this is indeed the will of the man and his estate. He said: so he upheld their testimony.

Then he narrated it from Amr ibn Ali al-Fallas, from Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi, from Shu'ba, from al-Mughira al-Azraq, from al-Sha'bi: that Abu Musa gave judgment in Daquqa.

These are two sound chains reaching al-Sha'bi, from Abu Musa al-Ash'ari.

As for his statement, "this is a matter that has not occurred since what occurred in the time of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ," the apparent meaning, and Allah knows best, is that he meant by this the story of Tamim and Adi ibn Badda'. They have mentioned that the acceptance of Islam by Tamim ibn Aws al-Dari, may Allah be pleased with him, was in the year nine after the Hijra. According to this, this ruling would be a later one, and whoever claims it was abrogated needs decisive proof on this point, and Allah knows best.

Asbat said, from al-Suddi, concerning {O you who believe, testimony among you when death approaches one of you at the time of the will, two just persons among you}: this concerns the will at the time of death; the person makes his will and has two Muslim men witness what is owed to him and what he owes. He said: this is when one is present at home. {Or two others not from among you}, this is while traveling. {If you are traveling in the land and the affliction of death befalls you}, this refers to a man overtaken by death during his journey, with no Muslim present with him, so he calls two men from the Jews, the Christians, or the Magians, and makes his will to them, and hands over his inheritance to them, and they accept it. If the family of the deceased is satisfied with the will and recognizes [the property of their relative], they leave the two men be; but if they have doubts, they bring the two men before the ruler. That is the meaning of His saying: {You detain the two of them after the prayer, and they swear by Allah if you have doubts}. Abdullah ibn Abbas said: it is as though I can see the two foreigners when they were brought before Abu Musa al-Ash'ari at his house. He opened the document, and the family of the deceased denied it and accused the two of betrayal. Abu Musa wanted to make them swear after Asr, so I said to him: they do not care about the Asr prayer, but rather make them swear after their own prayer, according to their own religion. So the two men are made to stand after their prayer, according to their religion, and they swear: by Allah, we will not sell it for a small price, even if it concerns a close relative, and we will not conceal the testimony of Allah, for we would then be among the sinners: that their companion indeed willed this, and that this is indeed his estate. The leader says to the two of them before they swear: if you conceal or betray, I will expose you before your people, your testimony will no longer be accepted, and I will punish you. When he says this to them, it is more likely that they will give testimony truthfully. Narrated by Ibn Jarir.

Ibn Jarir said: al-Husayn narrated to us, Hushaym narrated to us, Mughira informed us, from Ibrahim and Saeed ibn Jubayr, that they both said concerning this verse, {O you who believe, testimony among you}: when a man is dying while traveling, let him have two Muslim men witness it; if he finds no two Muslims, then two of the People of the Book. When the two of them arrive with his estate, if the heirs believe them, their statement is accepted; but if they suspect them, the two are made to swear after the Asr prayer: by Allah, we have not concealed, nor lied, nor betrayed, nor altered anything.

Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas, concerning the exegesis of this verse: If doubt arises concerning their testimony, the two of them are made to swear after the prayer, by Allah: "We have not sold our testimony for a small price." If the heirs then discover that the two disbelievers lied in their testimony, two men from among the heirs rise and swear by Allah: that the testimony of the two disbelievers is false, and that we have not transgressed. That is the meaning of His saying: {But if it is known that these two were guilty of sin} meaning: if it becomes known that the two disbelievers lied, {then two others shall stand in their place} meaning: from among the heirs, and they swear by Allah: that the testimony of the two disbelievers is false, and that we have not transgressed. Thus the testimony of the two disbelievers is rejected, and the testimony of the heirs is accepted.

Al-Awfi likewise narrated this from Ibn Abbas. Ibn Jarir related both of these reports.

Thus this ruling, based on the requirement of this verse, was established by more than one of the leading Successors and the early generations (Salaf), may Allah be pleased with them, and this is the position of Imam Ahmad, may Allah have mercy on him.

Concerning His saying: {That is more likely that they will give testimony in its true form} meaning: the legislating of this ruling in this approved manner, namely making the two dhimmi witnesses swear when doubt has arisen about them, is closer to ensuring that they give testimony in its true, approved form.

Concerning His saying: {or [that they] fear that other oaths will be taken after their oaths} meaning: what would motivate them to give testimony in its proper form is reverence for swearing by Allah, observing due regard for Him, and honoring Him, along with fear of public disgrace among the people if the oath is turned back upon the heirs, so that they swear and thereby become entitled to what they claim. This is why He said: {or [that they] fear that other oaths will be taken after their oaths}.

Then He said: {And fear Allah} meaning: in all your affairs, {and listen} meaning: and obey, {for Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people} meaning: those who go out from obedience to Him and from following His law.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

اشْتَمَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ الْكَرِيمَةُ عَلَى حُكْمٍ عَزِيزٍ، قِيلَ: إِنَّهُ مَنْسُوخٌ رَوَاهُ العَوْفي مِنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ. وَقَالَ حَمَّادُ بْنُ أَبِي سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ: إِنَّهَا مَنْسُوخَةٌ. وَقَالَ آخَرُونَ -وَهُمُ الْأَكْثَرُونَ، فِيمَا قَالَهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ-: بَلْ هُوَ مُحْكَمٌ؛ وَمَنِ ادَّعَى النَّسْخَ فَعَلَيْهِ الْبَيَانُ.

فَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا شَهَادَةُ بَيْنِكُمْ إِذَا حَضَرَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ حِينَ الْوَصِيَّةِ اثْنَانِ﴾ هَذَا هُوَ الْخَبَرُ؛ لِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿شَهَادَةُ بَيْنِكُمْ﴾ فَقِيلَ تَقْدِيرُهُ: "شَهَادَةُ اثْنَيْنِ"، حُذِفَ الْمُضَافُ، وَأُقِيمَ الْمُضَافُ إِلَيْهِ مَقَامه. وَقِيلَ: دَلَّ الْكَلَامُ عَلَى تَقْدِيرِ أَنْ يَشْهَدَ اثْنَانِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿ذَوَا عَدْلٍ﴾ وَصَفَ الِاثْنَيْنِ، بِأَنْ يَكُونَا عَدْلَيْنِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿مِنْكُمْ﴾ أَيْ: مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ. قَالَهُ الْجُمْهُورُ. قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿ذَوَا عَدْلٍ مِنْكُمْ﴾ قَالَ: مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: رُوي عَنْ عُبيدة، وَسَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ، وَالْحَسَنِ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، وَيَحْيَى بْنِ يَعْمُر، والسُّدِّي، وقتادةَ، ومُقاتل بْنِ حَيَّان، وَعَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ، نَحْوُ ذَلِكَ.

قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: وَقَالَ آخَرُونَ: عَنَى: ذَلِكَ ﴿ذَوَا عَدْلٍ مِنْكُمْ﴾ أَيْ: مِنْ حَي الْمُوصِي. وَذَلِكَ قَوْلٌ رُوِيَ عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ وَعُبَيْدَةَ وَعِدَّةٍ غَيْرِهِمَا.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿أَوْ آخَرَانِ مِنْ غَيْرِكُمْ﴾ قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ عَوْن، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَاحِدِ بْنُ زِيَادٍ، حَدَّثَنَا حَبِيبُ بْنِ أَبِي عَمْرَة، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جبير قَالَ: قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿أَوْ آخَرَانِ مِنْ غَيْرِكُمْ﴾ قَالَ: مِنْ غَيْرِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ، يَعْنِي: أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ: وَرُوِيَ عَنْ عُبَيْدَةَ، وشُرَيْح، وَسَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ، وَمُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِيرِينَ، وَيَحْيَى بْنِ يَعْمُرَ، وَعِكْرِمَةَ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، وَسَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، وَالشَّعْبِيِّ، وَإِبْرَاهِيمَ النَّخَعِي، وَقَتَادَةَ، وَأَبِي مِجْلزَ، والسُّديِّ، وَمُقَاتِلِ بْنِ حَيَّانَ، وَعَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ، نَحْوُ ذَلِكَ.

وَعَلَى مَا حَكَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ وَعُبَيْدَةَ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿مِنْكُمْ﴾ أَيِ: الْمُرَادُ مِنْ قَبِيلَةِ الْمُوصِي، يَكُونُ الْمُرَادُ هَاهُنَا: ﴿أَوْ آخَرَانِ مِنْ غَيْرِكُمْ﴾ أَيْ: مِنْ غَيْرِ قَبِيلَةِ الْمُوصِي. وَقَدْ رُوِيَ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ مِثْلُهُ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ الْبَصْرِيِّ، وَالزُّهْرِيِّ، رَحِمَهُمَا اللَّهُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿إِنْ أَنْتُمْ ضَرَبْتُمْ فِي الأرْضِ﴾ أَيْ: سَافَرْتُمْ، ﴿فَأَصَابَتْكُمْ مُصِيبَةُ الْمَوْتِ﴾ وَهَذَانَ شَرْطَانِ لِجَوَازِ اسْتِشْهَادِ الذِّمِّيِّينَ عِنْدَ فَقْدِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ، أَنْ يَكُونَ ذَلِكَ فِي سَفَرٍ، وَأَنْ يَكُونَ فِي وَصِيَّةٍ، كَمَا صَرَّحَ بِذَلِكَ شُرَيْحٌ الْقَاضِي.

قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا عَمْرُو بْنُ عَلِيٍّ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ ووَكِيع قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا الْأَعْمَشُ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ شُرَيْحٍ قال: لا تجوز شهادة اليهودي والنصراني إِلَّا فِي سَفَرٍ، وَلَا تَجُوزُ فِي سَفَرٍ إِلَّا فِي وَصِيَّةٍ.

ثُمَّ رَوَاهُ عَنْ أَبِي كُرَيْب، عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ السَّبِيعي قَالَ: قَالَ شُرَيْحٌ، فَذَكَرَ مِثْلَهُ.

وَقَدْ رُوِيَ مِثْلُهُ عَنِ الْإِمَامِ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ حَنْبَلٍ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى. وَهَذِهِ الْمَسْأَلَةُ مِنْ إِفْرَادِهِ، وَخَالَفَهُ الثَّلَاثَةُ فَقَالُوا: لَا تَجُوزُ شهادة أهل الذمة على المسلمين. وَأَجَازَهَا أَبُو حَنِيفَةَ فِيمَا بَيْنَ بَعْضِهِمْ بَعْضًا.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا عَمْرُو بْنُ عَلِيٍّ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، حَدَّثَنَا صَالِحُ بْنُ أَبِي الْأَخْضَرِ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ قَالَ: مَضَتِ السُّنَّةُ أَنَّهُ لَا تَجُوزُ شَهَادَةُ كَافِرٍ فِي حَضَرٍ وَلَا سفر، إنما هي في المسلمين. وَقَالَ ابْنُ زَيْدٍ: نَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ فِي رَجُلٍ تُوُفِّيَ وَلَيْسَ عِنْدَهُ أَحَدٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْإِسْلَامِ، وَذَلِكَ فِي أَوَّلِ الْإِسْلَامِ، وَالْأَرْضُ حَرْبٌ، وَالنَّاسُ كُفَّارٌ، وَكَانَ النَّاسُ يَتَوَارَثُونَ بِالْوَصِيَّةِ، ثُمَّ نُسخت الْوَصِيَّةُ وَفُرِضَتِ الْفَرَائِضُ، وَعَمِلَ النَّاسُ بِهَا.

رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ، وَفِي هَذَا نَظَرٌ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: اخْتُلِفَ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿شَهَادَةُ بَيْنِكُمْ إِذَا حَضَرَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ حِينَ الْوَصِيَّةِ اثْنَانِ ذَوَا عَدْلٍ مِنْكُمْ أَوْ آخَرَانِ مِنْ غَيْرِكُمْ﴾ هَلِ الْمُرَادُ بِهِ أَنْ يُوصِيَ إِلَيْهِمَا، أَوْ يُشْهِدَهُمَا؟ عَلَى قَوْلَيْنِ:

أَحَدُهُمَا: أَنْ يُوصِيَ إِلَيْهِمَا، كَمَا قَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ قُسَيط قَالَ: سُئِلَ ابْنُ مَسْعُودٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، عَنْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ قَالَ هَذَا رَجُلٌ سَافَرَ وَمَعَهُ مَالٌ، فَأَدْرَكَهُ قَدَرُهُ، فَإِنْ وَجَدَ رَجُلَيْنِ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ دَفَعَ إِلَيْهِمَا تَرِكَتَهُ، وَأَشْهَدَ عَلَيْهِمَا عَدْلَيْنِ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ.

رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ وَفِيهِ انْقِطَاعٌ.

وَالْقَوْلُ الثَّانِي: أَنَّهُمَا يَكُونَانِ شَاهِدَيْنِ. وَهُوَ ظَاهِرُ سِيَاقِ الْآيَةِ الْكَرِيمَةِ، فَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ وَصِيٌّ ثَالِثٌ مَعَهُمَا اجْتَمَعَ فِيهِمَا الْوَصْفَانِ: الْوِصَايَةُ وَالشَّهَادَةُ، كَمَا فِي قِصَّةِ تَمِيم الدَّارِيِّ، وعَدِيّ بْنِ بَدَّاء، كَمَا سَيَأْتِي ذِكْرُهَا آنِفًا، إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ وَبِهِ التَّوْفِيقُ.

وَقَدِ اسْتَشْكَلَ ابنُ جَرِيرٍ كَوْنَهُمَا شَاهِدَيْنَ، قَالَ: لِأَنَّا لَا نَعْلَمُ حُكْمًا يَحْلِفُ فِيهِ الشَّاهِدُ. وَهَذَا لَا يَمْنَعُ الْحُكْمَ الَّذِي تَضَمَّنَتْهُ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ الْكَرِيمَةُ، وَهُوَ حُكْمٌ مُسْتَقِلٌّ بِنَفْسِهِ، لَا يَلْزَمُ أَنْ يَكُونَ جَارِيًا عَلَى قِيَاسِ جَمِيعِ الْأَحْكَامِ، عَلَى أَنَّ هَذَا حُكْمٌ خَاصٌّ بِشَهَادَةٍ خَاصَّةٍ فِي مَحَلٍّ خَاصٍّ، وَقَدِ اغْتُفِرَ فِيهِ مِنَ الْأُمُورِ مَا لَمْ يُغْتَفَرْ فِي غَيْرِهِ، فَإِذَا قَامَتْ قَرَائِنُ الرِّيبَةِ حَلِفَ هَذَا الشَّاهِدُ بِمُقْتَضَى مَا دَلَّتْ عَلَيْهِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ الْكَرِيمَةُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿تَحْبِسُونَهُمَا مِنْ بَعْدِ الصَّلاةِ﴾ قَالَ [الْعَوْفِيُّ، عَنِ] ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: يَعْنِي صَلَاةَ الْعَصْرِ. وَكَذَا قَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ، وَإِبْرَاهِيمُ النَّخَعِي، وَقَتَادَةُ، وعِكْرِمة، وَمُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سِيرِينَ. وَقَالَ الزُّهْرِيُّ: يَعْنِي صَلَاةَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ، وَقَالَ السُّدِّيُّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: يَعْنِي صَلَاةَ أَهْلِ دِينِهِمَا.

وَالْمَقْصُودُ: أَنْ يُقَامَ هَذَانَ الشَّاهِدَانِ بَعْدَ صَلَاةٍ اجْتَمَعَ النَّاسُ فِيهَا بِحَضْرَتِهِمْ، ﴿فَيُقْسِمَانِ بِاللَّهِ﴾ أَيْ: فَيَحْلِفَانِ بِاللَّهِ ﴿إِنِ ارْتَبْتُمْ﴾ أَيْ: إِنْ ظَهَرَتْ لَكُمْ مِنْهُمَا رِيبَةٌ، أَنَّهُمَا قَدْ خَانَا أَوْ غَلَّا فَيَحْلِفَانِ حِينَئِذٍ بِاللَّهِ ﴿لَا نَشْتَرِي بِهِ﴾ أَيْ: بِأَيْمَانِنَا. قَالَهُ مُقاتِل بْنُ حَيَّانَ ﴿ثَمَنًا﴾ أَيْ: لَا نَعْتَاضُ عَنْهُ بِعِوَضٍ قَلِيلٍ مِنَ الدُّنْيَا الْفَانِيَةِ الزَّائِلَةِ، ﴿وَلَوْ كَانَ ذَا قُرْبَى﴾ أَيْ: وَلَوْ كَانَ الْمَشْهُودُ عَلَيْهِ قَرِيبًا إِلَيْنَا لَا نُحَابِيهِ، ﴿وَلا نَكْتُمُ شَهَادَةَ اللَّهِ﴾ أَضَافَهَا إِلَى اللَّهِ تَشْرِيفًا لَهَا، وَتَعْظِيمًا لِأَمْرِهَا.

وَقَرَأَ بَعْضُهُمْ: "وَلَا نَكْتُمُ شَهَادَةً آللَّهِ" مَجْرُورًا عَلَى الْقَسَمِ. رَوَاهَا ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ، عن عامر الشعبي.

وحَكَي عَنْ بَعْضِهِمْ أَنَّهُ قَرَأَ: "وَلَا نَكْتُمُ شَهَادَةً اللَّهُ"، وَالْقِرَاءَةُ الْأُولَى هِيَ الْمَشْهُورَةُ.

﴿إِنَّا إِذًا لَمِنَ الآثِمِينَ﴾ أَيْ: إِنْ فَعَلْنَا شَيْئًا مِنْ ذَلِكَ، مِنْ تَحْرِيفِ الشَّهَادَةِ، أَوْ تَبْدِيلِهَا، أَوْ تَغْيِيرِهَا أَوْ كَتْمِهَا بِالْكُلِّيَّةِ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَإِنْ عُثِرَ عَلَى أَنَّهُمَا اسْتَحَقَّا إِثْمًا﴾ أَيْ: فَإِنِ اشْتَهَرَ وَظَهَرَ وَتَحَقَّقَ مِنَ الشَّاهِدَيْنِ الْوَصِيَّيْنِ، أَنَّهُمَا خَانَا أَوْ غَلا شَيْئًا مِنَ الْمَالِ الْمُوصَى بِهِ إِلَيْهِمَا، وَظَهَرَ عَلَيْهِمَا بِذَلِكَ ﴿فَآخَرَانِ يَقُومَانِ مَقَامَهُمَا مِنَ الَّذِينَ اسْتَحَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الأوْلَيَانِ﴾ هَذِهِ قِرَاءَةُ الْجُمْهُورِ: "اسْتُحِقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الْأَوْلَيَانِ". ورُوي عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، وأُبيّ، وَالْحَسَنِ الْبَصْرِيِّ أَنَّهُمْ قَرَؤُوهَا: ﴿اسْتَحَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الأوْلَيَانِ﴾ .

وَقَدْ رَوَى الْحَاكِمُ فِي الْمُسْتَدْرَكِ مِنْ طَرِيقِ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ الفَرْوِي، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ بِلَالٍ، عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي رَافِعٍ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ؛ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ قَرَأَ: ﴿مِنَ الَّذِينَ اسْتَحَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الأوْلَيَانِ﴾ ثُمَّ قَالَ: صَحِيحٌ عَلَى شَرْطِ مُسْلِمٍ وَلَمْ يُخَرِّجَاهُ. 

وَقَرَأَ بَعْضُهُمْ، وَمِنْهُمُ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: "مِنَ الَّذِينَ اسْتَحَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الأوَّلِين". وَقَرَأَ الْحَسَنُ: "مِنَ الَّذِينَ اسْتَحَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الأوَّلان"، حَكَاهُ ابنُ جَرِيرٍ.

فَعَلَى قِرَاءَةِ الْجُمْهُورِ يَكُونُ الْمَعْنَى بِذَلِكَ: أَيْ مَتَى تَحَقَّقَ ذَلِكَ بِالْخَبَرِ الصَّحِيحِ عَلَى خِيَانَتِهِمَا، فَلْيَقُمِ اثْنَانِ مِنَ الْوَرَثَةِ الْمُسْتَحِقِّينَ لِلتَّرِكَةِ وَلِيَكُونَا مِنْ أوْلى مَنْ يَرِثُ ذَلِكَ الْمَالَ ﴿فَيُقْسِمَانِ بِاللَّهِ لَشَهَادَتُنَا أَحَقُّ مِنْ شَهَادَتِهِمَا﴾ أَيْ: لِقَوْلِنَا: إِنَّهُمَا خَانَا أحقُّ وَأَصَحُّ وَأَثْبَتُ مِنْ شَهَادَتِهِمَا الْمُتَقَدِّمَةِ ﴿وَمَا اعْتَدَيْنَا﴾ أَيْ: فِيمَا قُلْنَا مِنَ الْخِيَانَةِ ﴿إِنَّا إِذًا لَمِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ﴾ أَيْ: إِنْ كُنَّا قَدْ كَذَبْنَا عَلَيْهِمَا.

وَهَذَا التَّحْلِيفُ لِلْوَرَثَةِ، وَالرُّجُوعُ إِلَى قَوْلِهِمَا وَالْحَالَةُ هَذِهِ، كَمَا يَحْلِفُ أَوْلِيَاءُ الْمَقْتُولِ إِذَا ظَهَرَ لَوْث فِي جَانِبِ الْقَاتِلِ، فَيُقْسِمُ الْمُسْتَحِقُّونَ عَلَى الْقَاتِلِ فَيُدْفَعُ بِرُمَّتِهِ إِلَيْهِمْ، كَمَا هُوَ مُقَرَّرٌ فِي بَابِ "الْقَسَامَةِ" مِنَ الْأَحْكَامِ.

وَقَدْ وَرَدَتِ السُّنَّةُ بِمِثْلِ مَا دَلَّتْ عَلَيْهِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ الْكَرِيمَةُ، فَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا الْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ أَبِي النَّضْرِ، عَنْ بَاذَانَ -يَعْنِي: أَبَا صَالِحٍ مَوْلَى أَمِّ هَانِئٍ بِنْتِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ-عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، عَنْ تَمِيمٍ الدَّارِيِّ فِي هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا شَهَادَةُ بَيْنِكُمْ إِذَا حَضَرَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ﴾ قَالَ: بَرئ النَّاسُ مِنْهَا غَيْرِي وَغَيْرَ عَديّ بْنِ بَدَّاء. وَكَانَا نَصْرَانِيَّيْنِ، يَخْتَلِفَانِ إِلَى الشَّامِ قَبْلَ الْإِسْلَامِ، فَأَتَيَا الشَّامَ لِتِجَارَتِهِمَا وَقَدِمَ عَلَيْهِمَا مَوْلًى لِبَنِي سَهْمٍ، يُقَالُ لَهُ: بُدَيْل بْنُ أَبِي مَرْيَمَ، بِتِجَارَةٍ وَمَعَهُ جَامٌ مِنْ فِضَّةٍ يُرِيدُ بِهِ الْمُلْكَ، وَهُوَ عُظْم تِجَارَتِهِ. فَمَرِضَ فَأَوْصَى إِلَيْهِمَا، وَأَمَرَهُمَا أَنْ يُبَلِّغَا مَا تَرَكَ أَهْلَهُ -قَالَ تَمِيمٌ: فَلَمَّا مَاتَ أَخَذْنَا ذَلِكَ الْجَامَ، فبعناه بِأَلْفِ دِرْهَمٍ، ثُمَّ اقْتَسَمْنَاهُ أَنَا وَعَدِيُّ بْنُ بَدَّاءٍ. فَلَمَّا قَدِمْنَا إِلَى أَهْلِهِ دَفَعْنَا إِلَيْهِمْ مَا كَانَ مَعَنَا. وَفَقَدُوا الْجَامَ فَسَأَلُونَا عَنْهُ، فقلنا: ما ترك غير هذا، وما د فع إِلَيْنَا غَيْرَهُ-قَالَ تَمِيمٌ: فَلَمَّا أَسْلَمْتُ بَعْدَ قُدُومِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ الْمَدِينَةَ تَأَثَّمْتُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ، فَأَتَيْتُ أَهْلَهُ فَأَخْبَرْتُهُمُ الْخَبَرَ، وَدَفَعْتُ إِلَيْهِمْ خَمْسَمِائَةِ دِرْهَمٍ، وَأَخْبَرْتُهُمْ أَنَّ عِنْدَ صَاحِبِي مِثْلَهَا، فَوَثَبُوا إِلَيْهِ أَنْ يَسْتَحْلِفُوهُ بِمَا يُعَظَّمُ بِهِ عَلَى أَهْلِ دِينِهِ، فَحَلَفَ فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا شَهَادَةُ بَيْنِكُمْ﴾ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿فَيُقْسِمَانِ بِاللَّهِ لَشَهَادَتُنَا أَحَقُّ مِنْ شَهَادَتِهِمَا﴾ فَقَامَ عَمْرُو بْنُ الْعَاصِ وَرَجُلٌ آخَرُ مِنْهُمْ فَحَلَفَا، فنزعتْ الْخَمْسُمِائَةٍ مِنْ عَدي بْنِ بَدَّاء.

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو عِيسَى التِّرْمِذِيُّ وَابْنُ جَرِيرٍ كِلَاهُمَا عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أبي شعيب الحَرَّاني، عن محمد بن سلمة، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، بِهِ فَذَكَرَهُ -وَعِنْدَهُ: فَأَتَوْا بِهِ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَسَأَلَهُمُ الْبَيِّنَةَ فَلَمْ يَجِدُوا، فَأَمَرَهُمْ أَنْ يَسْتَحْلِفُوهُ بِمَا يُعَظَّم بِهِ عَلَى أَهْلِ دِينِهِ، فَحَلَفَ فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا شَهَادَةُ بَيْنِكُمْ إِذَا حَضَرَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ﴾ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿أَوْ يَخَافُوا أَنْ تُرَدَّ أَيْمَانٌ بَعْدَ أَيْمَانِهِمْ﴾ فَقَامَ عَمْرُو بْنُ الْعَاصِ وَرَجُلٌ آخَرُ، فَحَلَفَا. فَنزعَتْ الْخَمْسُمِائَةٍ مِنْ عَدِيِّ بْنِ بَدَّاء.

ثُمَّ قَالَ: هَذَا حَدِيثٌ غَرِيبٌ، وَلَيْسَ إِسْنَادُهُ بِصَحِيحٍ، وَأَبُو النَّضْرِ الَّذِي رَوَى عَنْهُ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ هَذَا الْحَدِيثَ هُوَ عِنْدِي مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ السَّائِبِ الْكَلْبِيُّ، يُكَنَّى أَبَا النَّضْرِ، وَقَدْ تَرَكَهُ أَهْلُ الْعِلْمِ بِالْحَدِيثِ، وَهُوَ صَاحِبُ التَّفْسِيرِ، سَمِعْتُ مُحَمَّدَ بْنَ إِسْمَاعِيلَ يَقُولُ: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ السَّائِبِ الْكَلْبِيُّ، يُكَنَّى أَبَا النَّضْرِ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: وَلَا نَعْرِفُ لِسَالِمٍ أَبِي النَّضْرِ رِوَايَةً عَنْ أَبِي صَالِحٍ مَوْلَى أَمِّ هَانِئٍ، وَقَدْ رُوي عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ شَيْءٌ مِنْ هَذَا عَلَى الِاخْتِصَارِ مِنْ غَيْرِ هَذَا الْوَجْهِ.

حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ وَكِيع، حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ آدَمَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي الْقَاسِمِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَير، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: خَرَجَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ بَنِي سَهْمٍ مَعَ تَمِيمٍ الدَّارِيِّ وَعَدِيِّ بْنِ بَدّاء، فَمَاتَ السَّهْمِيُّ بِأَرْضٍ لَيْسَ فِيهَا مُسْلِمٌ، فَلَمَّا قَدِمَا بِتَرِكَتِهِ فَقَدُوا جَامًا مِنْ فِضَّةٍ مُخَوّصًا بِالذَّهَبِ، فَأَحْلَفَهُمَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ، وَوَجَدُوا الْجَامَ بِمَكَّةَ، فَقِيلَ: اشْتَرَيْنَاهُ مِنْ تَمِيمٍ وَعَدِيٍّ. فَقَامَ رَجُلَانِ مِنْ أَوْلِيَاءِ السَّهْمِيِّ فَحَلَفَا بِاللَّهِ لَشَهَادَتُنَا أَحَقُّ مِنْ شَهَادَتِهِمَا، وَأَنَّ الْجَامَ لِصَاحبهم. وَفِيهِمْ نَزَلَتْ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا شَهَادَةُ بَيْنِكُمْ﴾

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ آدَمَ، بِهِ. ثُمَّ قَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: هَذَا حَدِيثٌ حَسَنٌ غَرِيبٌ، وَهُوَ حَدِيثُ ابْنِ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ. 

وَمُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي الْقَاسِمِ، كُوفِيٌّ، قِيلَ: إِنَّهُ صَالِحُ الْحَدِيثِ، وَقَدْ ذَكَرَ هَذِهِ الْقِصَّةَ مُرْسَلَةً غيرُ وَاحِدٍ مِنَ التَّابِعَيْنِ مِنْهُمْ: عِكْرِمَةُ، وَمُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سِيرِينَ، وَقَتَادَةُ. وَذَكَرُوا أَنَّ التَّحْلِيفَ كَانَ بَعْدَ صَلَاةِ الْعَصْرِ، رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ. وَكَذَا ذَكَرَهَا مُرْسَلَةً: مُجَاهِدٌ، وَالْحَسَنُ، وَالضَّحَّاكُ. وَهَذَا يَدُلُّ عَلَى اشْتِهَارِهَا فِي السَّلَفِ وَصِحَّتِهَا.

وَمِنَ الشَّوَاهِدِ لِصِحَّةِ هَذِهِ الْقِصَّةِ أَيْضًا مَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو جَعْفَرِ بْنُ جَرِيرٍ:

حَدَّثَنِي يَعْقُوبُ، حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْم، أَخْبَرَنَا زَكَرِيَّا، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ؛ أَنَّ رَجُلًا مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ حَضَرَتْهُ الْوَفَاةُ بدَقُوقا، قَالَ: فَحَضَرَتْهُ الْوَفَاةُ وَلَمْ يَجِدْ أَحَدًا مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ يُشْهِدُهُ عَلَى وَصِيَّتِهِ، فَأَشْهَدُ رَجُلَيْنِ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ. قَالَ: فَقَدِمَا الْكُوفَةَ، فَأَتَيَا الْأَشْعَرِيَّ -يَعْنِي: أَبَا مُوسَى الْأَشْعَرِيَّ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ-فَأَخْبَرَاهُ وَقَدَّمَا بِتَرِكَتِهِ وَوَصِيَّتِهِ، فَقَالَ الْأَشْعَرِيُّ: هَذَا أَمْرٌ لَمْ يَكُنْ بَعْدَ الَّذِي كَانَ فِي عَهْدِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قَالَ: فَأَحْلَفَهُمَا بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ: بِاللَّهِ مَا خَانَا وَلَا كَذَبَا وَلَا بَدّلا وَلَا كَتَمَا وَلَا غَيَّرَا، وَإِنَّهَا لَوَصِيَّةُ الرَّجُلِ وَتَرِكَتُهُ. قَالَ: فَأَمْضَى شَهَادَتَهُمَا.

ثُمَّ رَوَاهُ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ عَلِيٍّ الفَلاس، عَنْ أَبِي دَاوُدَ الطَّيَالِسِيِّ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ الْأَزْرَقِ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ؛ أَنَّ أَبَا مُوسَى قَضَى بِدَقُوقَا. 

وَهَذَانَ إِسْنَادَانِ صَحِيحَانِ إِلَى الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي مُوسَى الْأَشْعَرِيِّ.

فَقَوْلُهُ: "هَذَا أَمْرٌ لَمْ يَكُنْ بَعْدَ الَّذِي كَانَ فِي عَهْدِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ" الظَّاهِرُ -وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ-أَنَّهُ إِنَّمَا أَرَادَ بِذَلِكَ قِصَّةَ تَمِيمٍ وَعَدِيِّ بْنِ بَدّاء، قَدْ ذَكَرُوا أَنَّ إِسْلَامَ تَمِيم بْنِ أوْسٍ الدَّارِيِّ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، كَانَ فِي سَنَةِ تِسْعٍ مِنَ الْهِجْرَةِ فَعَلَى هَذَا يَكُونُ هَذَا الْحُكْمُ مُتَأَخِّرًا، يَحْتَاجُ مُدَّعِي نَسْخِهِ إِلَى دَلِيلٍ فَاصِلٍ فِي هَذَا الْمَقَامِ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَقَالَ أَسْبَاطٌ عَنِ السُّدِّي: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا شَهَادَةُ بَيْنِكُمْ إِذَا حَضَرَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ حِينَ الْوَصِيَّةِ اثْنَانِ ذَوَا عَدْلٍ مِنْكُمْ﴾ قَالَ: هَذَا فِي الْوَصِيَّةِ عِنْدَ الْمَوْتِ، يُوصِي وَيُشْهِدُ رَجُلَيْنِ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ عَلَى مَا لَهُ وَمَا عَلَيْهِ، قَالَ: هَذَا فِي الْحَضَرِ، ﴿أَوْ آخَرَانِ مِنْ غَيْرِكُمْ﴾ فِي السَّفَرِ، ﴿إِنْ أَنْتُمْ ضَرَبْتُمْ فِي الأرْضِ فَأَصَابَتْكُمْ مُصِيبَةُ الْمَوْتِ﴾ هَذَا الرَّجُلُ يُدْرِكُهُ الْمَوْتُ فِي سَفَرِهِ، وَلَيْسَ بِحَضْرَتِهِ أَحَدٌ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ، فَيَدْعُو رَجُلَيْنِ مِنَ الْيَهُودِ وَالنَّصَارَى وَالْمَجُوسِ، فَيُوصِي إِلَيْهِمَا، وَيَدْفَعُ إِلَيْهِمَا مِيرَاثَهُ فَيَقْبَلَانِ بِهِ، فَإِنْ رَضِيَ أَهْلُ الْمَيِّتِ الْوَصِيَّةَ وَعَرَفُوا [مَالَ صَاحِبِهِمْ] تَرَكُوا الرَّجُلَيْنِ وَإِنِ ارْتَابُوا رَفَعُوهُمَا إِلَى السُّلْطَانِ. فَذَلِكَ قَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿تَحْبِسُونَهُمَا مِنْ بَعْدِ الصَّلاةِ فَيُقْسِمَانِ بِاللَّهِ إِنِ ارْتَبْتُمْ﴾ قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: كَأَنِّي أَنْظُرُ إِلَى العلْجين حِينَ انتُهى بِهِمَا إِلَى أَبِي مُوسَى الْأَشْعَرِيِّ فِي دَارِهِ، فَفَتَحَ الصَّحِيفَةَ، فَأَنْكَرَ أَهْلُ الْمَيِّتِ وخَوّنوهما فَأَرَادَ أَبُو مُوسَى أَنْ يَسْتَحْلِفَهُمَا بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ، فَقُلْتُ لَهُ: إِنَّهُمَا لَا يُبَالِيَانِ صَلَاةَ الْعَصْرِ، وَلَكِنِ اسْتَحْلِفْهُمَا بَعْدَ صَلَاتِهِمَا فِي دِينِهِمَا، فَيُوقَفُ الرَّجُلَانِ بعد صلاتهما في دِينِهِمَا، فَيَحْلِفَانِ: بِاللَّهِ لَا نَشْتَرِي بِهِ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا وَلَوْ كَانَ ذَا قُرْبَى، وَلَا نَكْتُمُ شَهَادَةَ اللَّهِ إِنَّا إِذًا لَمِنَ الْآثِمِينَ: أَنَّ صَاحِبَهُمْ لَبِهَذَا أَوْصَى، وَأَنَّ هَذِهِ لَتَرِكَتُهُ. فَيَقُولُ لَهُمَا الْإِمَامُ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَحْلِفَا: إِنَّكُمَا إِنْ كَتَمْتُمَا أَوْ خُنْتُما فَضَحْتُكُما فِي قَوْمِكُمَا، وَلَمْ تَجُزْ لَكُمَا شَهَادَةٌ، وَعَاقَبْتُكُمَا. فَإِذَا قَالَ لَهُمَا ذَلِكَ، فَإِنَّ ذَلِكَ أَدْنَى أَنْ يَأْتُوا بِالشَّهَادَةِ عَلَى وَجْهِهَا. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ. 

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا الْحُسَيْنُ، حَدَّثَنَا هُشيْم، أَخْبَرَنَا مُغِيرَةُ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَسَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، أَنَّهُمَا قَالَا فِي هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا شَهَادَةُ بَيْنِكُمْ﴾ الْآيَةَ، قَالَا إِذَا حَضَرَ الرَّجُلَ الْوَفَاةُ فِي سَفَرٍ، فَلْيُشْهِدْ رَجُلَيْنِ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ، فَإِنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ رَجُلَيْنِ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ فَرَجُلَيْنِ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ فَإِذَا قَدِمَا بِتَرِكَتِهِ، فَإِنْ صَدَّقَهُمَا الْوَرَثَةُ قُبل قَوْلُهُمَا، وَإِنِ اتَّهَمُوهُمَا أُحْلِفَا بَعْدَ صَلَاةِ الْعَصْرِ: بِاللَّهِ مَا كَتَمْنَا وَلَا كَذَبْنَا وَلَا خُنَّا وَلَا غَيَّرنا.

وَقَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، فِي تَفْسِيرِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: فَإِنِ ارْتِيبَ فِي شَهَادَتِهِمَا اسْتَحْلَفَا بَعْدَ الصَّلَاةِ بِاللَّهِ: مَا اشْتَرَيْنَا بِشَهَادَتِنَا ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا. فَإِنِ اطَّلَعَ الْأَوْلِيَاءُ عَلَى أَنَّ الْكَافِرَيْنِ كَذَبَا فِي شَهَادَتِهِمَا، قَامَ رَجُلَانِ مِنَ الْأَوْلِيَاءِ فَحَلَفَا بِاللَّهِ: أَنَّ شَهَادَةَ الْكَافِرَيْنِ بَاطِلَةٌ، وَإِنَّا لَمْ نَعْتَدِ، فَذَلِكَ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿فَإِنْ عُثِرَ عَلَى أَنَّهُمَا اسْتَحَقَّا إِثْمًا﴾ يَقُولُ: إِنِ اطَّلَعَ عَلَى أَنَّ الْكَافِرَيْنِ كَذَبَا ﴿فَآخَرَانِ يَقُومَانِ مَقَامَهُمَا﴾ يَقُولُ: مِنَ الْأَوْلِيَاءِ، فَحَلَفَا بِاللَّهِ: أَنَّ شَهَادَةَ الكافرين باطلة، وإنا لم نعتد، فترد شَهَادَةَ الكافرَيْن، وَتَجُوزُ شَهَادَةُ الْأَوْلِيَاءِ.

وَهَكَذَا رَوَى العَوْفي، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ. رَوَاهُمَا ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ.

وَهَكَذَا قَرَّر هَذَا الْحُكْمَ عَلَى مُقْتَضَى هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ غيرُ وَاحِدٍ مِنْ أَئِمَّةِ التَّابِعِينِ وَالسَّلَفِ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ، وَهُوَ مَذْهَبُ الْإِمَامِ أَحْمَدَ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿ذَلِكَ أَدْنَى أَنْ يَأْتُوا بِالشَّهَادَةِ عَلَى وَجْهِهَا﴾ أَيْ: شَرْعِيَّةُ هَذَا الْحُكْمِ عَلَى هَذَا الْوَجْهِ الْمَرْضِيِّ مِنْ تَحْلِيفِ الشَّاهِدَيْنِ الذِّمِّيَّيْنِ وَقَدِ اسْتُرِيبَ بِهِمَا، أَقْرَبُ إِلَى إِقَامَتِهِمَا الشَّهَادَةَ عَلَى الْوَجْهِ الْمَرْضِيِّ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿أَوْ يَخَافُوا أَنْ تُرَدَّ أَيْمَانٌ بَعْدَ أَيْمَانِهِمْ﴾ أَيْ: يَكُونُ الْحَامِلُ لَهُمْ عَلَى الْإِتْيَانِ بِالشَّهَادَةِ عَلَى وَجْهِهَا، هُوَ تَعْظِيمُ الْحَلِفِ بِاللَّهِ وَمُرَاعَاةُ جَانِبِهِ وَإِجْلَالُهُ، وَالْخَوْفُ مِنَ الْفَضِيحَةِ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ إِذَا رُدَّتِ الْيَمِينُ عَلَى الْوَرَثَةِ، فَيَحْلِفُونَ وَيَسْتَحِقُّونَ مَا يَدْعُونَ، وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿أَوْ يَخَافُوا أَنْ تُرَدَّ أَيْمَانٌ بَعْدَ أَيْمَانِهِمْ﴾

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ﴾ أَيْ: فِي جَمِيعِ أُمُورِكُمْ ﴿وَاسْمَعُوا﴾ أَيْ: وَأَطِيعُوا ﴿وَاللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْفَاسِقِينَ﴾ يَعْنِي: الْخَارِجِينَ عَنْ طَاعَتِهِ وَمُتَابَعَةِ شريعته.

---

## 5:109 — verse 109

### Translation

This is an account of what Allah will say to the messengers on the Day of Resurrection, concerning what they were answered by their nations to whom they were sent, as He, Exalted is He, said: {Then We will most surely question those to whom [a message] was sent, and We will most surely question the messengers} [al-A'raf: 6], and He, Exalted is He, said: {So by your Lord, We will most surely question them all together about what they used to do} [al-Hijr: 92-93].

As for the messengers' statement, {We have no knowledge}, Mujahid, al-Hasan al-Basri, and al-Suddi said that they said this only out of the terror of that day.

Abd al-Razzaq said, from al-Thawri, from al-A'mash, from Mujahid, regarding {the day when Allah gathers the messengers and says: What answer were you given}: they will be terrified, and will say, {We have no knowledge}. This was narrated by Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim.

Ibn Jarir said: Ibn Humayd told us, Hakkam told us, Anbasah told us, saying: I heard an old man say: I heard al-Hasan say concerning His words {the day when Allah gathers the messengers}, the verse: he said, this is out of the terror of that day.

Asbat said, from al-Suddi, regarding {the day when Allah gathers the messengers and says: What answer were you given, they said: We have no knowledge}: this is because they will find themselves in a station where minds are bewildered, so when they are asked they say, {We have no knowledge}. Then they will move to another station, and there they will bear witness against their peoples. This was narrated by Ibn Jarir.

Then Ibn Jarir said: al-Qasim told us, al-Husayn told us, al-Hajjaj told us, from Ibn Jurayj, regarding His statement {the day when Allah gathers the messengers and says: What answer were you given}: meaning, what did they do after you, and what did they innovate after you? They said: {We have no knowledge, indeed You are the Knower of the unseen}.

Ali ibn Abi Talha said, from Ibn Abbas, regarding {the day when Allah gathers the messengers and says: What answer were you given, they said: We have no knowledge, indeed You are the Knower of the unseen}: they are saying to the Lord, Mighty and Majestic is He: we have no knowledge except knowledge which You know better than us.

This was narrated by Ibn Jarir, who then preferred this view over these three sayings. There is no doubt that it is a good statement, and it belongs to the category of showing proper etiquette (adab) with the Lord, Mighty and Majestic is He. That is, we have no knowledge in comparison to Your knowledge which encompasses all things. For even though we did receive answers and came to know those who answered us, among them were some whose outward state we could only perceive, while we had no knowledge of their inner reality. You are the One who has knowledge of all things and who perceives all things. So our knowledge in comparison to Your knowledge is as no knowledge at all, for indeed {You are the Knower of the unseen}.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

وَهَذَا إِخْبَارٌ عَمَّا يُخَاطِبُ اللَّهُ بِهِ الْمُرْسَلِينَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ، عَمَّا أُجِيبُوا بِهِ مِنْ أُمَمِهِمُ الَّذِينَ أَرْسَلَهُمْ إِلَيْهِمْ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَلَنَسْأَلَنَّ الَّذِينَ أُرْسِلَ إِلَيْهِمْ وَلَنَسْأَلَنَّ الْمُرْسَلِينَ﴾ [الْأَعْرَافِ: ٦] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَوَرَبِّكَ لَنَسْأَلَنَّهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ عَمَّا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ﴾ [الْحِجْرِ:٩٢، ٩٣] .

وَقَوْلُ الرُّسُلِ: ﴿لَا عِلْمَ لَنَا﴾ قَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ، وَالْحَسَنُ الْبَصْرِيُّ، والسُّدِّي: إِنَّمَا قَالُوا ذَلِكَ مِنْ هَوْلِ ذَلِكَ الْيَوْمِ.

قَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، عَنِ الثَّوْرِيِّ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ: ﴿يَوْمَ يَجْمَعُ اللَّهُ الرُّسُلَ فَيَقُولُ مَاذَا أُجِبْتُمْ﴾ فَيَفْزَعُونَ فَيَقُولُونَ: ﴿لَا عِلْمَ لَنَا﴾ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ وَابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ حُمَيْد، حَدَّثَنَا حَكَّام، حَدَّثَنَا عَنْبَسَة قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ شَيْخًا يَقُولُ: سَمِعْتُ الْحَسَنَ يَقُولُ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿يَوْمَ يَجْمَعُ اللَّهُ الرُّسُلَ﴾ الْآيَةَ، قَالَ: مِنْ هَوْلِ ذَلِكَ الْيَوْمِ.

وَقَالَ أَسْبَاطٌ، عَنِ السُّدِّي: ﴿يَوْمَ يَجْمَعُ اللَّهُ الرُّسُلَ فَيَقُولُ مَاذَا أُجِبْتُمْ قَالُوا لَا عِلْمَ لَنَا﴾ ذَلِكَ: أَنَّهُمْ نَزَلُوا مَنْزِلًا ذُهِلَتْ فِيهِ الْعُقُولُ، فَلَمَّا سُئِلُوا قَالُوا: ﴿لَا عِلْمَ لَنَا﴾ ثُمَّ نَزَلُوا مَنْزِلًا آخَرَ، فَشَهِدُوا عَلَى قَوْمِهِمْ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا الْقَاسِمُ، حَدَّثَنَا الْحُسَيْنُ، حَدَّثَنَا الْحَجَّاجُ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْج قَوْلُهُ: ﴿يَوْمَ يَجْمَعُ اللَّهُ الرُّسُلَ فَيَقُولُ مَاذَا أُجِبْتُمْ﴾ مَاذَا عَمِلُوا بَعْدَكُمْ؟ وَمَاذَا أَحْدَثُوا بَعْدَكُمْ؟ قَالُوا: ﴿لَا عِلْمَ لَنَا إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ عَلامُ الْغُيُوبِ﴾

وَقَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: ﴿يَوْمَ يَجْمَعُ اللَّهُ الرُّسُلَ فَيَقُولُ مَاذَا أُجِبْتُمْ قَالُوا لَا عِلْمَ لَنَا إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ عَلامُ الْغُيُوبِ﴾ يَقُولُونَ لِلرَّبِّ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: لَا عِلْمَ لَنَا، إِلَّا عِلْمَ أَنْتَ أَعْلَمُ بِهِ مِنَّا.

رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ. ثُمَّ اخْتَارَهُ عَلَى هَذِهِ الْأَقْوَالِ الثَّلَاثَةِ وَلَا شَكَّ أَنَّهُ قَوْلٌ حَسَنٌ، وَهُوَ مِنْ بَابِ التَّأَدُّبِ مَعَ الرَّبِّ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، أَيْ: لَا عِلْمَ لَنَا بِالنِّسْبَةِ إِلَى عِلْمِكَ الْمُحِيطِ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ، فَنَحْنُ وَإِنْ كُنَّا قَدْ أَجَبْنَا وَعَرَفْنَا مَنْ أَجَابَنَا، وَلَكِنَّ مِنْهُمْ مَنْ كُنَّا إِنَّمَا نَطَّلِعُ عَلَى ظَاهِرِهِ، لَا عِلْمَ لَنَا بِبَاطِنِهِ، وَأَنْتَ الْعَلِيمُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ، الْمُطَّلِعُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ. فَعِلْمُنَا بِالنِّسْبَةِ إِلَى عِلْمِكَ كَلَا عِلْم، فَإِنَّكَ ﴿أَنْتَ عَلامُ الْغُيُوبِ﴾

---

## 5:110-111 — verses 110-111 (shared commentary)

### Translation

He, exalted, mentions the favor which He bestowed upon His servant and messenger Jesus son of Mary, peace be upon him, in what He caused to occur at his hands of miracles and extraordinary events, saying, exalted is He: {When God said, "O Jesus son of Mary, remember My favor upon you"}, that is, in My creating you from a mother without a father, and My making you a sign and a decisive proof of the completeness of My power over all things, {and upon your mother}, in that I made you for her a proof of her innocence from what the ignorant wrongdoers attributed to her of indecency, {when I supported you with the Holy Spirit}, that is, Gabriel, peace be upon him, and made you a prophet calling to God in your youth and in your maturity, so I made you speak in the cradle while you were small, and you testified to your mother's innocence from every fault, and you acknowledged servitude to Me, and you informed of My message to you and your calling to My worship. This is why He, exalted, said: {you speak to the people in the cradle and in maturity}, that is, you call people to God in your youth and in your maturity. The word "speak" here carries the sense of "call," because his speaking to people in his maturity is not itself a wondrous matter.

Regarding His saying: {and when I taught you the Book and wisdom}, that is, writing and understanding, {and the Torah}, which is what was revealed to Moses son of Amram, the one who spoke directly with God (al-Kalim). The term "Torah" sometimes occurs in the hadith with a meaning broader than this.

Regarding His saying: {and when you fashioned from clay the shape of a bird by My permission}, that is, you shaped and formed it into the likeness of a bird by My permission granted to you for that, and it became a bird by My permission, that is, you breathed into that form which you had shaped by My permission granted to you for that, and it became a bird possessing a spirit by God's permission and His creating.

Regarding His saying: {and you healed the one born blind and the leper by My permission}, discussion of this has already preceded in Surat Al Imran in a manner sufficient, making its repetition unnecessary.

Regarding His saying: {and when you brought forth the dead by My permission}, that is, you called them and they arose from their graves by God's permission, His power, His will, and His decree.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Abu al-Hudhayl said: When Jesus son of Mary, peace be upon him, wished to bring the dead to life, he would pray two prostration-cycles, reciting in the first {Blessed is He in whose hand is the dominion} [Surat al-Mulk], and in the second {Alif Lam Mim. The revelation of the Book} [Surat al-Sajdah]. When he finished them, he would praise God and extol Him, then call upon Him by seven names: O Ancient, O Hidden, O Everlasting, O Single, O Odd, O One, O Samad (the one free of need, sought by all, self-sufficient). And when hardship befell him he would call upon another seven: O Living, O Self-Subsisting, O God, O Merciful, O Possessor of Majesty and Honor, O Light of the heavens and the earth and what is between them and Lord of the Mighty Throne, O my Lord.

This is a very remarkable report (athar).

Regarding His saying: {and when I restrained the Children of Israel from you, when you came to them with clear proofs, and those among them who disbelieved said, "This is nothing but plain magic"}, that is, remember My favor upon you in restraining them from you when you came to them with the proofs and decisive arguments of your prophethood and your message from God to them. They denied you and accused you of being a sorcerer, and they schemed to kill you and crucify you, so I saved you from them, raised you up to Me, purified you from their filth, and spared you their harm. This indicates that this favor came from God to him after his being raised to the lowest heaven, or else this favor is something that will occur on the Day of Resurrection, expressed in the past tense to indicate that its occurrence is inevitable. This is among the secrets of the unseen which God disclosed to His messenger Muhammad ﷺ.

Regarding His saying: {and when I inspired the disciples, "Believe in Me and in My messenger"}, this too is among the favors bestowed upon him, peace be upon him, in that He gave him companions and supporters. It has been said that what is meant by this "revelation" (wahy) is a revelation of inspiration, as He said: {and We inspired the mother of Moses, "Suckle him"} [al-Qasas: 7], which is a revelation of inspiration without fear, and as He, exalted, said: {and your Lord inspired the bee, "Take for yourselves dwellings among the mountains and among the trees and in what they construct. Then eat of all the fruits and follow the ways of your Lord, made easy for you"} [al-Nahl: 68-69]. Likewise, some of the predecessors said regarding this verse: {and when I inspired the disciples, "Believe in Me and in My messenger," they said, "We have believed"} [that is, in God and in the messenger of God] {"and bear witness that we are submitters (Muslims)"}, that is, they were inspired with that and complied with what they were inspired with.

Al-Hasan al-Basri said: God, mighty and majestic, inspired that in them. Al-Suddi said: He cast that into their hearts.

It is also possible that what is meant is: "When I revealed to them through your mediation," so that you called them to belief in God and in His messenger, and they responded to you and submitted and followed you, saying: {"We have believed, and bear witness that we are submitters (Muslims)"}.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يُذَكُرُ تَعَالَى مَا امْتَنَّ بِهِ عَلَى عَبْدِهِ وَرَسُولِهِ عِيسَى ابْنِ مَرْيَمَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ مِمَّا أَجْرَاهُ عَلَى يَدَيْهِ مِنَ الْمُعْجِزَاتِ وَخَوَارِقِ الْعَادَاتِ، فَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿إِذْ قَالَ اللَّهُ يَا عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ اذْكُرْ نِعْمَتِي عَلَيْكَ﴾ أَيْ: فِي خَلْقِي إِيَّاكَ مِنْ أُمٍّ بِلَا ذَكَرٍ، وَجَعْلِي إِيَّاكَ آيَةً وَدَلَالَةً قَاطِعَةً عَلَى كَمَالِ قُدْرَتِي عَلَى الْأَشْيَاءِ ﴿وَعَلى وَالِدَتِكَ﴾ حَيْثُ جَعلتُكَ لَهَا بُرْهَانًا عَلَى بَرَاءَتِهَا مِمَّا نَسَبَهُ الظَّالِمُونَ الْجَاهِلُونَ إِلَيْهَا مِنَ الْفَاحِشَةِ، ﴿إِذْ أَيَّدْتُكَ بِرُوحِ الْقُدُسِ﴾ وَهُوَ جِبْرِيلُ، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، وَجَعَلْتُكَ نَبِيًّا دَاعِيًا إِلَى اللَّهِ فِي صِغَرِكَ وَكِبَرِكَ، فَأَنْطَقْتُكَ فِي الْمَهْدِ صَغِيرًا، فَشَهِدْتَ بِبَرَاءَةِ أَمِّكَ مِنْ كُلِّ عَيْبٍ، وَاعْتَرَفْتَ لِي بِالْعُبُودِيَّةِ، وَأَخْبَرْتَ عَنْ رِسَالَتِي إِيَّاكَ وَدَعْوَتَكَ إِلَى عِبَادَتِي؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿تُكَلِّمُ النَّاسَ فِي الْمَهْدِ وَكَهْلا﴾ أَيْ: تَدْعُو إِلَى اللَّهِ النَّاسَ فِي صِغَرِكَ وَكِبَرِكَ. وَضِمْنُ "تُكَلِّمُ" تَدْعُو؛ لِأَنَّ كَلَامَهُ النَّاسَ فِي كُهُولَتِهِ لَيْسَ بِأَمْرٍ عَجِيبٍ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَإِذْ عَلَّمْتُكَ الْكِتَابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ﴾ أَيِ: الْخَطَّ وَالْفَهْمَ ﴿وَالتَّوْرَاةَ﴾ وَهِيَ الْمُنَزَّلَةُ عَلَى مُوسَى بْنِ عِمْرَانَ الْكِلِيمِ، وَقَدْ يَرِدُ لَفْظُ التَّوْرَاةِ فِي الْحَدِيثِ ويُرَاد بِهِ مَا هُوَ أَعَمُّ مِنْ ذَلِكَ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَإِذْ تَخْلُقُ مِنَ الطِّينِ كَهَيْئَةِ الطَّيْرِ بِإِذْنِي﴾ أَيْ: تُصَوِّرُهُ وَتُشَكِّلُهُ عَلَى هَيْئَةِ الطَّائِرِ بِإِذْنِي لَكَ فِي ذَلِكَ فَيَكُونُ طَائِرًا بِإِذْنِي، أَيْ: فَتَنْفُخُ فِي تِلْكَ الصُّورَةِ الَّتِي شَكَّلْتَهَا بِإِذْنِي لَكَ فِي ذَلِكَ، فَتَكُونُ طيرًا ذَا رُوحٍ بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ وَخَلْقِهِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَتُبْرِئُ الأكْمَهَ وَالأبْرَصَ بِإِذْنِي﴾ قَدْ تَقَدَّمَ الْكَلَامُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ فِي سُورَةِ آلِ عِمْرَانَ بِمَا أَغْنَى عَنْ إِعَادَتِهِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَإِذْ تُخْرِجُ الْمَوْتَى بِإِذْنِي﴾ أَيْ: تَدْعُوهُمْ فَيَقُومُونَ مِنْ قُبُورِهِمْ بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ وَقُدْرَتِهِ، وَإِرَادَتِهِ وَمَشِيئَتِهِ.

وَقَدْ قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا مَالِكُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ طَلْحَةَ -يَعْنِي ابْنَ مُصَرِّف-عَنْ أَبِي بِشْر، عَنْ أَبِي الْهُذَيْلِ قَالَ: كَانَ عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، إِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يُحَيِيَ الْمَوْتَى صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ، يَقْرَأُ فِي الْأُولَى: ﴿تَبَارَكَ الَّذِي بِيَدِهِ الْمُلْكُ﴾ [سُورَةَ الْمُلْكِ] ، وَفِي الثَّانِيَةِ: ﴿الم. تَنزيلُ الْكِتَابِ﴾

[سُورَةَ السَّجْدَةِ] . فَإِذَا فَرَغَ مِنْهُمَا مَدَحَ اللَّهَ وَأَثْنَى عَلَيْهِ، ثُمَّ دَعَا بِسَبْعَةِ أَسْمَاءَ: يَا قَدِيمُ، يَا خَفِيُّ، يَا دَائِمُ، يَا فَرْدُ، يَا وَتْرُ، يَا أَحَدُ، يَا صَمَدُ -وَكَانَ إِذَا أَصَابَتْهُ شِدَّةٌ دَعَا بِسَبْعَةٍ أُخَرَ: يَا حَيُّ، يَا قَيُّومُ، يَا اللَّهُ، يَا رَحْمَنُ، يَا ذَا الْجَلَالِ وَالْإِكْرَامِ، يَا نُورَ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ، وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا وَرَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ، يَا رَبِّ.

وَهَذَا أَثَرٌ عَجِيبٌ جِدًّا. 

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَإِذْ كَفَفْتُ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ عَنْكَ إِذْ جِئْتَهُمْ بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ فَقَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْهُمْ إِنْ هَذَا إِلا سِحْرٌ مُبِينٌ﴾ أَيْ: وَاذْكُرْ نِعْمَتِي عَلَيْكَ فِي كَفِّي إِيَّاهُمْ عَنْكَ حِينَ جِئْتَهُمْ بِالْبَرَاهِينِ وَالْحُجَجِ الْقَاطِعَةِ عَلَى نُبُوَّتِكَ وَرِسَالَتِكَ مِنَ اللَّهِ إِلَيْهِمْ، فَكَذَّبُوكَ وَاتَّهَمُوكَ بِأَنَّكَ سَاحِرٌ، وَسَعَوْا فِي قَتْلِكَ وَصَلْبِكَ، فَنَجَّيْتُكَ مِنْهُمْ، وَرَفَعْتُكَ إليَّ، وَطَهَّرْتُكَ مِنْ دَنَسِهِمْ، وَكَفَيْتُكَ شَرَّهُمْ. وَهَذَا يَدُلُّ عَلَى أَنَّ هَذَا الِامْتِنَانَ كَانَ مِنَ اللَّهِ إِلَيْهِ بَعْدَ رَفْعِهِ إِلَى السَّمَاءِ الدُّنْيَا، أَوْ يَكُونُ هَذَا الِامْتِنَانُ وَاقِعًا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ، وَعَبَّرَ عَنْهُ بِصِيغَةِ الْمَاضِي دَلَالَةً عَلَى وُقُوعِهِ لَا مَحَالَةَ. وَهَذَا مِنْ أَسْرَارِ الْغُيُوبِ الَّتِي أَطْلَعَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهَا رَسُولَهُ مُحَمَّدًا ﷺ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَإِذْ أَوْحَيْتُ إِلَى الْحَوَارِيِّينَ أَنْ آمِنُوا بِي وَبِرَسُولِي﴾ وَهَذَا أَيْضًا مِنَ الِامْتِنَانِ عَلَيْهِ، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، بِأَنْ جَعْلَ لَهُ أَصْحَابًا وَأَنْصَارًا. ثُمَّ قِيلَ: الْمُرَادُ بِهَذَا الْوَحْيِ وَحَيُ إِلْهَامٍ، كَمَا قَالَ: ﴿وَأَوْحَيْنَا إِلَى أُمِّ مُوسَى أَنْ أَرْضِعِيهِ﴾ الْآيَةَ [الْقَصَصِ:٧] ، وَهَذَا وَحَيُ إِلْهَامٍ بِلَا خَوْفٍ، وَكَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَأَوْحَى رَبُّكَ إِلَى النَّحْلِ أَنِ اتَّخِذِي مِنَ الْجِبَالِ بُيُوتًا وَمِنَ الشَّجَرِ وَمِمَّا يَعْرِشُونَ. ثُمَّ كُلِي مِنْ كُلِّ الثَّمَرَاتِ فَاسْلُكِي سُبُلَ رَبِّكِ ذُلُلا﴾ الْآيَةَ [النَّحْلِ:٦٨، ٦٩] . وَهَكَذَا قَالَ بَعْضُ السَّلَفِ فِي هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: ﴿وَإِذْ أَوْحَيْتُ إِلَى الْحَوَارِيِّينَ أَنْ آمِنُوا بِي وَبِرَسُولِي قَالُوا آمَنَّا﴾ [أَيْ: بِاللَّهِ وَبِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ] ﴿وَاشْهَدْ بِأَنَّنَا مُسْلِمُونَ﴾ أَيْ: أُلْهَمُوا ذَلِكَ فَامْتَثَلُوا مَا أُلْهَمُوا.

قَالَ الْحَسَنُ الْبَصْرِيُّ: أَلْهَمَهُمُ اللَّهُ. عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ذَلِكَ، وَقَالَ السُّدِّي: قَذَفَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ ذَلِكَ.

وَيُحْتَمَلُ أَنْ يَكُونَ الْمُرَادُ: وَإِذْ أَوْحَيْتُ إِلَيْهِمْ بِوَاسِطَتِكَ، فَدَعَوْتَهُمْ إِلَى الْإِيمَانِ بِاللَّهِ وَبِرَسُولِهِ، وَاسْتَجَابُوا لَكَ وَانْقَادُوا وَتَابَعُوكَ، فَقَالُوا: ﴿آمَنَّا وَاشْهَدْ بِأَنَّنَا مُسْلِمُونَ﴾

---

## 5:112-115 — verses 112-115 (shared commentary)

### Translation

This is the story of the Table, and to it the surah is attributed, being called "Surat al-Ma'idah" (the Table). It is one of the favors that God bestowed upon His servant and messenger Jesus, peace be upon him, when He answered his prayer for its descent, so God sent it down as a sign, a dazzling miraculous proof, and a decisive argument.

Some of the imams have mentioned that the story of the Table is not recorded in the Gospel, and that the Christians know of it only through the Muslims. God knows best.

As for His saying, {When the disciples said}, and they were the followers of Jesus, peace be upon him: {"O Jesus, son of Mary, is your Lord able"}, this is the reading of many; others read it as "hal tastati'u rabbaka" (are you able [to ask] your Lord), meaning: are you able to ask your Lord {to send down upon us a table from heaven}.

The ma'idah (table) is a spread with food upon it. Some have mentioned that they asked for this only because of their need and poverty, so they asked that a table descend upon them every day from which they could sustain themselves and gain strength for worship.

He said: {"Fear God, if you are believers"}, meaning: the Messiah, peace be upon him, answered them saying: fear God, and do not ask for this, for it may become a trial for you; and place your trust in God for seeking provision, if you are believers.

{They said, "We wish to eat from it}, meaning: we are in need of eating from it, {and our hearts be reassured}, when we witness its descent as provision for us from heaven, {and know that you have indeed told us the truth}, meaning: and our faith in you increase, and our certainty of your message, {and we be among the witnesses to it}, meaning: and we testify that it is a sign from God, and a proof and argument for your prophethood and the truth of what you have brought.

{Jesus, son of Mary, said, "O God, our Lord, send down upon us a table from heaven to be for us a festival for the first of us and the last of us}. Al-Suddi said: meaning, we will take that day on which it descends as a festival that we and those who come after us will honor. Sufyan al-Thawri said: meaning a day on which we pray. Qatadah said: they wanted it to be for their descendants after them. It is reported from Salman al-Farisi: an admonition for us and for those who come after us. It is also said: sufficient for the first of us and the last of us.

{and a sign from You}, meaning: a proof that You establish of Your power over all things, and of Your answering my supplication, so that they believe me in what I convey from You, {and provide for us}, meaning: from Your presence, a wholesome provision without effort or toil, {and You are the best of providers." God said, "I will send it down to you, but whoever disbelieves among you after that}, meaning: whoever among your nation, O Jesus, denies it and obstinately opposes it, {then I will punish him with a punishment such as I have not inflicted on anyone among the worlds}, meaning: among the worlds of your own time, as in His saying: {And on the Day the Hour arrives: "Admit the people of Pharaoh into the severest punishment"} [Ghafir: 46], and as in His saying: {Indeed, the hypocrites will be in the lowest depth of the Fire} [al-Nisa: 145].

Ibn Jarir has related, through the route of Awf al-A'rabi, from Abu al-Mughirah al-Qawwas, from Abdullah ibn Amr, who said: the most severely punished of people on the Day of Resurrection are three: the hypocrites, those who disbelieved among the People of the Table, and the people of Pharaoh.

Mention of reports transmitted from the predecessors concerning the descent of the Table upon the disciples:

Abu Ja'far ibn Jarir said: [chain] from Ibn Abbas, that he used to relate concerning Jesus, son of Mary, that he said to the Children of Israel: "Would you fast for God's sake for thirty days, then ask Him, and He will give you what you ask? For the reward of the worker is due from the one for whom he works." So they did so, then they said: "O teacher of good, you told us that the reward of the worker is due from the one for whom he works, and you commanded us to fast for thirty days, and we did so, and we never worked for anyone for thirty days without him feeding us food when we finished. So is your Lord able to send down upon us a table from heaven?" Jesus said: {Fear God, if you are believers.} They said, {We wish to eat from it, and our hearts be reassured, and know that you have indeed told us the truth, and we be among the witnesses to it.} Jesus, son of Mary, said, {O God, our Lord, send down upon us a table from heaven to be for us a festival for the first of us and the last of us, and a sign from You, and provide for us, and You are the best of providers.} God said, {I will send it down to you, but whoever disbelieves among you after that, then I will punish him with a punishment such as I have not inflicted on anyone among the worlds.} He said: the angels then came flying with a table from heaven, upon it seven fish and seven loaves, until they placed it before them, and the last of the people ate from it just as the first of them had eaten.

Thus Ibn Jarir related it, and Ibn Abi Hatim also related it, [chain] from Ibn Shihab, who said: Ibn Abbas used to relate this, and he mentioned something similar.

Ibn Abi Hatim also said: [chain] that Ibn Shihab informed him from Ibn Abbas, that they said to Jesus, son of Mary: "Call upon God to send down upon us a table from heaven." He said: the angels descended carrying a table, upon it seven fish and seven loaves, and the last of the people ate from it just as the first of them had eaten.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] from Ammar ibn Yasir, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "The Table descended from heaven, upon it bread and meat, and they were commanded not to betray trust and not to save any for the next day. But they betrayed trust, and stored some away, and saved it, so they were transformed into apes and swine."

Ibn Jarir related it likewise from al-Hasan ibn Qaz'ah; then Ibn Jarir related it from Ibn Bashshar, from Ibn Abi Adi, from Sa'id, from Qatadah, from Khilas, from Ammar, who said: the Table descended with fruit from the fruits of Paradise upon it, and they were commanded not to betray trust, not to hide any away, and not to store any. He said: but the people betrayed trust, and hid some away, and stored some, so God transformed them into apes and swine.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] from a man of Banu Ijl, who said: I prayed beside Ammar ibn Yasir, and when he finished he said: "Do you know how the matter of the Table of the Children of Israel came about?" I said: "No." He said: "They asked Jesus, son of Mary, for a table on which there would be food from which they could eat without it running out." He said: it was said to them: "It will remain established for you as long as you do not hide any away, or betray trust, or save any for later; but if you do that, then I will punish you with a punishment such as I have not inflicted on anyone among the worlds." He said: their day did not pass before they hid some away, saved some, and betrayed trust, and so they were punished with a punishment such as no one among the worlds had ever been punished with. "And you, O company of Arabs, used to follow after the tails of camels and sheep, then God sent among you a messenger from among yourselves, whose lineage and descent you know, and he informed you that you would prevail over the non-Arabs, and he forbade you to hoard gold and silver. And by God, night and day will not pass before you hoard them both, and God will punish you with a painful punishment."

He also said: [chain] from Ishaq ibn Abdullah, that the Table descended upon Jesus, son of Mary, with seven loaves and seven fish upon it, and they ate from it whatever they wished. He said: then some of them stole from it and said: "Perhaps it will not descend tomorrow." So it was withdrawn.

Al-Awfi said, from Ibn Abbas: it descended upon Jesus, son of Mary, and the disciples, a spread with bread and fish upon it, from which they ate wherever they stopped, whenever they wished. Khusayf said, from Ikrimah and Miqsam, from Ibn Abbas: the Table was a fish and loaves of bread. Mujahid said: it was food that would descend upon them wherever they stopped. Abu Abd al-Rahman al-Sulami said: the Table descended as bread and fish. Atiyyah al-Awfi said: the Table was a fish that had the taste of everything.

Wahb ibn Munabbih said: He sent it down from heaven upon the Children of Israel, and every day there would descend upon them on that table fruits of Paradise, and they ate whatever they wished of various kinds. Four thousand of them would sit at it, and when they finished eating, God would replace it with the like of it for the same number again. And they remained upon that for as long as God, Mighty and Majestic, willed.

Wahb ibn Munabbih said: A loaf of barley bread and some small fish were sent down upon them, and God filled between their layers with blessing, so that a group would eat and then leave, then others would come and eat and then leave, until all of them had eaten and there was still surplus remaining.

Al-A'mash said, from Muslim, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr: Everything was sent down upon it except meat.

Sufyan al-Thawri said, from Ata' ibn al-Sa'ib, from Zadhan and Maysarah, and Jarir, from Ata', from Maysarah, who said: When the table was set down for the Children of Israel, hands would reach for every kind of food upon it except meat.

And from Ikrimah: The bread of the table was made of rice. Ibn Abi Hatim narrated it.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: Ja'far ibn Ali informed us, in what he wrote to me, [chain] Isma'il ibn Abi Uways told us, Abu Abdullah Abd al-Qudus ibn Ibrahim ibn Ubaydullah ibn Mirdas al-Abdari, the freedman of the Banu Abd al-Dar, told me, from Ibrahim ibn Umar, from Wahb ibn Munabbih, from Abu Uthman al-Nahdi, from Salman al-Khayr, that he said: When the disciples asked Jesus son of Mary (peace be upon him) for the table, he disliked that very greatly and said: Be content with what God has provided for you on earth, and do not ask for the table from heaven, for if it is sent down upon you it will be a sign from your Lord, and Thamud were destroyed only because they asked their prophet for a sign, and they were tried by it until their destruction came through it. But they refused anything except that he bring it to them, and that is why they said: {We wish to eat from it and that our hearts be at rest} and the rest of the verse.

When Jesus saw that they refused anything but that he should pray to God for it, he stood and cast off his wool garment and put on black hair-cloth, a cloak of hair and an outer garment of hair, then he performed ablution and washed himself, and entered his place of prayer and prayed as long as God willed. When he finished his prayer, he stood facing the qiblah, aligning his feet until they were even, joining heel to heel and lining up the toes, placing his right hand over his left above his chest, lowering his gaze, bowing his head in humility. Then he let his eyes stream with weeping, and his tears kept flowing down his cheeks and dripping from the ends of his beard until the ground before his face was moistened from his humility. When he saw that, he called upon God and said: {O God, our Lord, send down upon us a table from heaven}. So God sent down upon them a red spread between two clouds, one cloud above it and one cloud beneath it, and they watched it in the air descending from the vault of heaven, falling down toward them, while Jesus wept in fear because of the conditions God had set upon them regarding it, among them that He would punish whoever disbelieved in it after its descent with a punishment He had not inflicted on anyone else among created beings, and he called upon God from his place, saying: O God, make it a mercy, my God do not make it a punishment, my God how many wondrous things have I asked of You and You have granted me, my God make us among those grateful to You, my God I seek refuge in You from its being sent down in wrath and as recompense, my God make it safety and well-being, and do not make it a trial or a disfigurement.

He kept praying until the spread settled before Jesus, while the disciples and his companions were around him, finding a pleasant fragrance the like of which they had never found before at any time. Jesus and the disciples fell down prostrate before God in gratitude for what He had provided them from where they had not expected, and He showed them in it a great sign, full of wonder and lesson. The Jews came forward looking, and they saw an astonishing thing that left them in grief and distress, then they turned away in intense rage. Jesus, the disciples and his companions came forward and sat around the spread, and upon it was a covered cloth. Jesus said: Who among us is boldest in uncovering the cloth from this spread, most confident in himself, and best in trial before his Lord? Let him uncover this sign so that we may see it, praise our Lord for it, mention His name over it, and eat from the provision He has given us. The disciples said: O Spirit of God and His Word, you are more entitled to that, and more deserving of uncovering it. So Jesus (peace be upon him) rose and performed a fresh ablution, then entered his place of prayer and prayed several units of prayer as before, then wept a long weeping and asked God to permit him to uncover it, and to make it, for him and his people, a blessing and provision. Then he turned and sat by the spread and took hold of the cloth, and said: "In the name of God, the best of providers," and uncovered the spread. There upon it was a huge grilled fish, without scales upon it, and without bones in its belly, with fat flowing from it in a stream, arranged around it were vegetables of every kind except leeks, at its head was vinegar, at its tail was salt, and around the vegetables were five loaves, on one of them olives, on another dates, and on another five pomegranates.

Simon, the head of the disciples, said to Jesus: O Spirit of God and His Word, is this food of this world or food of Paradise? He said: Has the time not yet come for you to take heed from the signs you see, and to stop probing into questions? How I fear for you that you will be punished on account of the cause of the sending down of this sign! Simon said: By the God of Israel, I did not intend by it any real question, O son of the truthful woman. Jesus (peace be upon him) said: It is nothing of what you see, neither food of Paradise nor food of this world, it is only something God originated in the air by supreme, overpowering power. He said to it: "Be," and it was, faster than the blink of an eye. So eat of what you asked for, in the name of God, and praise your Lord for it, He will increase you from it and add to you, for He is the Originator, the Powerful, the Grateful.

They said: O Spirit of God and His Word, we would love for you to show us a sign within this sign. Jesus said: Glory be to God! Were you not satisfied with what you saw in this sign, that you ask for another sign within it? Then Jesus (peace be upon him) turned to the fish and said: O fish, return by God's permission alive as you were. God brought it to life by His power, and it stirred and returned by God's permission alive and fresh, licking its lips as a lion licks its lips, its eyes turning with a gleam, and its scales returned upon it. The people were frightened by it and drew back. When Jesus saw that from them, he said: What is the matter with you, that you ask for a sign, and then when your Lord shows it to you, you dislike it? How I fear for you that you will be punished for what you do! O fish, return by God's permission as you were. So it returned by God's permission grilled as it had been in its original form.

They said to Jesus: You, O Spirit of God, be the one who begins eating from it, then we after you. Jesus said: God forbid that! The one who asked for it should be the one to begin eating from it. When the disciples and their companions saw their prophet refraining from it, they feared that its coming down might be a matter of wrath, and that eating it might bring disfigurement, so they avoided it. When Jesus saw that, he called to it the poor and the disabled, and said: Eat of the provision of your Lord and the prayer of your prophet, and praise God who sent it down for you, so that its pleasure will be yours and its punishment upon others than you, and begin your eating with the name of God and end it with praise of God. So they did, and a thousand three hundred people ate from it, men and women together, each one of them leaving it satisfied and belching. Jesus and the disciples looked, and what was upon it was in the same state as when it had been sent down from heaven, nothing of it diminished, then it was raised up to heaven while they watched. Every poor person who ate from it became well off, and every disabled person who ate from it was healed, and they remained wealthy and healthy until they left this world.

The disciples and their companions who had refused to eat from it regretted it with a regret from which their eyelashes streamed, and the sorrow of it remained in their hearts until the day of their death. He said: After that, whenever the table came down, the Children of Israel would come rushing toward it from every place, jostling one another, the rich and the poor, the young and the old, the healthy and the sick, some climbing upon others. When he saw that, he made it come at intervals, descending one day and not descending the next. They remained in that state for forty days, it descending upon them every other day, at the rising of the forenoon, and it would remain set out for eating until, when they rose, it would lift away from them by God's permission into the vault of heaven, while they watched its shadow on the ground until it disappeared from their sight.

He said: God revealed to His prophet Jesus (peace be upon him) that he should make My provision, the table, for orphans, the poor, and the disabled, rather than for the wealthy among the people. When he did that, the wealthy among the people became doubtful of it and belittled it, until they doubted it within themselves and cast doubt on it among the people, and they spread abroad about it what was foul and objectionable. Satan achieved his purpose with them and cast his whispering into the hearts of those who doubted, until they said to Jesus: "Tell us about the table, and its descent from heaven, is it true? For many of our people have doubted it." Jesus (peace be upon him) said: "You are destroyed, by the God of the Messiah! You asked your prophet to request the table for you from your Lord, and when He did so and sent it down to you as a mercy and a provision, and showed you in it signs and lessons, you denied it and doubted it. So receive the glad tidings of punishment, for it shall surely descend upon you, unless God shows you mercy."

And God revealed to Jesus: "I take those who deny by My condition, for I shall punish whoever among them disbelieves in the table after its descent with a punishment I shall not inflict upon anyone else among all creation." He said: When those who doubted it settled into their beds that evening, in the finest condition, with their wives, feeling secure, then at the end of the night God transformed them into swine, and in the morning they were found scavenging filth among the rubbish heaps.

This is a very gharib (singular) report. Ibn Abi Hatim divided it into pieces in several places within this account, and I have gathered it together so that its sequence would be more complete and whole. God, glorified and exalted, knows best.

All of these reports indicate that the table did descend upon the Children of Israel, in the days of Jesus son of Mary, as God's answer to his prayer, as is indicated by the apparent sense of this passage in the Mighty Qur'an: {God said, I will send it down to you} and the rest of the verse.

Some have said that it did not descend. Layth ibn Abi Sulaym related from Mujahid, concerning His saying {Send down upon us a table from heaven}, that he said: it is a parable given, and nothing was sent down.

This was related by Ibn Abi Hatim and Ibn Jarir. Then Ibn Jarir said: [chain] al-Harith told me, al-Qasim, that is, Ibn Sallam, told us, Hajjaj told us, from Ibn Jurayj, from Mujahid, who said: a table with food on it, but they refused it when the punishment was presented to them if they disbelieved, so they refused to have it sent down upon them.

He also said: [chain] Ibn al-Muthanna told us, Muhammad ibn Ja'far told us, Shu'bah told us, from Mansur ibn Zadhan, from al-Hasan, that he said concerning the table: it did not descend.

And [chain] Bishr told us, Yazid told us, Sa'id told us, from Qatadah, who said: al-Hasan used to say: when it was said to them, {but whoever among you disbelieves thereafter, I shall punish him with a punishment I shall not inflict upon anyone else among all creation}, they said: "We have no need of it," so it did not descend.
